EDCS-00380828.text_edition: \n Iovi Optimo Maximo Marcus Ulpius Valens beneficiarius consularis missus honesta missione Barbaro et Regulo consulibus votum solvit libens merito\n
EDCS-00380828.translation: To Jupiter Best and Greatest, Marcus Ulpius Valens, a consular beneficiarius granted an honorable discharge, willingly and deservedly fulfilled his vow, when Barbarus and Regulus were consuls.
EDCS-00380828.summary: This votive inscription records Marcus Ulpius Valens fulfilling a vow to Jupiter.

EDCS-00380829.text_edition: \n Iovi Optimo Maximo Iunoni et Minervae ceteris dis deabusque Acestius Victor beneficiarius consularis legionis II adiutricis X Kalendas Septembres Materno et Bradua consulibus\n
EDCS-00380829.translation: To Jupiter Best and Greatest, Juno, and Minerva, and all the other gods and goddesses, Acestius Victor, a consular beneficiarius of the Second Adjutrix Legion, willingly and deservedly fulfilled his vow on the 9th day before the Kalends of September, when Materno and Bradua were consuls.
EDCS-00380829.summary: This votive inscription records Acestius Victor fulfilling a vow to multiple deities.

EDCS-00380830.text_edition: \n Iovi Optimo Maximo Caius Aemilius Titianus beneficiarius consularis ex legione II adiutrice votum solvit libens merito Crispino et Aeliano consulibus\n
EDCS-00380830.translation: To Jupiter Best and Greatest, Caius Aemilius Titianus, a consular beneficiarius from the Second Adiutrix Legion, willingly and deservedly fulfilled his vow, when Crispino and Aelianus were consuls.
EDCS-00380830.summary: This votive inscription records Caius Aemilius Titianus fulfilling a vow to Jupiter.

EDCS-00380831.text_edition: \n Iovi Optimo Maximo ceterisque dis inmortalibus Tiberius Claudius Apollinaris beneficiarius consularis ex legione II adiutrice votum solvit libens merito duobus Silanis consulibus\n
EDCS-00380831.translation: To Jupiter Best and Greatest and all the immortal gods, Tiberius Claudius Apollinaris, a consular beneficiarius from the Second Adiutrix Legion, willingly and deservedly fulfilled his vow, when the two Silani were consuls.
EDCS-00380831.summary: This votive inscription records Tiberius Claudius Apollinaris fulfilling a vow to Jupiter and other gods.

EDCS-00380832.text_edition: \n Marcus Ulpius Frequentinus beneficiarius consularis ex voto posuit Pedone et Bradua consulibus\n
EDCS-00380832.translation: Marcus Ulpius Frequentinus, a consular beneficiarius, set this up in fulfillment of a vow, when Pedo and Bradua were consuls.
EDCS-00380832.summary: This votive inscription records Marcus Ulpius Frequentinus setting up a monument in fulfillment of a vow.

EDCS-00380833.text_edition: \n Iovi Optimo Maximo et Genio Imperatoris Marcus Ulpius Frequentinus beneficiarius consularis legionis II adiutricis iterata statione ex voto posuit Tertullo et Clemente consulibus\n
EDCS-00380833.translation: To Jupiter Best and Greatest and the Genius of the Emperor, Marcus Ulpius Frequentinus, a consular beneficiarius of the Second Adjutrix Legion, while serving a second term, set this up in fulfillment of a vow, when Tertullus and Clemens were consuls.
EDCS-00380833.summary: This votive inscription records Marcus Ulpius Frequentinus fulfilling a vow to Jupiter and the Emperor's Genius.

EDCS-00380834.text_edition: \n Iovi Optimo Maximo et Deo Silvano pro salute et reditu domminorum nnostrorum Impperatorum Luci Septimi Severi et Marci Aureli Antonini Auggustorum et Luci Septimi Getae Caesaris Titus Aelius Secundus beneficiarius consularis iterata statione sub Claudio Claudiano consulare votum solvit libens merito Anullino II consule\n
EDCS-00380834.translation: To Jupiter Best and Greatest and the God Silvanus, for the health and safe return of our Lords Emperors Lucius Septimius Severus and Marcus Aurelius Antoninus Augusti, and of Lucius Septimius Geta Caesar, Titus Aelius Secundus, a consular beneficiarius serving a second term, willingly and deservedly fulfilled his vow, when Claudius Claudianus was consul and Anullinus was consul for the second time.
EDCS-00380834.summary: This votive inscription records Titus Aelius Secundus fulfilling a vow to Jupiter and Silvanus for the emperors' well-being.

EDCS-00380835.text_edition: \n Iovi Optimo Maximo pro salute Auggustorum agens in statione sub Baebio Caeciliano legato Auggustorum pro praetore Tiberius Claudius Aeternalis beneficiarius consularis in patria sua votum solvit libens merito duobus Augustis consulibus\n
EDCS-00380835.translation: To Jupiter Best and Greatest, for the health of the Augusti, Tiberius Claudius Aeternalis, a consular beneficiarius serving in his native land, willingly and deservedly fulfilled his vow, while acting in a post under Baebius Caecilianus, legate of the Augusti pro praetore, when the two Augusti were consuls.
EDCS-00380835.summary: This votive inscription records Tiberius Claudius Aeternalis fulfilling a vow to Jupiter for the health of the emperors.

EDCS-00380836.text_edition: \n Iovi Optimo Maximo et Genio dominorum nnostorum Auggustorum et Getae Caesaris Caius Marcius Lupianus beneficiarius consularis legionis II adiutricis votum solvit libens merito P<l=I>autiano et Geta consulibus \n
EDCS-00380836.translation: To Jupiter Best and Greatest and the Genius of our Lords the Augusti and of Geta Caesar, Caius Marcius Lupianus, a consular beneficiarius of the Second Adjutrix Legion, willingly and deservedly fulfilled his vow, when Plautianus and Geta were consuls.
EDCS-00380836.summary: This votive inscription records Caius Marcius Lupianus fulfilling a vow to Jupiter and the emperors' Genius.

EDCS-00380837.text_edition: \n Iovi Optimo Maximo et Geniis Augustorum Publius Aelius Augurinianus beneficiarius consularis legionis II adiutricis Cilone et Libone consulibus\n
EDCS-00380837.translation: To Jupiter Best and Greatest and the Geniuses of the Augusti, Publius Aelius Augurinianus, a consular beneficiarius of the Second Adjutrix Legion, when Cilo and Libo were consuls.
EDCS-00380837.summary: This inscription records Publius Aelius Augurinianus, a consular beneficiarius, dedicating something to Jupiter and the emperors' Geniuses.

EDCS-00380838.text_edition: \n Iovi Optimo Maximo et Genio huius loci patriae suae Aulus Cassius Clemens beneficiarius consularis legionis II adiutricis votum solvit Imperatore Antonino II et Geta Caesare consulibus\n
EDCS-00380838.translation: To Jupiter, Best and Greatest, and to the Genius of this place and his fatherland, Aulus Cassius Clemens, a volunteer officer (beneficiarius consularis) of the II Adjutrix Legion, paid his vow, during the consulship of Emperor Antoninus II and Caesar Geta.
EDCS-00380838.summary: This inscription records a votive offering to Jupiter and the Genius of the place by Aulus Cassius Clemens, a beneficiarius consularis of the II Adjutrix Legion, dated to the consulship of Antoninus II and Geta.

EDCS-00380839.text_edition: \n Iovi Optimo Maximo et Geniis ddominorum nnostrorum Auggustorum Marcus Valerius Reburrus beneficiarius consularis legionis II adiutricis Albino et Aemiliano consulibus votum solvit libens merito\n
EDCS-00380839.translation: To Jupiter, Best and Greatest, and to the Geniuses of our Lords the Augusti, Marcus Valerius Reburrus, a volunteer officer (beneficiarius consularis) of the II Adjutrix Legion, paid his vow willingly and deservedly, during the consulship of Albinus and Aemilianus.
EDCS-00380839.summary: This inscription records a votive offering to Jupiter and the Geniuses of the Augusti by Marcus Valerius Reburrus, a beneficiarius consularis of the II Adjutrix Legion, dated to the consulship of Albinus and Aemilianus.

EDCS-00380840.text_edition: \n Iovi Optimo Maximo pro salute Imppperatorum Severi et Antonini Auggustorum et Getae Caesaris Marcus Valerius Reburrus beneficiarius consularis iterata statione in patria sua votum solvit libens merito Apro et Maximo consulibus\n
EDCS-00380840.translation: To Jupiter, Best and Greatest, for the welfare of the Emperors Severus and Antoninus Augusti and Caesar Geta, Marcus Valerius Reburrus, a volunteer officer (beneficiarius consularis) of the II Adjutrix Legion, serving a second time in his fatherland, paid his vow willingly and deservedly, during the consulship of Apro and Maximus.
EDCS-00380840.summary: This inscription records a votive offering for the welfare of the emperors by Marcus Valerius Reburrus, a beneficiarius consularis of the II Adjutrix Legion, dated to the consulship of Apro and Maximus.

EDCS-00380841.text_edition: \n Iovi Optimo Maximo et Genio huius loci patriae suae Titus Aurelius Valentinus beneficiarius legionis II adiutricis votum solvit Imperatore Antonino Augusto III et Geta Caesare II consulibus\n
EDCS-00380841.translation: To Jupiter, Best and Greatest, and to the Genius of this place and his fatherland, Titus Aurelius Valentinus, a volunteer officer (beneficiarius) of the II Adjutrix Legion, paid his vow, during the consulship of Emperor Antoninus Augustus III and Caesar Geta II.
EDCS-00380841.summary: This inscription records a votive offering to Jupiter and the Genius of the place by Titus Aurelius Valentinus, a beneficiarius of the II Adjutrix Legion, dated to the consulship of Antoninus Augustus III and Caesar Geta II.

EDCS-00380842.text_edition: \n Iovi Optimo Maximo pro salute dddominorum nnn(ostrorum) Auggg(ustorum) Publius Aelius Frequentianus beneficiarius consularis legionis II adiutricis Pompeiano et Avito consulibus votum solvit libens merito\n
EDCS-00380842.translation: To Jupiter, Best and Greatest, for the welfare of our Lords the Augusti, Publius Aelius Frequentianus, a volunteer officer (beneficiarius consularis) of the II Adjutrix Legion, paid his vow willingly and deservedly, during the consulship of Pompeianus and Avitus.
EDCS-00380842.summary: This inscription records a votive offering for the welfare of the emperors by Publius Aelius Frequentianus, a beneficiarius consularis of the II Adjutrix Legion, dated to the consulship of Pompeianus and Avitus.

EDCS-00380843.text_edition: \n Iovi Optimo Maximo et Genio [] Ulpius Vitalis et Iulius Secundianus bbenefficiarii consularis Grato et Seleuco consulibus votum solverunt libentes merito\n
EDCS-00380843.translation: To Jupiter, Best and Greatest, and to the Genius of [the place], Ulpius Vitalis and Iulius Secundianus, volunteer officers (beneficiarii consulares), paid their vows willingly and deservedly, during the consulship of Gratus and Seleucus.
EDCS-00380843.summary: This inscription records a votive offering to Jupiter and the Genius of the place by Ulpius Vitalis and Iulius Secundianus, beneficiarii consulares, dated to the consulship of Gratus and Seleucus.

EDCS-00380844.text_edition: \n I et Genio Imperatoris [domini nostri Severi Ale]xandri Augusti lv(ius) Vitalis et Iulius Secundianus bbenefficiarii consulares legionis II adiutricis Maximo II et Aeliano consulibus votum solverunt libentes merito\n
EDCS-00380844.translation: To Jupiter and the Genius of the Emperor [our Lord Severus Alexander Augustus], Iulius Vitalis and Iulius Secundianus, volunteer officers (beneficiarii consulares) of the II Adjutrix Legion, paid their vows willingly and deservedly, during the consulship of Maximus II and Aelianus.
EDCS-00380844.summary: This inscription records a votive offering to Jupiter and the Genius of the emperor Severus Alexander by Iulius Vitalis and Iulius Secundianus, beneficiarii consulares of the II Adjutrix Legion, dated to the consulship of Maximus II and Aelianus.

EDCS-00380845.text_edition: \n Iovi Optimo Maximo et Genio Imperatoris Severi Alexandri Augusti Octavius Avitus et Iulius Secundianus bbenefficiarii consulares legionis I et II adiutricis Crispino et Iuliano consulibus merito votum libentes solverunt\n
EDCS-00380845.translation: To Jupiter, Best and Greatest, and to the Genius of the Emperor Severus Alexander Augustus, Octavius Avitus and Iulius Secundianus, volunteer officers (beneficiarii consulares) of the I and II Adjutrix Legions, paid their vows willingly and deservedly, during the consulship of Crispinus and Iulianus.
EDCS-00380845.summary: This inscription records a votive offering to Jupiter and the Genius of the emperor Severus Alexander by Octavius Avitus and Iulius Secundianus, beneficiarii consulares of the I and II Adjutrix Legions, dated to the consulship of Crispinus and Iulianus.

EDCS-00380846.text_edition: \n Iovi Optimo Maximo et Genio huius loci pro salute domini nostri Severi Alexandri Augusti totaeque domus eius Claudius Aurelius et Valerius Caesianus bbenefficiarii consulares votum solverunt libentes merito Modesto et Probo consulibus\n
EDCS-00380846.translation: To Jupiter, Best and Greatest, and to the Genius of this place, for the welfare of our Lord Severus Alexander Augustus and all his household, Claudius Aurelius and Valerius Caesianus, volunteer officers (beneficiarii consulares), paid their vows willingly and deservedly, during the consulship of Modestus and Probus.
EDCS-00380846.summary: This inscription records a votive offering to Jupiter and the Genius of the place for the welfare of the emperor Severus Alexander and his household by Claudius Aurelius and Valerius Caesianus, beneficiarii consulares, dated to the consulship of Modestus and Probus.

EDCS-00380847.text_edition: \n Iovi Optimo Maximo et Genio huius loci pro salute domini nostri Imperatoris Marci Aureli Severi Alexandri Pii Felicis Augusti Aurelius Domitianus et Iulius Potentinus bbenefficiarii consulares Imperatore Severo Alexandro Augusto et Dione consulibus votum solverunt libentes merito\n
EDCS-00380847.translation: To Jupiter, Best and Greatest, and to the Genius of this place, for the welfare of our Lord the Emperor Marcus Aurelius Severus Alexander Pius Felix Augustus, Aurelius Domitianus and Iulius Potentinus, volunteer officers (beneficiarii consulares), paid their vows willingly and deservedly, during the consulship of Severus Alexander Augustus and Dion.
EDCS-00380847.summary: This inscription records a votive offering to Jupiter and the Genius of the place for the welfare of the emperor Severus Alexander by Aurelius Domitianus and Iulius Potentinus, beneficiarii consulares, dated to the consulship of Severus Alexander and Dion.

EDCS-00380848.text_edition: \n Iovi Optimo Maximo et Genio domini nostri [Imperatoris Severi] [Alexandri] [Augusti] Domitianus et Florentinus bbenefficiarii consulares ex voto posuerunt Agricola Clementino consulibus\n
EDCS-00380848.translation: To Jupiter Optimus Maximus and the Genius of our lord Emperor Severus Alexander Augustus, Domitianus and Florentinus, consular beneficiaries, set this up in fulfillment of a vow, when Agricola and Clementinus were consuls.
EDCS-00380848.summary: This is a votive dedication to Jupiter and the Emperor's Genius by two consular beneficiaries, likely dating to the consulship of Agricola and Clementinus in the early 3rd century CE.

EDCS-00380849.text_edition: \n Iovi Optimo Maximo Aelius Dignianus et Titius Faustinus bbenefficiarii consulares ex voto posuerunt Agricola et Clementino consulibus\n
EDCS-00380849.translation: To Jupiter Optimus Maximus, Aelius Dignianus and Titius Faustinus, consular beneficiaries, set this up in fulfillment of a vow, when Agricola and Clementinus were consuls.
EDCS-00380849.summary: This is a votive dedication to Jupiter by two consular beneficiaries, likely dating to the consulship of Agricola and Clementinus in the early 3rd century CE.

EDCS-00380850.text_edition: \n Iovi Optimo Maximo pro salutem domini nostri Imperatoris Caesa[ris] [Marci Aureli] [Severi] [Alexandri] [Augusti] Titius Faustinus et Lautius Emeritus bbenefficiarii consulares ex voto posuerunt Pompeiano et Paeligniano consulibus\n
EDCS-00380850.translation: To Jupiter Optimus Maximus for the safety of our lord Emperor Caesar Marcus Aurelius Severus Alexander Augustus, Titius Faustinus and Lautius Emeritus, consular beneficiaries, set this up in fulfillment of a vow, when Pompeianus and Paelignianus were consuls.
EDCS-00380850.summary: This is a votive dedication to Jupiter for the Emperor's safety by two consular beneficiaries, likely dating to the consulship of Pompeianus and Paelignianus in the early 3rd century CE.

EDCS-00380851.text_edition: \n Iovi Optimo Maximo Titus Aconius Karus beneficiarius consularis votum solvit libens merito\n
EDCS-00380851.translation: To Jupiter Optimus Maximus, Titus Aconius Karus, consular beneficiary, willingly and deservedly fulfilled his vow.
EDCS-00380851.summary: This is a votive inscription by Titus Aconius Karus, a consular beneficiary, likely from the 3rd century CE.

EDCS-00380852.text_edition: \n Iovi Optimo Maximo Gaius Annius Quietus agrimensor miles legionis X geminae piae fidelis beneficiarius consularis votum solvit libens merito\n
EDCS-00380852.translation: To Jupiter Optimus Maximus, Gaius Annius Quietus, surveyor and soldier of the Tenth Gemina Legion, pious and faithful, a consular beneficiary, willingly and deservedly fulfilled his vow.
EDCS-00380852.summary: This is a votive inscription by Gaius Annius Quietus, a surveyor and soldier of the Tenth Legion who also served as a consular beneficiary, likely from the 3rd century CE.

EDCS-00380853.text_edition: \n Iovi Optimo Maximo Publius Aelius Porcius beneficiarius consularis votum solvit libens merito\n
EDCS-00380853.translation: To Jupiter Optimus Maximus, Publius Aelius Porcius, consular beneficiary, willingly and deservedly fulfilled his vow.
EDCS-00380853.summary: This is a votive inscription by Publius Aelius Porcius, a consular beneficiary, likely from the 3rd century CE.

EDCS-00380854.text_edition: \n Iovi Optimo Maximo Publius Aelius Sextilianus beneficiarius consularis legionis II adiutricis votum solvit libens merito\n
EDCS-00380854.translation: To Jupiter Optimus Maximus, Publius Aelius Sextilianus, consular beneficiary of the Second Adjutrix Legion, willingly and deservedly fulfilled his vow.
EDCS-00380854.summary: This is a votive inscription by Publius Aelius Sextilianus, a consular beneficiary of the Second Adjutrix Legion, likely from the 3rd century CE.

EDCS-00380856.text_edition: \n Iovi Optimo Maximo Publius Aelius Vegetus beneficiarius consularis votum solvit libens merito\n
EDCS-00380856.translation: To Jupiter Optimus Maximus, Publius Aelius Vegetus, consular beneficiary, willingly and deservedly fulfilled his vow.
EDCS-00380856.summary: This is a votive inscription by Publius Aelius Vegetus, a consular beneficiary, likely from the 3rd century CE.

EDCS-00380857.text_edition: \n Iovi Optimo Maximo et Genio Imperatoris et Genio collegii Sextus Aemilius Victor beneficiarius consularis votum solvit libens merito\n
EDCS-00380857.translation: To Jupiter Optimus Maximus, and the Genius of the Emperor, and the Genius of the collegium, Sextus Aemilius Victor, consular beneficiary, willingly and deservedly fulfilled his vow.
EDCS-00380857.summary: This is a votive inscription by Sextus Aemilius Victor, a consular beneficiary, dedicated to Jupiter, the Emperor's Genius, and the Genius of a collegium, likely from the 3rd century CE.

EDCS-00380858.text_edition: \n Iovi Optimo Maximo Silvano et Libero Patri et Genio Sirmii Marcus Aufidius Victor beneficiarius consularis legionis II adiutricis votum solvit libens merito\n
EDCS-00380858.translation: To Jupiter Optimus Maximus, Silvanus and Liber Pater, and the Genius of Sirmium, Marcus Aufidius Victor, consular beneficiary of the Second Adjutrix Legion, willingly and deservedly fulfilled his vow.
EDCS-00380858.summary: This is a votive inscription by Marcus Aufidius Victor, a consular beneficiary of the Second Adjutrix Legion, dedicated to Jupiter and several other deities including Silvanus, Liber Pater, and the Genius of Sirmium, likely from the 3rd century CE.

EDCS-00380859.text_edition: \n Iovi Optimo Maximo et Genio Imperatoris Caesaris [] Aurelius Septimus et Septimius Quintilianus bbenefficiarii consulares\n
EDCS-00380859.translation: To Jupiter Optimus Maximus and the Genius of the Emperor Caesar, Aurelius Septimus and Septimius Quintilianus, consular beneficiaries.
EDCS-00380859.summary: This is a dedication to Jupiter and the Emperor's Genius by two consular beneficiaries, likely setting up a shrine or monument.

EDCS-00380860.text_edition: \n Iovi Optimo Maximo et ceteris Dis Deabus Marcus Aurelius Titullus beneficiarius consularis legionis II adiutricis D C votum solvit libens merito\n
EDCS-00380860.translation: To Jupiter Optimus Maximus and the other gods and goddesses, Marcus Aurelius Titullus, a consular beneficiary of the Second Legion Adjutrix, willingly paid his vow, deserving the reward.
EDCS-00380860.summary: This inscription records Marcus Aurelius Titullus, a consular beneficiary of the Second Legion Adjutrix, fulfilling a vow to Jupiter and other deities.

EDCS-00380861.text_edition: \n Iovi Optimo Maximo Aulus Barbius Sabinus beneficiarius consularis votum solvit libens merito\n
EDCS-00380861.translation: To Jupiter Optimus Maximus, Aulus Barbius Sabinus, a consular beneficiary, willingly paid his vow, deserving the reward.
EDCS-00380861.summary: This is a record of Aulus Barbius Sabinus, a consular beneficiary, fulfilling a vow to Jupiter Optimus Maximus.

EDCS-00380862.text_edition: \n Iovi Optimo Maximo Publius Bassius Pietas beneficiarius consularis legionis II adiutricis piae fidelis votum solvit libens merito\n
EDCS-00380862.translation: To Jupiter Optimus Maximus, Publius Bassius Pietas, a consular beneficiary of the loyal and faithful Second Legion Adjutrix, willingly paid his vow, deserving the reward.
EDCS-00380862.summary: This inscription documents Publius Bassius Pietas, a consular beneficiary of the Second Legion Adjutrix, fulfilling a vow to Jupiter Optimus Maximus.

EDCS-00380863.text_edition: \n Iovi Optimo Maximo Sextus Calavius Maximus beneficiarius consularis legionis II adiutricis votum solvit libens merito\n
EDCS-00380863.translation: To Jupiter Optimus Maximus, Sextus Calavius Maximus, a consular beneficiary of the Second Legion Adjutrix, willingly paid his vow, deserving the reward.
EDCS-00380863.summary: This inscription records Sextus Calavius Maximus, a consular beneficiary of the Second Legion Adjutrix, fulfilling a vow to Jupiter Optimus Maximus.

EDCS-00380864.text_edition: \n Lucius Cassius Praesens beneficiarius Caecili Faustini legati Augusti pro praetore Iovi Optimo Maximo votum solvit libens merito\n
EDCS-00380864.translation: Lucius Cassius Praesens, a consular beneficiary of Caecilius Faustinus, legate of the emperor with proatorial power, willingly paid his vow to Jupiter Optimus Maximus, deserving the reward.
EDCS-00380864.summary: This inscription records Lucius Cassius Praesens, a consular beneficiary serving under the legate Caecilius Faustinus, fulfilling a vow to Jupiter Optimus Maximus.

EDCS-00380865.text_edition: \n Iovi Optimo Maximo Tiberius Claudius Dius beneficiarius consularis votum solvit libens merito\n
EDCS-00380865.translation: To Jupiter Optimus Maximus, Tiberius Claudius Dius, a consular beneficiary, willingly paid his vow, deserving the reward.
EDCS-00380865.summary: This inscription records Tiberius Claudius Dius, a consular beneficiary, fulfilling a vow to Jupiter Optimus Maximus.

EDCS-00380866.text_edition: \n Iovi Optimo Maximo Tiberius Claudius Fortis beneficiarius consularis votum solvit libens merito\n
EDCS-00380866.translation: To Jupiter Optimus Maximus, Tiberius Claudius Fortis, a consular beneficiary, willingly paid his vow, deserving the reward.
EDCS-00380866.summary: This inscription documents Tiberius Claudius Fortis, a consular beneficiary, fulfilling a vow to Jupiter Optimus Maximus.

EDCS-00380867.text_edition: \n Marti Augusto sacrum Tiberius Claudius Fortis beneficiarius consularis votum solvit libens merito\n
EDCS-00380867.translation: Sacred to Mars Augustus, Tiberius Claudius Fortis, a consular beneficiary, willingly paid his vow, deserving the reward.
EDCS-00380867.summary: This inscription records Tiberius Claudius Fortis, a consular beneficiary, dedicating something to Mars Augustus.

EDCS-00380868.text_edition: \n Iovi Optimo Maximo Tiberius Claudius Maximinus beneficiarius consularis legionis II adiutricis piae fidelis votum solvit libens merito\n
EDCS-00380868.translation: To Jupiter Optimus Maximus, Tiberius Claudius Maximinus, a consular beneficiary of the loyal and faithful Second Legion Adjutrix, willingly paid his vow, deserving the reward.
EDCS-00380868.summary: This inscription documents Tiberius Claudius Maximinus, a consular beneficiary of the Second Legion Adjutrix, fulfilling a vow to Jupiter Optimus Maximus.

EDCS-00380869.text_edition: \n Iovi Optimo Maximo Tiberius Claudius Postuminus beneficiarius consularis votum solvit libens merito\n
EDCS-00380869.translation: To Jupiter, Best and Greatest, Tiberius Claudius Postuminus, consular beneficiarius, willingly and deservedly fulfilled his vow.
EDCS-00380869.summary: This inscription records a votive offering by Tiberius Claudius Postuminus to Jupiter, likely from the 2nd or 3rd century CE.

EDCS-00380870.text_edition: \n Marti Tiberius Claudius Postuminus beneficiarius consularis votum solvit libens merito\n
EDCS-00380870.translation: To Mars, Tiberius Claudius Postuminus, consular beneficiarius, willingly and deservedly fulfilled his vow.
EDCS-00380870.summary: This inscription records a votive offering by Tiberius Claudius Postuminus to Mars, likely from the 2nd or 3rd century CE.

EDCS-00380871.text_edition: \n Iovi Optimo Maximo Iunoni Reginae Minervae ceteris Deis sic Deabusque et Genio Flaviae Sirmii Tiberius Claudius Valentinus beneficiarius consularis ex legione IIII Flavia felice factus ex numero frumentariorum votum solvit libens merito\n
EDCS-00380871.translation: To Jupiter, Best and Greatest, Juno Regina, Minerva, to the other Gods and Goddesses, and to the Genius of Flavia Sirmium, Tiberius Claudius Valentinus, consular beneficiarius from the IIII Flavia Felix legion, formerly a frumentarius, willingly and deservedly fulfilled his vow.
EDCS-00380871.summary: This inscription records a complex votive offering by Tiberius Claudius Valentinus, a former frumentarius and soldier of Legio IIII Flavia Felix, to multiple deities and the Genius of Flavia Sirmium.

EDCS-00380872.text_edition: \n Iovi Optimo Maximo Publius Dindius Latinus beneficiarius consularis votum solvit libens merito\n
EDCS-00380872.translation: To Jupiter, Best and Greatest, Publius Dindius Latinus, consular beneficiarius, willingly and deservedly fulfilled his vow.
EDCS-00380872.summary: This inscription records a votive offering by Publius Dindius Latinus to Jupiter, likely from the 2nd or 3rd century CE.

EDCS-00380873.text_edition: \n Iovi Optimo Maximo Marcus Egnatius Magnus beneficiarius consularis votum solvit libens merito\n
EDCS-00380873.translation: To Jupiter, Best and Greatest, Marcus Egnatius Magnus, consular beneficiarius, willingly and deservedly fulfilled his vow.
EDCS-00380873.summary: This inscription records a votive offering by Marcus Egnatius Magnus to Jupiter, likely from the 2nd or 3rd century CE.

EDCS-00380874.text_edition: \n Iovi Optimo Maximo Iunoni Reginae Minervae Fortunae Marti Campestri Dis Deabusque sacrum Titus Flavius Aulus beneficiarius consularis ex numero frumentariorum votum solvit libens merito\n
EDCS-00380874.translation: Sacred to Jupiter, Best and Greatest, Juno Regina, Minerva, Fortuna, and Mars Campestris, and to all the Gods and Goddesses, Titus Flavius Aulus, consular beneficiarius from the frumentarii, willingly and deservedly fulfilled his vow.
EDCS-00380874.summary: This inscription records a votive offering by Titus Flavius Aulus, a frumentarius, to a wide array of deities including Jupiter, Juno, Minerva, Fortuna, and Mars Campestris.

EDCS-00380875.text_edition: \n Iovi Optimo Maximo Titus Flavius Claudianus beneficiarius consularis votum solvit libens merito\n
EDCS-00380875.translation: To Jupiter, Best and Greatest, Titus Flavius Claudianus, consular beneficiarius, willingly and deservedly fulfilled his vow.
EDCS-00380875.summary: This inscription records a votive offering by Titus Flavius Claudianus to Jupiter, likely from the 2nd or 3rd century CE.

EDCS-00380876.text_edition: \n Iovi Optimo Maximo Flavius Romanus beneficiarius consularis\n
EDCS-00380876.translation: To Jupiter, Best and Greatest, Flavius Romanus, consular beneficiarius.
EDCS-00380876.summary: This inscription records a dedication by Flavius Romanus, a consular beneficiarius, to Jupiter, Best and Greatest.

EDCS-00380877.text_edition: \n Iovi Optimo Maximo Caius Gallonius Iustus beneficiarius consularis votum solvit libens merito\n
EDCS-00380877.translation: To Jupiter, Best and Greatest, Caius Gallonius Iustus, consular beneficiarius, willingly and deservedly fulfilled his vow.
EDCS-00380877.summary: This inscription records a votive offering by Caius Gallonius Iustus to Jupiter, likely from the 2nd or 3rd century CE.

EDCS-00380878.text_edition: \n Iovi Optimo Maximo Caius Iulius Aquila beneficiarius consularis votum solvit libens merito\n
EDCS-00380878.translation: To Jupiter, Best and Greatest, Caius Iulius Aquila, consular beneficiarius, willingly and deservedly fulfilled his vow.
EDCS-00380878.summary: This inscription records a votive offering by Caius Iulius Aquila to Jupiter, likely from the 2nd or 3rd century CE.

EDCS-00380879.text_edition: \n Iovi Optimo Maximo et Genio loci Iulius Iulianus et Aelius Viator bbenef<f=E>(iciarii) consularis \n
EDCS-00380879.translation: To Jupiter Best and Greatest, and to the Genius of the place, Iulius Iulianus and Aelius Viator, imperial staff officers, (dedicated this).
EDCS-00380879.summary: This inscription records a dedication to Jupiter and the Genius loci by two imperial staff officers, Iulius Iulianus and Aelius Viator.

EDCS-00380880.text_edition: \n Iovi Optimo Maximo Tiberius Iulius Paulinus beneficiarius consularis votum solvit libens merito\n
EDCS-00380880.translation: To Jupiter Best and Greatest, Tiberius Iulius Paulinus, imperial staff officer, paid his vow willingly and deservedly.
EDCS-00380880.summary: This inscription records Tiberius Iulius Paulinus, an imperial staff officer, fulfilling a vow to Jupiter Optimus Maximus.

EDCS-00380881.text_edition: \n Iovi Optimo Maximo sacrum Caius Iulius Valens beneficiarius consularis votum solvit libens merito\n
EDCS-00380881.translation: Sacred to Jupiter Best and Greatest, Caius Iulius Valens, imperial staff officer, paid his vow willingly and deservedly.
EDCS-00380881.summary: This inscription records Caius Iulius Valens, an imperial staff officer, fulfilling a vow to Jupiter Optimus Maximus.

EDCS-00380882.text_edition: \n Marti Augusto sacrum Caius Iulius Valens beneficiarius consularis votum solvit libens merito\n
EDCS-00380882.translation: Sacred to Mars Augustus, Caius Iulius Valens, imperial staff officer, paid his vow willingly and deservedly.
EDCS-00380882.summary: This inscription records Caius Iulius Valens, an imperial staff officer, fulfilling a vow to Mars Augustus.

EDCS-00380883.text_edition: \n Iovi Optimo Maximo Gaius Licinius Sparsus beneficiarius consularis votum solvit libens merito\n
EDCS-00380883.translation: To Jupiter Best and Greatest, Gaius Licinius Sparsus, imperial staff officer, paid his vow willingly and deservedly.
EDCS-00380883.summary: This inscription records Gaius Licinius Sparsus, an imperial staff officer, fulfilling a vow to Jupiter Optimus Maximus.

EDCS-00380884.text_edition: \n Iovi Optimo Maximo et Genio coloniae Sirmii Lucius Licinius Valens beneficiarius consularis legionis IIII Flaviae votum solvit libens merito\n
EDCS-00380884.translation: To Jupiter Best and Greatest and to the Genius of the colony of Sirmium, Lucius Licinius Valens, imperial staff officer of the Fourth Flavian Legion, paid his vow willingly and deservedly.
EDCS-00380884.summary: This inscription records Lucius Licinius Valens, an imperial staff officer of Legio IIII Flavia, fulfilling a vow to Jupiter and the Genius of Sirmium.

EDCS-00380885.text_edition: \n Iovi Optimo Maximo dis deabusque Lucius Magius Proculus veteranus ex beneficiario consularis missus honesta missione Idibus Decembribus votum solvit hoc die libens merito\n
EDCS-00380885.translation: To Jupiter Best and Greatest, and to the gods and goddesses, Lucius Magius Proculus, a veteran who was discharged honorably from his post as imperial staff officer, paid his vow on this day, the Ides of December, willingly and deservedly.
EDCS-00380885.summary: This inscription records Lucius Magius Proculus, a veteran imperial staff officer, fulfilling a vow on the Ides of December.

EDCS-00380886.text_edition: \n Iovi Optimo Maximo Marcus Popilius Bassus beneficiarius consularis votum solvit libens merito\n
EDCS-00380886.translation: To Jupiter Best and Greatest, Marcus Popilius Bassus, imperial staff officer, paid his vow willingly and deservedly.
EDCS-00380886.summary: This inscription records Marcus Popilius Bassus, an imperial staff officer, fulfilling a vow to Jupiter Optimus Maximus.

EDCS-00380887.text_edition: \n Titus Publicius Flavia Martinus Sirmio beneficiarius consularis ex legione IIII Flavia votum solvit libens merito\n
EDCS-00380887.translation: Titus Publicius Flavia Martinus, from the Fourth Flavian Legion in Sirmium, imperial staff officer, paid his vow willingly and deservedly.
EDCS-00380887.summary: This inscription records Titus Publicius Flavia Martinus, an imperial staff officer from Legio IIII Flavia, fulfilling a vow.

EDCS-00380888.text_edition: \n Iovi Optimo Maximo Titus Quintius Augustalis beneficiarius consularis votum solvit libens merito\n
EDCS-00380888.translation: To Jupiter Best and Greatest, Titus Quintius Augustalis, imperial staff officer, paid his vow willingly and deservedly.
EDCS-00380888.summary: This inscription records Titus Quintius Augustalis, an imperial staff officer, fulfilling a vow to Jupiter Optimus Maximus.

EDCS-00380889.text_edition: \n Iovi Optimo Maximo Lucius Rubrius Macedo beneficiarius consularis votum solvit libens merito\n
EDCS-00380889.translation: To Jupiter Best and Greatest, Lucius Rubrius Macedo, a soldier under the command of a consular legate, willingly and deservedly fulfilled his vow.
EDCS-00380889.summary: This is a votive inscription dedicated to Jupiter Optimus Maximus by Lucius Rubrius Macedo, a beneficiarius consularis, who fulfilled his vow.

EDCS-00380890.text_edition: \n Iovi Optimo Maximo Marcus Ulpius Claudianus beneficiarius consularis votum solvit libens merito\n
EDCS-00380890.translation: To Jupiter Best and Greatest, Marcus Ulpius Claudianus, a soldier under the command of a consular legate, willingly and deservedly fulfilled his vow.
EDCS-00380890.summary: This is a votive inscription dedicated to Jupiter Optimus Maximus by Marcus Ulpius Claudianus, a beneficiarius consularis, who fulfilled his vow.

EDCS-00380891.text_edition: \n Iovi Optimo Maximo Marcus Ulpius Iulianus beneficiarius consularis votum solvit libens merito\n
EDCS-00380891.translation: To Jupiter Best and Greatest, Marcus Ulpius Iulianus, a soldier under the command of a consular legate, willingly and deservedly fulfilled his vow.
EDCS-00380891.summary: This is a votive inscription dedicated to Jupiter Optimus Maximus by Marcus Ulpius Iulianus, a beneficiarius consularis, who fulfilled his vow.

EDCS-00380892.text_edition: \n Iovi Optimo Maximo Marcus Ulpius Ulpia Lucius Ratiaris beneficiarius consularis votum solvit libens merito\n
EDCS-00380892.translation: To Jupiter Best and Greatest, Marcus Ulpius Ulpia Lucius Ratiaris, a soldier under the command of a consular legate, willingly and deservedly fulfilled his vow.
EDCS-00380892.summary: This is a votive inscription dedicated to Jupiter Optimus Maximus by Marcus Ulpius Ulpia Lucius Ratiaris, a beneficiarius consularis, who fulfilled his vow.

EDCS-00380893.text_edition: \n Iovi Optimo Maximo Gaius Vaberius Urbanus beneficiarius consularis legionis II adiutricis votum solvit libens merito\n
EDCS-00380893.translation: To Jupiter Best and Greatest, Gaius Vaberius Urbanus, a soldier under the command of a consular legate of the Second Adjutant Legion, willingly and deservedly fulfilled his vow.
EDCS-00380893.summary: This is a votive inscription dedicated to Jupiter Optimus Maximus by Gaius Vaberius Urbanus, a beneficiarius consularis of Legio II Adjutrix, who fulfilled his vow.

EDCS-00380894.text_edition: \n Iovi Optimo Maximo Caius Valerius Iulianus beneficiarius consularis legionis II adiutricis votum solvit libens merito\n
EDCS-00380894.translation: To Jupiter Best and Greatest, Caius Valerius Iulianus, a soldier under the command of a consular legate of the Second Adjutant Legion, willingly and deservedly fulfilled his vow.
EDCS-00380894.summary: This is a votive inscription dedicated to Jupiter Optimus Maximus by Caius Valerius Iulianus, a beneficiarius consularis of Legio II Adjutrix, who fulfilled his vow.

EDCS-00380895.text_edition: \n Iovi Optimo Maximo Lucius Veturius Maximus beneficiarius consularis votum solvit libens merito\n
EDCS-00380895.translation: To Jupiter Best and Greatest, Lucius Veturius Maximus, a soldier under the command of a consular legate, willingly and deservedly fulfilled his vow.
EDCS-00380895.summary: This is a votive inscription dedicated to Jupiter Optimus Maximus by Lucius Veturius Maximus, a beneficiarius consularis, who fulfilled his vow.

EDCS-00380896.text_edition: \n Iovi Optimo Maximo Vibidius Priscus beneficiarius consularis legionis II adiutricis votum solvit libens merito\n
EDCS-00380896.translation: To Jupiter Best and Greatest, Vibidius Priscus, a soldier under the command of a consular legate of the Second Adjutant Legion, willingly and deservedly fulfilled his vow.
EDCS-00380896.summary: This is a votive inscription dedicated to Jupiter Optimus Maximus by Vibidius Priscus, a beneficiarius consularis of Legio II Adjutrix, who fulfilled his vow.

EDCS-00380897.text_edition: \n Aelius Martinus beneficiarius consularis legionis II adiutricis votum solvit libens merito\n
EDCS-00380897.translation: Aelius Martinus, a soldier under the command of a consular legate of the Second Adjutant Legion, willingly and deservedly fulfilled his vow.
EDCS-00380897.summary: This is a votive inscription dedicated to Jupiter Optimus Maximus by Aelius Martinus, a beneficiarius consularis of Legio II Adjutrix, who fulfilled his vow.

EDCS-00380898.text_edition: \n Iovi Optimo Maximo Marcus Aurelius Iustus beneficiarius consularis \n
EDCS-00380898.translation: To Jupiter Best and Greatest, Marcus Aurelius Iustus, a soldier under the command of a consular legate.
EDCS-00380898.summary: This is a votive inscription dedicated to Jupiter Optimus Maximus by Marcus Aurelius Iustus, a beneficiarius consularis.

EDCS-00380899.text_edition: \n Iovi Optimo Maximo et Genio loci Aelius Atticilianus M CE s bbenefficiarii \n
EDCS-00380899.translation: To Jupiter, Best and Greatest, and to the Genius of the place, Aelius Atticilianus, staff officer of the legion, willingly.
EDCS-00380899.summary: This is a dedication to Jupiter and the local Genius by a staff officer, likely from the 2nd or 3rd century CE.

EDCS-00380900.text_edition: \n Iovi Optimo Maximo Titus Calvinius Frontonianus beneficiarius consularis votum solvit libens merito\n
EDCS-00380900.translation: To Jupiter, Best and Greatest, Titus Calvinius Frontonianus, staff officer of the governor, willingly and deservedly fulfilled his vow.
EDCS-00380900.summary: This is a votive inscription to Jupiter by Titus Calvinius Frontonianus, a staff officer, likely from the 2nd or 3rd century CE.

EDCS-00380901.text_edition: \n Iovi Optimo Maximo et Genio loci Valerianus et Iulius Valentinus bbenefficiarii consulares\n
EDCS-00380901.translation: To Jupiter, Best and Greatest, and to the Genius of the place, Valerianus and Iulius Valentinus, consular staff officers.
EDCS-00380901.summary: This is a dedication to Jupiter and the local Genius by two consular staff officers, likely from the 2nd or 3rd century CE.

EDCS-00380902.text_edition: \n REL beneficiarius consularis ex legione II adiutrice pia fideli votum solvit libens merito\n
EDCS-00380902.translation: REL, a consular staff officer from the Second Legion Adjutrix, Pious and Faithful, willingly and deservedly fulfilled his vow.
EDCS-00380902.summary: This is a votive inscription by a consular staff officer of the Second Legion Adjutrix, likely from the 2nd or 3rd century CE.

EDCS-03000772.text_edition: \n Expacto nutrimento valetudinarii legionis II adiutricis\n
EDCS-03000772.translation: From the nourishment of the sick-bay attendant of the Second Legion Adjutrix.
EDCS-03000772.summary: This inscription likely records a contribution to the health services of the Second Legion Adjutrix, perhaps from the 2nd or 3rd century CE.

EDCS-03300763.text_edition: \n Dis Manibus Caraco miles cohortis I Alpinorum equitatae centuria Publi <qui=O> vixit nnos L posuit \n
EDCS-03300763.translation: To the Spirits of the Departed, Caraco, a soldier of the 1st Cohort of Alpine Cavalry, in the century of Publius, who lived 50 years, erected this.
EDCS-03300763.summary: This is a tombstone for Caraco, a soldier of the 1st Cohort of Alpine Cavalry, who died at the age of 50, likely from the 1st or 2nd century CE.

EDCS-03700684.text_edition: \n Iovi Optimo Maximo et Genio Imperatoris item Genio collegii Marcus Ulpius Frequentinus beneficiarius consularis legionis II adiutricis piae fidelis\n
EDCS-03700684.translation: To Jupiter, Best and Greatest, and to the Genius of the Emperor, and also to the Genius of the college, Marcus Ulpius Frequentinus, consular staff officer of the Second Legion Adjutrix, Pious and Faithful.
EDCS-03700684.summary: This is a dedication to Jupiter, the Emperor's Genius, and the college's Genius by Marcus Ulpius Frequentinus, a staff officer of the Second Legion Adjutrix, likely from the 2nd or 3rd century CE.

EDCS-03700685.text_edition: \n Iovi Optimo Maximo Titus Flavius Tranquillus beneficiarius consularis votum solvit libens merito Iovi Optimo Maximo\n
EDCS-03700685.translation: To Jupiter, Best and Greatest, Titus Flavius Tranquillus, staff officer of the governor, willingly and deservedly fulfilled his vow to Jupiter, Best and Greatest.
EDCS-03700685.summary: This is a votive inscription to Jupiter by Titus Flavius Tranquillus, a staff officer, likely from the 2nd or 3rd century CE.

EDCS-03700686.text_edition: \n Marinus v et Marcus Aurelius Mamaeanus miles praetorianus\n
EDCS-03700686.translation: Marinus and Marcus Aurelius Mamaeanus, praetorian soldiers.
EDCS-03700686.summary: This records two individuals, Marinus and Marcus Aurelius Mamaeanus, who were praetorian soldiers, likely from the 2nd or 3rd century CE.

EDCS-03700687.text_edition: \n Dis Manibus Curtius Felix veteranus cohortis |(miliariae) Maurorum vixit nnos LX Aelia Annamata coniux Aelia Carina filia annorum X Aelius Primitivus filius coniux carissimis posuerunt\n
EDCS-03700687.translation: To the Spirits of the Departed, Curtius Felix, a veteran of the Maurorum cohort (miliaria), who lived 60 years; Aelia Annamata, his wife; and Aelia Carina, his daughter, aged 10 years; his dearest family erected this.
EDCS-03700687.summary: This is a tombstone set up by the family of Curtius Felix, a veteran of the Maurorum cohort, who died at 60, and his young daughter who died at 10, likely from the 2nd or 3rd century CE.

EDCS-03700688.text_edition: \n Dis Manibus Publio Comatio Publi filio Claudia Adiutori Viruno veterano ex decurione cohortis I Alpinorum equitatae annorum LXX hic situs est Publius Comatius Lamyrus libertus patrono pientissimo faciendum curavit\n
EDCS-03700688.translation: To the Divine Spirits. Publius Comatius Adiutor, son of Publius, of the Claudia tribe, a veteran from the 1st cavalry cohort of the Alpiners, aged 70, is buried here. Publius Comatius Lamyrus, his freedman, had this made for his most devoted patron.
EDCS-03700688.summary: This funerary epitaph records the burial of Publius Comatius Adiutor, a veteran, and was commissioned by his freedman, Publius Comatius Lamyrus.

EDCS-03700690.text_edition: \n Iovi Optimo Maximo Claudius Candidinus miles cohortis Maurorum votum solvit libens merito\n
EDCS-03700690.translation: To Jupiter, Best and Greatest, Claudius Candidinus, a soldier from the cohort of the Moors, gladly and deservedly fulfilled his vow.
EDCS-03700690.summary: This is a votive inscription by Claudius Candidinus, a soldier in the cohort of the Moors, dedicated to Jupiter Optimus Maximus.

EDCS-03700691.text_edition: \n Libero Patri et Liberae voverat hanc aram Ulpius Baccho Marinus veteranus ex tesserario legionis I adiutricis pro salute sua suorumque omnium et nunc solvit laetus cum honore sacrorum Albino II et Aemili ano consulibus\n
EDCS-03700691.translation: To Liber Pater and Libera, Ulpius Baccho Marinus, a veteran formerly a tesserarius of the I Adiutrix legion, had vowed this altar for the safety of himself and all his family, and now gladly fulfills it with honor in the sacred rites, under consuls Albinus II and Aemilianus.
EDCS-03700691.summary: This inscription records a veteran, Ulpius Baccho Marinus, fulfilling a vow to Liber Pater and Libera for his and his family's safety during the consulship of Albinus II and Aemilianus.

EDCS-04900895.text_edition: \n Dis Manibus Aurelio Agrippae ex provincia Arabia civitate Cilis Sahareno qui vixit nnos XXIIII Marcus Aurelius Bassus eques Romanus nepoti dulcissimo\n
EDCS-04900895.translation: To the Divine Spirits. For Aurelius Agrippa from the province of Arabia, citizen of Cilicia, from Saharenum, who lived 24 years. Marcus Aurelius Bassus, a Roman knight, to his dearest grandson.
EDCS-04900895.summary: This is a funerary epitaph for Aurelius Agrippa, a young man from Arabia and Cilicia, set up by his Roman knight grandfather, Marcus Aurelius Bassus.

EDCS-04900896.text_edition: \n Caius Attius Quadratus miles legionis II adiutricis annorum XXXXV stipendiorum XXV hic situs est Capitonius Gesatus signifer legionis eiiusdem sic heres Conduni Saturnini ex testamento faciendum curavit\n
EDCS-04900896.translation: Caius Attius Quadratus, a soldier of the II Adiutrix legion, aged 45, with 25 years of service, is buried here. Capitonius Gesatus, a standard-bearer of the same legion, had this made as instructed by his heir, Condunus Saturninus, in accordance with the will.
EDCS-04900896.summary: This funerary epitaph records the burial of Caius Attius Quadratus, a soldier and standard-bearer of the II Adiutrix legion, with his heir Capitonius Gesatus overseeing its construction.

EDCS-04900898.text_edition: \n Silvanabes sic Lucilla TA SVAATA IVEIA GRISP PRO IVI SOLVI\n
EDCS-04900898.summary: This is a very fragmentary inscription dedicated to Silvanus, with a partial dedication by Lucilla.

EDCS-04900899.translation: To holy Fortune, Agapa placed her vow.
EDCS-04900899.summary: This is a votive inscription by Agapa dedicated to the goddess Fortuna.

EDCS-05000669.summary: This is a dedication to Silvanus Viator.

EDCS-05000677.text_edition: \n Vivianus Atrasi <fi=IF>lius nnorum III hic situs est titulum posuit sibi vivo Enamaico Aponis filius nnorum XXV vivus et Secunda Atrasi filia annorum XXII defuncta \n
EDCS-05000677.translation: Vivianus, son of Atrasius, aged 3 years, is buried here. This tombstone was erected by Enamaicus Aponis, son of Atrasius, aged 25, while living, and by Secunda Atrasi, daughter of Aponis, aged 22, deceased.
EDCS-05000677.summary: This funerary inscription records the burial of young Vivianus and was jointly erected by his living father, Enamaicus, and his deceased mother, Secunda.

EDCS-05000678.text_edition: \n Dis Manibus Flavia Mathi nnorum XXII hic sita est \n
EDCS-05000678.translation: To the Divine Spirits. Flavia Mathi, aged 22, is buried here.
EDCS-05000678.summary: This is a funerary epitaph for Flavia Mathi, a young woman.

EDCS-05000680.text_edition: \n Priepo sic Vettius Marcianus et Iuli\n
EDCS-05000680.translation: To Priapus, thus Vettius Marcianus and Iulius...
EDCS-05000680.summary: A dedication to the god Priapus by Vettius Marcianus and Iulius, likely on a monument related to fertility or protection.

EDCS-05000681.text_edition: \n Iovi Optimo Maximo Aelius VS L--- F V P--- R posuerunt \n
EDCS-05000681.translation: To Jupiter Optimus Maximus, Aelius and... placed this...
EDCS-05000681.summary: A dedication to Jupiter Optimus Maximus by Aelius and others, recording their action of setting up an object, possibly a statue or altar.

EDCS-05000683.summary: This inscription identifies a workshop belonging to someone named Maxentius.

EDCS-05000684.summary: This inscription identifies a workshop belonging to someone named Maxentius.

EDCS-05000685.translation: Of the masters of the Tenth Legion Vindobonensis, of Maxentius.
EDCS-05000685.summary: This inscription refers to the masters, specifically Maxentius, associated with the Tenth Legion Vindobonensis.

EDCS-05000686.translation: Of the masters of the Tenth Legion, of Maxentius.
EDCS-05000686.summary: This inscription refers to the masters, specifically Maxentius, associated with the Tenth Legion.

EDCS-05000687.translation: Of the Tenth Legion GG, Ursicinus the centurion.
EDCS-05000687.summary: This inscription identifies Ursicinus, a centurion of the Tenth Legion GG.

EDCS-05000688.translation: Of the Tenth Legion GG, Saturninus.
EDCS-05000688.summary: This inscription identifies Saturninus, associated with the Tenth Legion GG.

EDCS-05000689.text_edition: \n Iovi Optimo Maximo Teutano pro salute domini nostri Imperatoris Marci Aureli Severi Alexandri Augusti et incolumitate civitatis Eraviscorum Publius Aelius Praesens et Marcus Aurelius Proculus IIviri coloniae Aquincensium dedicaverunt III Iunias Imperatore Caesare Severo Alexandro II et Marcello consulibus\n
EDCS-05000689.translation: To Jupiter Optimus Maximus, for the safety of our lord Emperor Marcus Aurelius Severus Alexander Augustus and the well-being of the city of the Eravisci, Publius Aelius Praesens and Marcus Aurelius Proculus, the two duumvirs of the colony of Aquincensium, dedicated this on the 3rd of June, when the Emperor Caesar Severus Alexander II and Marcellus were consuls.
EDCS-05000689.summary: A dedication to Jupiter Optimus Maximus by the duumvirs of Aquincensium for the emperor's safety and the city's well-being, dated to 224 AD.

EDCS-05000692.summary: This is a simple inscription bearing the name Publius Iturius Sabinus, likely a tombstone or dedication.

EDCS-05200563.text_edition: \n Iovi Optimo Maximo Uccinus Curmillus solvit votum libens merito\n
EDCS-05200563.translation: Uccinus Curmillus gladly and deservedly paid his vow to Jupiter, Best and Greatest.
EDCS-05200563.summary: This is a votive inscription dedicated to Jupiter by Uccinus Curmillus.

EDCS-05200564.text_edition: \n Iovi Optimo Maximo Titus Aelius Rusticus et Aelia Vera eius pro salute Titi Aeli Veri fili sui ex voto libens merito posuerunt Silanis II consulibus\n
EDCS-05200564.translation: Titus Aelius Rusticus and his wife Aelia Vera gladly and deservedly set this up, in fulfillment of a vow, for the health of their son Titus Aelius Verus, during the consulship of Silanis II.
EDCS-05200564.summary: This is a votive inscription dedicated to Jupiter by Titus Aelius Rusticus and Aelia Vera for their son's health, dating to the consulship of Silanis II.

EDCS-05200565.text_edition: \n Dis Manibus Mariae Euphrosinae quondam uxori Publius Aelius Crispus maritus et Maria Prima coniugi et patronae carissimae faciendum curaverunt\n
EDCS-05200565.translation: To the Spirits of the Departed. Publius Aelius Crispus, husband, and Maria Prima, his dearest wife and patroness, arranged for this to be made for Maria Euphrosina, formerly his wife.
EDCS-05200565.summary: This is a funerary inscription for Maria Euphrosina, commissioned by her husband Publius Aelius Crispus and her patroness Maria Prima.

EDCS-05200566.text_edition: \n Dis Manibus Septimiae Crescentinae coniugi carissimae et obsequentissimae quae vixit nnos XL Sittius Felix maritus cum Sittia Ursa filia communi et Septimi-- Quintiius faciendum curaverunt optamus sit tibi terra levis\n
EDCS-05200566.translation: To the Spirits of the Departed. Her husband Sittius Felix, with their daughter Sittia Ursa, and Septimius Quintius arranged for this to be made for Septimia Crescentina, their dearest and most obedient wife and mother, who lived 40 years. We wish the earth may lie lightly on you.
EDCS-05200566.summary: This is a funerary inscription for Septimia Crescentina, set up by her husband, daughter, and another individual, who lived 40 years.

EDCS-05200569.text_edition: \n Publius Aelius Publi filius Aelia Licinius Mursa legionis II adiutricis piae fidelis \n
EDCS-05200569.translation: Publius Aelius Licinius, son of Publius Aelius, of the Second Legion Adjutrix, loyal and faithful.
EDCS-05200569.summary: This is a military inscription identifying Publius Aelius Licinius, a soldier in the Second Legion Adjutrix.

EDCS-05200570.text_edition: \n Caius Valerius Cai filius Publilia Macer Verona miles legionis II adiutricis piae fidelis centuria Caecili Quinti annorum XL stipendiorum XX hic situs est testamento fieri iussit Marcus Calabonius Secundus veteranus legionis eiusdem heres faciendum curavit\n
EDCS-05200570.translation: Caius Valerius Macer, son of Caius, of the Publilia tribe, a soldier of the Second Legion Adjutrix, loyal and faithful, in the century of Caecilius Quintus, aged 40 years and having completed 20 years of service, is buried here; he ordered this to be made by his will. Marcus Calabonius Secundus, a veteran of the same legion, arranged for it to be made as heir.
EDCS-05200570.summary: This is a funerary inscription for Caius Valerius Macer, a soldier aged 40 with 20 years of service in the Second Legion Adjutrix, whose will directed its construction by Marcus Calabonius Secundus.

EDCS-05200571.text_edition: \n Deo Soli I(nvicto) M(ithrae) Cassius Pius? Marcellinus tribunus laticlavius legionis II adiutricis piae fidelis Acmazon locum Deo constituerunt votum solverunt libentes merito\n
EDCS-05200571.translation: Cassius Pius Marcellinus, military tribune with broad stripe of the Second Legion Adjutrix, loyal and faithful, and Acmazon established this place for the Unconquered Sun Mithras and gladly and deservedly paid their vow.
EDCS-05200571.summary: This inscription records the establishment of a place and the payment of a vow to Sol Invictus Mithras by Cassius Pius Marcellinus, a military tribune, and Acmazon.

EDCS-05200572.text_edition: \n Deo Soli Invicto Mithrae locum constituit Pius Marcellinus\n
EDCS-05200572.translation: Pius Marcellinus established this place for the Unconquered Sun Mithras.
EDCS-05200572.summary: This inscription records the establishment of a place for Sol Invictus Mithras by Pius Marcellinus.

EDCS-05200573.text_edition: \n Soli Invicto sacrum Caius Iulius Cai filius Arnensi Lepidus Tertullus tribunus laticlavius legionis II adiutricis piae fidelis votum exsolvit\n
EDCS-05200573.translation: Caius Iulius Lepidus Tertullus, son of Caius, of the Arnensis tribe, military tribune with broad stripe of the Second Legion Adjutrix, loyal and faithful, paid his vow to the Unconquered Sun.
EDCS-05200573.summary: This votive inscription to Sol Invictus was made by Caius Iulius Lepidus Tertullus, a military tribune of the Second Legion Adjutrix.

EDCS-05200574.text_edition: \n Soli Invicto Mitrhae sic Caius Minucius Ticidianus Annius Faustus tribunus laticlavius legionis II adiutricis piae fidelis votum solvit\n
EDCS-05200574.translation: Caius Minucius Faustus, son of Ticidianus Annius, military tribune with broad stripe of the Second Legion Adjutrix, loyal and faithful, thus paid his vow to the Unconquered Sun Mithras.
EDCS-05200574.summary: This votive inscription to Sol Invictus Mithras was made by Caius Minucius Faustus, a military tribune of the Second Legion Adjutrix.

EDCS-05200575.text_edition: \n Soli Invicto Mithrae Sextus Decimius Verus Barbarus tribunus legionis II ad<i=L>(utricis) clarissimus iuvenis pro salute sua et suorum ex voto \n
EDCS-05200575.translation: To the Unconquered Sun-god Mithras, Sextus Decimius Verus Barbarus, tribune of the II Utricensis legion, a most distinguished young man, for his own health and that of his family, in fulfillment of a vow.
EDCS-05200575.summary: This inscription is a votive offering to Sol Invictus Mithras by a military tribune, likely from the 2nd or 3rd century CE.

EDCS-05200576.text_edition: \n Invicto Mithrae pro salute Luci Aureli Galli tribuni laticlavii Aurelius Aliphus posuit\n
EDCS-05200576.translation: For the Unconquered Mithras, for the health of Lucius Aurelius Gallus, a broad-striped tribune, Aurelius Aliphus set this up.
EDCS-05200576.summary: This is a dedication to Mithras for the well-being of a high-ranking military officer, made by a freedman, likely from the 2nd or 3rd century CE.

EDCS-05200577.text_edition: \n Dis Manibus hic iacet ecce vides pupa sine fine decoris quae mihi tam subito rapta est neque adhuc credere possim non e<g=C>o tam modica potui maculare parentem Cassia sum vocita iace<o=T> sub saxo reclusa IIII annos vixi et menses V tu quidem quem recitas ablatu pietate parentis quisquis es vale \n
EDCS-05200577.translation: To the Spirits of the Departed. Here lies, behold, you see a maiden of endless beauty, who was so suddenly snatched from me, and I still cannot believe that I, so insignificant, could wound my parent. I am called Cassia. Lie beneath this stone, shut away. I lived for 4 years and 5 months. You, whoever you are, whom I recite with filial piety, farewell.
EDCS-05200577.summary: This tomb inscription records the premature death of a young woman named Cassia, lamenting her beauty and the grief of her parent, likely dating to the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-05200606.text_edition: \n Legio XIII gemina P V C\n
EDCS-05200606.translation: Legio XIII Gemina, P V C.
EDCS-05200606.summary: This is a military inscription, possibly a building dedication or a unit marker, for the Thirteenth Legion Gemina, with an incomplete abbreviation, likely from the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-05200607.text_edition: \n Dis Manibus Gaio Antonio Victori quondam optioni spei legionis III Gallicae qui vixit nnos XLVII Trachenti origine DEISTA civitate Lasseorum curam Antonio Prot col amico pos(uit)\n
EDCS-05200607.translation: To the Spirits of the Departed. For Gaius Antonius Victor, formerly optio of the guard of the Third Gallic Legion, who lived 47 years, Trachenti by origin, DEISTA by citizenship, of the Lassei tribe, the care was placed by his friend Antonius Protus.
EDCS-05200607.summary: This funerary inscription commemorates Gaius Antonius Victor, a former optio of the Legio III Gallica, who died at 47, and was set up by his friend, likely from the 2nd or 3rd century CE.

EDCS-07000826.text_edition: \n Iovi Optimo Maximo Augustales municipii libentes fecerunt\n
EDCS-07000826.translation: To Jupiter, Best and Greatest, the Augustales of the municipality willingly made this.
EDCS-07000826.summary: This is a dedication to Jupiter Optimus Maximus made by the local priests (Augustales) of a Roman municipality, likely from the Imperial period.

EDCS-07600380.text_edition: \n Iovi Optimo Maximo et Iunoni Reginae Aurelius Monimus decurio cohortis |(miliariae) novae Severianae pro salute sua et suorum votum solvit libens merito\n
EDCS-07600380.translation: To Jupiter, Best and Greatest, and to Juno Regina, Aurelius Monimus, decurion of the first cohort of the new Severian cohort (miliariae), fulfilled his vow willingly and deservedly for his own health and that of his family.
EDCS-07600380.summary: This inscription records a votive offering to Jupiter and Juno by Aurelius Monimus, a decurion in a Roman auxiliary cohort, for his well-being and that of his family, likely from the 2nd or 3rd century CE.

EDCS-07600381.text_edition: \n Solia Medullionis filia Bruti Risimarus Scutalonis filius titulum posuit sibi vivo et coniugi Ducae Dimiuci filiae annorum LX et Ritimauro filio annorum XXX\n
EDCS-07600381.translation: Solia, daughter of Medullion, son of Brutus Risimarus; Scutalo, son of Brutus, set up this monument for himself while alive, and for his wife Duca, daughter of Dimiucius, aged 60, and for his son Ritimaurus, aged 30.
EDCS-07600381.summary: This funerary monument was erected by Scutalo for himself, his wife Duca, and his son Ritimaurus, indicating their ages and parentage, likely from the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-07600382.text_edition: \n io e licus coniugi carissimae et sibi vivo posuit\n
EDCS-07600382.translation: [Name] to his dearest wife and for himself while alive set up.
EDCS-07600382.summary: This is a funerary inscription fragment recording the erection of a monument by an individual for his wife and himself, likely from the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-07600383.summary: This inscription, possibly a tomb marker or a boundary stone, mentions a length of 20 units (IODVIA XX) and an heir named Tiro, likely from the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-07600384.text_edition: \n Matiata R filia vixit annos filius posuit titulum sibi vivo et coniugi\n
EDCS-07600384.translation: Matiata, daughter of R., lived [number] years; her son erected this monument for himself and his living wife.
EDCS-07600384.summary: This is a funerary inscription for Matiata, erected by her son.

EDCS-07600385.text_edition: \n Dis Manibus Tito Aurelio iano vixit annos stipendiorum X et Tito Aurelio vixit nnos XXVIII et Tito Aurelio Valenti vixit annos stipendiorum VI duplarius sic Aurelius Iustus veteranus et Aelia Quinta parentes filiis dulcissimis ponendum curaverunt\n
EDCS-07600385.translation: To the spirits of the departed. Tito Aurelius Ianus lived 10 years of service, and Tito Aurelius lived 28 years, and Tito Aurelius Valens lived 6 years of service, a duplicarius. Aurelius Iustus, a veteran, and Aelia Quinta, his parents, saw to the erection of this for their dearest sons.
EDCS-07600385.summary: This is a funerary inscription for three sons, Tito Aurelius Ianus, Tito Aurelius, and Tito Aurelius Valens, erected by their parents Aurelius Iustus and Aelia Quinta.

EDCS-07600387.text_edition: \n Aelius Valerius speculator legionis II adiutricis votum solvit libens merito\n
EDCS-07600387.translation: Aelius Valerius, a speculator of the II Adjutrix legion, paid his vow willingly and deservedly.
EDCS-07600387.summary: This is a votive inscription by Aelius Valerius, a soldier of the II Adjutrix legion.

EDCS-07600391.text_edition: \n Terrae Matri Marcus Antonius Victorinus IIvir coloniae Aquincensium cum Ulpia Spectata coniuge votum solvit libens merito\n
EDCS-07600391.translation: To the Earth Mother, Marcus Antonius Victorinus, duovir of the colony of Aquincum, with his wife Ulpia Spectata, paid his vow willingly and deservedly.
EDCS-07600391.summary: This is a votive inscription to the Earth Mother by Marcus Antonius Victorinus and his wife Ulpia Spectata from the colony of Aquincum.

EDCS-07600392.text_edition: \n Iunoni Reginae Augustae Marcus Aurelius Epigonus decurio coloniae Aquincensium pro salute sua et suorum omnium votum solvit libens merito \n
EDCS-07600392.translation: To Juno Regina Augusta, Marcus Aurelius Epigonus, decurion of the colony of Aquincum, paid his vow willingly and deservedly for his own health and that of all his family.
EDCS-07600392.summary: This is a votive inscription to Juno Regina Augusta by Marcus Aurelius Epigonus, a decurion of the colony of Aquincum, for his health and that of his family.

EDCS-07600393.text_edition: \n Augusti et Antonini Augustorum e[t Getae] sanctissimi Cae[saris] Quintus Cornelius Quinti filius Arnensis Secundus praefectus cohortis |(miliariae) Maurorum posuit\n
EDCS-07600393.translation: For the Augusti Emperors Antoninus and our most sacred Caesar Geta, Quintus Cornelius Secundus, son of Quintus, of the Arnensis tribe, prefect of a milliary cohort of Moors, erected this.
EDCS-07600393.summary: This inscription records the erection of a monument by Quintus Cornelius Secundus, prefect of a cohort of Moors, during the reigns of Emperors Antoninus and Geta.

EDCS-07600394.text_edition: \n Iovi Optimo Maximo Paterno Ulpius Florentinus magister canabarum ad legionem II adiutricem votum solvit libens merito\n
EDCS-07600394.translation: To Jupiter Optimus Maximus Paterno, Ulpius Florentinus, master of the taverns near the II Adjutrix legion, paid his vow willingly and deservedly.
EDCS-07600394.summary: This is a votive inscription to Jupiter Optimus Maximus Paterno by Ulpius Florentinus, who managed taverns near the II Adjutrix legion.

EDCS-07600395.text_edition: \n Dis Manibus Aurelius Oceanus miles stipendiorum II vixit annos XX menses VI et dies VII vivito\n
EDCS-07600395.translation: To the spirits of the departed. Aurelius Oceanus, a soldier with 2 years of service, lived 20 years, 6 months, and 7 days. May he live on!
EDCS-07600395.summary: This is a funerary inscription for Aurelius Oceanus, a soldier who died at the age of 20 years, 6 months, and 7 days.

EDCS-07600397.text_edition: \n Turma Maximi Macedonis Turma Maximi Constantis\n
EDCS-07600397.translation: The troop of Maximi Macedonis. The troop of Maximi Constantis.
EDCS-07600397.summary: This inscription lists two troops, possibly indicating a military unit or a group with shared leadership.

EDCS-07600398.summary: This is a fragmentary inscription that appears to be a funerary epitaph.

EDCS-08400441.text_edition: \n veteranus alae Silianae pro salute votum solvit libens merito\n
EDCS-08400441.translation: A veteran of the Silian cavalry regiment paid his vow freely and deservedly for the safety [of the emperor].
EDCS-08400441.summary: This inscription records a vow paid by a veteran of the Silian cavalry, likely during the Roman Empire.

EDCS-08500524.text_edition: \n Dullibogius Flori filius nnorum XL hic situs est Flavia Giboruna coniugi pientissimo et sibi vivae faciendum curavit\n
EDCS-08500524.translation: Dullibogius, son of Florus, aged 40, lies here; Flavia Giboruna had this made for her most devoted husband and for herself while alive.
EDCS-08500524.summary: This is a tombstone inscription for Dullibogius, paid for by his wife Flavia Giboruna, dating to the Roman period.

EDCS-08500525.text_edition: \n Imperatori Caesari divi Antonini Magni Pii filio divi Severi nepoti Marco Aurelio Antonino Pio Felici Augusto pontifici maximo tribunicia potestate consuli patri patriae proconsuli curante Pontio Pontiano legato Augusti pro praetore ab Aquinco milia passuum XXIII\n
EDCS-08500525.translation: To the Emperor Caesar, son of the deified Antoninus Magnus Pius, grandson of the deified Severus, Marcus Aurelius Antoninus Pius Felix Augustus, chief priest, holder of tribunician power, consul, father of the fatherland, proconsul, under the supervision of Pontius Pontianus, imperial legate with praetorian authority, 23 miles from Aquincum.
EDCS-08500525.summary: This inscription records a dedication to Emperor Marcus Aurelius Antoninus Pius Felix Augustus (Caracalla), likely indicating a milestone or boundary marker related to the road from Aquincum, dating to his reign (AD 211-217).

EDCS-08500526.text_edition: \n Imperatori Caesari Caio Iulio Vero Maximino Pio Felici Augusto patri patriae ab Aquinco milia passuum XXII\n
EDCS-08500526.translation: To the Emperor Caesar, Gaius Julius Verus Maximinus Pius Felix Augustus, father of the fatherland, 22 miles from Aquincum.
EDCS-08500526.summary: This inscription records a dedication to Emperor Maximinus Thrax, likely indicating a milestone or boundary marker related to the road from Aquincum, dating to his reign (AD 310-313).

EDCS-08500527.text_edition: \n Imperatori Caesari Marco Antonio Gordiano Pio Felici Invicto \n
EDCS-08500527.translation: To the Emperor Caesar, Marcus Antoninus Gordianus Pius Felix Invictus.
EDCS-08500527.summary: This inscription records a dedication to Emperor Gordian III, likely part of a larger monument or milestone, dating to his reign (AD 238-244).

EDCS-08600606.text_edition: \n Deae Syriae et Balti Deae pro salute Augusti Caius Iulius Sextinus conductor ex decreto ordinis kanabarum secundum conductionem arcum cum ianuis tegula tectum inpendiis suis fecit magistris Iulio Viatore et Bellicio Firmino locus publice datus decreto decurionum\n
EDCS-08600606.translation: To the Syrian Goddess and the Goddess Baltis, for the safety of the Augustus. Caius Julius Sextinus, contractor, by decree of the council of the kanabaroi, according to the contract, built the arch with doors and covered it with tiles at his own expense, with Iulius Viator and Bellicius Firminus as overseers. The site was publicly granted by decree of the decurions.
EDCS-08600606.summary: This inscription records the construction of an arch with doors by Caius Julius Sextinus, a contractor, for the safety of the emperor, dedicated to the Syrian Goddess and the Goddess Baltis, likely in the civilian settlement (kanabaroi) near Aquincum, in the early imperial period.

EDCS-08600607.text_edition: \n Iovi Optimo Maximo Aurelius Marcianus votum solvit libens merito\n
EDCS-08600607.translation: To Jupiter Best and Greatest, Aurelius Marcianus paid his vow freely and deservedly.
EDCS-08600607.summary: This inscription records a votive offering to Jupiter Best and Greatest by Aurelius Marcianus, likely during the Roman Empire.

EDCS-08600608.text_edition: \n Asclepio et Hygiae Caius Gavillius Eutychus\n
EDCS-08600608.translation: To Asclepius and Hygieia, Caius Gavillius Eutychus.
EDCS-08600608.summary: This inscription records a dedication to the gods Asclepius and Hygieia by Caius Gavillius Eutychus, likely a private offering from the Roman period.

EDCS-08600609.text_edition: \n Apollini et Seranae sic Titus Iulius Mercator decurio coloniae votum solvit libens merito\n
EDCS-08600609.translation: To Apollo and Seranus, Titus Iulius Mercator, decurion of the colony, paid his vow freely and deservedly.
EDCS-08600609.summary: This inscription records a votive offering to Apollo and Seranus by Titus Iulius Mercator, a decurion of the colony, likely in Aquincum during the Roman Empire.

EDCS-08600610.text_edition: \n Silvano Anticessori Titus Flavius Lucius decurio coloniae Aquincensium quaestorius votum solvit libens merito\n
EDCS-08600610.translation: To Silvanus Anticessor, Titus Flavius Lucius, quaestor and decurion of the colony of Aquincum, paid his vow freely and deservedly.
EDCS-08600610.summary: This inscription records a votive offering to Silvanus Anticessor by Titus Flavius Lucius, a decurion and former quaestor of the colony of Aquincum, likely during the Roman Empire.

EDCS-08600612.text_edition: \n Soli Invicto Aurelius Vindicanus votum solvit libens merito\n
EDCS-08600612.translation: To Sol Invictus, Aurelius Vindicanus willingly fulfilled his vow as deserved.
EDCS-08600612.summary: This is a votive inscription dedicated to Sol Invictus by Aurelius Vindicanus.

EDCS-08600613.text_edition: \n Dis Manibus Publius Afranius Victor |(centurio) cohortis I Alpinorum annorum L hic situs est Claudia In<g=C>enua coiiu<g=C>i sic pientissimo et sibi viva posuit \n
EDCS-08600613.translation: To the Spirits of the Departed. Publius Afranius Victor, centurion of the I Alpine Cohort, aged 50, lies here. Claudia Ingenua placed this for her most devoted husband and for herself while alive.
EDCS-08600613.summary: This is a funerary epitaph for Publius Afranius Victor, a centurion, set up by his wife Claudia Ingenua.

EDCS-08600617.text_edition: \n Dis Manibus Claudius Ingenuus bucinator cohortis |(miliariae) Maurorum et Flavia Paulina coniunx vixit annos XXVI et Claudius Paulinus et Claudia Ingenua patri et coniugi eius \n
EDCS-08600617.translation: To the Spirits of the Departed. Claudius Ingenuus, horn-blower of the Mauri Cohort (miliariae), and his wife Flavia Paulina, who lived 26 years, and Claudius Paulinus and Claudia Ingenua for their father and husband.
EDCS-08600617.summary: This is a funerary epitaph for Claudius Ingenuus and his wife Flavia Paulina, erected by their children.

EDCS-08600621.summary: This is a brief funerary epitaph for Aelius Iulianus, identified by his occupation as a mortar-maker.

EDCS-08600622.text_edition: \n Herculi Augusto sacrum Septimii libentes m(erito)\n
EDCS-08600622.translation: To Hercules Augustus, the Septimii willingly (dedicated this) as deserved.
EDCS-08600622.summary: This is a dedication to Hercules Augustus made by the Septimii family.

EDCS-08600624.text_edition: \n Ulpiae Quaetae annorum LX Ulpio Quaetino annorum XV Ulpiae Crispinae annorum XX Ulpia Quaetina mater \n
EDCS-08600624.translation: For Ulpia Quaeta, aged 60, for Ulpius Quaetinus, aged 15, for Ulpia Crispina, aged 20. Ulpia Quaetina, mother.
EDCS-08600624.summary: This is a funerary epitaph listing several individuals and identifying Ulpia Quaetina as the mother.

EDCS-08900539.text_edition: \n Imperator Caesar Caius Iulius Verus Maximinus Pius Felix Augustus pontifex maximus tribunicia potestate pater patriae consul ab Aquinco milia passuum VI\n
EDCS-08900539.translation: Emperor Caesar Gaius Julius Verus Maximinus Pius Felix Augustus, chief priest, with tribunician power, father of the fatherland, consul, 6 miles from Aquinum.
EDCS-08900539.summary: This inscription records the distance of 6 miles from Aquinum, likely related to a road or public works project under Emperor Maximinus Thrax.

EDCS-08900540.text_edition: \n Imperatori Caesari Marco Antonio Gordiano Pio Felici Augusto \n
EDCS-08900540.translation: To Emperor Caesar Marcus Antonius Gordianus Pius Felix Augustus.
EDCS-08900540.summary: This is an honorific inscription dedicated to Emperor Gordian III.

EDCS-08900541.text_edition: \n Imperator Caesar Marcus Aurellius sic Severus Alexander Pius Felix Augustus pontifex maximus tribunicia <p=R>otestate VIIII consul III pater patriae restituit Aquinco milia passuum III \n
EDCS-08900541.translation: Emperor Caesar Marcus Aurellius Severus Alexander Pius Felix Augustus, chief priest, with tribunician power VIIII, consul III, father of the fatherland, restored 3 miles from Aquinum.
EDCS-08900541.summary: This inscription records the restoration of 3 miles of road near Aquinum under Emperor Severus Alexander.

EDCS-08900542.text_edition: \n Imperator Caesar Marcus Opellius Severus Macrinus Pius Augustus consul proconsul tribunicia potestate Opellius Diadum[enianus] nobilissimus Caesar vias et pontes vetustate corruptas restituerunt cura agente Aelio T[ricciano] legato Auggustorum pro praetore ab Aquinco milia passuum XLIII\n
EDCS-08900542.translation: Emperor Caesar Marcus Opellius Severus Macrinus Pius Augustus, consul, proconsul, with tribunician power, and Opellius Diadumenianus, most noble Caesar, restored the roads and bridges worn out by age, under the supervision of Aelius Triccianus, legate of the Emperors with proconsular authority, 43 miles from Aquinum.
EDCS-08900542.summary: This inscription records the restoration of roads and bridges near Aquinum by Emperors Macrinus and Diadumenianus, overseen by legate Aelius Triccianus.

EDCS-08900543.translation: To the Spirits of the Departed. Felix, while alive.
EDCS-08900543.summary: This is a funerary inscription for Felix, likely set up by himself before his death.

EDCS-08900544.text_edition: \n Marti Auggustorum sacrum Apolaustus Auggustorum libertus \n
EDCS-08900544.translation: Sacred to Mars Augustus. Dedicated by Apolaustus, freedman of the Emperors.
EDCS-08900544.summary: This is a dedication to Mars Augustus by a freedman of the emperors.

EDCS-08900549.text_edition: \n Dis Manibus Publio Aelio Valeno et Bitutiae Marcellinae uxori \n
EDCS-08900549.translation: To the Spirits of the Departed. For Publius Aelius Valens and his wife Bitutia Marcellina.
EDCS-08900549.summary: This is a funerary inscription for Publius Aelius Valens and his wife Bitutia Marcellina.

EDCS-08900550.text_edition: \n G Ulpi-- Florcoloniae Cibalensium Marcus ViValentinus dec(urio)? col(oniae) Cibalensium equo publico Coccei-- V flamen pi\n
EDCS-08900550.translation: G. Ulpius... Marcus Valentinus, decurion of the colony of Cibalae, with public support, priest...
EDCS-08900550.summary: This is a fragmentary inscription mentioning a decurion of Cibalae and a priest, likely of local or imperial cult, with a name partially preserved.

EDCS-08900551.text_edition: \n Iovi Optimo Maximo Marcus Aurelius Quintilianus decurio coloniae Cibalensium quaestorius ob incolumitatem suam suorumque omnium votum solvit libens merito\n
EDCS-08900551.translation: To Jupiter Optimus Maximus. Marcus Aurelius Quintilianus, decurion of the colony of Cibalae, former quaestor, willingly and deservedly fulfilled his vow for his own safety and that of all his family.
EDCS-08900551.summary: This is a votive inscription by Marcus Aurelius Quintilianus, a decurion and former quaestor of Cibalae, for his and his family's well-being.

EDCS-08901098.text_edition: \n Dis Manibus Iuliae Secundinae Quinti Iuli Secundi filiae domo municipio A C nnorum XL Aurelius Victor veteranus legionis II adiutricis ex stratore consularis coniugi karissimae faciendum curavit\n
EDCS-08901098.translation: To the Spirits of the Departed. For Julia Secundina, daughter of Quintus Julius Secundus, from the municipium of A... [XX?] years old, forty years old. Aurelius Victor, a veteran of the II Adjutrix legion, former orderly to a consular legate, had this made for his dearest wife.
EDCS-08901098.summary: This is a funerary inscription for Julia Secundina, wife of a veteran and former orderly to a consular legate, dating to the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-09200445.text_edition: \n Dis Manibus Quintus Valerius Felix Augustalis coloniae Aquincensium et Septimia Vibia coniunx sibi et suis filiis vivis fecerunt\n
EDCS-09200445.translation: To the Spirits of the Departed. Quintus Valerius Felix, Augustalis of the colony of Aquincum, and his wife Septimia Vibia, made this for themselves and their descendants while alive.
EDCS-09200445.summary: This is a funerary inscription for Quintus Valerius Felix, an Augustalis of Aquincum, and his wife Septimia Vibia, who dedicated it for themselves and their family.

EDCS-09300476.translation: ...which had been vowed; [fulfilled by] a centurion [of the] IIII Flavia legion, appointed by Caecilius Rufus, imperial legate, proconsul. He fulfilled his vow willingly and deservedly.
EDCS-09300476.summary: This is a dedication recording the fulfillment of a vow by a centurion of the IIII Flavia legion, appointed by the legate of the province.

EDCS-09300480.text_edition: \n Nomina heredum Rutilii Ven Agathemerus Ven\n
EDCS-09300480.translation: Names of the heirs of Rutilius Ven: Agathemerus Ven.
EDCS-09300480.summary: This is a very brief inscription listing the heirs of someone named Rutilius Ven.

EDCS-09300481.text_edition: \n Sexto Lucanio M causidico qui vixit annos XXVIII Lucanius D\n
EDCS-09300481.translation: For Sextus Lucanius, a lawyer, who lived 28 years. Lucanius D...
EDCS-09300481.summary: This is a funerary inscription for Sextus Lucanius, a lawyer who died at the age of 28.

EDCS-09300485.text_edition: \n Titus Flavius Titi filius Quirina Ius<t=I>inus veteranus legionis II adiutricis domo Sirmio nnorum LXX sibi et Iuliae Optatae coniugi vivaes faciendum curavit decreto decurionum Laterano et Rufino consulibus \n
