EDCS-09900541.summary: This inscription records a vow fulfilled to the god Sol Invictus by Aurelius Arbas, Aelia Innina, and their son Aelius Vitalianus, likely dating to the 2nd or 3rd century CE.

EDCS-09900542.text_edition: \n Deo Soli Aurelius Barsamsus optio cohortis ex voto paterno cum suis posuit\n
EDCS-09900542.translation: To the Sun-god, Aurelius Barsamsus, optio of the cohort, has placed this offering with his family, in accordance with his father's vow.
EDCS-09900542.summary: This inscription records a votive offering to the god Sol by Aurelius Barsamsus, a military officer, likely dating to the 2nd or 3rd century CE.

EDCS-09900543.text_edition: \n Deo Soli Iulius Donatus summus curator votum solvit libens laetus merito\n
EDCS-09900543.translation: To the Sun-god, Iulius Donatus, the chief curator, has willingly, joyfully, and deservedly fulfilled his vow.
EDCS-09900543.summary: This inscription records a vow fulfilled to the god Sol by Iulius Donatus, a high-ranking official, likely dating to the 2nd or 3rd century CE.

EDCS-09900544.text_edition: \n Deo Soli Aelagabalo sic pro salute Impperatorum Luci Septimi Severi Pii et Marci Aureli Antonini Pii et Cai Septimi G<e=A>tae Caesaris Auggustorum cohors |(miliaria) Antoniniana Hemesenorum civium Romanorum sagittariorum cui sub Baebio Caeciliano legato Auggustorum praeest Quintus Modius Quinti filius Quirina Ru<f=E>inus tribunus temp<l=I>um a solo extruxit \n
EDCS-09900544.translation: For the health of the Emperors Lucius Septimius Severus Pius and Marcus Aurelius Antoninus Pius and Caesar Augustus Gaius Septimius Geta Augustus, the cohort (miliaria) Antoniniana Hemesenorum of Roman citizens, archers, commanded by Quintus Modius Ruinus, son of Quintus, of the Quirina tribe, under Baebius Caecilianus, legate of the Emperors, has built this temple from the ground up.
EDCS-09900544.summary: This inscription records the construction of a temple by the Antoninian Hemesenorum cohort, dating to the joint reign of Septimius Severus, Caracalla, and Geta (209-211 CE).

EDCS-09900545.summary: This is a short dedication to the Sun-god Elagabalus, likely dating to the early 3rd century CE.

EDCS-09900547.text_edition: \n Aelagabali sic VI umtario filio Eupr<e=F>pete lib<e=F>rto \n
EDCS-09900547.translation: To Aelagabalus, son of Sextus, a freedman Euprepes.
EDCS-09900547.summary: This inscription is a dedication to the emperor Elagabalus, likely on a public monument, with the dedicant identified as a freedman named Euprepes.

EDCS-09900548.summary: This is a dedication to the goddess Venus Victorious, with the inscription incomplete.

EDCS-09900549.text_edition: \n I Rusticus Aurelia Italia mater votum libens solvit\n
EDCS-09900549.translation: I, Aurelia Italia, mother, willingly fulfilled her vow.
EDCS-09900549.summary: This inscription records a woman, Aurelia Italia, fulfilling a vow.

EDCS-09900550.translation: Castus willingly and deservedly fulfilled his vow.
EDCS-09900550.summary: This is a votive inscription where a person named Castus fulfilled a vow.

EDCS-09900551.text_edition: \n C<hr=RH>ysanthus servus arkarius sic votum solvit libens merito \n
EDCS-09900551.translation: Chrysanthus, the accountant slave, willingly and deservedly fulfilled his vow.
EDCS-09900551.summary: This is a votive inscription by Chrysanthus, a slave working as an accountant.

EDCS-09900552.text_edition: \n Iulius Sallustianus veteranus ex de<c=O>(urione) cohortis |(miliariae) Hemesenorum votum solvit libens merito \n
EDCS-09900552.translation: Julius Sallustianus, a veteran decurion of the 1st cohort of the Hemesenorum, willingly and deservedly fulfilled his vow.
EDCS-09900552.summary: This inscription records a vow fulfilled by Julius Sallustianus, a veteran decurion of a Syrian cohort.

EDCS-09900553.text_edition: \n Primitivus Zoili votum solvit libens merito\n
EDCS-09900553.translation: Primitivus fulfilled his vow to Zoilos, willingly and deservedly.
EDCS-09900553.summary: This is a votive inscription by Primitivus dedicated to someone named Zoilos.

EDCS-09900555.translation: The worshippers by the judgment of the oath...
EDCS-09900555.summary: This incomplete inscription likely refers to a group of worshippers united by an oath.

EDCS-09900556.text_edition: \n le<g=C>(ionis) II adiutricis piae fidelis ex voto pro se et suis \n
EDCS-09900556.translation: ...of the 2nd Adiutrix legion, loyal and faithful, according to a vow for himself and his family.
EDCS-09900556.summary: This inscription refers to members of the 2nd Adiutrix legion fulfilling a vow for themselves and their families.

EDCS-09900557.text_edition: \n Utere fel<i=E>x Severe coniux dulcissime somnis \n
EDCS-09900557.translation: Use happy dreams, my dearest wife, Severa.
EDCS-09900557.summary: This is an epitaph or a personal dedication, possibly on a tomb or memorial, for a beloved wife named Severa.

EDCS-09900558.text_edition: \n SI sacrum m sol con KRO \n
EDCS-09900558.summary: This inscription, likely fragmentary, is a dedication to the sun god Sol Invictus.

EDCS-09900562.text_edition: \n Pro salute Imperatoris Caesaris Traiani Hadriani Augusti patris patriae Publius Aelius Firmus Rusconis filius ob honorem aedilitatis locus datus decreto decurionum\n
EDCS-09900562.translation: For the health and safety of the Emperor Caesar Trajan Hadrian Augustus, father of the fatherland, Publius Aelius Firmus, son of Rusco, on account of the honor of the aedilship, was granted a site by decree of the decurions.
EDCS-09900562.summary: This inscription records a grant of land to Publius Aelius Firmus by the local council (decurions) for his achievement of the office of aedile, dedicated to the health of Emperor Hadrian.

EDCS-09900563.text_edition: \n Imperatore Caesare Tito Aelio Hadriano Antonino Augusto Pio patre patriae pontes facti iussu Gai Gemini Capelliani legati Augusti pro praetore curante Tiberio Claudio Euprepete\n
EDCS-09900563.translation: Under Emperor Caesar Titus Aelius Hadrianus Antoninus Augustus Pius, father of the fatherland, bridges were built by order of Gaius Geminius Capellianus, imperial legate with praetorian authority, under the supervision of Tiberius Claudius Euprepes.
EDCS-09900563.summary: This inscription records the construction of bridges under Emperor Antoninus Pius, initiated by his legate Gaius Geminius Capellianus.

EDCS-09900567.text_edition: \n Imperator Caesar Marcus Aurelius Commodus Antoninus Augustus Pius Sarmaticus Germanicus pontifex maximus tribunicia potestate VI imperator IIII consul IIII pater patriae ripam omnem burgis a solo extructis item praesidis per loca opportuna ad clandestinos latrunculorum transitus oppositis munivit per Lucium Cornelium Felicem Plotianum legatum pro praetore\n
EDCS-09900567.translation: Emperor Caesar Marcus Aurelius Commodus Antoninus Augustus Pius, Sarmaticus, Germanicus, chief priest, in his sixth year of tribunician power, four times consul, father of the fatherland, fortified the entire riverbank with fortifications built from the ground up, and also with defenses placed in suitable locations to prevent clandestine crossings by brigands, through Lucius Cornelius Felix Plotianus, his legate with praetorian authority.
EDCS-09900567.summary: This inscription details the extensive defensive works, including fortifications and garrisons, undertaken along the riverbank under the command of Emperor Commodus, implemented by his legate Lucius Cornelius Felix Plotianus, to combat brigandage.

EDCS-09900569.text_edition: \n Imperator Caesar Marcus Aurelius Commodus Antoninus Augustus Pius Sarmaticus Germanicus pontifex maximus tribunicia potestate VI imperator IIII consul IIII pater patriae ripam omnem burgis a solo extructis item praesidiis per loca opportuna ad clandestinos latrunculorum transitus oppositis munivit per Lucium Cornelium Felicem Plotianum legatum pro praetore\n
EDCS-09900569.translation: Emperor Caesar Marcus Aurelius Commodus Antoninus Augustus Pius, Sarmaticus, Germanicus, chief priest, in his sixth year of tribunician power, four times consul, father of the fatherland, fortified the entire riverbank with fortifications built from the ground up, and also with defenses placed in suitable locations to prevent clandestine crossings by brigands, through Lucius Cornelius Felix Plotianus, his legate with praetorian authority.
EDCS-09900569.summary: This inscription details the extensive defensive works, including fortifications and garrisons, undertaken along the riverbank under the command of Emperor Commodus, implemented by his legate Lucius Cornelius Felix Plotianus, to combat brigandage.

EDCS-09900573.text_edition: \n Imperator Caesar Marcus Aurelius Commodus Antoninus Augustus Pius Sarmaticus Germanicus Brittannicus pontifex maximus tribunicia potestate VI imperator IIII consul IIII pater patriae ripam omnem burgis a solo extructis item praesidiis per loca opportuna ad clandestinos latrunculorum transitus oppositis munivit per Lucium Cornelium Felicem Plotianum legatum pro praetore\n
EDCS-09900573.translation: Emperor Caesar Marcus Aurelius Commodus Antoninus Augustus Pius, Sarmaticus, Germanicus, Britannicus, chief priest, in his sixth year of tribunician power, four times consul, father of the fatherland, fortified the entire riverbank with fortifications built from the ground up, and also with defenses placed in suitable locations to prevent clandestine crossings by brigands, through Lucius Cornelius Felix Plotianus, his legate with praetorian authority.
EDCS-09900573.summary: This inscription details the extensive defensive works, including fortifications and garrisons, undertaken along the riverbank under the command of Emperor Commodus, implemented by his legate Lucius Cornelius Felix Plotianus, to combat brigandage.

EDCS-09900574.text_edition: \n Imperator Caesar Marcus Aurelius Commodus Antoninus Augustus Pius Sarmaticus Germanicus Britannicus pontifex maximus tribunicia potestate VI imperator IIII consul IIII pater patriae ripam omnem burgis a solo extructis item praesidiis per loca opportuna ad clandestinos latrunculorum transitus oppositis munivit per Lucium Cornelium Felicem Plotianum legatum pro praetore\n
EDCS-09900574.translation: Emperor Caesar Marcus Aurelius Commodus Antoninus Augustus Pius, Sarmaticus, Germanicus, Britannicus, chief priest, in his sixth year of tribunician power, four times consul, father of the fatherland, fortified the entire riverbank with fortifications built from the ground up, and also with defenses placed in suitable locations to prevent clandestine crossings by brigands, through Lucius Cornelius Felix Plotianus, his legate with praetorian authority.
EDCS-09900574.summary: This inscription details the extensive defensive works, including fortifications and garrisons, undertaken along the riverbank under the command of Emperor Commodus, implemented by his legate Lucius Cornelius Felix Plotianus, to combat brigandage.

EDCS-09900577.text_edition: \n Pro salute et victoria Germanica Imperatoris Caesaris Marci Aureli Severi Antonini Pii Felicis Augusti Parthici maximi Britannici maximi Germanici maximi pontificis maximi patris patriae tribunicia potestate consulis IIII Deo Patrio Soli Elagabalo milites cohortis |(miliariae) Hemesenorum Antoninianae dedicatum opus X Kalendas Septembres Messala et Sabino consulibus\n
EDCS-09900577.translation: For the health and victory in Germany of the Emperor Caesar Marcus Aurelius Severus Antoninus Pius Felix Augustus, greatest Parthian, greatest Briton, greatest German, chief priest, father of the fatherland, in his consular year, the soldiers of the first cohort (miliaria) of Hemesenian Antoninian archers dedicated this work to the Father God Sol Elagabalus on the 23rd of August, when Messala and Sabinus were consuls.
EDCS-09900577.summary: This inscription records a dedication by soldiers of the cohort of Hemesenian archers to the god Sol Elagabalus for the military successes and well-being of Emperor Caracalla, dated to 215 AD.

EDCS-09900578.text_edition: \n Imperatori Caesari Marco Aurelio Severo Antonino Pio Felici Augusto Parthico Britannico maximo pontifici maximo Germanico maximo cohors I |(miliaria) Hemesenorum Aurelia Antoniniana sagittariorum equitata civium Romanorum devotissima numini eius\n
EDCS-09900578.translation: The first cohort (miliaria) of Hemesenian Aurelian Antoninian archers, equestrian, Roman citizens, most devoted to his divine majesty, dedicated this to the Emperor Caesar Marcus Aurelius Severus Antoninus Pius Felix Augustus, greatest Parthian, greatest Briton, greatest German, chief priest.
EDCS-09900578.summary: This inscription records a dedication by soldiers of a Hemesenian archer cohort to Emperor Caracalla, expressing their devotion to him.

EDCS-09900581.text_edition: \n Sanctissimo ac super omnes principes clementissimo Imperatori Caesari Gaio Vibio Treboniano Gallo Pio Felici Invicto Augusto pontifici maximo tribunicia potestate II consuli II patri patriae proconsuli cohors I |(miliaria) Hemesenorum maiestati eorum devotissima\n
EDCS-09900581.translation: The first cohort (miliaria) of Hemesenian, most devoted to their majesty, dedicated this to the most holy and most merciful Emperor Caesar Gaius Vibius Trebonianus Gallus Pius Felix Invictus Augustus, chief priest, in his second year of tribunician power, twice consul, father of the fatherland, proconsul.
EDCS-09900581.summary: This inscription records a dedication by soldiers of a Hemesenian cohort to Emperor Trebonianus Gallus, expressing their devotion to him during his reign (c. 251-253 AD).

EDCS-09900583.text_edition: \n Absucus Adnamonis filius annorum LX hic situs est Nertomarus et Locco Atedunus filii et heredes titulum memoriae posuerunt\n
EDCS-09900583.translation: Absucus, son of Adnamon, aged 60, lies here; Nertomarus and Locco, sons of Atedunus, and heirs, placed this monument to his memory.
EDCS-09900583.summary: A tombstone recording the death of Absucus, son of Adnamon, aged 60, with the monument erected by his sons Nertomarus and Locco.

EDCS-09900584.text_edition: \n Adnamata Carveicionis filia nnorum L hic sita est Claudius Cesoris coniugi piissimae titulum memoriae posuit\n
EDCS-09900584.translation: Adnamata, daughter of Carveicion, aged 50, lies here; Claudius Cesoris erected this monument to his most devoted wife.
EDCS-09900584.summary: A tombstone recording the death of Adnamata, daughter of Carveicion, aged 50, with the monument erected by her husband Claudius Cesoris.

EDCS-09900585.text_edition: \n Adinamo Acuti filius annorum XXXX hic est situs eques occisus in civitate Eraviscorum defunctus Tibeurnus et Montanus filii devotissimi pateri sic suo titulum posuerunt memoriae\n
EDCS-09900585.translation: Acutus, son of Adinamio, aged 40, lies here; a cavalryman killed in the territory of the Eravisci. His most devoted sons Tibeurnus and Montanus erected this monument to their father.
EDCS-09900585.summary: A tombstone for Acutus, son of Adinamio, a 40-year-old cavalryman killed fighting the Eravisci, erected by his sons Tibeurnus and Montanus.

EDCS-09900587.text_edition: \n Dis Manibus Aeliae Mattunae quae et Au hic <e=C>(st) sita nnorum XXX coniuigi bene merenti Verecumberae pientissimae Aurelius Vindo et sibi vivo posuit \n
EDCS-09900587.translation: To the Divine Spirits. Aelia Mattuna, who is also called Au, aged 30, lies here; Aurelius Vindo erected this for his most devoted wife and for himself while alive.
EDCS-09900587.summary: A tombstone dedicated to Aelia Mattuna, also known as Au, aged 30, erected by her husband Aurelius Vindo for her and himself.

EDCS-09900588.translation: To Publius Aelius, son of OlyM Aelius.
EDCS-09900588.summary: An incomplete funerary dedication to Publius Aelius, son of OlyM Aelius.

EDCS-09900589.text_edition: \n Aelius Cesumarius Libbonis filius sibi et coniugi et filio vivo posuit\n
EDCS-09900589.translation: Aelius Cesumarius, son of Libbo, erected this for himself, his wife, and his son while alive.
EDCS-09900589.summary: A funerary monument erected by Aelius Cesumarius, son of Libbo, for himself, his wife, and his son.

EDCS-09900590.text_edition: \n Dis Manibus Aelio Munatio capsario cohortis |(miliariae) Hemesenorum stupendiorum sic XXVIII domo Samosata Aurelia Cansauna coniunx Antonio Basso vexillario secundo herede sanctissimo coniugi con sic se sic natibusque suis fecit monumentum memoriae\n
EDCS-09900590.translation: To the Divine Spirits. Aurelia Cansauna erected this monument to her most sacred husband, Antonio Basso, a second-class standard-bearer of the 28th mixed cohort, from Samosata, and to their descendants.
EDCS-09900590.summary: A tombstone for Antonio Basso, a standard-bearer from Samosata, erected by his wife Aurelia Cansauna and intended for their descendants.

EDCS-09900593.text_edition: \n Publius Aelius Vitalis annorum XIII et Publius Aelius Victor annorum I hic siti sunt Marcus Ulpius Ianuarius filiis posuit\n
EDCS-09900593.translation: Publius Aelius Vitalis, aged 13, and Publius Aelius Victor, aged 1, lie here; Marcus Ulpius Ianuarius erected this for his sons.
EDCS-09900593.summary: A tombstone recording the deaths of Publius Aelius Vitalis, aged 13, and Publius Aelius Victor, aged 1, erected by their father Marcus Ulpius Ianuarius.

EDCS-09900595.text_edition: \n Dis Manibus Marcus Antonius Censorinus domo Apamia annorum LXXX et Marcus Antonius Optatus nepos eius annorum VII hic siti sunt et Ulpiae Zosimenae vivae coniugi eius Marcus Antonius Marcianus parentibus et filio eorum faciendum curavit\n
EDCS-09900595.translation: To the Divine Spirits. Marcus Antonius Marcianus had this monument prepared for his parents, Marcus Antonius Censorinus, aged 80, from Apamia, and Ulpia Zosimena, his wife, and for their son.
EDCS-09900595.summary: A funerary monument prepared by Marcus Antonius Marcianus for his parents, Marcus Antonius Censorinus and Ulpia Zosimena, and their son.

EDCS-09900596.text_edition: \n militi legionis qui vixit nns Appius filius et Serena filia pientissima cum? --- ---? Equite et Claudio Cy\n
EDCS-09900596.translation: For a soldier of the legion who lived... Appius and Serena, a most devoted daughter, with? --- ---? a cavalryman and Claudio Cy.
EDCS-09900596.summary: An incomplete funerary inscription for a soldier of a legion, with dedications from his children Appius and Serena, and mentions of a cavalryman.

EDCS-09900597.text_edition: \n Dis Manibus Aureliae Barachae vixit nnos XXXV et Aur<e=H>liae Germanillae vixit nnos IIII et altera filia Aurelia Germanilla vixit nnos II et Immostae matri suae vixit nnos LX Germanius Valens miles cohortis Mmiliariae Hemesenorum uxori et matri et filiis posuit et sibi vivius fecit \n
EDCS-09900597.translation: To the spirits of the departed: Aurelia Baracha lived 35 years, and Aurelia Germanilla lived 4 years, and another daughter Aurelia Germanilla lived 2 years, and [to] her mother Immosta, who lived 60 years. Germanius Valens, a soldier of the thousand-man cohort of the Hemesenorum, erected this for his wife, mother, and sons, and made this for himself while alive.
EDCS-09900597.summary: This is a funerary epitaph for Aurelia Baracha, her two daughters, and her mother, dedicated by her husband, Germanius Valens, a soldier, likely from the 2nd to 3rd century CE.

EDCS-09900598.text_edition: \n Dis Manibus Aureliae Heraclitae filiae quae vixit annis XVII mensibus VII diebus XXII Marcus Aurelius Heraclitus hastatus legionis VII Claudiae pater et Pia mater et ante eos Eppius Flavianus evokatus sic maritus pietatis causa posuerunt\n
EDCS-09900598.translation: To the spirits of the departed: For Aurelia Heraclita, daughter, who lived 17 years, 7 months, and 22 days. Marcus Aurelius Heraclitus, standard-bearer of the VII Claudia legion, her father, and Pia, her mother, and before them Eppius Flavianus, a recalled soldier, her husband, erected this out of dutiful affection.
EDCS-09900598.summary: This funerary epitaph records the death of Aurelia Heraclita at age 17, dedicated by her father, mother, and husband, dating to the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-09900599.text_edition: \n inae Corneliae Aurelia Iustina filia et mater titulum posuoerunt Aurelio Sal Augustali coloniae Aquincensium\n
EDCS-09900599.translation: To their mother Cornelia Aurelia Iustina, daughter and mother erected this monument for Aurelius Sal, Augustalis of the colony of Aquincensium.
EDCS-09900599.summary: This is a funerary monument erected by a daughter and mother for Aurelius Sal, an Augustalis in the colony of Aquincensium, likely from the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-09900600.text_edition: \n Dis Manibus Aurelia Marcia et Flavius Avo Aureliae Agrippinae filiae quae vixit nnos VI titulum bene merenti posuerunt\n
EDCS-09900600.translation: To the spirits of the departed: For Aurelia Marcia and her daughter Aurelia Agrippina, who lived 6 years. Flavius Avo erected this monument for the well-deserving.
EDCS-09900600.summary: This funerary epitaph records Aurelia Agrippina, who died at age 6, and is dedicated by her parents, Aurelia Marcia and Flavius Avo, from the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-09900601.text_edition: \n Dis Manibus Aureliae Nardanosae cives sic Armeniacae vixit nnos XLIII Aurelius Manaia veteranus maritus et Aurelius Tata Pusintulus filius memoriae posuerunt\n
EDCS-09900601.translation: To the spirits of the departed: For Aurelia Nardanosa, citizen of Armenia, who lived 43 years. Aurelius Manaia, a veteran, her husband, and Aurelius Tata Pusintulus, her son, erected this in memory.
EDCS-09900601.summary: This funerary epitaph commemorates Aurelia Nardanosa, who lived 43 years, dedicated by her veteran husband Aurelius Manaia and son Aurelius Tata Pusintulus, likely from the 2nd to 3rd century CE.

EDCS-09900602.text_edition: \n Dis Manibus memoriae Aureliae Proculinae qua e vixit annos XVII Aurelia Ursula avia posuit\n
EDCS-09900602.translation: To the spirits of the departed: For Aurelia Proculina, who lived 17 years. Aurelia Ursula, her grandmother, erected this in memory.
EDCS-09900602.summary: This funerary epitaph records Aurelia Proculina, who died at age 17, dedicated by her grandmother Aurelia Ursula, from the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-09900603.text_edition: \n Dis Manibus Aurelia Serena suo coniugi Aristio Fortunato fecit memoriam qui fuit defunctus centurio nnorum quinquaginta ligionis sic II adiutricis\n
EDCS-09900603.translation: To the spirits of the departed: Aurelia Serena made this memorial for her husband Aristius Fortunatus, who died at the age of fifty, a centurion of the II Adiutrix legion.
EDCS-09900603.summary: This funerary epitaph records the death of Aristius Fortunatus, a centurion of the II Adiutrix legion, dedicated by his wife Aurelia Serena, likely from the 2nd to 3rd century CE.

EDCS-09900604.text_edition: \n Dis Manibus V vixit nnos LIX et Aureliuss summus curator cohortis |(miliariae) Hemesenorum filius patri piissimo posuit\n
EDCS-09900604.translation: To the spirits of the departed: [Someone] lived 59 years. And Aurelius, chief curator of the thousand-man cohort of the Hemesenorum, her son, erected this for his most dutiful father.
EDCS-09900604.summary: This funerary epitaph records the death of an unnamed individual aged 59, dedicated by their son Aurelius, who was a curator in the Hemesenorum cohort, likely from the 2nd to 3rd century CE.

EDCS-09900605.text_edition: \n Aurelius qui vixit nnos qui et A Marcus Aurelius \n
EDCS-09900605.translation: Aurelius, who lived... also Marcus Aurelius.
EDCS-09900605.summary: This is a very fragmentary inscription that appears to record the name and lifespan of an Aurelius, possibly Marcus Aurelius, from the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-09900606.summary: This fragmentary inscription likely records a lifespan of 70 years and may refer to an individual named Marcus Aurelius, from the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-09900607.summary: This is a simple inscription naming Marcus Aurelius, likely the subject of a monument or the dedicator.

EDCS-09900608.text_edition: \n X et Marco no et Marco Aurelio Maxentinae fili- \n
EDCS-09900608.translation: To X and Marcus, and to Marcus Aurelius Maxentinus, son of [---].
EDCS-09900608.summary: This inscription records a dedication to multiple individuals, including Marcus Aurelius Maxentinus, possibly a funerary monument.

EDCS-09900610.text_edition: \n Aurelius Abibelus ria filiae coniugi piissimis faciendum curavit instante As\n
EDCS-09900610.translation: Aurelius Abibelus, with his most dutiful daughter and wife, arranged for this to be made, under the supervision of As[---].
EDCS-09900610.summary: This text records that Aurelius Abibelus commissioned a monument for his dutiful daughter and wife.

EDCS-09900611.text_edition: \n Marco Aurelio Alexandro veterano legionis II adiutricis qui vixit nnos LXX et Aureliae Rufinae eius quae vixit nnos LXI et Marco Aurelio Valeriano militi cohortis |(miliariae) Hemesenorum qui vixit nnos XXV filio et Aureliae Valeri<a>nae quae vixit nnos XVIII filiae eorum Marcus Aurelius Aurelianus veteranus legionis supra scriptae ex stratore consularis co<g=C>natis dignissimis \n
EDCS-09900611.translation: To Marcus Aurelius Alexander, veteran of the Second Adjutrix Legion, who lived 70 years, and to Aurelia Rufina, his wife, who lived 61 years, and to Marcus Aurelius Valerianus, soldier of the Hemesenorum milliaria cohort, who lived 25 years, their son, and to Aurelia Valeria, their daughter, who lived 18 years; Marcus Aurelius Aurelianus, veteran of the aforementioned legion, former consular orderly, arranged for this monument for his most worthy parents.
EDCS-09900611.summary: This is a funerary inscription for multiple family members, including veterans and a soldier, commissioned by their son, a veteran and former orderly.

EDCS-09900613.text_edition: \n Dis Manibus Aurelius Antonius veteranus ex beneficiario tribuni cohortis |(miliariae) Hemesenorum posuit sibi vivus et Aureliae coniugi vivae posuit et Aureliae Iuliae fil<iae=EIA> carissimae quae vixit annos \n
EDCS-09900613.translation: To the Spirits of the Underworld. Aurelius Antonius, veteran, former beneficiarius of a tribune of the Hemesenorum milliaria cohort, set this up for himself while alive and for Aurelia, his wife, while alive, and for Aurelia Iulia, their dearest daughter, who lived [---] years.
EDCS-09900613.summary: This funerary inscription was set up by Aurelius Antonius, a veteran and former beneficiarius, for himself, his wife, and his daughter.

EDCS-09900614.text_edition: \n Dis Manibus memoriae Aureli Barbae veterani legionis II adiutricis et Aureliis Probiano militi liegionis supra scriptae Gaudentio et Firminiano qui vixit nnos VIII Aurelia Heraclia liberta ex testamento faciendum curavit\n
EDCS-09900614.translation: To the Spirits of the Underworld, in memory of Aurelius Barba, veteran of the Second Adjutrix Legion, and of Aurelius Probius, soldier of the aforementioned legion, Gaudentius and Firminianus, who lived 8 years. Aurelia Heraclia, a freedwoman, arranged for this to be made according to the will.
EDCS-09900614.summary: This funerary inscription commemorates veterans and soldiers, and was commissioned by a freedwoman according to a will.

EDCS-09900616.text_edition: \n Dis Manibus Aurelio Cassiano filio vixit annis IIII Aurelius Silvanus beneficiarius tribuni pater bene merenti posuit\n
EDCS-09900616.translation: To the Spirits of the Underworld. For Aurelius Cassianus, son, who lived 4 years. Aurelius Silvanus, father, former beneficiarius of a tribune, set this up for his well-deserving son.
EDCS-09900616.summary: This funerary inscription records the death of a young boy, Aurelius Cassianus, and was erected by his father, Aurelius Silvanus.

EDCS-09900617.text_edition: \n Dis Manibus Marcus Aurelius Cerdon veteranus ex curatore cohortis |(miliariae) Hemesenorum domo Arethusa sibi et Aureliae Iuliae suae et Marco Aurelio Silvano filio qui vixit annos XVI et Aureliae Heliodorae quae vixit nnos XXVII et Aureliae Immedaru quae vixit nnos XXIIII filiis et Marco Aurelio Vincentio qui vixit nnos VIII et Marco Aurelio Heliodoro nepotibus faciendum curavit monumentum memoriae\n
EDCS-09900617.translation: To the Spirits of the Underworld. To Marcus Aurelius Cerdon, veteran, former curator of the Hemesenorum milliaria cohort, from Arethusa. He arranged for this monument for memory for himself and Aurelia Iulia, his wife, and Marcus Aurelius Silvanus, their son, who lived 16 years, and Aurelia Heliodora, who lived 27 years, and Aurelia Immedara, who lived 24 years, their children, and Marcus Aurelius Vincentius, who lived 8 years, and Marcus Aurelius Heliodorus, their grandsons.
EDCS-09900617.summary: This funerary inscription, commissioned by a veteran named Marcus Aurelius Cerdon, commemorates himself, his wife, his children, and his grandsons.

EDCS-09900618.text_edition: \n Dis Manibus Aurelio Constituto militi legionis II adiutricis piae fidelis vixit annos XXIIII stipendiorum IIII filio cecidit in bello Germanico et Septimiae tillae matri vivae et Aureliae Constitutae filiae vivae et Aureliae Pudentillae filiae vivae et Aurelio Cupito filio vivo et Aurelio Cupitiano filio vivo Aurelius catus veteranus ex ala I Thracum pater faciendum curavit\n
EDCS-09900618.translation: To the Spirits of the Underworld. To Aurelius Constitutus, soldier of the Second Adjutrix Legion, dutiful and faithful, who lived 24 years, with 4 years of service; he fell in the German War. And to Septimia, his mother, while alive, and to Aurelia Constituta, his sister, while alive, and to Aurelia Pudentilla, his sister, while alive, and to Aurelius Cupitus, his brother, alive, and to Aurelius Cupitianus, his brother, alive. Aurelius Catus, veteran of the First Thracian Wing, his father, arranged for this to be made.
EDCS-09900618.summary: This is a funerary inscription for Aurelius Constitutus, a soldier of the Second Adjutrix Legion who died in the German War, commissioned by his father, a veteran.

EDCS-09900619.text_edition: \n Dis Manibus Marcus Aurelius Heraclitus veteranus ex |(centurione) cohortis |(miliariae) Hemesenorum annorum LXX vivus fecit sibi et Aureliis Heraclito et Sereno vivis filiis suis et Serenae hoc monumentum heredem non sequetur\n
EDCS-09900619.translation: To the Spirits of the Underworld. Marcus Aurelius Heraclitus, veteran, former centurion of the Hemesenorum milliaria cohort, aged 70, made this while alive for himself and for his sons Aurelius Heraclitus and Aurelius Sereno, while alive, and for Serena; he will not have an heir.
EDCS-09900619.summary: This funerary inscription was set up by Marcus Aurelius Heraclitus, a veteran and former centurion, for himself and his living sons and Serena, stipulating he would have no heir.

EDCS-09900621.translation: To the memory of Marcus Aurelius Luciferus.
EDCS-09900621.summary: This inscription is a dedication to the memory of Marcus Aurelius Luciferus.

EDCS-09900622.text_edition: \n Marcus Aurelius Malcihianus veteranus legionis II adiutricis et Valeriae Verencundae coniugi quae vixit nnos XXXV et Marco Aurelio Marcellino militi legionis supra scriptae filio qui vixit nnos XVIIII et Valeriae Pulc<hr=RH>ae coniugi et Valeriae Optatae filiae eorumanta fecit \n
EDCS-09900622.translation: Marcus Aurelius Malcianus, a veteran of the Second Adiutrix legion, and Valeria Verencunda, his wife who lived 35 years, and Marcus Aurelius Marcellinus, a soldier of the aforementioned legion who lived 19 years, and Valeria Pulchra, their daughter, made this monument.
EDCS-09900622.summary: This monument was erected by Marcus Aurelius Malcianus and his wife Valeria Verencunda, commemorating their deceased son and daughter, likely from the 2nd or 3rd century CE.

EDCS-09900623.text_edition: \n Dis Manibus Marco Aurelio Marco militi cohortis |(miliariae) Hemesenorum qui vixit annos XXX et Theodorae et Marcus Aurelius Marianus patri et sibi et matri karissimae vivus monumentum posuit\n
EDCS-09900623.translation: To the spirits of the underworld for Marcus Aurelius Marcus, a soldier of the Hemesenorum cohort (milliaia), who lived 30 years, and for Theodora, and Marcus Aurelius Marianus, father, and for himself and his dearest mother, he erected this monument during his lifetime.
EDCS-09900623.summary: Marcus Aurelius Marianus, a soldier of the Hemesenorum cohort, erected this monument during his lifetime to honor his deceased son, wife Theodora, and himself, likely dating to the 2nd or 3rd century CE.

EDCS-09900624.text_edition: \n Dis Manibus Aurelio Maximiano militi cohortis |(miliariae) Hemesenorum stipendiorum II qui vixit annos XVI menses VII et Aurelio Prisco fratri ipsius qui vixit annos XI Aurelius Bassus miles cohortis supra scriptae heres ex testamento eorum posuit\n
EDCS-09900624.translation: To the spirits of the underworld for Aurelius Maximianus, a soldier of the Hemesenorum cohort (milliaia) with 2 years of service, who lived 16 years and 7 months, and for Aurelius Priscus, his brother, who lived 11 years. Aurelius Bassus, a soldier of the aforementioned cohort, as heir by their will, placed this monument.
EDCS-09900624.summary: Aurelius Bassus, a soldier of the Hemesenorum cohort, erected this monument as heir to his deceased brother Aurelius Maximianus and his brother Aurelius Priscus, likely from the 2nd or 3rd century CE.

EDCS-09900625.text_edition: \n Dis Manibus Aurelio Monimo beneficiario tribuni cohortis |(miliariae) Hemesenorum stipendiorum XXIIII vixit annos XLV G Bassus librarius bene merenti posuit ex ipsius praecepto\n
EDCS-09900625.translation: To the spirits of the underworld for Aurelius Monimus, a beneficiarius of a tribune of the Hemesenorum cohort (milliaia) with 24 years of service, who lived 45 years. G. Bassus, a scribe, placed this monument for the well-deserving man, by his own instruction.
EDCS-09900625.summary: G. Bassus, a scribe, erected this monument at the request of Aurelius Monimus, a beneficiarius of the Hemesenorum cohort, who lived 45 years, likely dating to the 2nd or 3rd century CE.

EDCS-09900626.text_edition: \n Dis Manibus Marco Aurelio Monimo veterano ex decurione cohortis |(miliariae) Hemesenorum domo Hemesa qui vixit nnos LXIIII coniugi Iulia Ticima et Aurelius Iulianus strator? consularis filius \n
EDCS-09900626.translation: To the spirits of the underworld for Marcus Aurelius Monimus, a veteran and former decurion of the Hemesenorum cohort (milliaia) from Hemesa, who lived 64 years, for his wife Iulia Ticima, and for Aurelius Iulianus, his son, a strator? of a consular official.
EDCS-09900626.summary: This monument commemorates Marcus Aurelius Monimus, a veteran and former decurion of the Hemesenorum cohort from Hemesa, his wife Iulia Ticima, and their son Aurelius Iulianus, likely dating to the 2nd or 3rd century CE.

EDCS-09900627.text_edition: \n Marco Aurelio Monimo veterano legionis II adiutricis Sabini quondam veterani domo Hemesa filio patri qui vixit nnos LV et Aureliae Alexandriae matri quae vixit nnos XXXV et Aureliae Athenu sorori quae vixit nnos XX Marcus Aurelius Alexander miles legionis librarius officialis praesidis\n
EDCS-09900627.translation: For Marcus Aurelius Monimus, a veteran of the Second Adiutrix legion, son of Sabinus, a former veteran from Hemesa, who lived 55 years, and for Aurelia Alexandria, his mother, who lived 35 years, and for Aurelia Athenu, his sister, who lived 20 years. Marcus Aurelius Alexander, a soldier of the legion and scribe of the governor, [erected this].
EDCS-09900627.summary: This monument was erected by Marcus Aurelius Alexander, a scribe of the governor and soldier of the Second Adiutrix legion, in memory of his father Marcus Aurelius Monimus, his mother Aurelia Alexandria, and his sister Aurelia Athenu.

EDCS-09900628.text_edition: \n Dis Manibus Marcus Aurelius Primianus veteranus ex decurione cohortis |(milliariae) Hemesenorum nnorum L et Septimiae Gratae uxori pientissimae annorum XXXVI et Aureliae Ianuariae filiae annorum XX Marcus Aurelius Primianus sibi et suis vivos faciendum curavit\n
EDCS-09900628.translation: To the spirits of the underworld for Marcus Aurelius Primianus, a veteran and former decurion of the Hemesenorum cohort (milliaria), aged 50, and for Septimia Grata, his most devoted wife, aged 36, and for Aurelia Ianuarie, their daughter, aged 20. Marcus Aurelius Primianus arranged for this monument to be made during his lifetime for himself and his family.
EDCS-09900628.summary: Marcus Aurelius Primianus, a veteran and former decurion of the Hemesenorum cohort, commissioned this monument during his lifetime for himself, his wife Septimia Grata, and their daughter Aurelia Ianuarie, likely dating to the 2nd or 3rd century CE.

EDCS-09900631.summary: This inscription is a dedication to Marcus Aurelius Romanus.

EDCS-09900632.text_edition: \n Dis Manibus Marco Aurelio Rufiniano qui vixit nnos X filio item Aureliae Rufinae filiae superstiti Marcus Aurelius Rufinus miles legionis I adiutricis et Ulpia Firmina coniux eius parentes sibi quoque posuerunt\n
EDCS-09900632.translation: To the spirits of the underworld for his son Marcus Aurelius Rufinianus, who lived 10 years, and also for his daughter Aurelia Rufina, who survived him. Marcus Aurelius Rufinus, a soldier of the First Adiutrix legion, and Ulpia Firmina, his wife, the parents, also placed this monument for themselves.
EDCS-09900632.summary: Marcus Aurelius Rufinus, a soldier of the First Adiutrix legion, and his wife Ulpia Firmina erected this monument for their deceased son Marcus Aurelius Rufinianus and surviving daughter Aurelia Rufina, also dedicating it for themselves.

EDCS-09900633.text_edition: \n Dis Manibus Aurelio Salamati veterano ex <d=I>ucenario civi Aedessitano sic qui vixit annis LXX in his militavit nnis XL Aurelius Pusintulus gener ex faciendum curavit \n
EDCS-09900633.translation: To the spirits of the departed. Aurelius Salamatus, a veteran duicenarius, citizen of Aedessa, who lived for 70 years, having served for 40 years, was laid here. His son-in-law, Aurelius Pusintulus, had this made.
EDCS-09900633.summary: A funerary epitaph for Aurelius Salamatus, a veteran duicenarius from Aedessa, likely dating to the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-09900634.text_edition: \n Dis Manibus Marco Aurelio Silvano veterano ex benficiario tribuni domo Hemesa vexit sic annis LX Aurelia Hilara coniuxs sic et Aurelii Salvianus et Silvanus e<t=I> et Silvina filii carissimi et conheredes bene merenti et digno posuerunt \n
EDCS-09900634.translation: To the spirits of the departed. Marcus Aurelius Silvanus, a veteran beneficiarius tribuni, from Hemesa, who lived for 60 years, was laid here. Aurelia Hilara, his wife, and his dearest sons Aurelius Salvianus and Silvanus, and Silvina, and co-heirs, placed this for him, a deserving and worthy man.
EDCS-09900634.summary: A funerary epitaph for Marcus Aurelius Silvanus, a veteran beneficiarius tribuni from Hemesa, and his family, dating to the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-09900635.text_edition: \n Dis Manibus Marcus Aurelius Silvanus bene<f=E>(iciarius) consularis legionis I adiutricis et Aurelia Firmina coniux eius vivi sibi posuerunt VIVI \n
EDCS-09900635.translation: To the spirits of the departed. Marcus Aurelius Silvanus, beneficiarius consularis of the I Adiutrix legion, and Aurelia Firmina, his wife, placed this for themselves while living.
EDCS-09900635.summary: A joint funerary epitaph for Marcus Aurelius Silvanus, a beneficiarius consularis of Legio I Adiutrix, and his wife Aurelia Firmina, likely dating to the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-09900636.text_edition: \n Aurelius Monimus eques fratri carissimo memoriam posuit Dis Manibus Aurelius Valens barbaricatus numeri eqquitum Scutariorum sub cura Marini praepositi\n
EDCS-09900636.translation: To the spirits of the departed. Aurelius Valens, a barbarized cavalryman of the Scutarii unit under the command of the prefect Marinus, placed this memorial for his dearest brother, Aurelius Monimus, an equestrian.
EDCS-09900636.summary: A funerary epitaph for Aurelius Valens, a cavalryman of the Scutarii, set up by his brother Aurelius Monimus, likely dating to the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-09900637.text_edition: \n Dis Manibus Axeto curator alae I Thracum hic situs est Ulpia Ingenua bene merenti et Ulpio Optato et Ulpiae Sensutae filiis vivis et sibi viva posuit curam agente Arruntio Postumo herede\n
EDCS-09900637.translation: To the spirits of the departed. Axetus, curator of the I Thracian ala, rests here. Ulpia Ingenua placed this for her deserving daughter and for her sons Ulpius Optatus and Ulpia Sensuta, and for herself while living, with Arruntius Postumus, the heir, managing the arrangements.
EDCS-09900637.summary: A funerary epitaph for Axetus, curator of the I Thracian ala, and his family, likely dating to the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-09900638.text_edition: \n Basia Arionis filia annorum L hic sita est Vercomberus Tattunis filius \n
EDCS-09900638.translation: Basia, daughter of Arion, aged 50, rests here. Vercomberus, son of Tattunus.
EDCS-09900638.summary: A simple funerary epitaph for Basia, daughter of Arion, and Vercomberus, son of Tattunus, likely dating to the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-09900639.text_edition: \n Dis Manibus Caeciliae Ingenua<e=V> quae vixit annis XVI diebus XVII Iulia Fortunata mater \n
EDCS-09900639.translation: To the spirits of the departed. For Caecilia Ingenua, who lived for 16 years and 17 days. Her mother, Iulia Fortunata.
EDCS-09900639.summary: A funerary epitaph for Caecilia Ingenua, who died at a young age, set up by her mother Iulia Fortunata, likely dating to the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-09900640.text_edition: \n Caeserini filia rtus GOI T et Optato filio fecit\n
EDCS-09900640.translation: Caeserinus' daughter, [---] GOI T and for Optatus her son, made this.
EDCS-09900640.summary: A fragmentary funerary epitaph mentioning Caeserinus' daughter and a son named Optatus, likely dating to the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-09900641.text_edition: \n Dis Manibus Caius Calpurnius Dionysius annorum XLV hic situs est Calpurnia Procula fratri pientissimo et Caius Calpurnius Philetus posuerunt\n
EDCS-09900641.translation: To the spirits of the departed. Caius Calpurnius Dionysius, aged 45, rests here. Calpurnia Procula, for her most dutiful brother, and Caius Calpurnius Philetus, placed this.
EDCS-09900641.summary: A funerary epitaph for Caius Calpurnius Dionysius, set up by his sister Calpurnia Procula and his brother Caius Calpurnius Philetus, likely dating to the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-09900642.text_edition: \n CVI I ANIC Castor Publio Faladi amico posuit\n
EDCS-09900642.translation: CVI I ANIC Castor placed this for Publius Faladi, his friend.
EDCS-09900642.summary: A funerary epitaph recording the friendship between Castor and Publius Faladi, likely dating to the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-09900645.text_edition: \n Tiberius Claudius onis filius Pintamus |(sesquiplicarius) natione Zoela sic eques alae Asturum II nnorum LIII stipendiorum \n
EDCS-09900645.translation: Tiberius Claudius Pintamus, son of Onis, a sesquiplicarius, Zoela by nationality, a cavalryman of the Astures Ala, aged 53, with 22 years of service.
EDCS-09900645.summary: This inscription records the burial of Tiberius Claudius Pintamus, a cavalryman from the Astures Ala, who died at age 53 after 22 years of service.

EDCS-09900646.text_edition: \n Dis Manibus Claudius Antonius vixit annis V Antoninus Russus vexillarius filio merenti posuit\n
EDCS-09900646.translation: To the spirits of the departed. Claudius Antonius lived for 5 years. Antoninus Russus, a vexillarius, erected this for his deserving son.
EDCS-09900646.summary: This is a funerary epitaph for Claudius Antonius, who died at the age of 5, erected by his father Antoninus Russus.

EDCS-09900647.text_edition: \n Crispina annorum C Enigenus titulum posuit\n
EDCS-09900647.translation: Crispina, aged 100. Enigenus erected this memorial.
EDCS-09900647.summary: This inscription records the erection of a memorial by Enigenus for Crispina, who died at the age of 100.

EDCS-09900648.text_edition: \n Cusa Nertomari filius annorumX hic situs est Bucla Saturnini filia titulum posuit coniugi pientissimo et sibi viva viv\n
EDCS-09900648.translation: Cusa, son of Nertomarus, aged 10. Bucla, daughter of Saturninus, erected this memorial for her most devoted husband and for herself while still living.
EDCS-09900648.summary: This is a funerary epitaph for Cusa, son of Nertomarus, erected by his wife Bucla Saturnini for her husband and herself.

EDCS-09900649.text_edition: \n Cusides Disalae filius eques alae Frontonianae hic situs est Bitugentus heres titulum memoriae posuit\n
EDCS-09900649.translation: Cusides, son of Disala, a cavalryman of the Frontonian Ala, is buried here. Bitugentus, his heir, erected this memorial.
EDCS-09900649.summary: This inscription marks the burial place of Cusides, a cavalryman of the Frontonian Ala, with the memorial erected by his heir Bitugentus.

EDCS-09900650.text_edition: \n Danuius Diassumari filius annorum LXXX hic situs est et Concordiae Flori filiae coniugi eius annorum LXXX vivae titulum memoriae posuit Maturus filius eorum parentibus pientissimis\n
EDCS-09900650.translation: Danuvius, son of Diassumarus, aged 80, and his wife Concordia, daughter of Florus, aged 80, are buried here. Their son Maturus erected this memorial for his most devoted parents while they were alive.
EDCS-09900650.summary: This inscription is a memorial erected by Maturus for his parents, Danuvius and Concordia, who were both 80 years old, indicating they were buried while alive.

EDCS-09900653.text_edition: \n Osa Demunici liberta Dis Manibus Demiuncus Couci filius annorum C et Angulata Campionis filia annorum C coniunx titulum posuerunt Batalus et Louco liberti faciendum curaverunt et Veneriae sorori vivae\n
EDCS-09900653.translation: To the spirits of the departed. Demiuncus, son of Coucus, aged 100, and his wife Angulata, daughter of Campio, aged 100, are buried here. Osa, freedwoman of Demunnicus, and her freedmen Batalus and Loucus took care of its construction. Also for Veneria, sister [of Osa], while alive.
EDCS-09900653.summary: This funerary inscription records the burial of Demiuncus and his wife Angulata, with the monument arranged by their freedwoman Osa and her freedmen, also including Osa's sister.

EDCS-09900654.text_edition: \n Deomarus DurRati filius hic situs est\n
EDCS-09900654.translation: Deomarus, son of DurRatus, is buried here.
EDCS-09900654.summary: This simple inscription records the burial of Deomarus, son of DurRatus.

EDCS-09900655.text_edition: \n Derva Sisinae filiae annorum XX hic sita est Ceresces coniugi\n
EDCS-09900655.translation: Derva, daughter of Sisina, aged 20, is buried here. Ceresces [erected this] for her spouse.
EDCS-09900655.summary: This inscription records the burial of Derva, daughter of Sisina, at the age of 20, with the memorial erected by her spouse Ceresces.

EDCS-09900656.text_edition: \n Dis Manibus Domitio Longino veterano cohortis |(miliariae) Hemesenorum <e>t Domit<i>ae Avitae parentibus posuerunt Domitias sic Revocata et Amata filias sic vivae instantibus PA<u>relio Firmo veterano et Aurelio Aunio ge<n=A>eris \n
EDCS-09900656.translation: To the spirits of the departed. For Domitius Longinus, a veteran of the Hemesenian cohort (miliariae), and Domitia Avita, parents. Their daughters Domitia Revocata and Domitia Amata erected this for them while alive. The building was overseen by the veteran Aurelius Firmus and Aurelius Aunius of the family...
EDCS-09900656.summary: This funerary inscription records the erection of a memorial by two daughters for their parents, a veteran Domitius Longinus and Domitia Avita, with the construction overseen by two other individuals.

EDCS-09900657.text_edition: \n Dis Manibus Marcus Domitius Super miles legionis II adiutricis qui vixit nnos XXXII menses VI et defunctus bello Germanico et Aurelius Iulius qui vixit nnos XXVI menses V et Revocata mater eorum qui sic vixit annos L Concordius lib(ertus) eorum posuit\n
EDCS-09900657.translation: To the Spirits of the Departed. Marcus Domitius Super, a soldier of the II Adjutrix Legion, who lived 32 years and 6 months, and died in the German war; and Aurelius Iulius, who lived 26 years and 5 months; and their mother Revocata, who lived 50 years. Concordius, their freedman, erected this.
EDCS-09900657.summary: A funerary monument for Marcus Domitius Super, Aurelius Iulius, and Revocata, set up by their freedman Concordius, likely from the 2nd century AD.

EDCS-09900658.text_edition: \n qui vixit annos et Donatiano N qui vixit nnos XVII et Marinae quae vixit annos et Aureliae quae vixit nnos X Marinia et Donatus Dubitatus posuerunt\n
EDCS-09900658.translation: who lived [X] years and Donatianus, who lived 17 years, and Marina, who lived [X] years, and Aurelia, who lived 10 years. Marinia and Donatus Dubitatus erected this.
EDCS-09900658.summary: A funerary inscription recording the lives of Donatianus, Marina, and Aurelia, set up by Marinia and Donatus Dubitatus, with missing details about the deceased.

EDCS-09900660.text_edition: \n Tito Flavio Ato veterano ex summo curatore cohortis III Batavorum vixit nnos L Titus Flavius Atticinus |(centurio) voluntariorum \n
EDCS-09900660.translation: To Titus Flavius Ato, a veteran and former chief administrator of the III Cohort of Batavians, who lived 50 years. Titus Flavius Atticinus, centurion of the volunteers.
EDCS-09900660.summary: A tombstone for Titus Flavius Ato, a veteran and former administrator, possibly erected by his son Titus Flavius Atticinus, a centurion, likely from the 1st or 2nd century AD.

EDCS-09900661.text_edition: \n Marco Herennio Pudenti veterano legionis II adiutricis piae fidelis nnorum LXXVIII domo Claudia Apamie sic collegium cultorum posuit\n
EDCS-09900661.translation: To Marcus Herennius Pudentus, a veteran of the II Adjutrix Pia Fidelis Legion, aged 78. From Claudia Apamia. The college of cultores erected this.
EDCS-09900661.summary: A funerary monument for Marcus Herennius Pudentus, a veteran of the II Adjutrix Legion, from Apamea, erected by his collegium, likely from the 2nd century AD.

EDCS-09900662.text_edition: \n Iantumalius Blatonis filius eques alae I Ituraeorum annorum XXX stipendiorumCVZO \n
EDCS-09900662.translation: To Iantumalius, son of Blato, an equestrian of the I Ala of Ituraeans, aged 30 years, with 35 years of service.
EDCS-09900662.summary: An inscription honoring Iantumalius, an officer in the I Ala of Ituraeans, who served for 35 years and died at age 30, likely from the 1st or 2nd century AD.

EDCS-09900664.text_edition: \n Dis Manibus Iulia Proculi filia annorum LX et Bietumara Bucconis filia annorum XXV hic sitae sunt Bucco Deivis<i=E>s sibi vivos sic coniugi et filiae titulum faciendum curavit \n
EDCS-09900664.translation: To the Spirits of the Departed. Julia, daughter of Proculus, aged 60 years, and Bietumara, daughter of Bucco, aged 25 years, lie here. Bucco Deivis caused this monument to be made for himself and his wife and daughter while alive.
EDCS-09900664.summary: A funerary monument commissioned by Bucco Deivis for himself, his wife (likely Julia), and daughter (Bietumara), indicating it was prepared during their lifetimes.

EDCS-09900665.text_edition: \n Iuliae Silvinae matri dulcissimae quae vixit nnos LXVI et Aelio Valentiano veterano cohortis |(miliariae) Hemesenorum ex duplicario vivo patri dulcissimo Aelius Vitalis beneficiarius consularis faciendum curavit\n
EDCS-09900665.translation: To Julia Silvina, dearest mother, who lived 66 years; and to Aelius Valentianus, a veteran of the Hemesenorum Cohort (miliaria), former duplicarius, dearest father, living. Aelius Vitalis, a beneficiarius consularis, caused this to be made.
EDCS-09900665.summary: A funerary monument for Julia Silvina and her husband Aelius Valentianus, erected by their son Aelius Vitalis, a beneficiarius consularis, likely from the 2nd century AD.

EDCS-09900666.text_edition: \n Dis Manibus Lentinus Prudentis eques cohortis III Batavorum annorum XLV stipendiorum XX Germanus vexillarius \n
EDCS-09900666.translation: To the Spirits of the Departed. Lentinus Prudentis, an equestrian of the III Cohort of Batavians, aged 45 years, with 20 years of service. A German standard-bearer.
EDCS-09900666.summary: A funerary inscription for Lentinus Prudentis, a German equestrian and standard-bearer of the III Cohort of Batavians, who died at age 45 after 20 years of service.

EDCS-09900667.text_edition: \n Au eques alae annorum X con eius a Lupo Bas \n
EDCS-09900667.translation: Au, an equestrian of the [X] Ala, aged 10 years. His wife [X] by Lupus Bas.
EDCS-09900667.summary: A partial inscription possibly recording the death of a young equestrian named Au from an ala, with very little context.

EDCS-09900668.translation: Magnus Equosonis, an Eraviscus, aged 40 years.
EDCS-09900668.summary: A simple funerary inscription recording the name, ethnicity (Eraviscus), and age of Magnus, likely from the 1st or 2nd century AD.

EDCS-09900670.text_edition: \n Dis Manibus Marino Silvani militi cohortis |(miliariae) Hemesenorum annorum L<II=TC> stipendiorum XXVIII Marcus Aurelius miles cohortis suprascriptae heres bene merenti posuit \n
EDCS-09900670.translation: To the Divine Spirits. Marcus Aurelius, a soldier of the aforementioned cohort, erected this for his well-deserving fellow soldier Marino, a soldier of the Hemesenian cohort (miliariae), aged 52, with 28 years of service.
EDCS-09900670.summary: This is a funerary inscription for a soldier named Marino, erected by his colleague.

EDCS-09900671.text_edition: \n Dis Manibus Marco Mario Mariniano qui vixit nnos VI menses VII Marcus Aurelius Marius Marinus decurio municipii Volg et Annia Marina parentes filio dulcissimo Thalassius Encolpio\n
EDCS-09900671.translation: To the Divine Spirits. Marcus Aurelius Marius Marinus, a decurion of the municipium of Volg, and Annia Marina, parents of Marcus Mario Mariniano, who lived 6 years and 7 months, and Thalassius Encolpius, erected this for their most beloved son.
EDCS-09900671.summary: This is a funerary inscription for a young boy named Marcus Mario Mariniano, erected by his parents.

EDCS-09900673.text_edition: \n Matutina Flaviani filia annorum XX hic sita est Flavianus Sabini filiae pientissimae et Comatimarae Colvedionis coniugi et Flavi<a>nae Flaviani filiae et Marcelliano nepoti et sibi vivus vivis posuit\n
EDCS-09900673.translation: Matutina, daughter of Flavius, aged 20, is buried here. Flavianus dedicated this monument to his most dutiful daughter Matutina, to his wife Comatimara, daughter of Colvedius, to his daughter Flaviana, daughter of Flavius, to his grandson Marcellianus, and to himself while he was alive.
EDCS-09900673.summary: This is a funerary inscription for Matutina and other family members, erected by Flavianus.

EDCS-09900674.text_edition: \n Dis Manibus Marcus C Messius annorum LX hic situs est Ulpia Amma coniux pientissima C Iustus filius parentibus faciendum curavit\n
EDCS-09900674.translation: To the Divine Spirits. Marcus C. Messius, aged 60, is buried here. Ulpia Amma, his most dutiful wife, and C. Iustus, his son, arranged for this to be made for their parents.
EDCS-09900674.summary: This is a funerary inscription for Marcus C. Messius, erected by his wife and son.

EDCS-09900675.text_edition: \n Mulsus Aivisae filius annorum XX hic est situs e<q=C>(ues) occisus in civitate Litu<g=C>enus Aivisae filius annorum XVIII hic est situs milites alae primae Frontonianae in civitate Heraviscorum sic defuncti frater Bellicus titulum posuit memoriae \n
EDCS-09900675.translation: Mulsus, son of Aivisa, aged 20, is buried here, killed in the territory of the Litugeni. Aivisa, son of Aivisa, aged 18, is buried here. Bellicus, brother of the soldiers of the First Frontonian Ala in the territory of the Heravisci, erected this monument in memory of those who died thus.
EDCS-09900675.summary: This is a funerary inscription commemorating soldiers Mulsus and Aivisa, erected by their brother Bellicus.

EDCS-09900676.text_edition: \n Dis Manibus Olumnius Valens veteranus cohortis I Antoninae |(miliariae) Hemesenorum cohortis primae Novae |(miliariae) Severianae Surorum sagittariorum et Gaius Maximus veteranus secundus heres bene merenti posuerunt\n
EDCS-09900676.translation: To the Divine Spirits. Olumnius Valens, a veteran of the First Cohort Antoniniana (miliariae) Hemesenorum, and Gaius Maximus, a veteran of the First Cohort Nova Severiana Surorum sagittariorum, erected this for the well-deserving second heir.
EDCS-09900676.summary: This is a funerary inscription for two veterans, Olumnius Valens and Gaius Maximus, who jointly erected it.

EDCS-09900677.text_edition: \n c hic situs est Optatus heres filius et Germaniu coniux titulum memoriae posuerunt\n
EDCS-09900677.translation: Here is buried Optatus. His son, the heir, and Germaniu, his wife, erected this monument in memory.
EDCS-09900677.summary: This is a funerary inscription for Optatus, erected by his son and wife.

EDCS-09900678.text_edition: \n Otiouna vixit annos XII et Regilia annorum IIII hic sitae sunt Ianuarius Otiouna parvis sibi vivi posuerunt\n
EDCS-09900678.translation: Otiouna, aged 12, and Regilia, aged 4, are buried here. Ianuarius erected this for himself and his little ones while he was alive.
EDCS-09900678.summary: This is a funerary inscription for two young girls, Otiouna and Regilia, erected by Ianuarius.

EDCS-09900681.text_edition: \n Respectus Gellini filius annorum XIIX hic situs es<t=I> Cania Carsulia mater filio posuit \n
EDCS-09900681.translation: Respectus, son of Gellus, aged 18, is buried here. His mother Cania Carsulia erected this for her son.
EDCS-09900681.summary: This is a funerary inscription for Respectus, erected by his mother.

EDCS-09900683.text_edition: \n Senio Comatonis filius natione Eraviscus <a=H>(nnorum) XXX in civitate Eraviscorum in Aquinco et Comatuia mater eius sibi et filio viva titulum posuit \n
EDCS-09900683.translation: Senio, son of Comatus, of the Eravisci tribe, aged 30, in the territory of the Eravisci in Aquincum, and his mother Comatuia erected this monument for themselves and for their son while they were alive.
EDCS-09900683.summary: This is a funerary inscription for Senio, erected by himself and his mother Comatuia while alive.

EDCS-09900684.text_edition: \n Dis Manibus Septimiae Constantinae coniugi quae vixit nnos XXXII et Aureliae Florentinae filiae quae vixit nnum I Marcus Aurelius Bassus faciendum curavit\n
EDCS-09900684.translation: To the Divine Spirits. Marcus Aurelius Bassus arranged for this to be made for his wife Septimia Constantina, who lived 32 years, and his daughter Aurelia Florentina, who lived 1 year.
EDCS-09900684.summary: A funerary monument set up by Marcus Aurelius Bassus for his wife and daughter, likely from the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-09900687.text_edition: \n ntino filiae quae vixit annos VIII pientissim faciendum curavit\n
EDCS-09900687.translation: [...] for his daughter, who lived 8 years, most devoted; he arranged for this to be made.
EDCS-09900687.summary: A funerary inscription, likely incomplete, recording the age of a devoted daughter and the person who commissioned the monument.

EDCS-09900688.text_edition: \n Veriuga Danui filia annorum XXX hic sita est Florus Egretari filius coniugi piae titulum memoriae posuit\n
EDCS-09900688.translation: Veriuga, daughter of Danuius, aged 30, lies here. Florus, son of Egretarius, erected this memorial stone for his devoted wife.
EDCS-09900688.summary: A tombstone recording the death of Veriuga, daughter of Danuius, and erected by her husband Florus Egretarius, likely from the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-09900689.text_edition: \n Ulpio Basso filio qui vixit nnos X et Ulpiae Tert libertae vivae sibi coniux viva Titia Paulina filio et marito bene merentibus faciendum curavit\n
EDCS-09900689.translation: Titia Paulina, while alive, arranged for this to be made for her son Ulpius Bassus, who lived 10 years, and for her freedwoman Ulpia Tertia, and for her husband, well deserving of both.
EDCS-09900689.summary: A funerary monument commissioned by Titia Paulina for her son, freedwoman, and husband, indicating familial relationships and affection.

EDCS-09900690.text_edition: \n Ulpius Enubico sesquiplicarius alae I Flaviae Britannicae nnorum XXXV hic situs est Ressatus et Succo fratres et heredes faciendum curaverunt\n
EDCS-09900690.translation: Ulpius Enubicus, a sesquiplicarius (a type of cavalry soldier) of the I Flavia Britannica alae, aged 35, lies here. His brothers and heirs Ressatus and Succo arranged for this to be made.
EDCS-09900690.summary: A tombstone for Ulpius Enubicus, a Roman cavalry soldier, erected by his brothers and heirs, likely from the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-09900691.text_edition: \n Dis Manibus Marco Ulpio Iuliano veterano ex |(centurione) vixit annos LXIIII\n
EDCS-09900691.translation: To the Divine Spirits. For Marcus Ulpius Iulianus, a veteran and former centurion, who lived 64 years.
EDCS-09900691.summary: A funerary dedication to Marcus Ulpius Iulianus, a veteran and former centurion, likely from the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-09900694.text_edition: \n s annorum XXV frater posuit memoriae\n
EDCS-09900694.translation: [...] aged 25 years, his brother erected this in memory.
EDCS-09900694.summary: A funerary inscription recording the age of the deceased and commissioned by their brother.

EDCS-09900695.text_edition: \n e annorum XII hic sitae s posuit i fili- coniux posuerunt curaverunt\n
EDCS-09900695.translation: [...] aged 12 years lies here. [...] erected. The son and wife arranged for this.
EDCS-09900695.summary: An incomplete funerary inscription mentioning a deceased child and the family members who commissioned the monument.

EDCS-09900697.text_edition: \n ens us et Ene Tempias immunes V \n
EDCS-09900697.translation: [...] us and Ene Tempias, exempt (from duties) V.
EDCS-09900697.summary: A fragmented inscription possibly related to individuals exempt from service, potentially within a military or civic context.

EDCS-09900698.summary: A very fragmentary inscription that likely refers to a military cohort.

EDCS-09900709.summary: This is a funerary inscription dedicated to Iaia, with the beginning lost.

EDCS-10000341.text_edition: \n Iovi Optimo Maximo dis deabusque pro se et suis Caius Antonius Vitalis <|>(centurio) legionis II adiutricis votum solvit libens merito Torquato et Iuliano consulibus\n
EDCS-10000341.translation: To Jupiter, Best and Greatest, and to the gods and goddesses, Gaius Antonius Vitalis, centurion of the Second Adjutant Legion, paid his vow freely and deservedly, with Torquatus and Julianus as consuls.
EDCS-10000341.summary: This is a dedication to Jupiter and other deities by a centurion of the Second Adjutant Legion, likely from the consulship of Torquatus and Julianus.

EDCS-10000342.text_edition: \n Dis Manibus Aelio Ingennuo sic claviculario ex officio praesides decidit in bello Aelia Procella coniugi karissimo memoriam posuit Septimio Decorato claviculario ex officio praesides decidit in b<e=I>llo Aelia Basilissa coniugi karissimo memoriam posuit \n
EDCS-10000342.translation: To the Spirits of the Departed, to Aelius Ingennuus, a clavicularius from the office, who died in the war; Aelia Procella dedicated this memorial to her most beloved husband. To Aelius Decoratus, a clavicularius from the office, who died in the war; Aelia Basilissa dedicated this memorial to her most beloved husband.
EDCS-10000342.summary: This is a pair of funerary inscriptions for two clavicularii who died in the war, erected by their wives.

EDCS-10000343.text_edition: \n Dis Manibus Gratius Artilleus et Cludius sic Glamosus ||(centuriones) legionis VIII Augustae bello Serdicensi desideratis schola ||(centurionum) legionis supra scriptae collegis bene merentibus de se p(osuit)\n
EDCS-10000343.translation: To the Spirits of the Departed, Gratius Artilleus and Cludius Glamosus, centurions of the Eighth Augustan Legion, who perished in the Serdicensian war; the school of centurions of the aforementioned legion erected this for their well-deserving colleagues.
EDCS-10000343.summary: This funerary inscription records the death of two centurions of the Eighth Augustan Legion in the Serdicensian war, commemorated by their colleagues.

EDCS-10000344.text_edition: \n Dis Manibus Restitutius Silvanus |(centurio) legionis I Menerviae sic Aurelius Martius heres et Aurelia Bona secundus heres coniugi bene merenti faciendum curaverunt\n
EDCS-10000344.translation: To the Spirits of the Departed, Restitutius Silvanus, centurion of the First Minerva Legion, and Aurelius Martius, the heir, and Aurelia Bona, the second heir, took care to have this made for their well-deserving husband.
EDCS-10000344.summary: This is a funerary inscription for a centurion of the First Minerva Legion, commissioned by his heir and second heir.

EDCS-10000345.text_edition: \n Sammonius Samminus imaginifer legionis VIII Augustae fecit Verina coniux\n
EDCS-10000345.translation: Sammonius Samminus, an imaginifer of the Eighth Augustan Legion, made this; his wife Verina.
EDCS-10000345.summary: This is a funerary inscription for an imaginifer of the Eighth Augustan Legion, made by his wife.

EDCS-10000346.summary: This inscription likely records the names of two individuals, possibly a dedication or a funerary marker with incomplete text.

EDCS-10000347.text_edition: \n IVCI parentes et Petronius Septumianus sic\n
EDCS-10000347.translation: To the IVCI, parents, and Petronius Septumianus.
EDCS-10000347.summary: This inscription seems to record a dedication or memorial to the IVCI deities and Petronius Septumianus, with the purpose partially lost.

EDCS-10000349.text_edition: \n Iovi Optimo Maximo Paterno Iulius Celer scriba classis votum solvit libens laetus merito Pisone consule\n
EDCS-10000349.translation: To Jupiter Best and Greatest, Paternanus, Julius Celer, scribe of the fleet, paid his vow freely, happily, and deservedly, with Piso as consul.
EDCS-10000349.summary: This is a dedication to Jupiter by Julius Celer, a fleet scribe, likely from the consulship of Piso.

EDCS-10000350.text_edition: \n Dibus maioribus sacrum Aurelius Valentinus pro se et suis monitus somnio votum libens posuit\n
EDCS-10000350.translation: Sacred to the greater gods. Aurelius Valentinus, prompted by a dream, placed this vow freely for himself and his family.
EDCS-10000350.summary: This is a votive inscription to the greater gods, dedicated by Aurelius Valentinus following a dream.

EDCS-10000351.text_edition: \n Titus Flavius Proculus princeps praefectus Scordiscorum annorum XXXXI hic situs est Titus Flavius Dulcis pater filio suo pientissimo fecit\n
EDCS-10000351.translation: Titus Flavius Proculus, princeps and prefect of the Scordisci, aged 41, lies here. Titus Flavius Dulcis made this for his most devoted son.
EDCS-10000351.summary: This is a funerary inscription for Titus Flavius Proculus, set up by his father.

EDCS-10000359.text_edition: \n Deo Invicto Mithrae Antonius Barbilus negotiator votum solvit libens merito\n
EDCS-10000359.translation: To the Unconquered god Mithras, Antonius Barbilus, a merchant, gladly and deservedly fulfilled his vow.
EDCS-10000359.summary: This is a votive inscription dedicated to Mithras by a merchant named Antonius Barbilus.

EDCS-10000362.text_edition: \n Urbana Aterigis liberta nnorum L et Gaetulus Urbanae filius Aterigis libertus nnorum XVI hic siti sunt Arcto heres posuit fratres et sorores fratri pientissimo\n
EDCS-10000362.translation: Urbana, freedwoman of Aterigis, aged 50, and Gaetulus, freedman of Urbana, aged 16, lie here. Arcto erected this for his most devoted brothers and sisters and brother.
EDCS-10000362.summary: This is a funerary inscription for Urbana and Gaetulus, set up by Arcto, their relative.

EDCS-10001044.text_edition: \n Iovi Optimo Maximo Lucius Licinius Urbicus beneficiarius consularis votum libens solvit merito\n
EDCS-10001044.translation: To Jupiter, Best and Greatest, Lucius Licinius Urbicus, a *beneficiarius consularis*, gladly and deservedly fulfilled his vow.
EDCS-10001044.summary: This is a votive inscription dedicated to Jupiter by Lucius Licinius Urbicus, a *beneficiarius consularis*.

EDCS-10001045.summary: This inscription is too fragmentary to determine its purpose or content.

EDCS-10001046.text_edition: \n Iovi Optimo Maximo pro salute domini nostri [] Invicti Augusti Aurelius Mucianus \n
EDCS-10001046.translation: To Jupiter, Best and Greatest, for the safety of our lord [---] the Unconquered Augustus, Aurelius Mucianus [fulfilled his vow].
EDCS-10001046.summary: This is a votive inscription for the safety of the Emperor, dedicated to Jupiter by Aurelius Mucianus.

EDCS-10001048.text_edition: \n Iovi Optimo Maximo Titus Gramonius Lucilianus signifer legionis II adiutricis votum solvit libens merito Maximo et Aeliano consulibus\n
EDCS-10001048.translation: To Jupiter, Best and Greatest, Titus Gramonius Lucilianus, standard-bearer of the II Adjutrix legion, gladly and deservedly fulfilled his vow, when Maximo and Aeliano were consuls.
EDCS-10001048.summary: This is a votive inscription dedicated to Jupiter by Titus Gramonius Lucilianus, a standard-bearer, during the consulship of Maximo and Aeliano.

EDCS-10001049.text_edition: \n TIus et - Pontius Dalmatius filius votum libentes merito posuerunt\n
EDCS-10001049.translation: Tius and his son Pontius Dalmatius gladly and deservedly set this up.
EDCS-10001049.summary: This is a dedicatory inscription by Tius and his son Pontius Dalmatius.

EDCS-10001052.text_edition: \n Dis Manibus Publius Aelius Dasius decurio municipii Bassianensium pontifex municipii eiusdem aedilitate functus municipio Spodentium vixit annis LXX et Aelis Licinio et Honorato et Silano et Iustillo et Ainato marito et filis karissimis Aelia Iusta viva sibi et suis posuit\n
EDCS-10001052.translation: To the Spirits of the Departed. Publius Aelius Dasius, *decurio* and *pontifex* of the municipality of Bassianenses, who served as *aedile* in the municipality of Spodentium, lived 70 years. Aelia Iusta, alive, set this up for herself and her dearest husband Aeliano and sons Licinius, Honoratus, Silanus, Iustillus, and Aenatus.
EDCS-10001052.summary: This is a funerary inscription for Publius Aelius Dasius and his family, set up by his wife Aelia Iusta.

EDCS-10001053.text_edition: \n Dis Manibus perpetuae securitati Aurelia Bittelliana sic ex provincia Italia vixit annos XX Florentino filio Aurelius Florus protector ducenarius coniugi \n
EDCS-10001053.translation: To the Spirits of the Departed, for perpetual security. Aurelia Bittelliana, who lived 20 years, [died] from the province of Italy. Aurelius Florus, *protector ducenarius*, [erected this] for his wife.
EDCS-10001053.summary: This is a funerary inscription for Aurelia Bittelliana, set up by her husband Aurelius Florus.

EDCS-10001054.text_edition: \n Iovi Optimo Maximo Aelius Victorinus decurio Bassianensium votum libens solvit\n
EDCS-10001054.translation: To Jupiter, Best and Greatest, Aelius Victorinus, decurion of the Bassianenses, gladly fulfilled his vow.
EDCS-10001054.summary: This inscription records a votive offering to Jupiter by Aelius Victorinus, a decurion from Bassianae.

EDCS-10001055.text_edition: \n Iovi Optimo Maximo Aurelius Propincus sic princeps cohortis primae Campanorum votum solvit libens merito\n
EDCS-10001055.translation: To Jupiter, Best and Greatest, Aurelius Propincus, thus princeps of the first cohort of the Campani, gladly and deservedly fulfilled his vow.
EDCS-10001055.summary: This inscription records a votive offering to Jupiter by Aurelius Propincus, princeps of the first cohort of the Campani.

EDCS-10001057.text_edition: \n - Aurelius Super veteranus legionis II adiutricis missus honesta missione Apro et Maximo consulibus votum solvit libens merito\n
EDCS-10001057.translation: Aurelius Super, veteran of the II Adjutrix legion, discharged with honorable release, under consuls Apro and Maximus, gladly and deservedly fulfilled his vow.
EDCS-10001057.summary: This inscription records a votive offering to an unnamed deity by Aurelius Super, a veteran of the II Adjutrix legion, during the consulship of Apro and Maximus.

EDCS-10001058.text_edition: \n Iovi Optimo Maximo Aelius Proculus decurio coloniae Cibalensium votum solvit libens merito\n
EDCS-10001058.translation: To Jupiter, Best and Greatest, Aelius Proculus, decurion of the colony of Cibalenses, gladly and deservedly fulfilled his vow.
EDCS-10001058.summary: This inscription records a votive offering to Jupiter by Aelius Proculus, a decurion from the colony of Cibalenses.

EDCS-10001059.translation: Finctor, live thus for your masters.
EDCS-10001059.summary: This inscription is a short, possibly personal, message related to loyalty or service.

EDCS-10001061.text_edition: \n Iovi Optimo Maximo Marcus Aurelius Cocceius Eutychianus immunis pro se et suis\n
EDCS-10001061.translation: To Jupiter, Best and Greatest, Marcus Aurelius Cocceianus Eutychianus, immunis, for himself and his family.
EDCS-10001061.summary: This inscription records a dedication to Jupiter by Marcus Aurelius Cocceianus Eutychianus, who held the status of 'immunis'.

EDCS-10001062.text_edition: \n Iovi Optimo Maximo Caius Iulius Iulianus beneficiarius consularis legionis IIII Flaviae \n
EDCS-10001062.translation: To Jupiter, Best and Greatest, Gaius Julius Julianus, beneficiarius consularis of the IIII Flavia legion.
EDCS-10001062.summary: This inscription records a dedication to Jupiter by Gaius Julius Julianus, a beneficiarius consularis of the IIII Flavia legion.

EDCS-10001063.text_edition: \n Iovi Optimo Maximo Fortunatus Augusti libertus \n
EDCS-10001063.translation: To Jupiter, Best and Greatest, Fortunatus, freedman of the Emperor.
EDCS-10001063.summary: This inscription records a dedication to Jupiter by Fortunatus, an imperial freedman.

EDCS-10001064.text_edition: \n Iovi Optimo Maximo Iunoni Minervae sacrum Caius Iulius Magnus Augustalis coloniae Mursensium votum solvit libens merito\n
EDCS-10001064.translation: Sacred to Jupiter, Best and Greatest, Juno, and Minerva. Gaius Julius Magnus, Augustalis of the colony of Mursenses, gladly and deservedly fulfilled his vow.
EDCS-10001064.summary: This inscription records a votive offering to Jupiter, Juno, and Minerva by Gaius Julius Magnus, an Augustalis from the colony of Mursenses.

EDCS-10001066.text_edition: \n Silvano Augusto Marcus Aurelius Severus equo publico votum solvit libens merito\n
EDCS-10001066.translation: To Silvanus Augustus, Marcus Aurelius Severus, holding public equestrian rank, gladly and deservedly fulfilled his vow.
EDCS-10001066.summary: This inscription records a votive offering to Silvanus Augustus by Marcus Aurelius Severus, who held equestrian status.

EDCS-10001067.text_edition: \n Terrae Matri Marcus Marius Secundinus pro se et suis votum solvit libens merito\n
EDCS-10001067.translation: Marcus Marius Secundinus willingly and deservedly fulfilled his vow to Mother Earth for himself and his family.
EDCS-10001067.summary: A private individual, Marcus Marius Secundinus, dedicates a vow to the Earth Mother.

EDCS-10001068.text_edition: \n ntius Antoninus tribunus militum legionum I et II adiutricium praefectus cohortis I Ulpiae Traianae Cugernorum votum solvit libens merito\n
EDCS-10001068.translation: Anti[us?] Antoninus, military tribune of Legions I and II Adjutrix, prefect of the first cohort of Cugerni of the Ulpian Trajan Cohort, willingly and deservedly fulfilled his vow.
EDCS-10001068.summary: An army officer, Anti[us?] Antoninus, dedicates a vow, likely in fulfillment of military service.

EDCS-10001073.translation: To the DIVINE LORD (DOMNI CL - likely referring to the Emperor): Live and enjoy good fortune!
EDCS-10001073.summary: A short dedicatory or well-wishing inscription to a divine lord, possibly the emperor.

EDCS-10102009.text_edition: \n Dis Manibus sacrum Caius Iulius Serenus veteranus legionis IIII Flaviae felicis sarcophagum condidit et vivus posuit nnis LXXX mensibus diebus V sepultas Iulias Heracliam et Sabinillam filias et Aureliam Reginam uxorem\n
EDCS-10102009.translation: Sacred to the Spirits of the Departed. Gaius Julius Serenus, a veteran of the Fourth Flavian Legion, built this sarcophagus and placed it here while alive. He lived 80 years, [X] months, and 5 days. Buried here are his daughters Iulia Heraclia and Sabinilla, and his wife Aurelia Regina.
EDCS-10102009.summary: A funerary inscription for a veteran, Gaius Julius Serenus, who dedicated his own sarcophagus and specified the burial of his wife and daughters.

EDCS-10102010.translation: Cornutus willingly and deservedly fulfilled his vow.
EDCS-10102010.summary: A private individual named Cornutus dedicates a vow.

EDCS-10102012.text_edition: \n III Aurelius Inteulus vixit nnos IIII Aurelius Vitalianus filibus sic bene merentibus hic siti sunt\n
EDCS-10102012.translation: Aurelius Inteulus lived 4 years. Aurelius Vitalianus [placed this] for his well-deserving children. They are buried here.
EDCS-10102012.summary: A funerary inscription commemorating a child named Aurelius Inteulus and erected by his father Aurelius Vitalianus for his deserving children.

EDCS-10102013.text_edition: \n Dis Manibus Publius Aelius Quintinus miles cohortis \n
EDCS-10102013.translation: Sacred to the Spirits of the Departed. Publius Aelius Quintinus, soldier of the [ ] cohort...
EDCS-10102013.summary: A fragmented funerary inscription for a soldier, Publius Aelius Quintinus.

EDCS-10102015.text_edition: \n Sen nnos XV hic sit-- est Lic\n
EDCS-10102015.translation: [Name] lived 15 years. He/She is buried here. Lic[...]
EDCS-10102015.summary: A partial funerary inscription recording the age of a deceased individual.

EDCS-10102017.text_edition: \n Ager vici Iosista adsignatus Tiberio Claudio Prisco praefecto alae I civium Romanorum caput agri excepti\n
EDCS-10102017.translation: Land of the village of Iosista was assigned to Tiberius Claudius Priscus, prefect of the First Cohort of Roman Citizens, as the head of the excepted land.
EDCS-10102017.summary: An official inscription documenting the assignment of land to Tiberius Claudius Priscus, a prefect.

EDCS-10102018.text_edition: \n Libero Patri sacrum Caius Antonius Sabinus beneficiarius consularis votum solvit libens merito\n
EDCS-10102018.translation: Sacred to Liber Pater. Gaius Antonius Sabinus, a beneficiary of a consular legate, willingly and deservedly fulfilled his vow.
EDCS-10102018.summary: A private individual, Gaius Antonius Sabinus, dedicates a vow to Liber Pater.

EDCS-10102019.text_edition: \n Invicto deo Mithrae Veturius Dubitatus veteranus alae I civium Romanorum ex duplicario votum solvit libens merito\n
EDCS-10102019.translation: To the unconquered god Mithras, Veturius Dubitatus, a veteran of the I Ala Civium Romanorum, willingly and deservedly fulfilled his vow.
EDCS-10102019.summary: This is a votive inscription to the god Mithras by a veteran of the I Ala Civium Romanorum, fulfilling a vow made while serving as a duplicarius. The approximate date can be inferred from the veteran status and unit.

EDCS-10102020.text_edition: \n Aurelio De equiti alae armorum cohortis? vixit annos stipendiorum X heres ex testamento faciendum curavit\n
EDCS-10102020.translation: To Aurelius De, a cavalryman of the ala armorum, who lived for 10 years of service, his heir had this made according to the will.
