EDCS-10102020.summary: This inscription records the funerary monument of Aurelius De, a cavalryman, erected by his heir in accordance with his will, dating to the period of his service of 10 years.

EDCS-10102021.text_edition: \n Iovi Optimo Maximo Quintus Sabinius Maximus miles legionis I adiutricis centuria Egnati Paeti solvit libens merito\n
EDCS-10102021.translation: To Jupiter Optimus Maximus, Quintus Sabinius Maximus, a soldier of the I Adjutrix legion, in the century of Egnatius Paetus, willingly and deservedly paid his vow.
EDCS-10102021.summary: This is a votive inscription to Jupiter Optimus Maximus by Quintus Sabinius Maximus, a soldier of the I Adjutrix legion, from the century of Egnatius Paetus.

EDCS-10102022.text_edition: \n Iovi Optimo Maximo Valerius Florentinus veteranus \n
EDCS-10102022.translation: To Jupiter Optimus Maximus, Valerius Florentinus, a veteran.
EDCS-10102022.summary: This is a dedication to Jupiter Optimus Maximus by Valerius Florentinus, a veteran.

EDCS-10102024.text_edition: \n Dis Manibus Aurelia Asc<l=I>epiodota vixit annos XIIII Aurelius Alexsander sic sorori carissimae posuit c p f \n
EDCS-10102024.translation: To the Spirits of the Departed. Aurelia Asclepidota lived 14 years. Aurelius Alexander placed this for his dearest sister, as a true offering.
EDCS-10102024.summary: This is a funerary epitaph for Aurelia Asclepidota, who died at the age of 14, erected by her brother Aurelius Alexander.

EDCS-10102033.summary: This is a fragmentary inscription, possibly a dedication to the god Janus, with a partial personal name.

EDCS-10102034.text_edition: \n A In pace \xcf\x89 innocenti Timotheo Timotheus et parentes filio \n
EDCS-10102034.translation: In peace. For the innocent Timotheus. Timotheus and his parents for their son.
EDCS-10102034.summary: This is a funerary inscription for a child named Timotheus, placed by his parents, indicating a Christian context with the phrase 'In pace'.

EDCS-10102038.text_edition: \n ES AVOSm sola merito rus ubi M -PR \n
EDCS-10102038.translation: ES AVOSm [she] deservedly [lived?] in the country where M -PR
EDCS-10102038.summary: This is a very fragmentary inscription, possibly recording a person's name and a reference to her residence, with illegible portions.

EDCS-10102045.translation: ] for Valeria / He[3] // ]ie[3] / [3]da for an[3] / [3] honor [
EDCS-10102045.summary: This is a fragmentary inscription that appears to be a funerary dedication to a woman named Valeria, with the dedicant and further details missing.

EDCS-10102083.summary: This is a fragmentary inscription, possibly containing a personal name like Asterius, with the rest of the text illegible.

EDCS-10102093.translation: To the Eternal God, Publius Aelius Eutyches.
EDCS-10102093.summary: This inscription records a dedication to the god Aeternus by Publius Aelius Eutyches.

EDCS-10102094.text_edition: \n Herculi Augusto Caius Valerius Mucianus pro salute sua suorumque omnium votum solvit libens merito\n
EDCS-10102094.translation: To Hercules Augustus, Gaius Valerius Mucianus willingly and deservedly fulfilled his vow for the safety of himself and all his family.
EDCS-10102094.summary: This inscription records a votive offering to Hercules Augustus by Gaius Valerius Mucianus.

EDCS-10102095.text_edition: \n Iovi Optimo Maximo pro salute Titi Flavi Martini decurionis coloniae Mursensium praefecti collegii centonariorum IIviri designati Flavius Philippus libertus votum solvit libens merito\n
EDCS-10102095.translation: To Jupiter Best and Greatest, for the safety of Titus Flavius Martinus, decurion of the colony of Mursa, prefect of the guild of clothiers, and designated duovir, Flavius Philippus, his freedman, willingly and deservedly fulfilled his vow.
EDCS-10102095.summary: This inscription records a votive offering to Jupiter Optimus Maximus by Flavius Philippus, a freedman, for the safety of Titus Flavius Martinus, a local official.

EDCS-10102096.text_edition: \n Iovi Optimo Maximo sacrum Messius votum solvit libens merito\n
EDCS-10102096.translation: To Jupiter Best and Greatest, sacred. Messius willingly and deservedly fulfilled his vow.
EDCS-10102096.summary: This inscription records a votive offering to Jupiter Optimus Maximus by Messius.

EDCS-10102097.text_edition: \n Iovi Optimo Maximo Iunoni Minervae sacrum Caius Iulius nus \n
EDCS-10102097.translation: To Jupiter Best and Greatest, Juno, Minerva, sacred. Gaius Julius [---]
EDCS-10102097.summary: This inscription records a dedication to Jupiter, Juno, and Minerva by Gaius Julius.

EDCS-10102098.summary: This inscription records a dedication to Silvanus Magnus by Gaius [---]nius.

EDCS-10102100.text_edition: \n Dis Manibus Caio Iulio Cai filio Claudia Verecundo Iuvavo militi legionis X geminae piae fidelis centuria Paeti stipendiorum XX heres faciendum curavit\n
EDCS-10102100.translation: To the Spirits of the Dead. For Gaius Julius Caius son, a soldier of the Tenth Gemina Pia Fidelis legion, in the century of Paetus, with 20 years of service, his heir had this made.
EDCS-10102100.summary: This inscription is a tombstone for Gaius Julius Caius son, a soldier with 20 years of service, erected by his heir.

EDCS-10102101.text_edition: \n Velagenus Manti filius Ulattius eques cohortis II Alpinorum nnorum XXXVIII stipendiorum XVI hic situs est Longinus |(centurio) et Primus custos armorum beneficiarius heredes posuerunt\n
EDCS-10102101.translation: Velagenus, son of Mantius, Ulattius, an eques of the II Cohort of Alpiners, aged 38, with 16 years of service, lies here. Longinus (centurion) and Primus, guardian of arms, beneficiary, his heirs, placed this.
EDCS-10102101.summary: This inscription is a tombstone for Velagenus Mantius, an auxiliary soldier, erected by his heirs.

EDCS-10102102.text_edition: \n Dis Manibus Val<e=I>rio Martiali ex subaedianis collegae lapidari posuerunt \n
EDCS-10102102.translation: To the Spirits of the Dead. Colleagues, stonecutters, placed this for Valerius Martialis, from the subaediani.
EDCS-10102102.summary: This inscription is a tombstone for Valerius Martialis, a stonecutter, erected by his colleagues.

EDCS-10102104.text_edition: \n Iovi Optimo Maximo Fulguratori Caius Aelius Surinus decurio coloniae Mursensium IIviralis quinquennalis designatus votum solvit libens merito\n
EDCS-10102104.translation: To Jupiter Best and Greatest, the Thunderer, Gaius Aelius Surinus, decurion of the colony of Mursa, designated quinquennial duovir, willingly and deservedly fulfilled his vow.
EDCS-10102104.summary: This inscription records a votive offering to Jupiter Fulgurator by Gaius Aelius Surinus, a local official.

EDCS-10200169.text_edition: \n Imperator Caesar Vespasianus Augustus pontifex maximus tribunicia potestate II imperator VI pater patriae consul III veteranis qui militaverunt in classe Misenensi sub Sexto Lucilio Basso qui sena et vicena stipendia aut plura meruerant et sunt deducti Paestum quorum nomina subscripta sunt ipsis liberis posterisque eorum civitatem dedit et conubium cum uxoribus quas tunc habuissent cum est civitas iis data aut si qui caelibes essent cum iis quas postea duxissent dumtaxat singuli singulas ante diem V Idus Februarias Imperatore Caesare Vespasiano Augusto III Marco Cocceio Nerva consulibus |(centurioni) Liccaio Birsi filio Marsunnia loco XXIII descriptum et recognitum ex tabula aenea quae fixa est Romae in Capitolio in podio arae gentis Iuliae Imperator Caesar Vespasianus Augustus pontifex maximus tribunicia potestate II imperator VI pater patriae consul III veteranis qui militaverunt in classe Misenensi sub Sexto Lucilio Basso qui sena et vicena stipendia aut plura meruerant et sunt deducti Paestum quorum nomina subscripta sunt ipsis liberis posterisque eorum civitatem dedit et conubium cum uxoribus quas tunc habuissent cum est civitas iis data aut si qui caelibes essent cum iis quas postea duxissent dumtaxat singuli singulas ante diem V Idus Februarias Imperatore Caesare Vespasiano Augusto III Marco Cocceio Nerva consulibus |(centurioni) Liccaius sic Birsi filio Marsunnia loco XXIII descriptum et recognitum ex tabula aenea quae fixa est Romae in Capitolio in podio arae gentis Iuliae Marci Viri Marcelli decurionis legionis Savariensis Luci Domiti Severi veterani Breuci Cai Marci Nobilis Emonensis Cai Pidieni Aquileiensis Luci Valeri Pauonis veterani Breucio Cai Iuli Clari Aquileiensis Luci Minei Iucundi Aquileiensis\n
EDCS-10200169.translation: Emperor Caesar Vespasian Augustus, chief priest, with tribunician power for the second time, imperator for the sixth time, father of the country, consul for the third time. To the veterans who served in the Misenian fleet under Sextus Lucilius Bassus, who had served twenty or more years, and were settled at Paestum, whose names are subscribed: he granted citizenship and the right to marry to them, their children, and their descendants, with the wives they then had when citizenship was granted, or if they were unmarried, with those whom they would later marry, provided each man had only one wife. This was done on the fifth day before the Ides of February, when Emperor Caesar Vespasian Augustus was consul for the third time and Marcus Cocceius Nerva was consul. (Centurion) Liccaius, son of Birsius of Marsunnia, position 23. This was inscribed and verified from a bronze tablet fixed in Rome on the Capitol, on the platform of the altar of the Julian family. Emperor Caesar Vespasian Augustus, chief priest, with tribunician power for the second time, imperator for the sixth time, father of the country, consul for the third time. To the veterans who served in the Misenian fleet under Sextus Lucilius Bassus, who had served twenty or more years, and were settled at Paestum, whose names are subscribed: he granted citizenship and the right to marry to them, their children, and their descendants, with the wives they then had when citizenship was granted, or if they were unmarried, with those whom they would later marry, provided each man had only one wife. This was done on the fifth day before the Ides of February, when Emperor Caesar Vespasian Augustus was consul for the third time and Marcus Cocceius Nerva was consul. (Centurion) Liccaius, son of Birsius of Marsunnia, position 23. This was inscribed and verified from a bronze tablet fixed in Rome on the Capitol, on the platform of the altar of the Julian family. Marcus Virius Marcellus, decurion of the Savarian legion; Lucius Domitius Severus, veteran of Breuci; Caius Marcius Nobilis of Emona; Caius Pidienus of Aquileia; Lucius Valerius Paunus, veteran of Breuci; Caius Julius Clarulus of Aquileia; Lucius Mincius Iucundus of Aquileia.
EDCS-10200169.summary: This is a copy of an imperial grant of citizenship and marriage rights issued by Emperor Vespasian in 71 AD to veterans of the Misenian fleet, with the names of some recipients appended.

EDCS-10200171.text_edition: \n Iovi Optimo Maximo Marcus Flavius Miles praefectus cohortis III Batavorum miliariae equitatae\n
EDCS-10200171.translation: To Jupiter Optimus Maximus, Marcus Flavius Miles, prefect of the Third Cohort of Batavians, an auxiliary unit with cavalry.
EDCS-10200171.summary: This is a dedication to Jupiter Optimus Maximus by Marcus Flavius Miles, prefect of an auxiliary cohort, likely from the 1st or 2nd century AD.

EDCS-10701838.text_edition: \n Iovi Optimo Maximo et Silvano Domestico Caius Iulius Super votum solvit libens merito\n
EDCS-10701838.translation: To Jupiter Optimus Maximus and Silvanus Domesticos, Caius Julius Super willingly and deservedly fulfilled his vow.
EDCS-10701838.summary: This is a votive inscription to Jupiter Optimus Maximus and Silvanus Domesticos, dedicated by Caius Julius Super, likely from the 2nd century AD.

EDCS-10701840.summary: This is a fragment of an inscription, possibly a funerary monument, mentioning the name Aelianus, date uncertain.

EDCS-10701842.translation: [---] Quintus to his father Postumianus, aged [---].
EDCS-10701842.summary: This is a fragment of a funerary inscription recording the name Quintus dedicating something to his father Postumianus, date uncertain.

EDCS-11800553.text_edition: \n Dis Manibus Septimia Ocna vixit nnos XXXV et Ulpiae Emeritae filiae eius vixit nnos XII et alio filio ipseus sic qui vixit menses VI Aelius Martinianus optio coniugi et filiis pientissimis titulum posuit\n
EDCS-11800553.translation: To the Spirits of the Departed. Septimia Ocna lived 35 years, and Ulpia Emerita, her daughter, lived 12 years, and another son of his, who lived 6 months. Aelius Martinianus, optio, set up this monument to his most devoted wife and children.
EDCS-11800553.summary: This is a funerary inscription recording the deaths of Septimia Ocna, her daughter Ulpia Emerita, and an infant son, with the monument erected by Aelius Martinianus, likely from the 2nd or 3rd century AD.

EDCS-11800554.text_edition: \n Dis Manibus Publius Aelius Respectus decurio municipii vivus fecit sibi et Ulpiae Amasiae coniugi Aelia Materio filia nnorum X hic sita est parentes titulum memoriae posuerunt\n
EDCS-11800554.translation: To the Spirits of the Departed. Publius Aelius Respectus, decurion of the municipium, made this for himself and his wife Ulpia Amasia while alive. Aelia Materio, daughter, aged 10, is buried here. Her parents erected this memorial.
EDCS-11800554.summary: This is a funerary inscription set up by Publius Aelius Respectus for himself and his wife, and also records the death of their daughter Aelia Materio, likely from the 2nd or 3rd century AD.

EDCS-11800555.summary: This is a dedication to Jupiter Optimus Maximus by someone named Aelius, date uncertain.

EDCS-11800556.text_edition: \n P Nesergouna annorum LX hic sita est P Dubnomara filia et P3 Valerianus nepos? f(aciendum)? c(uraverunt)\n
EDCS-11800556.translation: P. Nesergouna, aged 60, is buried here. P. Dubnomara, daughter, and P. Valerianus, grandson, took care of the construction.
EDCS-11800556.summary: This is a funerary inscription recording the death of Nesergouna and mentioning her daughter Dubnomara and grandson Valerianus, likely from the 2nd or 3rd century AD.

EDCS-11800602.text_edition: \n Lucius Beducius Claudia Secundus Viruno miles legionis II adiutricis \n
EDCS-11800602.translation: Lucius Beducius Claudia Secundus, a soldier of the Second Legion Adjutrix, from Virunum.
EDCS-11800602.summary: This inscription records Lucius Beducius Claudia Secundus, a soldier from Virunum serving in the Second Legion Adjutrix, likely from the 1st or 2nd century AD.

EDCS-11800604.text_edition: \n Aelius Saturninus veteranus legionis II adiutricis et Tullia Sperata coniux et Aelius Florianus et Aelius Verus filii Aelia Saturnina soror Septimius Quadratus pater vixit annis LXXX et Septimia Pusina mater vixit annis LXXX et Aeliae Florinae coniugi vixit annis XXV et Saturnae filiae vixit nnis IIII vivus sibi et suis faciendum curavit\n
EDCS-11800604.translation: To Aelius Saturninus, veteran of the II Adjutrix legion, and his wife Tullia Sperata, and his sons Aelius Florianus and Aelius Verus, and his sister Aelia Saturnina. His father Septimius Quadratus, who lived 80 years, and his mother Septimia Pusina, who lived 80 years, and his wife Aeliae Florinae, who lived 25 years, and his daughter Saturnae, who lived 4 years. He arranged for this to be made for himself and his family while alive.
EDCS-11800604.summary: This epitaph records the family of Aelius Saturninus, a veteran of the II Adjutrix legion, who arranged for his own tombstone as well as those of his deceased wife, daughter, and parents.

EDCS-11800605.text_edition: \n Dis Manibus Prisciano quaestori tribuni legionis II adiutricis vixit annos XL dies XXIIII domo Mursa stipendiorum XXI Gaia ria coniux et et Priscilla et filias sic et \n
EDCS-11800605.translation: To the Divine Manes. To Priscianus, quaestor and tribune of the II Adjutrix legion, who lived 40 years and 24 days. His wife Gaia ria, and Priscilla, and his daughters, arranged for this to be made.
EDCS-11800605.summary: This epitaph commemorates Priscianus, a quaestor and tribune of the II Adjutrix legion, with his wife and daughters arranging for the monument.

EDCS-11800608.text_edition: \n Dis Manibus Herculanus vixit annos VIIII et Sterceia vixit annos XII et Revocata vixit annos III Aurelius Eutycies sic pater et Iulia Mamata filiis faciendum curaverunt \n
EDCS-11800608.translation: To the Divine Manes. To Herculanus, who lived 9 years, and Sterceia, who lived 12 years, and Revocata, who lived 3 years. Their father Aurelius Eutycies and Iulia Mamata arranged for this to be made for their sons.
EDCS-11800608.summary: This epitaph is a dedication by Aurelius Eutycies and Iulia Mamata to their deceased children Herculanus, Sterceia, and Revocata.

EDCS-11800609.text_edition: \n Dis Manibus et Paterno matres quorum parvulis suis praecedunt in Orcum et Aurelio Victorino iuniori vixit annos XVII Aurelii Hermes et Bagia et Pitheus parentes filiis merentibus faciendum curaverunt\n
EDCS-11800609.translation: To the Divine Manes and Paterno. To their children who preceded them to the underworld, and to Aurelius Victorinus the Younger, who lived 17 years. His parents Aurelii Hermes, Bagia, and Pitheus arranged for this to be made for their deserving sons.
EDCS-11800609.summary: This epitaph is a dedication by Aurelii Hermes, Bagia, and Pitheus to their children, including Aurelius Victorinus the Younger, who died young.

EDCS-11800640.text_edition: \n Dis Manibus Marco Ulpio Victori quondam veterano legionis II adiutricis qui vixit nnos LV et Marco Ulpio Victorino quaestori coloniae Aquincensis et Ulpiae Victorinae quondam filiis eius Marcus Aurelius Marcellinus nepos et heres eius perfaciendum sic curavit\n
EDCS-11800640.translation: To the Divine Manes. To Marcus Ulpius Victor, formerly a veteran of the II Adjutrix legion, who lived 55 years, and to Marcus Ulpius Victorinus, quaestor of the colony of Aquincum, and to Ulpia Victorina, his former children. His grandson and heir Marcus Aurelius Marcellinus arranged for this to be fully completed.
EDCS-11800640.summary: This epitaph commemorates Marcus Ulpius Victor, a veteran of the II Adjutrix legion, his son Marcus Ulpius Victorinus, a quaestor, and his daughter Ulpia Victorina, with the monument arranged by his grandson and heir.

EDCS-11800641.text_edition: \n Dis Manibus Septimio Messiano veterano ex beneficiario legati qui vixit nnos LX menses VII dies V Aurelia Vera marito dignissimo ob merita faciendum curavit\n
EDCS-11800641.translation: To the Divine Manes. To Septimius Messianus, a veteran formerly in the rank of beneficiarius of the legate, who lived 60 years, 7 months, and 5 days. Aurelia Vera arranged for this to be made for her most worthy husband because of his merits.
EDCS-11800641.summary: This epitaph is a dedication by Aurelia Vera to her deceased husband, Septimius Messianus, a veteran who served as a beneficiarius.

EDCS-11800642.text_edition: \n Dis Manibus Lucius Varius Pudens veteranus legionis X geminae domo Parma annorum LX hic situs est Maria GEIS con<i=E>ugi pientissimo posuit \n
EDCS-11800642.translation: To the Divine Manes. Lucius Varius Pudens, a veteran of the X Gemina legion, from Parma, aged 60 years, lies here. His wife Maria GEIS placed this for her most devoted husband.
EDCS-11800642.summary: This epitaph commemorates Lucius Varius Pudens, a veteran of the X Gemina legion from Parma, and was erected by his wife.

EDCS-11800643.text_edition: \n Silvano Silvestro sic Septimius Karus miles legionis II adiutricis votum libens solvit\n
EDCS-11800643.translation: To Silvanus Silvestris. Septimius Karus, a soldier of the II Adjutrix legion, gladly fulfilled his vow.
EDCS-11800643.summary: This is a votive inscription by Septimius Karus, a soldier of the II Adjutrix legion, to Silvanus Silvestris.

EDCS-11800644.text_edition: \n Deo Soli Omnipotenti Iulius Marcellinus miles legionis II adiutricis ex voto posuit\n
EDCS-11800644.translation: To the all-powerful god Sol. Iulius Marcellinus, a soldier of the II Adjutrix legion, placed this in fulfillment of a vow.
EDCS-11800644.summary: This is a votive inscription by Iulius Marcellinus, a soldier of the II Adjutrix legion, dedicated to the god Sol.

EDCS-11800645.text_edition: \n Valeria Cara Silvanabus votum solvit libens merito\n
EDCS-11800645.translation: Valeria Cara gladly and deservedly fulfilled her vow to the Silvani.
EDCS-11800645.summary: This is a votive inscription by Valeria Cara to the Silvani (nature spirits).

EDCS-12000849.text_edition: \n Bono Evento renovavi ex voto pro salute ordinis Sirmiensium patronorum virorum em<i=E>nentissimorum Flavius Constantius custo<s=R> Matris deae libertus \n
EDCS-12000849.translation: For the good god Bono Evento, I, Flavius Constantius, freedman of the Custos Matris deae, restored (this) from a vow for the safety of the most eminent order of patrons of Sirmium.
EDCS-12000849.summary: This inscription records a restoration of a monument, dedicated by Flavius Constantius, a freedman, likely in the late Roman period.

EDCS-12000850.text_edition: \n Dis Manibus Postumio Leoni viro egregio proximo liberto sacro Postumius Achilleus fratri benignissimo curante Crescentino amico suo et Dyscolio amico et coque liberto\n
EDCS-12000850.translation: To the spirits of the underworld. Postumius Achilleus, to his most kind brother, the freedman Postumius Leon, a distinguished man, his closest freedman, had this made, with his friend Crescentinus and his friend Dyscolius also acting as overseers.
EDCS-12000850.summary: This is a tomb inscription for Postumius Leon, a freedman, commissioned by his brother Postumius Achilleus, and dating to the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-12000851.text_edition: \n ISACTOS tribunos et illustris actis notarii splendidi[ssimi ------\n
EDCS-12000851.translation: To the tribunes and the notaries, splendid...
EDCS-12000851.summary: This inscription appears to be a dedication to Roman officials, possibly tribunes and notaries, with the remaining text lost or illegible.

EDCS-12000853.text_edition: \n Aureliae Valentinae quae vixit nnos XXVII Marcellus eques numeri III Thracum coniugi faciendum curavit\n
EDCS-12000853.translation: Marcellus, a cavalryman of the III Thracum cohort, had this made for his wife Aurelia Valentina, who lived for 27 years.
EDCS-12000853.summary: This tombstone records the death of Aurelia Valentina at the age of 27 and was commissioned by her husband, Marcellus, a soldier in the III Thracum cohort, likely dating to the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-12000856.text_edition: \n Iovi Optimo Maximo Caius Calpurnius Ceius Aemilianus tribunus laticlavius legionis II adiutricis piae fidelis votum solvit libens merito\n
EDCS-12000856.translation: To Jupiter Best and Greatest, Gaius Calpurnius Ceius Aemilianus, a senatorial tribune of the II Adiutrix Pia Fidelis legion, willingly and deservedly fulfilled his vow.
EDCS-12000856.summary: This is a votive inscription to Jupiter, dedicated by Gaius Calpurnius Ceius Aemilianus, a military tribune of the II Adiutrix legion, likely from the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-12000858.text_edition: \n Impperatores Caessares Gaius Iulius Verus Maximinus Pius Felix Augustus pontifex maximus tribunicia potestate II imperator III consul proconsul pater patriae et Gaius Iulius Verus Maximus nobilissimus Caesar filio sic Augusti nostri Dacici Germanici Sarmatici maximi ab Aquinco milia passuum CVII\n
EDCS-12000858.translation: The Emperors Caesars Gaius Julius Verus Maximinus Pius Felix Augustus, chief priest, with tribunician power for the second time, imperator for the third time, consul, proconsul, father of the fatherland, and Gaius Julius Verus Maximus, most noble Caesar, son of our Augustus, from Dacicus, Germanicus, Sarmaticus Maximus, 107 miles from Aquincum.
EDCS-12000858.summary: This inscription names Emperors Maximinus Thrax and his son Maximus Caesar, likely from the 3rd century CE, marking a distance from Aquincum.

EDCS-12100858.inscription_type: diplomata militaria, tituli operum, tituli sacri
EDCS-12100858.text_edition: \n Nero Claudius divi Claudi filius Germanici Caesaris nepos Tiberi Caesaris Augusti pronepos divi Augusti abnepos Caesar Augustus Germanicus pontifex maximus tribunicia potestate XI imperator VIIII pater patriae consul IIII peditibus et equitibus qui militant <in> cohortibus tribus quae appellantur I et II Thracum et VII Breucorum et sunt in Germania sub Publio Sulpicio Scribonio Proculo qui quina et vicena stipendia aut plura meruerant quorum nomina subscripta sunt ipsis liberis posterisque eorum civitatem dedit et conubium cum uxoribus quas tunc habuissent cum est civitas iis data aut siqui caelibes essent cum iis quas postea duxissent dumtaxat singuli singulas ante diem XV Kalendas Iulias Aulo Licinio Nerva Siliano Publio Pasidieno Firmo consulibus pediti cohortis VII Breucorum cui praeest Caius Numisius Cai filius Velina Maximus Liccaio Liccai filio Breuco descriptum et recognitum ex tabula aeniaquae fixa est Romae in Capitolio ante aerarium militare in basi Claudiorum Marcellorum Cai Marci Nobilis Emoniensis Sexti Tei Nicerotis Aquileiensis Cai Caecinae Hermaes sic Aquileiensis Titi Picati Carpi Aquileiensis Luci Hostili Blaesi Emoniensis Marci Treboni Hygini Aquileiensis Luci Anni Potentis Aquileiensis\n
EDCS-12100858.translation: Nero Claudius Caesar Augustus Germanicus, grandson of Germanicus Caesar, great-grandson of Tiberius Caesar Augustus, great-great-grandson of the divine Augustus, chief priest, with tribunician power for the eleventh time, imperator for the ninth time, consul for the fourth time, father of the fatherland, granted citizenship and the right to marry to the foot soldiers and cavalrymen who serve in the three cohorts called I and II of the Thracians, and VII of the Breuci, and are stationed in Germany under Publius Sulpicius Scribonius Proculus, who had served for twenty-five years or more, whose names are listed below, to them, their children, and their descendants, and to any who were unmarried with those whom they would later marry, one each, on the day before the 15th of the Kalends of July, when Aulus Licinius Nerva Silianus and Publius Pasidenius Firmus were consuls. Foot soldier of the I Thracum Germanica cohort, commanded by Gaius Numisius Maximus, son of Cai, of the Velina tribe, from Emona, to Liccaio, son of Liccai, a Breucus. Recorded and recognized from a bronze tablet fixed in Rome on the Capitol, in front of the military treasury, on the base of the Claudii Marcelli, [names of witnesses follow].
EDCS-12100858.summary: This diploma, granted under Nero, grants Roman citizenship and marriage rights to soldiers serving in specific Thracian and Breuci cohorts in Germania, with the text referencing a bronze tablet as the official record and listing witnesses, dating to 55 CE.

EDCS-12100879.inscription_type: diplomata militaria, tituli operum, tituli sacri
EDCS-12100879.text_edition: \n Imperator Caesar divi Hadriani filius divi Traiani Parthici nepos divi Nervae pronepos Titus Aelius Hadrianus Antoninus Augustus Pius pontifex maximus tribunicia potestate XIX imperator II consul IIII pater patriae equitibus et peditibus qui militaverunt in alis V quae appe<l=T>lantur I Thracum veterana sagittariorum et I <c=T>(ivium) Romanorum et I praetoria civium Romanorum et I Flavia Augusta Breti sic Mmiliaria et I Augusta Ituraeorum et cohortibus XIII I Alpinorum equitata et III Batavorum et I Thracum Germanica et I Alpinorum peditata et I Noricorum et III Lusitanorum et II Asturum et Callaecorum et VII Breucorum et I Lusitanorum et II Augusta Thracum et I Montanorum et I C<am=MA>panorum voluntariorum et I Thracum civium Romanorum et sunt in Pannonia inferiore sub Iallio Basso legato quinque et viginti item classicis sex et viginti stipendiis emeritis dimissis <h=C>onesta miss<i=T>one quorum nomina subscrepta sic sunt civitatem Romanam qui eorum non haberent dedit et conubium cum uxoribus quas tunc habuissent tum sic est civitas is <d=O>ata <a=M>ut cum is quas postea duxissent dumtaxat singulis ante diem VI Idus Februarias Marco Civica Barbaro Marco Metilio Regulo consulibus cohortis I Thracum Germanicae cui praeest Caius Turpilius Verecundus ex pedite Monno Tessimari filio Eravisco et Niciae Tricani filiae uxori eius Canac descriptum et recognitum ex tabula aerea quae fixa est Romae in muro post templum divi Augusti ad Minervam Imperator Caesar divi Hadriani filius divi Traiani Parthici nepos divi Nervae pronepos Titus Aelius Hadrianus Antoninus Augustus Pius pantifex sic maximus tribunicia potestate XIX imperator II consul IIII pater patriae equitibus et peditibus qui militaverunt in alis V quae appellantur I Thracum veterana sagittariorum et I civium Romanorum et I praetoria civium Romanorum et I Fl(avia) Augusta Britannica Mmiliaria et I Augusta Ituraeorum et cohortibus XIII I Alpinorum equitata et III Batavorum et I Thracum Germanica et I Alpinorum peditata et I Noricorum et III Lusitanorum et II Asturum et Callaecorum et VII Breucorum et I Lusitanorum et II Augusta Thracum et I Montanorum et I Campanorum voluntariorum et I Thracum civium Romanorum et sunt in Pannonia inferiore sub Iallio Basso legato XXV item classicis XXVI stipendiis emeritis dimissis honesta missione quorum nomina subscripta sunt civitatem Romanam qui eorum non haberent dedit et conubium cum uxoribus quas tunc habuissent tum est civitas is data aut cum is quas postea duxissent dumtaxat singulis Cohortis I Thracum Germanicae cui praeest Caius Turpilius Verecundus ex pedite Monno Tessimari filio Eravisco et Niciae Tricani filiae uxori eius Canac Marci Servili Getae Luci Pulli Chresimi Marci Sentili Iasi Tiberi Iuli Felicis Cai Belli Urbani Cai Pomponi Statiani Publi Ocili Prisci \n
EDCS-12100879.translation: Emperor Caesar Titus Aelius Hadrianus Antoninus Augustus Pius, son of the divine Hadrian, grandson of the divine Trajan Parthicus, great-grandson of the divine Nerva, chief priest, with tribunician power for the nineteenth time, imperator for the second time, consul for the fourth time, father of the fatherland, granted Roman citizenship and the right to marry to the cavalrymen and foot soldiers who served in the five alae and thirteen cohorts listed, and who were stationed in Pannonia Inferior under Iallus Bassus, having completed twenty-five years of service, and to the legionaries who completed twenty-six years of service, and were honorably discharged, whose names are subscribed, to them, and to those who did not have citizenship, and to their wives whom they then had when citizenship was granted to them, or to those whom they would later marry, one each, on the day before the 6th of the Ides of February, when Marcus Civica Barbarus and Marcus Metilius Regulus were consuls. From the I Thracum Germanica cohort, commanded by Gaius Turpilius Verecundus, to the foot soldier Monnus, son of Tessimus, an Eraviscus, and to his wife Nicia, daughter of Tricanus, an Eraviscan. Recorded and recognized from a bronze tablet fixed in Rome on the wall behind the temple of the divine Augustus near the Minervae. [Repeats emperor's titles and list of alae and cohorts, with some textual variations and additional names].
EDCS-12100879.summary: This military diploma, issued under Antoninus Pius, grants Roman citizenship and marriage rights to auxiliary soldiers serving in Pannonia Inferior, detailing the units and consuls, with the original grant recorded on a bronze tablet in Rome, dating to 145 CE.

EDCS-12100880.inscription_type: diplomata militaria, tituli operum, tituli sacri
EDCS-12100880.text_edition: \n Imperator Caesar divi Hadriani filius divi Traiani Parthici nepos divi Nervae pronepos Titus Aelius Hadrianus Antoninus Augustus Pius pontifex maximus tribunicia potestate XIX imperator II consul IIII pater patriae equitibus et peditibus qui militaverunt in alis V quae appellantur I Thracum veterana sagittariorum et I civium Romanorum et I praetoria civium Romanorum et I Flavia Augusta Britannica Mmiliaria et I Augusta Ituraeorum et cohortibus XIII I Alpinorum equitata et III Batavorum et I Thracum Germanica et I Alpinorum peditata et I Noricorum et III Lusitanorum et II Asturum et Callaecorum et VII Breucorum et I Lusitanorum et II Augusta Thracum et I Montanorum et I Campanorum voluntariorum et I Thracum civium Romanorum et sunt in Pannonia inferiore sub Iallio Basso legato quinque et viginti item classicis sex et viginti stipendiis emeritis dimissis honesta missione quorum nomina subscripta sunt civitatem Romanam qui eorum non haberent dedit et conubium cum uxoribus quas tunc habuissent cum est civitas is data aut cum is quas postea duxissent dumtaxat singulis ante diem VI Idus Februarias Marco Civica Barbaro Marco Metilio Regulo consulibus cohortis I Thracum Germanicae cui praeest Caius Turpilius Verecundus ex pedite Culso Atedumi filio Eravisco et Vervedae Tessimari filiae uxori eius Eraviscae descriptum et recognitum ex tabula aerea quae fixa est Romae in muro post templum divi Augusti ad Minervam Imperator Caesar divi Hadriani filius divi Traiani Parthici nepos divi Nervae pronepos Titus Aelius Hadrianus Antoninus Augustus Pius pontifex maximus tribunicia potestate XVIIII imperator II consul IIII pater patriae equitibus et peditibus qui militaverunt in alis V quae appellantur I Thracum veterana sagittariorum et I civium Romanorum et I praetoria civium Romanorum et I Flavia Augusta Britannica Mmiliaria et I Augusta Ituraeorum et cohortibus XIII I Alpinorum equitata et III Batavorum et I Thracum Germanica et I Alpinorum peditata et I Noricorum et III Lusitanorum et II Asturum et Callaecorum et VII Breucorum et I Lusitanorum et II Augusta Thracum et I Montanorum et I Campanorum voluntariorum et I Thracum civium Romanorum et sunt in Pannonia inferiore sub Iallio Basso legato XXV item classicis XXVI stipendiis emeritis dimissis honesta missione quorum nomina subscripta sunt civitatem Romanam qui eorum non haberent dedit et conubium cum uxoribus quas tunc habuissent cum est civitas is data aut cum is quas postea duxissent dumtaxat singulis ante diem VI Idus Februarias Marco Civica Barbaro et Regulo consulibus cohortis I Thracum Germanicae cui praeest Caius Turpilius Verecundus ex pedite Culso Atedumi filio Eravisco et Vervedae Tessimari filiae uxori eius Canac3 descriptum et recognitum ex tabula aerea quae fixa est Romae in muro post templum divi Augusti ad Minervam Marci Servili Getae Luci Pulli Chresimi Marci Sentili Iasi Tiberi Iuli Felicis Cai Belli Urbani Cai Pomponi Statiani Publi Ocili Prisci\n
EDCS-12100880.translation: Emperor Caesar Titus Aelius Hadrianus Antoninus Augustus Pius, son of the divine Hadrian, grandson of the divine Trajan Parthicus, great-grandson of the divine Nerva, chief priest, with tribunician power for the nineteenth time, imperator for the second time, consul for the fourth time, father of the fatherland, granted Roman citizenship and the right to marry to the cavalrymen and foot soldiers who served in the five alae and thirteen cohorts listed, and who were stationed in Pannonia Inferior under Iallus Bassus, having completed twenty-five years of service, and to the legionaries who completed twenty-six years of service, and were honorably discharged, whose names are subscribed, to them, and to those who did not have citizenship, and to their wives whom they then had when citizenship was granted to them, or to those whom they would later marry, one each, on the day before the 6th of the Ides of February, when Marcus Civica Barbarus and Marcus Metilius Regulus were consuls. From the I Thracum Germanica cohort, commanded by Gaius Turpilius Verecundus, to the foot soldier Culso, son of Atedumus, an Eraviscus, and to his wife Vereda, daughter of Tessimus, an Eraviscan. Recorded and recognized from a bronze tablet fixed in Rome on the wall behind the temple of the divine Augustus near the Minervae. [Repeats emperor's titles and grant details, with a variant dating to his 17th tribunician power and different consuls, also specifying the cohort and recipient family names].
EDCS-12100880.summary: This military diploma, issued under Antoninus Pius, grants Roman citizenship and marriage rights to auxiliary soldiers and legionaries serving in Pannonia Inferior, with two versions presented citing different tribunician powers and consuls, but the same commander and recipient family, dating to 149 CE and 152 CE.

EDCS-12300207.inscription_type: diplomata militaria, tituli operum, tituli sacri
EDCS-12300207.text_edition: \n caelibes essent cum iis quas postea duxissent dumtaxat singuli singulas Idibus Februariis cohortis II Hispanorum cui praeest Caius Cavarius Priscus equiti Dasenti Dasmeni filio Cornacati et Iorae Prososii filiae uxori eius et Emerito filio eius et Turunae filiae eius et Emeritae filiae eius descriptum et recognitum ex tabula aenea quae fixa est Romae in Capitolio in aede Fidei populi Romani latere sinisteriore extrinsecus Luci Vitelli Sossiani Quinti Vibi Saurici Titi Gratti Valentis Cai Antist<i=S> Marini Publi Servili Adiutoris Auli Cascelli Successi Marci Heleni Primi \n
EDCS-12300207.translation: If they were unmarried, with those whom they would later marry, one each. On the Ides of February, from the II Spanish cohort, commanded by Gaius Cavarius Priscus, to the cavalryman Dasentus, son of Dasmenus, a Cornacatus, and to his wife Iora, daughter of Prososius, and to their son Emeritus, and to their daughter Turuna, and to their daughter Emerita. Recorded and recognized from a bronze tablet fixed in Rome on the Capitol, in the Temple of Fides, on the left outer side of the Roman people, [names of witnesses follow].
EDCS-12300207.summary: This is a military diploma granting citizenship and marriage rights to a cavalryman of the II Spanish cohort and his family, with the record being displayed on a bronze tablet in Rome, dating to the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-12300222.text_edition: \n si peregrini iuris feminas matrimonio suo iunxerint proinde liberos tollant ac si ex duobus civibus Romanis natos ante diem III Kalendas Iunias Lucio Aelio Oculato Quinto Gavio Attico consulibus Lucio Flavio Luci filio Claudia Sabino Savariensi descriptum et recognitum ex tabula aenea quae fixa est Romae in Capitolio Cai Aconi Maximi Sisciensis Titi Flavi Festi Sisciensis Sexti Iuventi Ingenui Sirmiensis Cai Curti Secundi Sirmiensis Marci Statori Sabini Sirmiensis Marci Lucili Saturnini Sisciensis Marci Rutili Hermetis Sisciensis\n
EDCS-12300222.translation: If foreigners join women of peregrine status in marriage, they shall have their children in the same way as if born from two Roman citizens, before the third day before the Kalends of June, with Lucius Aelius Oculus and Quintus Gavius Atticus as consuls. Recorded and confirmed from a bronze tablet which is fixed in Rome on the Capitol, [listing names of witnesses].
EDCS-12300222.summary: This is a legal decree concerning the marriage of Roman citizens to women of peregrine status, issued during the consulship of Lucius Aelius Oculus and Quintus Gavius Atticus.

EDCS-12300246.inscription_type: diplomata militaria, tituli operum, tituli sacri
EDCS-12300246.translation: Emperor Caesar, son of the deified Nerva, Nerva Traianus Augustus Germanicus, pontifex maximus, holder of tribunician power, consul for the second time, has granted citizenship and the right of legal marriage to the foot soldiers and cavalrymen serving in two alae and five cohorts, namely Siliana (of Roman citizens) and I Augusta Ituraeorum, I Montanorum (of Roman citizens), I Batavorum (miliaria) Pia Fidelis, I Lusitanorum, I Augusta Ituraeorum, and II Batavorum (miliaria), who are in Pannonia under Gnaeus Pinarius Aemilius Cicatricula Pompeius Longinus, and also to those discharged with an honorable discharge who have completed twenty or more years of service, whose names are listed below, to them, their children, and their descendants, and the right of legal marriage with the wives they then had when citizenship was granted to them, or if they were unmarried, with those they subsequently married, provided each man only one wife. This was granted on the tenth day before the Kalends of March, with Emperor Caesar Traianus Augustus Germanicus (consul for the second time) and Sextus Julius Frontinus (consul for the second time) as consuls, for the foot soldier Publius Insteius Agrippae f. Cyrrhus, discharged with an honorable discharge from the cohort I Augusta Ituraeorum, which is commanded by Lucius Callidius Lucius f. Ste(llatina) Camidienus. Recorded and confirmed from a bronze tablet which is fixed in Rome on the wall behind the temple of the deified Augustus near Minerva.
EDCS-12300246.summary: This imperial edict, issued under Emperor Trajan, grants Roman citizenship and the right of legal marriage to auxiliary soldiers serving in Pannonia who have completed at least twenty years of service, dated to the consulship of Trajan II and Sex. Julius Frontinus II.

EDCS-12300251.inscription_type: diplomata militaria, tituli operum, tituli sacri
EDCS-12300251.translation: Emperor Caesar, son of the deified Nerva, Nerva Traianus Augustus Germanicus, pontifex maximus, holder of tribunician power for the sixth time, imperator for the fourth time, father of the country, consul for the fourth time, designated for the fifth, has granted citizenship and the right of legal marriage to the cavalrymen and foot soldiers serving in three alae and five cohorts, namely I Aravorum, I Flavia Britanniciana miliaria (of Roman citizens), Siliana (of Roman citizens), I Augusta Ituraeorum, I Alpinorum, I Montanorum, II Alpinorum, and VIII Raetorum, who are in Pannonia under Quintus Glitius Atilius Agricola, and also to those discharged with an honorable discharge who have completed twenty or more years of service, whose names are listed below, to them, their children, and their descendants, and the right of legal marriage with the wives they then had when citizenship was granted to them, or if they were unmarried, with those they subsequently married, provided each man only one wife. This was granted on the thirteenth day before the Kalends of December, with Lucius Antonius Albus and Marcus Junius Homullus as consuls, for the foot soldier Lucius Singeius Lucius f. Rufianus from Lucens (from Lucens?), discharged with an honorable discharge from cohort I Montanorum, which is commanded by Quintus Caecilius Redditus. Recorded and confirmed from a bronze tablet which is fixed in Rome on the wall behind the temple of the deified Augustus near Minerva.
EDCS-12300251.summary: This imperial edict, issued under Emperor Trajan, grants Roman citizenship and the right of legal marriage to auxiliary soldiers in Pannonia who have completed at least twenty years of service, dated to the consulship of L. Antonius Albus and M. Junius Homullus.

EDCS-12300304.inscription_type: diplomata militaria, tituli operum, tituli sacri
EDCS-12300304.translation: Emperor Caesar, son of the deified Hadrian, grandson of the deified Trajan Parthicus, great-grandson of the deified Nerva, Titus Aelius Hadrianus Antoninus Augustus Pius, pontifex maximus, holder of tribunician power for the fifteenth time, imperator for the second time, consul for the fourth time, father of the country, has granted Roman citizenship and the right of legal marriage with the wives they then had when citizenship was granted to them, or if they were unmarried, with those they subsequently married, provided each man only one wife, to those who served in the praetorian fleet at Ravenna, which is under the prefect Tuticanius Capito, having completed twenty-six years of service and discharged with an honorable discharge, whose names are listed below, to them, their children, and their descendants. This was granted on the Non(es) of September, with Priscus and Romulus as consuls, for Gaius Valerius Annaei f. Dasio Scirto from Dalmatia, from the rank of gregale. Recorded and confirmed from a bronze tablet which is fixed in Rome on the wall behind the temple of the deified Augustus near Minerva.
EDCS-12300304.summary: This imperial edict, issued under Emperor Antoninus Pius, grants Roman citizenship and the right of legal marriage to sailors of the praetorian fleet at Ravenna who have completed twenty-six years of service, dated to the consulship of C. Novius Priscus and L. Iunius Romulus.

EDCS-12300327.inscription_type: diplomata militaria, tituli operum, tituli sacri
EDCS-12300327.translation: Emperor Caesar Marcus Aurelius Antoninus Augustus, called Armenian, Mede, Parthian, pontifex maximus, holder of tribunician power for the twenty-first time, imperator for the fifth time, consul for the third time, and Emperor Caesar Lucius Aurelius Verus Augustus, called Armenian, Mede, Parthian, pontifex maximus, holder of tribunician power for the seventh time, imperator for the fifth time, consul for the third time, fathers of the country, sons of the deified Antoninus, grandsons of the deified Hadrian, great-grandsons of the deified Trajan Parthicus, great-great-grandsons of the deified Nerva, have granted Roman citizenship to the cavalrymen and foot soldiers who served in three alae, namely I Thracum veteran (miliaria) of Roman citizens, I Britannica (miliaria) of Roman citizens, and I Augusta Ituraeorum, and ten cohorts, namely I Thracum Germanica (of Roman citizens), I Alpinorum peditata, I Noricorum, III Lusitanorum, II Asturum et Callaecorum, VII Breucorum, I Lusitanorum, II Augusta Thracum, I Montanorum, I Augusta Thracum (of Roman citizens), who are in Pannonia Inferior under Claudius Pompeianus as legate, and have completed twenty-five or more years of service and been discharged with an honorable discharge, whose names are listed below; and the right of legal marriage with the wives they then had when citizenship was granted to them, or with those they subsequently married, provided each man only one wife. This was granted on the third day before the Nones of May, with Dentilianus and Pallans as consuls, for Oxetius, son of Naevio, from Eraviscus, from the ala I Thracum veteran(a), which is commanded by Flavius Macer, from the rank of gregale. Recorded and confirmed from a bronze tablet which is fixed in Rome on the wall behind the temple of the deified Augustus near Minerva.
EDCS-12300327.summary: This imperial edict, issued under Emperors Marcus Aurelius and Lucius Verus, grants Roman citizenship and marriage rights to auxiliary soldiers in Pannonia Inferior who completed twenty-five or more years of service, dated to the consulship of Q. Caecilius Dentilianus and M. Antonius Pallans.

EDCS-12300346.inscription_type: diplomata militaria, tituli operum, tituli sacri
EDCS-12300346.translation: Emperor Caesar, son of the deified Antoninus Magnus Pius, grandson of the deified Severus Pius, Marcus Aurelius Severus Alexander Pius Felix Augustus, pontifex maximus, holder of tribunician power for the fourth time, consul, father of the country, [lists] the names of the soldiers who served in the ten praetorian cohorts Severianae, the Pious Vindicating, who performed their service piously and bravely, [grants] the right of marriage, with a single and first wife only, so that even if they join women of peregrine status in marriage, they shall have their children in the same way as if born from two Roman citizens. This was granted on the seventh day before the Ides of January, with Tiberius Manilius Fuscus (consul for the second time) and Servius Domitius Dexter as consuls, for the cohort II praetoria Severiana Pia Vindex, for [name illegible]trioni Valeri f. Papiria Martialis? from Poetovio. Recorded and confirmed from a bronze tablet which is fixed on the wall behind the temple of the deified Augustus near Minerva.
EDCS-12300346.summary: This imperial edict, issued under Emperor Severus Alexander, grants marriage rights to soldiers of the praetorian cohorts Severianae, allowing them to marry peregrine women and legitimize their children, dated to the consulship of T. Manilius Fuscus II and S. Domitius Dexter.

EDCS-12300381.inscription_type: diplomata militaria, tituli operum, tituli sacri
EDCS-12300381.text_edition: \n Imperator Caesar divi Hadriani filius divi Traiani Parthici nepos divi Nervae pronepos Titus Aelius Hadrianus Antoninus Augustus Pius pontifex maximus tribunicia potestate VIIII imperator II consul IIII pater patriae equitibus et peditibus qui militaverunt in alis V et cohortibus VII quae appellantur I Ulpia contariorum |(miliaria) et I Thracum victrix et I Cannanefatium civium Romanorum et I Hispanorum Arvacorum et III Augusta Thracum sagittariorum et I Aelia |(miliaria) sagittariorum et I Ulpia contariorum |(miliaria) et I Thracum civium Romanorum et II Alpinorum et V Callaecorum Lucensium et IV voluntariorum civium Romanorum et XVIII voluntariorum civium Romanorum et sunt in nnia superiore sub Pontio Laeliano quinis et vicenis pluribusve stipendiis emeritis dimissis honesta missione quorum nomina subscripta sunt civitatem Romanam qui eorum non haberent dedit et conubium cum uxoribus quas tunc habuissent cum est civitas is data aut cum is quas postea duxissent dumtaxat singulis ante diem XIV Kalendas Augustas Cnaeo Terentio Iuniore Lucio Aurelio Gallo consulibus alae I Hispanorum Arvacorum cui praeest Lucius Aburnius Severus Heracleia ex gregale Viatori Romani filio Asalo descriptum et recognitum ex tabula aerea quae fixa est Romae in muro post templum divi Augusti ad Minervam Imperator Caesar divi Hadriani filius divi Traiani Parthici nepos divi Nervae pronepos Titus Aelius Hadrianus Antoninus Augustus Pius pontifex maximus tribunicia potestate VIIII imperator II consul IIII pater patriae equitibus et peditibus qui militaverunt in alis V et cohortibus VII et sunt in nnonia superiore sub Pontio Laeliano XXV pluribusve stipendiis emeritis dimissis honesta missione quorum nomina subscripta sunt civitatem Romanam qui eorum non haberent dedit et conubium cum uxoribus quas tunc habuissent cum est civitas data aut cum is quas postea duxissent dumtaxat singulis ante diem XIV Kalendas Augustas Iuniore et Gallo consulibus alae I Hispanorum Aravacorum cui praeest Lucius Aburnius Severus Heracleia ex gregale Viatori Romani filio Asalo Publi Atti Severi Luci Pulli Daphni Marci Servili Getae Luci Pulli Chresimi Marci Sentili Iasi Titi Iuli Felicis Cai Iuli Silvani\n
EDCS-12300381.translation: Emperor Caesar, son of the deified Hadrian, grandson of the deified Trajan Parthicus, great-grandson of the deified Nerva, Titus Aelius Hadrianus Antoninus Augustus Pius, pontifex maximus, holder of tribunician power for the ninth time, imperator for the second time, consul for the fourth time, father of the country, to the cavalrymen and foot soldiers who served in five alae and seven cohorts, namely I Ulpia contariorum (miliaria), I Thracum victrix, I Cannanefatium (of Roman citizens), I Hispanorum Arvacorum, III Augusta Thracum sagittariorum, I Aelia (miliaria) sagittariorum, I Ulpia contariorum (miliaria), I Thracum (of Roman citizens), II Alpinorum, V Callaecorum Lucensium, IV voluntariorum (of Roman citizens), XVIII voluntariorum (of Roman citizens), who are in Pannonia Superior under Pontius Laelianus, and have completed twenty-five or more years of service and been discharged with an honorable discharge, whose names are listed below, has granted Roman citizenship to those among them who did not possess it, and the right of legal marriage with the wives they then had when citizenship was granted to them, or with those they subsequently married, provided each man only one wife. This was granted on the fourteenth day before the Kalends of August, with Gnaeus Terentius Iunior and Lucius Aurelius Gallus as consuls, for Viator, son of Romanus, from Asalo, from the ala I Hispanorum Arvacorum, which is commanded by Lucius Aburnius Severus from Heracleia, from the rank of gregale. Recorded and confirmed from a bronze tablet which is fixed in Rome on the wall behind the temple of the deified Augustus near Minerva.
EDCS-12300381.summary: This imperial edict, issued under Emperor Antoninus Pius, grants Roman citizenship and marriage rights to auxiliary soldiers in Pannonia Superior who completed at least twenty-five years of service, dated to the consulship of Cn. Terentius Iunior and L. Aurelius Gallus.

EDCS-12300382.inscription_type: diplomata militaria, tituli operum, tituli sacri
EDCS-12300382.text_edition: \n Imperator Caesar divi Hadriani filius divi Traiani Parthici nepos divi Nervae pronepos Titus Aelius Hadrianus Antoninus Augustus Pius pontifex maximus tribunicia potestate XI imperator II consul IIII pater patriae equitibus et peditibus qui militaverunt in alis V et cohortibus XIII quae appellantur I Flavia Britannica |(miliaria) et I Thracum veterana sagittaria et I Brittonum civium Romanorum et I praetoria civium Romanorum et I Augusta Ituraeorum et III Batavorum |(miliaria) vexillatio et I Alpinorum equitata et I Thracum Germanica et I Alpinorum peditata et I Noricorum et III Lusitanorum et II Nerviorum et Callaecorum et VII Breucorum et I Lusitanorum et II Augusta Thracum et I Montanorum et I Campanorum voluntariorum civium Romanorum et I Thracum civium Romanorum et sunt in <P=D>nnia inferiore sub Cominio Secundo quinis et vicenis pluribusve item classicis sex et viginti stipendiis emeritis dimissis honesta missione quorum nomina subscripta sunt civitatem Romanam qui eorum non haberent dedit et conubium cum uxoribus quas tunc habuissent cum est civitas data aut cum is quas postea duxissent dumtaxat singulis ante diem VII Idus Octobres Caio Fabio Agrippino Marco Antonio Zenone consulibus alae I Flaviae Britannicae |(miliariae) cui praeest Marcus Licinius Victor Savaria ex gregale Reidomaro Siuppi filio Eravisco descriptum et recognitum ex tabula aerea quae fixa est Romae in muro post templum divi Augusti ad Minervam Imperator Caesar divi Hadriani filius divi Traiani Parthici nepos divi Nervae pronepos Titus Aelius Hadrianus Antoninus Augustus Pius pontifex maximus tribunicia potestate XI imperator II consul IIII pater patriae equitibus et peditibus qui militaverunt in alis V et cohortibus XIII et sunt in nnonia inferiore sub Cominio Secundo XXV pluribusve item classicis XXVI stipendiis emeritis dimissis honesta missione quorum nomina subscripta sunt civitatem Romanam qui eorum non haberent item filis classicis dedit et conubium cum uxoribus quas tunc habuissent cum est civitas data aut cum is quas postea duxissent dumtaxat singulis ante diem VII Idus Octobres Agrippino et Zenone consulibus alae I Flaviae Brittannicae |(miliariae) cui praeest Marcus Licinius Victor Savaria ex gregale Reidomaro Siuppi filio Eravisco Luci Pulli Daphni Marci Servili Getae Luci Pulli Chresimi Marci Ulpi Blasti Tiberi Iuli Felicis Cai Iuli Silvani Publi Ocili Prisci \n
EDCS-12300382.translation: Emperor Caesar, son of the deified Hadrian, grandson of the deified Trajan Parthicus, great-grandson of the deified Nerva, Titus Aelius Hadrianus Antoninus Augustus Pius, pontifex maximus, holder of tribunician power for the eleventh time, imperator for the second time, consul for the fourth time, father of the country, to the cavalrymen and foot soldiers who served in five alae and thirteen cohorts, namely I Flavia Britannica (miliaria), I Thracum veteran (sagittaria), I Brittonum (of Roman citizens), I praetoria (of Roman citizens), I Augusta Ituraeorum, III Batavorum (miliaria) vexillatio, I Alpinorum equitata, I Thracum Germanica, I Alpinorum peditata, I Noricorum, III Lusitanorum, II Nerviorum, Callaecorum, VII Breucorum, I Lusitanorum, II Augusta Thracum, I Montanorum, I Campanorum voluntariorum (of Roman citizens), I Thracum (of Roman citizens), who are in Pannonia Inferiore under Cominius Secundus, and also to sailors having completed twenty-six years of service, having been discharged with an honorable discharge, whose names are listed below, has granted Roman citizenship to those among them who did not possess it, and the right of legal marriage with the wives they then had when citizenship was granted to them, or with those they subsequently married, provided each man only one wife. This was granted on the seventh day before the Ides of October, with Gaius Fabius Agrippinus and Marcus Antonius Zeno as consuls, for Reidomarus, son of Siuppus, from Eraviscus, from the ala I Flaviae Britannicae (miliariae), which is commanded by Marcus Licinius Victor from Savaria, from the rank of gregale. Recorded and confirmed from a bronze tablet which is fixed in Rome on the wall behind the temple of the deified Augustus near Minerva.
EDCS-12300382.summary: This imperial edict, issued under Emperor Antoninus Pius, grants Roman citizenship and marriage rights to auxiliary soldiers and sailors in Pannonia Inferiore who completed twenty-five or twenty-six years of service respectively, dated to the consulship of C. Fabius Agrippinus and M. Antonius Zeno.

EDCS-12300383.inscription_type: diplomata militaria, tituli operum, tituli sacri
EDCS-12300383.text_edition: \n Imperator Caesar divi Hadriani filius divi Traiani Parthici nepos divi Nervae pronepos Titus Aelius Hadrianus Antoninus Augustus Pius pontifex maximus tribunicia potestate XI imperator sic et consul IIII pater patriae equitibus et peditibus qui militaverunt in alis V et cohortibus XIII quae appellantur I Flavia Britannica |(miliaria) et I Thracum veterana sagittaria et I Brittonum civium Romanorum et I praetoria civium Romanorum et I Augusta Ituraeorum et III Batavorum |(miliariae) vexillatio et I Alpinorum equitata et I Thracum Germanica et I Alpinorum peditata et I Noricorum et III Lusitanorum et II Nerviorum et Ca<ll=H>aecorum et VII Breucorum et I Lusitanorum et II Augusta Thracum et I Montanorum et I Campanorum voluntariorum civium Romanorum et I Thracum civium Romanorum et sunt in nnia inferiore sub Cominio Secundo quinis et vicenis <pluribusve> item classicis sex et viginti stipendiis emeritis dimissis honesta missione quorum nomina subscripta sunt civitatem Romanam qui eorum non haberent item <fi=EE>lis classicorum dedit et conubium cum uxoribus quas tunc habuissent cum est civitas is data aut cum is quas postea duxissent dumtaxat singulis ante diem VII Idus Octobres Caio Fabio Arippino Marco Antonio Zenone consulibus alae I Flaviae Britannicae |(miliariae) cui praeest Marcus Licinius Victor Savaria ex sesquipliciario Fusco Luci filio Azalo descriptum et recognitum ex tabula aerea quae fixa est Romae in muro post templum divi Augusti ad Minervam Imperator Caesar divi Hadriani filius divi Traiani Parthici nepos divi Nervae pronepos Titus Aelius Hadrianus Antoninus Augustus Pius pontifex maximus tribunicia potestate XI imperator II consul IIII pater patriae equitibus et peditibus qui militaverunt in alis V et cohortibus XIII et sunt in Pannonia inferiore sub Cominio Secundo XXV pluribusve item classicis XXVI stipendiis emeritis dimissis honesta missione quorum nomina subscripta sunt civitatem Romanam qui eorum non haberent item filis classicorum dedit et conubium cum uxoribus quas tunc habuissent cum est civitas data aut cum is quas postea duxissent dumtaxat singulis ante diem VII Idus Octobres Agrippino et Zenone consulibus alae I Flaviae nnicae |(miliariae) cui praeest Marcus Licinius Victor Savaria ex sesquipliciario Fusco Luci filio Azalo Luci Pulli Daphni Marci Servili Getae Luci Pulli Chresimi Marci Ulpi Blasti Tiberi Iuli Felicis Cai Iuli Silvani Publi Ocili Prisci \n
EDCS-12300383.translation: Emperor Caesar, son of the deified Hadrian, grandson of the deified Trajan Parthicus, great-grandson of the deified Nerva, Titus Aelius Hadrianus Antoninus Augustus Pius, pontifex maximus, holder of tribunician power for the eleventh time, imperator, consul for the fourth time, father of the country, to the cavalrymen and foot soldiers who served in five alae and thirteen cohorts, namely I Flavia Britannica (miliaria), I Thracum veteran (sagittaria), I Brittonum (of Roman citizens), I praetoria (of Roman citizens), I Augusta Ituraeorum, III Batavorum (miliariae) vexillatio, I Alpinorum equitata, I Thracum Germanica, I Alpinorum peditata, I Noricorum, III Lusitanorum, II Nerviorum, Callaecorum, VII Breucorum, I Lusitanorum, II Augusta Thracum, I Montanorum, I Campanorum voluntariorum (of Roman citizens), I Thracum (of Roman citizens), who are in Pannonia Inferiore under Cominius Secundus, and also to sailors having completed twenty-six years of service, having been discharged with an honorable discharge, whose names are listed below, has granted Roman citizenship to those among them who did not possess it, and the right of legal marriage with the wives they then had when citizenship was granted to them, or with those they subsequently married, provided each man only one wife. This was granted on the seventh day before the Ides of October, with Caius Fabius Agrippinus and Marcus Antonius Zeno as consuls, for Fuscus, son of Lucius, from Azalos, from the rank of sesquiplicarius, from the ala I Flaviae Britannicae (miliariae), which is commanded by Marcus Licinius Victor from Savaria. Recorded and confirmed from a bronze tablet which is fixed in Rome on the wall behind the temple of the deified Augustus near Minerva.
EDCS-12300383.summary: This imperial edict, issued under Emperor Antoninus Pius, grants Roman citizenship and marriage rights to auxiliary soldiers and sailors in Pannonia Inferiore who completed twenty-five or twenty-six years of service respectively, dated to the consulship of C. Fabius Agrippinus and M. Antonius Zeno.

EDCS-12700089.summary: This is a very fragmentary inscription, possibly part of a funerary monument or an epitaph, mentioning lions.

EDCS-12700093.text_edition: \n Marti Gradivo quem aput sic legionem VII Claudiam tempore quo ad eradendum nomen saevissimae dominationis missus cum vexillationes Moesiae inferioris voltus hhostium ppublicorum de vexillis et cantabris ultro detrahere nollent auctores improsperi conatus coercerat congressione facta prima in acie constitutus invocaverat Clodius Celsinus signum quod in bello voverat posuit\n
EDCS-12700093.translation: To Mars Gradivus, whom, when the Seventh Claudian Legion was dispatched to erase the name of a most savage tyranny, Clodius Celsinus, having attacked the enemy standards and Cantabrian auxiliaries, who were unwilling to be pulled away from the vexillations of Lower Moesia, coercing the authors of a disastrous attempt, had established himself on the first line in battle, dedicated the standard which he had vowed in war.
EDCS-12700093.summary: This inscription is a dedication of a military standard to Mars Gradivus by Clodius Celsinus, likely a high-ranking officer, commemorating his actions during a campaign to suppress a rebellion, probably in the late Roman Empire.

EDCS-13302402.text_edition: \n Lucius Attius Macro legatus Augusti pro praetore cons(ul) designatus\n
EDCS-13302402.translation: Lucius Attius Macro, imperial legate with praetorian power, consul-designate.
EDCS-13302402.summary: This is a titulature for Lucius Attius Macro, an important Roman official serving as a legate (governor) of a province and designated consul, likely from the 2nd or 3rd century CE.

EDCS-13302534.text_edition: \n Iovi Optimo Maximo pro salute Luci Corneli Latiniani legati Augusti pro praetore Imbrasus libertus votum solvit libens merito\n
EDCS-13302534.translation: To Jupiter Optimus Maximus, for the health of Lucius Cornelius Latinianus, imperial legate with praetorian power, Imbrasus, a freedman, paid his vow willingly and deservedly.
EDCS-13302534.summary: This is a votive inscription to Jupiter Optimus Maximus by Imbrasus, a freedman, for the well-being of his patron, Lucius Cornelius Latinianus, an imperial legate, likely from the 2nd or 3rd century CE.

EDCS-13400042.text_edition: \n Aurelio Muciano veterano domo Thracia qui vixit nnis LXX mensibus X diebus IIII Aurelia Domnina coniux pientissimo faciendum curavit\n
EDCS-13400042.translation: Aurelia Domnina arranged for the erection of this monument for her most devoted husband, Aurelius Mucianus, a veteran from Thrace, who lived 70 years, 10 months, and 4 days.
EDCS-13400042.summary: This is a funerary epitaph for Aurelius Mucianus, a veteran from Thrace, erected by his wife Aurelia Domnina, dating to the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-13400043.text_edition: \n Iovi Optimo Maximo Publius Aelius Carus veteranus legionis II adiutricis piae fidelis Antoninianae pro salute sua suorumque omnium votum solvit libens merito\n
EDCS-13400043.translation: To Jupiter Optimus Maximus, Publius Aelius Carus, a veteran of the Second Adiutrix Legion, pia fidelis Antoniniana, paid his vow willingly and deservedly for his own health and that of all his family.
EDCS-13400043.summary: This is a votive inscription to Jupiter Optimus Maximus by Publius Aelius Carus, a veteran of the Second Adiutrix Legion, for his own health and that of his family, likely from the 2nd or 3rd century CE.

EDCS-13400044.text_edition: \n Iovi Optimo Maximo lustro Aureli Pisonis primi pili legionis II adiutricis Aurelius Donatus mensor miles emeritus legionis eiusdem votum solvit libens merito Severo et Quintiano consulibus\n
EDCS-13400044.translation: To Jupiter Optimus Maximus, Aurelius Donatus, a retired surveyor and soldier of the same legion, paid his vow willingly and deservedly during the censorship of Aurelius Pison, first-rank centurion of the Second Adiutrix Legion, in the consulship of Severus and Quintianus.
EDCS-13400044.summary: This is a votive inscription to Jupiter Optimus Maximus by Aurelius Donatus, a retired soldier, for the purification ('lustrum') of Aurelius Pison, a high-ranking officer, during the consulship of Severus and Quintianus, likely in the 3rd century CE.

EDCS-13400045.text_edition: \n Dis Manibus Caius Aelius Alexander Fabia Ancora sic custos armorum legionis IIII Flaviae felicis stipendiorum XXII vixit nnos XLII Caius Iulius Frugi heres faciendum curavit\n
EDCS-13400045.translation: To the Spirits of the Departed, Caius Aelius Alexander and Fabia Ancora, keepers of the arms of the Fourth Flavian Felix Legion, served for 22 years and lived 42 years; Caius Iulius Frugi, the heir, arranged for this monument to be erected.
EDCS-13400045.summary: This is a funerary inscription for Caius Aelius Alexander and Fabia Ancora, a soldier and his wife who served as keepers of the arms for the Fourth Flavian Felix Legion, erected by their heir, dating to the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-13400046.text_edition: \n Dis Manibus Valerius Proclianus veteranus qui vixit nnos LXX menses X dies X Aurelia Procla coniunx et Flavius Proclianus miles legionis I adiutricis filius una cum matre patri carissimo faciendum curavit\n
EDCS-13400046.translation: To the Spirits of the Departed, Aurelia Procla, his wife, and Flavius Proclianus, a soldier of the First Adiutrix Legion, together with his mother, arranged for this monument to be erected for their dearest father, Valerius Proclianus, a veteran who lived 70 years, 10 months, and 10 days.
EDCS-13400046.summary: This is a funerary epitaph for Valerius Proclianus, a veteran of the Roman army, erected by his wife and son, dating to the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-13400047.text_edition: \n --- ann(orum) V Marcus Saenius Nepotianus hastatus |(centurio) legionis II adiutricis et Flavia Festa parentes filio dulcissimo\n
EDCS-13400047.translation: Marcus Saenius Nepotianus, hastate (officer) of the Second Adiutrix Legion, and Flavia Festa, parents, for their dearest son, who lived 5 years.
EDCS-13400047.summary: This is a funerary epitaph for a young child, erected by his parents Marcus Saenius Nepotianus, an officer in the Second Adiutrix Legion, and Flavia Festa, dating to the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-13400048.text_edition: \n us A primus pilus legionis piae fidelis\n
EDCS-13400048.translation: [...], first-rank centurion of the pious and faithful legion.
EDCS-13400048.summary: This is a fragment of a military inscription mentioning a high-ranking officer (primus pilus) of a legion identified as 'pious and faithful', likely dating to the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-13400049.text_edition: \n Iovi Optimo Maximo et Laribus Militaribus Marcus Iallius Bassus legatus Augusti pro praetore\n
EDCS-13400049.translation: To Jupiter, Best and Greatest, and to the Military Lares, Marcus Iallius Bassus, legate of the emperor, acting as governor.
EDCS-13400049.summary: This is a dedication to Jupiter and the Military Lares by Marcus Iallius Bassus, a Roman legate, likely from the 2nd century AD.

EDCS-13400050.text_edition: \n Iovi Optimo Maximo et dis deabusque Tiberius Haterius Saturninus legatus Auggustorum pro praetore sodalis Augustalis cum Latroniano filio tribuno militum\n
EDCS-13400050.translation: To Jupiter, Best and Greatest, and to the gods and goddesses, Tiberius Haterius Saturninus, legate of the emperors, acting as governor, a member of the sodality of Augustales, with his son Latronianus, a military tribune.
EDCS-13400050.summary: This inscription records a dedication to Jupiter and the gods by Tiberius Haterius Saturninus, a Roman legate, and his son, a military tribune, likely from the late 2nd or early 3rd century AD.

EDCS-13400051.text_edition: \n Iovi Iunoni Minervae Caius Valerius Sabinianus legatus Augusti pro praetore consul designatus et Salviae Luciolae eius\n
EDCS-13400051.translation: To Jupiter, Juno, and Minerva, Gaius Valerius Sabinianus, legate of the emperor, acting as governor, consul-designate, and to his wife Salviae Luciolae.
EDCS-13400051.summary: This is a dedication to the Capitoline Triad by Gaius Valerius Sabinianus, a Roman legate and consul-designate, and his wife, likely from the late 2nd century AD.

EDCS-13400052.text_edition: \n Silvano Silvestri Gaius Titius Platanus Augustalis muncipii Aquincensium votum solvit libens merito\n
EDCS-13400052.translation: To Silvanus of the Woods, Gaius Titius Platanus, an Augustalis of the municipality of Aquincum, fulfilled his vow willingly and deservedly.
EDCS-13400052.summary: This records a votive offering to Silvanus by Gaius Titius Platanus, a local official in Aquincum, likely from the 2nd or 3rd century AD.

EDCS-13600130.text_edition: \n Imperatori Caesari Lucio Septimio Severo Pio Pertinaci Augusto Arabico Adiabenico Parthico maximo divi Marci Aureli divi Commodi fratri divi Antonini Pii nepoti divi Hadriani pronepoti divi Traiani Parthici abnepoti divi Nervae adnepoti imperatori XI tribunicia potestate VI consuli II proconsuli Imperatori Marco Aurelio Antonino Augusto Luci Septimi Severo sic Pii Pertenacis sic Augusti nostri filio divi Antonini nepoti divi Antonini Pii pronepoti divi Hadriani abnepoti divi Traiani Parthici et divi Nervae adnepoti et Publio Septimio Getae Caesari Augusti nostri filio curante Tiberio Claudio Claudidiano legato Auggustorum pro praetore ab Aquinco milia passuum XLIII\n
EDCS-13600130.translation: For the Emperor Caesar Lucius Septimius Severus Pius Pertinax Augustus, Arabicus, Adiabenicus, Parthicus Maximus, brother of the deified Marcus Aurelius, nephew of the deified Antoninus Pius, great-nephew of the deified Hadrian, great-great-nephew of the deified Trajan Parthicus, great-great-great-nephew of the deified Nerva, Emperor (consul XI), holder of tribunician power (VI), consul (II), proconsul; and for Emperor Marcus Aurelius Antoninus Augustus, son of our Emperor Lucius Septimius Severus Pius Pertinax, nephew of the deified Antoninus, great-nephew of the deified Antoninus Pius, great-great-nephew of the deified Hadrian, great-great-great-nephew of the deified Trajan Parthicus and the deified Nerva; and for Publius Septimius Geta Caesar, son of our Emperor: overseen by Tiberius Claudius Claudidianus, legate of the emperors, acting as governor, 43 miles from Aquincum.
EDCS-13600130.summary: This is a milestone inscription recording road construction or repair under the emperors Septimius Severus, Caracalla, and Geta, overseen by the legate Tiberius Claudius Claudidianus, likely from the early 3rd century AD.

EDCS-13700284.text_edition: \n Nemesi Reginae Augustae Aurelius Vindicianus cornicularius praefecti et Marcus Aurelius Vindex Iunior filius eius \n
EDCS-13700284.translation: To Nemesis Regina Augusta, Aurelius Vindicianus, cornicularius (secretary) of the prefect, and his son Marcus Aurelius Vindex the Younger.
EDCS-13700284.summary: This is a dedication to the goddess Nemesis by Aurelius Vindicianus, an official in the prefect's office, and his son, likely from the 3rd century AD.

EDCS-13700286.text_edition: \n Telesphoro sacrum Titus Flavius Priscus veteranus legionis IIII Flaviae ex optione valetudinarii dono dedit\n
EDCS-13700286.translation: Sacred to Telesphorus. Titus Flavius Priscus, a veteran of the Fourth Flavian Legion, from the optio valetudinarii (medical orderly), dedicated this as a gift.
EDCS-13700286.summary: This records a dedication to the healing deity Telesphorus by Titus Flavius Priscus, a veteran and medical orderly from the Fourth Flavian Legion, likely from the 2nd or 3rd century AD.

EDCS-13900031.text_edition: \n Aelio Licinio Iu Singiduno decurioni canabarum qui vixit annis LXX sibi et Aeliae Iuliae filiae et Aelio filio Lupo qui vixit annis\n
EDCS-13900031.translation: For Aelius Licinius Iu Singidun, decurion of the canabae, who lived 70 years, for himself and his daughter Aelia Julia, and his son Aelius Lupus, who lived [years].
EDCS-13900031.summary: This is a funerary dedication for Aelius Licinius Iu Singidun, a decurion of the canabae (settlement for soldiers' families), and his children, likely from the 2nd or 3rd century AD.

EDCS-13900032.text_edition: \n Imperator Caesar Marcus Opellius Severus Macrinus Pius Augustus consul proconsul tribunicia potestate Opellius Diadumenianus nobilissimus Caesar vias et pontes vetustate corruptas restituerunt cura agente Aelio Tricciano legato Auggustorum pro praetore ab Aquinco milia passuum XLV\n
EDCS-13900032.translation: Emperor Caesar Marcus Opellius Severus Macrinus Pius Augustus, consul, proconsul, holder of tribunician power; and the most noble Caesar Opellius Diadumenianus: they restored roads and bridges corrupted by age, under the administration of Aelius Triccianus, legate of the emperors, acting as governor, 45 miles from Aquincum.
EDCS-13900032.summary: This is a milestone inscription recording the restoration of roads and bridges under emperors Macrinus and Diadumenianus, overseen by legate Aelius Triccianus, likely from 217-218 AD.

EDCS-13900033.text_edition: \n Imperatori Caesari Caio Vibio Gallo Pio Felici Augusto pontifici maximo tribunicia potestate II patri patriae et Imperatori Caesari Caio Vibio Afinio Gallo Velldumniano Volusiano Pio Felici Invicto Augusto tribunicia poestate II legio II adiutrix ter pia ter fidelis constans Galliana Volusiana curante Cosinio Felice legato Auggustorum pro praetore ab Aquinco milia passuum \n
EDCS-13900033.translation: For Emperor Caesar Gaius Vibius Gallus Pius Felix Augustus, chief priest, holder of tribunician power (II), father of the country; and for Emperor Caesar Gaius Vibius Afinius Gallus Vell(edumnianus) Volusianus Pius Felix Invictus Augustus, holder of tribunician power (II): the Legio II Adiutrix, three times dutiful, three times faithful, constant, Galliana Volusiana; under the administration of Cosinius Felix, legate of the emperors, acting as governor, [distance] miles from Aquincum.
EDCS-13900033.summary: This milestone inscription records work done by the Legio II Adiutrix under the emperors Trebonianus Gallus and Volusianus, overseen by legate Cosinius Felix, likely from 251-253 AD.

EDCS-13900034.text_edition: \n Iovi Optimo Maximo et Iunoni Reginae pro salute et incolumitate civitatis Eraviscorum Titus Aelius Valens et Publius Aelius Dubitatus IIviri municipii Aquinci\n
EDCS-13900034.translation: To Jupiter Best and Greatest and to Juno Regina, for the safety and well-being of the community of the Eravisci, Titus Aelius Valens and Publius Aelius Dubitatus, the two duoviri of the municipium of Aquincum.
EDCS-13900034.summary: A dedication to Jupiter and Juno by two officials of the municipium of Aquincum, likely from the 2nd or 3rd century CE.

EDCS-14800177.text_edition: \n M Minervae Publius P Certus votum solvit libens merito\n
EDCS-14800177.translation: To Minerva, Publius P. Certus willingly and deservedly paid his vow.
EDCS-14800177.summary: A votive dedication to Minerva by Publius P. Certus, likely a private offering from the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-14800184.summary: A fragmentary inscription mentioning a tribune named Lupicinus, its context and date are uncertain.

EDCS-15000151.inscription_type: carmina, tituli fabricationis, tituli sacri, tituli sepulcrales
EDCS-15000151.text_edition: \n Memoriae Quinto Aelio Apolloni militi cohortis |(miliariae) novae Surorum stipendiorum III vixit nnos XX Aelia Marcia mater filio dulcissimo et Aelia Apollonia soror eius faciendum curaverunt lubrica quassa levis fragilis bona vel mala fallax vita data est homini non certo limite cretae per varios casus tenuato stamine pendes vivito mortalis dumdum dant tibi tempora Parcae seu te rura tenent urbes seu castra vel aequor flores ama Veneris Cereris bona munera carpe et Nysyi larga et pinguia dona Minervae candida vita cole iustissima mente serenus iam puer et iuvenis iam vir et fessus ab annis talis eris tumulo superumque oblitus honores\n
EDCS-15000151.translation: To the memory of Quintus Aelius Apollonius, a soldier of the cohort (miliaria) of the Suri, with three years of service; he lived 20 years. Aelia Marcia, his mother, and Aelia Apollonia, his sister, had this made. Life, slippery, broken, light, fragile, good or bad, is given to man, not by a certain boundary of fate; it hangs by a thin thread through various chances. Live, mortal, while the Fates give you time, whether the countryside, cities, camps, or the sea holds you. Love the flowers of Venus, enjoy the good gifts of Ceres, and the rich and abundant gifts of Nysos (Dionysus) and Minerva. Cultivate a life of bright justice with a serene mind. So you will be, soon a boy, soon a youth, soon a man, and worn out by years, then forgotten in the tomb and of the gods' honors.
EDCS-15000151.summary: A funerary epitaph for Quintus Aelius Apollonius, a soldier who died at 20, inscribed with a philosophical reflection on life's transience by his mother and sister, likely from the 2nd or 3rd century CE.

EDCS-15200180.text_edition: \n Herculi Illyrico Caius Oppius Ingenuus tribunus militum legionis II adiutricis piae fidelis Severianae \n
EDCS-15200180.translation: To Hercules Illyricus, Gaius Oppius Ingenuus, tribune of soldiers of the II Legion Adjutrix Pia Fidelis Severiana.
EDCS-15200180.summary: A dedication to Hercules Illyricus by a military tribune of the II Adjutrix Legion, likely dating to the Severan period (late 2nd to early 3rd century CE).

EDCS-15300221.text_edition: \n Soli Invicto Mithrae Lucius Septimius Victor armorum custos legionis II adiutricis piae fidelis ex viso posuit Praesente et Extricato consulibus\n
EDCS-15300221.translation: To Sol Invictus Mithras, Lucius Septimius Victor, guardian of the arms of the II Legion Adjutrix Pia Fidelis, set this up in fulfillment of a vision, when Praesens and Extricatus were consuls.
EDCS-15300221.summary: A dedication to Sol Invictus Mithras by an armorer of the II Adjutrix Legion, set up in fulfillment of a vision during the consulship of Praesens and Extricatus, likely in the early 3rd century CE.

EDCS-15500115.text_edition: \n Gaius Iulius Viatorinus decurio coloniae Aquincensium aedilicius praefectus collegii centonariorum hydram collegio supra scripto de suo dono dedit Modesto et Probo consulibus\n
