EDCS-15500115.translation: Gaius Julius Viatorinus, decurion of the colony of Aquincum, aedilician, prefect of the guild of fullers, gave the above-mentioned hydra to the guild at his own expense, when Modestus and Probus were consuls.
EDCS-15500115.summary: A dedication of a hydra by Gaius Julius Viatorinus, an official of the colony of Aquincum and leader of the fullers' guild, during the consulship of Modestus and Probus (likely 226 CE).

EDCS-15500116.translation: Hilarus made the gate; may it be prosperous.
EDCS-15500116.summary: A short inscription recording the construction of a gate by Hilarus, likely a private or public work from the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-15700084.text_edition: \n Valentianus signifer legionis II adiutricis piae fidelis Antoninianae domo Mursa donum dedit Imperatore Caesare Antonino Augusto [III] et Comaszonte II consulibus\n
EDCS-15700084.translation: Valentianus, standard-bearer of the II Legion Adjutrix Pia Fidelis Antoniniana, from Mursa, gave this gift in the time of Emperor Caesar Antoninus Augustus [III] and Comasontes II, consuls.
EDCS-15700084.summary: A dedication by Valentianus, a standard-bearer of the II Adjutrix Legion, from Mursa, likely during the reign of Caracalla (early 3rd century CE).

EDCS-15700112.text_edition: \n Immune in rationem valetudinarii legionis II adiutricis\n
EDCS-15700112.translation: For the account of the infirmary of the II Legion Adjutrix.
EDCS-15700112.summary: A record related to the expenses or management of the infirmary of the II Adjutrix Legion, likely from the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-15800369.text_edition: \n Luci-- Vepintania Luci libert-- Sergia Aquinco annorum LXX hic situs est collegiato Salutis Augustae Agrippinenses Transalpini posuer(unt) \n
EDCS-15800369.translation: Lucius Vepintanius, freedman of Sergia Aquinco, aged 70, is buried here; the collegium of the Augusta Agrippinenses Transalpini Salutis erected this.
EDCS-15800369.summary: This is a funerary monument for Lucius Vepintanius, erected by a collegium. It dates to the 2nd or 3rd century CE based on the collegium's likely activity.

EDCS-15900106.text_edition: \n Flavus Blandi filius domo Batavus sic eques alae Frontonianae \n
EDCS-15900106.translation: Flavus, son of Blandus, from the Batavian region, served as a cavalryman in the ala Frontoniana.
EDCS-15900106.summary: This epitaph records Flavus, a cavalryman from the Batavians, who served in the ala Frontoniana, likely dating to the 1st-3rd century CE.

EDCS-15900107.text_edition: \n Deae Vagdaevercusti Marcus Simplicius Quietus tribunus cohortis III Batavorum |(miliariae) equitatae Antoninianae\n
EDCS-15900107.translation: To the goddess Vagdaevercustis, Marcus Simplicius Quietus, tribune of the 3rd cohort of Batavians (miliaria) equitata Antoniniana, dedicated this.
EDCS-15900107.summary: This is a dedication to the goddess Vagdaevercustis by Marcus Simplicius Quietus, a tribune of a Batavian cohort, likely dating to the 2nd or 3rd century CE.

EDCS-15900420.text_edition: \n Aesculapio Tiberius Martius Castrensis medicus legionis II adiutricis sub Quinto Fuficio Cornuto consule designato\n
EDCS-15900420.translation: To Aesculapius, Tiberius Martius Castrensis, physician of the Legio II Adiutrix, dedicated this under Quintus Fuficius Cornutus, consul-designate.
EDCS-15900420.summary: This is a dedication to Aesculapius by Tiberius Martius Castrensis, a physician of Legio II Adiutrix, made during the consulship of Quintus Fuficius Cornutus, likely dating to the late 1st or early 2nd century CE.

EDCS-15900421.text_edition: \n C S Iovi Optimo Maximo sacrum Aurelius Atta votum solvit libens merito\n
EDCS-15900421.translation: Sacred to Jupiter Optimus Maximus. Aurelius Atta willingly fulfilled his vow, deservedly.
EDCS-15900421.summary: This is a votive dedication to Jupiter Optimus Maximus by Aurelius Atta, likely dating to the 2nd or 3rd century CE.

EDCS-15900423.text_edition: \n Iovi Optimo Maximo Silvanus votum solvit libens merito\n
EDCS-15900423.translation: To Jupiter Optimus Maximus, Silvanus willingly fulfilled his vow, deservedly.
EDCS-15900423.summary: This is a votive dedication to Jupiter Optimus Maximus by Silvanus, likely dating to the 2nd or 3rd century CE.

EDCS-15900424.text_edition: \n Aesculapio et Hygiae Augustis sacrum Titus Venusius Titi filius Menenia Aper Praeneste optio valetudinarii votum solvit laetus libens merito V Kalendas Octobres posuit\n
EDCS-15900424.translation: Sacred to Aesculapius and Augusta Hygiae. Titus Venusius Aper, son of Titus, of the Menenia tribe, from Praeneste, optio of the infirmary, happily and deservedly fulfilled his vow. He set this up on the 5th day before the Kalends of October.
EDCS-15900424.summary: This is a votive dedication to Aesculapius and Hygiae by Titus Venusius Aper, an optio of the infirmary, including a specific date in the 2nd or 3rd century CE.

EDCS-15900425.text_edition: \n Asculepio sic et Hygiae Iulius Iulianus votum libens merito solvit\n
EDCS-15900425.translation: To Aesculapius and Hygiae. Julius Julianus willingly fulfilled his vow, deservedly.
EDCS-15900425.summary: This is a votive dedication to Aesculapius and Hygiae by Julius Julianus, likely dating to the 2nd or 3rd century CE.

EDCS-15900427.text_edition: \n Genio |(centuriae) Luci Aebuti Certi primi pili Titus Flavius Flavianus optio eeius sic\n
EDCS-15900427.translation: To the Genius of the century of Lucius Aebutius Certus, primus pilus, Titus Flavius Flavianus, optio of him, [dedicated this].
EDCS-15900427.summary: This is a dedication to the Genius of a century by Titus Flavius Flavianus, an optio, likely dating to the 2nd or 3rd century CE.

EDCS-15900428.text_edition: \n Iovi Optimo Maximo Aelius Quintus Aelius Quintianus fratres milites legionis II adiutricis piae fidelis votum solverunt libentes merito\n
EDCS-15900428.translation: To Jupiter Optimus Maximus. Aelius Quintus and Aelius Quintianus, brothers and soldiers of the Legio II Adiutrix Pia Fidelis, willingly fulfilled their vow, deservedly.
EDCS-15900428.summary: This is a votive dedication to Jupiter Optimus Maximus by two brothers, Aelius Quintus and Aelius Quintianus, soldiers of Legio II Adiutrix, likely dating to the 2nd or 3rd century CE.

EDCS-15900429.text_edition: \n Iovi Optimo Maximo Publius Aelius Tertius cornicen legionis II adiutricis ex voto posuit pro salute sua et suorum votum solvit libens merito Imperatore Maximino et Africano consulibus\n
EDCS-15900429.translation: To Jupiter Optimus Maximus, Publius Aelius Tertius, a horn player of the II Adiutrix Legion, set this up in fulfillment of a vow for the safety of himself and his family, and gladly paid his vow as was fitting, when the emperors were Maximino and Africanus.
EDCS-15900429.summary: A military man, Publius Aelius Tertius, dedicates an offering to Jupiter Optimus Maximus for his and his family's well-being during the consulship of Maximino and Africanus.

EDCS-15900431.text_edition: \n Deae Dianae Licinius <G=C>audentius Papia legionis II adiutricis ex voto posuit \n
EDCS-15900431.translation: To the Goddess Diana, Licinius Gaudentius Papia of the II Adiutrix Legion set this up in fulfillment of a vow.
EDCS-15900431.summary: Licinius Gaudentius Papia, a soldier of the II Adiutrix Legion, dedicates an offering to the Goddess Diana.

EDCS-15900432.text_edition: \n Iovi Optimo Maximo equites singulares Hateri Saturnini legati Auggustorum pro praetore \n
EDCS-15900432.translation: To Jupiter Optimus Maximus, the elite cavalrymen of Haterius Saturninus, governor with the rank of propraetor, dedicated this.
EDCS-15900432.summary: The equites singulares of the governor Haterius Saturninus dedicate an offering to Jupiter Optimus Maximus.

EDCS-15900434.text_edition: \n Iovi Optimo Maximo Libero Patri Aelius Anntianus pro salute suae sic et vexillariorum collegii centonariorum Ulpius Victorinus Aurelius Antoninus Dubius Florentinus Vegetius Septiminus VIBVL ISTATA PHELVM votum solverunt libentes merito\n
EDCS-15900434.translation: To Jupiter Optimus Maximus, Liber Pater, Aelius Antianus, for the safety of himself and of the standard-bearers of the guild of clothiers, Ulpius Victorinus, Aurelius Antoninus, Dubius Florentinus, Vegetius Septiminus, VIBVL ISTATA PHELVM, gladly paid their vow as was fitting.
EDCS-15900434.summary: Members of the guild of clothiers and their standard-bearers dedicate an offering to Jupiter Optimus Maximus and Liber Pater for their well-being.

EDCS-15900435.text_edition: \n Libero Patri et Liberae Iulius Iulianus veteranus ex evokato sic votum solvit libens merito\n
EDCS-15900435.translation: To Liber Pater and Libera, Iulius Iulianus, a veteran and former evocatus, gladly paid his vow as was fitting.
EDCS-15900435.summary: A veteran named Iulius Iulianus dedicates an offering to Liber Pater and Libera.

EDCS-15900438.text_edition: \n Deo Invicto Mithrae Caelius Anicetus cum filio suo votum solvit libens merito\n
EDCS-15900438.translation: To the Unconquered God Mithras, Caelius Anicetus, with his son, gladly paid his vow as was fitting.
EDCS-15900438.summary: Caelius Anicetus and his son dedicate an offering to the god Mithras.

EDCS-15900439.summary: A brief dedication to the 'Mother Rock'.

EDCS-15900441.text_edition: \n cius decurio municipii Aquincensium IIvir iure dicundo praefectus collegii fabrum votum solvit\n
EDCS-15900441.translation: [Name] the decurion of the municipality of Aquincum, twice chief magistrate, prefect of the guild of craftsmen, paid his vow.
EDCS-15900441.summary: An official in the municipality of Aquincum, holding multiple civic and guild positions, dedicates an offering.

EDCS-15900442.text_edition: \n Neptuno sac(rum) Marcus O P MV AI O \n
EDCS-15900442.translation: Sacred to Neptune. Marcus O P MV AI O.
EDCS-15900442.summary: A dedication to Neptune by Marcus, followed by illegible text.

EDCS-15900443.translation: To Silvanus Domestics, in fulfillment of a vow.
EDCS-15900443.summary: A dedication to Silvanus Domestics, likely related to a vow.

EDCS-15900444.text_edition: \n Silvano Domestico sacrum Polyidus Aureli Aniceti votum solvit libens merito\n
EDCS-15900444.translation: Sacred to Silvanus Domesticus. Polyidus Aurelius Anicetus willingly and deservedly fulfilled his vow.
EDCS-15900444.summary: A dedication to the god Silvanus Domesticus by Polyidus Aurelius Anicetus, likely a private vow, dated probably to the 2nd-3rd century CE.

EDCS-15900446.translation: Flavius Abascantus dedicated a vow to Silvanus.
EDCS-15900446.summary: A private dedication to the god Silvanus by Flavius Abascantus, likely a vow fulfillment, probably dating to the 2nd-3rd century CE.

EDCS-15900447.text_edition: \n Silvano Magno Claudius Maximinus pro salute Claudi Probini fili quod voverat votum solvit libens merito III Idus Iunias Perpetuo et Corneliano consulibus\n
EDCS-15900447.translation: Claudius Maximinus fulfilled his vow to Silvanus Magnus for the safety of his son Claudius Probinus, which he had vowed, willingly and deservedly, on the third day before the Ides of June, when Perpetuus and Cornelianus were consuls.
EDCS-15900447.summary: A vow fulfillment by Claudius Maximinus to Silvanus Magnus for his son's health, with a precise date in 202 CE.

EDCS-15900448.text_edition: \n Silvanis sacrum Appius Rosionis votum libens merito\n
EDCS-15900448.translation: Sacred to the Silvani. Appius Rosionis willingly and deservedly dedicated a vow.
EDCS-15900448.summary: A dedication to the gods Silvani by Appius Rosionis, likely a vow fulfillment, probably dating to the 2nd-3rd century CE.

EDCS-15900449.text_edition: \n Soli Deo sacrum Caius Iulius Primus votum solvit libens merito\n
EDCS-15900449.translation: Sacred to the Only God. Gaius Julius Primus willingly and deservedly fulfilled his vow.
EDCS-15900449.summary: A dedication to the god Sol Invictus by Gaius Julius Primus, likely a private vow fulfillment, probably dating to the 2nd-3rd century CE.

EDCS-15900450.text_edition: \n Soli Deo Callistus ex voto votum solvit libens merito\n
EDCS-15900450.translation: Callistus fulfilled his vow to the Only God, as vowed, willingly and deservedly.
EDCS-15900450.summary: A vow fulfillment by Callistus to the god Sol Invictus, likely a private dedication, probably dating to the 2nd-3rd century CE.

EDCS-15900451.text_edition: \n Sulevis sacrum Ulpia Ilurica votum solvit libens merito\n
EDCS-15900451.translation: Sacred to Sulevis. Ulpia Ilurica willingly and deservedly fulfilled her vow.
EDCS-15900451.summary: A dedication to the goddess Sulevis by Ulpia Ilurica, likely a private vow fulfillment, possibly originating from Gaulish religious context, probably dating to the 2nd-3rd century CE.

EDCS-15900453.text_edition: \n eques ala Hispanorum primae Lugius nnorum XL stipendiorum XXII hic situs est Tranco Iorae libertus et Marcus Apisius heredes posuerunt\n
EDCS-15900453.translation: A horseman of the first wing of the Spanish cavalry, aged 40 years, with 22 years of service, is buried here. Tranco Iorae's freedman and Marcus Apisius, his heirs, set this up.
EDCS-15900453.summary: A tombstone for a soldier of the ala Hispanorum, detailing his age and service length, set up by his freedman and heirs, likely dating to the Imperial period.

EDCS-15900454.text_edition: \n Dis Manibus Publio Aelio Publi Favoriano collegium centonariorum\n
EDCS-15900454.translation: To the Divine Manes. The guild of blanket makers [dedicates this] to Publius Aelius Publi Favorianus.
EDCS-15900454.summary: A funerary dedication by the guild of centonarii (cloth/blanket makers) to Publius Aelius Publius Favorianus, likely from the Imperial period.

EDCS-15900455.translation: To the unconquered, happy, triumphant Constantine.
EDCS-15900455.summary: An acclamation or dedication likely to Emperor Constantine, praising his victories and fortune, probably dating to the early 4th century CE.

EDCS-16000052.text_edition: \n Titus Plotius Pamphilus Celeia miles legionis II adiutricis piae fidelis centuria Nimphidiani defunctus Alaxandria sic stipendiorum XV natione Noricus posuit heres Magnius Atticus\n
EDCS-16000052.translation: Titus Plotius Pamphilus from Celeia, a soldier of the II Adjutrix Pia Fidelis legion, in the centuria of Nimphidianus, having died in Alexandria after fifteen years of service, a native of Noricum, erected this; his heir Magnius Atticus placed it.
EDCS-16000052.summary: This is a soldier's tombstone erected by his heir, dating to the period when the II Adjutrix legion was active.

EDCS-16100290.translation: To Concord, for the happy rule of the Augusti. Quintus M. Verus [erected this].
EDCS-16100290.summary: This is a dedication to the deities Concord and the ruling emperors, likely erected by Quintus M. Verus.

EDCS-16100291.text_edition: \n Dis Manibus Lucius Valerius Seutes domo Bessus sic annorum LXXX Marcus Ulpius Philumenus heres ex testamento faciendum curavit ad hoc sepulcrum collegium fabrum contulit |(denarios) \n
EDCS-16100291.translation: To the Spirits of the Underworld. Lucius Valerius Seutes, a native of Bessus, who lived eighty years, had this tomb made by his heir Marcus Ulpius Philumenus according to his will; the guild of craftsmen also contributed to this tomb.
EDCS-16100291.summary: This is a tombstone for Lucius Valerius Seutes, with his heir and a guild contributing to its construction, dating possibly to the 2nd century CE or later.

EDCS-16400366.text_edition: \n Invicto Mithrae Publius Aelius Atta actuarius legionis II adiutricis piae fidelis Antoninianae votum solvit libens merito\n
EDCS-16400366.translation: To the Invincible Mithras, Publius Aelius Atta, an actuary of the II Adjutrix Pia Fidelis Antoniniana legion, willingly fulfilled his vow according to his deserts.
EDCS-16400366.summary: This is a votive inscription dedicated to the god Mithras by Publius Aelius Atta, a soldier of the II Adjutrix legion, likely from the Antonine period.

EDCS-16400368.text_edition: \n Dis Manibus Aeliae Secundinae filiae Aelii Secundini veterani ex beneficiario consularis quae vixit annis XXIIII diebus XXX et II Aelius Caesianus beneficiarius consularis coniugi dulcissimae faciendum curavit\n
EDCS-16400368.translation: To the Spirits of the Underworld. Aelia Secundina, daughter of Aelius Secundinus, a veteran and former beneficiary of a consul, who lived twenty-four years and thirty-two days, had this made by Aelius Caesianus, a beneficiary of a consul, for his most beloved wife.
EDCS-16400368.summary: This is a tombstone for Aelia Secundina, erected by her husband, a military official, and provides her lifespan and parentage.

EDCS-16400370.text_edition: \n Iovi Dulcheno sic Publius Aelius Lucilius |(centurio) cohortis I Alpinorum peditatae\n
EDCS-16400370.translation: To Jove Dulchenus, Publius Aelius Lucilius, a centurion of the I Cohort of Alpine Infantry, [dedicates this].
EDCS-16400370.summary: This is a dedication to the god Jove Dulchenus by Publius Aelius Lucilius, a centurion in an Alpine infantry cohort.

EDCS-16400404.text_edition: \n Deo Optimo Maximo Aelius Dasius veteranus v<o>tum libens solvit\n
EDCS-16400404.translation: To the Best and Greatest God, Aelius Dasius, a veteran, willingly fulfilled his vow.
EDCS-16400404.summary: This is a votive inscription to Jupiter Optimus Maximus by Aelius Dasius, a veteran.

EDCS-16700131.text_edition: \n Imperatori Caesari divi Septimi Severi Arabici Adiabenici Parthici maximi Britannici maximi fi<l=I>(io) divi Marci Antonini Pii Germanici Sarmatici nepoti divi Antonini Pii pronepoti divi Hadriani abnepoti divi Traiani Parthici et divi Nervae adnepoti Marco Aurelio Antonino Pio \n
EDCS-16700131.translation: To the Emperor Caesar, son of the deified Septimius Severus Arabicus Adiabenicus Parthicus Maximus Britannicus Maximus, grandson of the deified Marcus Antoninus Pius Germanicus Sarmaticus, great-grandson of the deified Antoninus Pius, great-great-grandson of the deified Hadrian, great-great-great-grandson of the deified Trajan Parthicus and the deified Nerva, Marcus Aurelius Antoninus Pius.
EDCS-16700131.summary: This is a lengthy titulature for Emperor Marcus Aurelius Antoninus Pius (likely Caracalla), listing his imperial ancestry and titles.

EDCS-16800457.text_edition: \n Iustinianus sesquiplicarius ex voto posuit Liibero <l=L>(aetus) libens merito \n
EDCS-16800457.translation: Iustinianus, a sesquiplicarius, erected this by vow, willingly and according to his deserts, to Liber Pater.
EDCS-16800457.summary: This is a votive inscription dedicated to Liber Pater by Iustinianus, a soldier of sesquiplicarius rank.

EDCS-17300082.text_edition: \n Dianae sacrum Caius Pompeius Crispinus votum solvit libens merito\n
EDCS-17300082.translation: Sacred to Diana. Caius Pompeius Crispinus willingly fulfilled his vow according to his deserts.
EDCS-17300082.summary: This is a votive inscription dedicated to the goddess Diana by Caius Pompeius Crispinus.

EDCS-17300083.text_edition: \n Apollini conservatori Caius Calvisius Faustinianus tribunus militum legionis IIII Flaviae votum sovit libens merito\n
EDCS-17300083.translation: To Apollo the preserver, Gaius Calvisius Faustinianus, military tribune of the Fourth Legion Flavia, willingly and deservedly fulfilled his vow.
EDCS-17300083.summary: This is a dedication to Apollo by a military tribune of the Fourth Legion Flavia.

EDCS-17300084.text_edition: \n Dianae conservatrici Caius Calvisius Faustinianus tribunus militum votum solvit libens merito\n
EDCS-17300084.translation: To Diana the preserver, Gaius Calvisius Faustinianus, military tribune, willingly and deservedly fulfilled his vow.
EDCS-17300084.summary: This is a dedication to Diana by a military tribune, likely the same as EDCS-17300083.

EDCS-17300087.text_edition: \n Dis Manibus Caio I<ul=AE>io V<erec=AIISO>undo <c=G>(ustodi) armorum legionis II adiutricis stipendiorum vixit annis I \n
EDCS-17300087.translation: To the Spirits of the Underworld, for Gaius Iulius Verecundus, guardian of the arms of the Second Legion Adiutrix, who lived 1 year.
EDCS-17300087.summary: This is a tombstone inscription for a young boy, Gaius Iulius Verecundus, associated with the Second Legion Adiutrix.

EDCS-17300088.summary: This is a very brief inscription, likely a name on a tomb or monument.

EDCS-17300089.text_edition: \n Dis Manibus Aelio Sabiniano beneficiario consularis legionis II adiutricis stipendiorum XXIIII vixit annos XLV Aelio Sabino patri eius et Aeliae Sabinillae filiae eius Aelius Donatus libertus patronis dignissimis faciendum curavit\n
EDCS-17300089.translation: To the Spirits of the Underworld, Aelius Donatus, freedman, had this made for his worthy patrons, Aelius Sabinianus, a beneficiarius of the consul of the Second Legion Adiutrix, with 24 years of service, who lived 45 years, and for his father Aelius Sabinus and his daughter Aelia Sabinilla.
EDCS-17300089.summary: This is a funerary monument erected by a freedman for his patrons, a soldier in the Second Legion Adiutrix and his family, indicating the soldier lived to 45 and served for 24 years.

EDCS-17300090.text_edition: \n Titus Mercasius Hermes sibi vivos sic et coniugi fecit\n
EDCS-17300090.translation: Titus Mercasius Hermes made this for himself and his wife while alive.
EDCS-17300090.summary: This is a funerary inscription recording a man, Titus Mercasius Hermes, who prepared his tomb in advance for himself and his wife.

EDCS-17300093.text_edition: \n Marcus Aurelius Reditus beneficiarius legati legionis II adiutricis piae fidelis Severianae votum solvit libens merito suscepta fide ex orco\n
EDCS-17300093.translation: To Marcus Aurelius Reditus, beneficiarius of the legate of the Second Legion Adiutrix Pia Fidelis Severiana, willingly and deservedly fulfilled his vow, having undertaken his duty with faith from the underworld.
EDCS-17300093.summary: This is a votive inscription by Marcus Aurelius Reditus, a beneficiarius of the Second Legion Adiutrix, expressing gratitude for a vow fulfilled, possibly related to a near-death experience.

EDCS-17300094.translation: Basia, daughter of Avvius, a rampart guard.
EDCS-17300094.summary: This is an inscription identifying Basia, daughter of Avvius, as a 'vallista', likely a female guard of a rampart.

EDCS-17300097.text_edition: \n Iovi Optimo Maximo sacrum Lulo libens laetus merito A\n
EDCS-17300097.translation: Sacred to Jupiter Best and Greatest. Lulo willingly, happily, and deservedly.
EDCS-17300097.summary: This is a dedication to Jupiter Optimus Maximus by an individual named Lulo.

EDCS-17300099.text_edition: \n Comatimara Quutai filia nnorum XV posuit titulum memoriae pater\n
EDCS-17300099.translation: Comatimara, daughter of Quutas, 15 years old, placed this memorial tablet, her father.
EDCS-17300099.summary: This is a tombstone inscription for Comatimara, a 15-year-old girl, erected by her father.

EDCS-17300100.text_edition: \n Dis Manibus <L=I>ucio Septimio <L=I>upiano annorum sic I et Lucio Septimio Caesiano annorum sic I faciendum curavit Septimius Cel<e=F>r sexvirum coloniae Aquincensium Aurelia Caensa parentes filiis caris <f=I>(ecerunt) Praesenti sic et Extricado sic consulibus \n
EDCS-17300100.translation: To the Divine Spirits. Septimius Celebratus, a member of the six-man college of Aquincense, and Aurelia Caensa, parents for their dear sons Lucius Septimius Iupianus, aged one year, and Lucius Septimius Caesianus, aged one year, saw to the making of this monument, [dedicated] in the consulship of Praesens and Extricatus.
EDCS-17300100.summary: A funerary monument dedicated by parents to their young sons, likely from the Roman colony of Aquincense.

EDCS-17300101.text_edition: \n Iovi Optimo Maximo Aelii Marinianus et \n
EDCS-17300101.translation: To Jupiter Optimus Maximus. Aelius Marinianus [dedicated this].
EDCS-17300101.summary: A dedication to Jupiter Optimus Maximus by Aelius Marinianus, likely a private offering.

EDCS-17300102.text_edition: \n Nymphis perennibus Marcus Valerius Karus decurio coloniae Aquincensium IIvir votum solvit libens merito\n
EDCS-17300102.translation: To the Everlasting Nymphs, Marcus Valerius Karus, a decurion of the colony of Aquincense and a former duovir, gladly and deservedly fulfilled his vow.
EDCS-17300102.summary: A votive dedication to the Nymphs by Marcus Valerius Karus, a local magistrate of Aquincense.

EDCS-17300105.text_edition: \n Longinianus miles legionis II adiutricis piae fidelis et Iulia Spes coniux aram posuerunt pro pietate sua et suorum suo arbitratu fecit \n
EDCS-17300105.translation: Longinianus, a soldier of the Second Adiutrix Legion, Pious and Faithful, and his wife Iulia Spes erected this altar for their piety and that of their family, and he made it according to his own will.
EDCS-17300105.summary: An altar dedicated by a soldier and his wife, likely to a deity, for their piety and well-being.

EDCS-17300106.text_edition: \n Iovi Optimo Maximo voverunt in espeditione sic Germica sic Septimius Quintianus et Aurelius <G=C>entilis votum solverunt libentes merito \n
EDCS-17300106.translation: To Jupiter Optimus Maximus. In the Germanic expedition, Septimius Quintianus and Aurelius Gentilis vowed and gladly and deservedly fulfilled their vow.
EDCS-17300106.summary: A votive offering to Jupiter Optimus Maximus by two individuals, made after a Germanic campaign.

EDCS-17300107.text_edition: \n Dis Manibus Aurelia Castorina qua<e=I> vixsit sic annis XLII mensibus tribus diebus III Iulius Iuventianus \n
EDCS-17300107.translation: To the Divine Spirits. Aurelia Castorina, who lived 42 years, 3 months, and 3 days, [this monument was erected by] Iulius Iuventianus.
EDCS-17300107.summary: A funerary inscription for Aurelia Castorina, detailing her age and erected by Iulius Iuventianus.

EDCS-17300110.text_edition: \n Deo Invicto Mithrae Marcus Aurelius Frontinianus Marcus Aurelius Fronto milites legionis II adiutricis pro salute sua et suorum templum constituerunt\n
EDCS-17300110.translation: To the Unconquered God Mithras, Marcus Aurelius Frontinianus and Marcus Aurelius Fronto, soldiers of the Second Adiutrix Legion, established this temple for their own safety and that of their families.
EDCS-17300110.summary: The establishment of a temple to Mithras by two soldiers of the Second Adiutrix Legion for their welfare.

EDCS-17300111.translation: To Silvanus Anticestus, Lucius Claudius [dedicated this].
EDCS-17300111.summary: A dedication to the god Silvanus Anticestus by Lucius Claudius.

EDCS-17300112.text_edition: \n Soli Socio Marcus Aurelius Frontinianus et Marcus Aurelius Fronto milites legionis II adiutricis fratres templum constituerunt Antonino IIII consule\n
EDCS-17300112.translation: To Sol the Companion, Marcus Aurelius Frontinianus and Marcus Aurelius Fronto, soldiers of the Second Adiutrix Legion, brothers, established this temple in the consulship of Antoninus for the fourth time.
EDCS-17300112.summary: Two brothers and soldiers of the Second Adiutrix Legion dedicated a temple to Sol Socio during the consulship of Antoninus.

EDCS-17300114.text_edition: \n Iovi Optimo Maximo pro salute ddominorum nnostrorum Auggustorum et Cai Iuli Castini legati Auggustorum pro praetore equites singulares curam agente Aurelio Victorino |(centurione) legionis II adiutricis administrante Aurelio Bito decurione Aelius Florianus Aurelius Maturus subcuratores ex voto\n
EDCS-17300114.translation: To Jupiter Optimus Maximus, for the safety of our lords the Augusti and of Caius Iulius Castinus, legate of the Augusti, pro-praetor, the *equites singulares* under the supervision of Aurelius Victorinus, a centurion of the Second Adiutrix Legion, and the administration of Aurelius Bito, a decurion, Aelius Florianus and Aurelius Maturus, supervisors, [fulfilled their vow] according to their vow.
EDCS-17300114.summary: A dedication to Jupiter Optimus Maximus by cavalrymen and their supervisors for the welfare of the emperors and a legate, likely from the Second Adiutrix Legion.

EDCS-17500070.text_edition: \n Dis Manibus Aurelius Respectus veteranus legionis II adiutricis piae fidelis missus honesta missione et Aureliae Ianuariae coniugi eius et Aureliis Respectiano et Supero filiis et Aureliae Superae filiae eius vivi sibi posuerunt et Septimio Ianuario quondam patri et Septimiae Quintae quondam matri et Septimio Martino quondam qui militavit in legione II supra scripta pia fideli an<n=I>um I et Septimio Dubitato quondam qui militavit in legione II supra scripta pia fideli annos III et Septimio Quintiano \n
EDCS-17500070.translation: To the Divine Manes. Aurelius Respectus, a veteran of the Second Adjutant Legion, honourably discharged, and Aurelia Ianuaria, his wife, and their sons Aurelius Respectianus and Aurelius Superus, and their daughter Aurelia Supera, erected this for themselves while alive, and also for their deceased father Septimius Ianuarius, and their deceased mother Septimia Quinta, and for Septimius Martinus, who served in the aforementioned Second Adjutant Legion, for one year, and for Septimius Dubitatus, who served in the aforementioned Second Adjutant Legion, for three years, and for Septimius Quintianus.
EDCS-17500070.summary: This tombstone was erected by Aurelius Respectus, a veteran of the Second Adjutant Legion, and his family for themselves and several deceased relatives, including soldiers who served in the same legion.

EDCS-17500071.text_edition: \n Dis Manibus Ulpiae Valentinae quae vixit annos XVIII dies Ulpius Valentinus pater filiae carissimae\n
EDCS-17500071.translation: To the Divine Manes. Ulpia Valentina, who lived 18 years. Ulpius Valentinus, her father, for his dearest daughter.
EDCS-17500071.summary: This tombstone records the death of Ulpia Valentina at the age of 18, dedicated by her father.

EDCS-17500073.text_edition: \n Atressa Vindonis filia nnorum XXX et Proculus Busionis nnorum III hic siti pater Busio titulum memoriae posuit\n
EDCS-17500073.translation: Atressa, daughter of Vindos, aged 30, and Proculus, son of Busion, aged 3. Here they lie. The father, Busio, erected this memorial.
EDCS-17500073.summary: This monument commemorates Atressa and Proculus, erected by their father Busio.

EDCS-17500074.text_edition: \n Dis Manibus Marco Granio Dato veterano legionis II adiutricis domo Africa Sufetula Granii Martinus et Felix liberti vivo patrono optimo pecunia sua titulum faciendum curaverunt\n
EDCS-17500074.translation: To the Divine Manes. For Marcus Granius Datum, a veteran of the Second Adjutant Legion, from Africa, from Sufetula. Granii Martinus and Felix, freedmen, had this monument made at their own expense for their living, excellent patron.
EDCS-17500074.summary: Freedmen Martinus and Felix dedicated this monument to their former patron, Marcus Granius Datum, a veteran of the Second Adjutant Legion.

EDCS-17500075.text_edition: \n Verondagus Vervici filius annorum cuadraginta sic et Verbacius Verondagi filius annorum trium\n
EDCS-17500075.translation: Verondagus, son of Vervicus, aged forty, and Verbacius, son of Verondagus, aged three.
EDCS-17500075.summary: This inscription records the names and ages of Verondagus and Verbacius, likely a father and son.

EDCS-17500076.text_edition: \n Viator annorum Vibius annorum Silanus annorum II Severus nnni I Paulini principis fili hic siti sunt pater titulum memoriae posuit\n
EDCS-17500076.translation: Viator, aged [?]; Vibius, aged [?]; Silanus, aged 2; Severus, aged 1. Sons of Paulinus the principal. Here they lie. The father erected this memorial.
EDCS-17500076.summary: This tombstone, erected by their father Paulinus, records the deaths of his young sons Viator, Vibius, Silanus, and Severus.

EDCS-17500077.text_edition: \n Iovi Optimo Maximo Iunoni Baebius Draco signifer legionis II adiutricis votum solvit libens merito\n
EDCS-17500077.translation: To Jupiter Optimus Maximus and Juno. Baebius Draco, standard-bearer of the Second Adjutant Legion, fulfilled his vow willingly and deservedly.
EDCS-17500077.summary: Baebius Draco, a standard-bearer in the Second Adjutant Legion, dedicated this offering to Jupiter and Juno, fulfilling a vow.

EDCS-17500078.text_edition: \n Libero Patri sacrum Marcus Ulpius Quadratus decurio municipii Aquinci quinquennalis votum solvit libens merito\n
EDCS-17500078.translation: Sacred to Liber Pater. Marcus Ulpius Quadratus, decurion and quinquennial magistrate of the municipality of Aquincum, fulfilled his vow willingly and deservedly.
EDCS-17500078.summary: Marcus Ulpius Quadratus, a decurion and quinquennial magistrate from Aquincum, dedicated this offering to Liber Pater.

EDCS-17500079.text_edition: \n Pro salute Imperatoris Caesaris Luci P(ubli) Septimi Severi Pertinacis Augusti Pii consulis II patris patriae et Marci Aureli Antonini Caesaris Tiberius Claudius Claudianus legatus Augusti pro praetore praesidium vetustate collapsum mutato loco manu militum restitui iussit\n
EDCS-17500079.translation: For the safety of the Emperor Caesar Lucius Septimius Severus Pertinax Augustus Pius, consul for the second time, father of the country, and of Marcus Aurelius Antoninus Caesar. Tiberius Claudius Claudianus, imperial legate with praetorian power, ordered the fortification, which had collapsed from age, to be rebuilt in a new location by the labour of soldiers.
EDCS-17500079.summary: The imperial legate Tiberius Claudius Claudianus ordered the rebuilding of a collapsed fortification, dedicated to the safety of Emperors Septimius Severus and Marcus Aurelius Antoninus.

EDCS-17500080.text_edition: \n Deo Soli Invicto Mithrae Septimius Messorinus |(centurio) ordinarius legionis II adiutricis votum solvit libens merito\n
EDCS-17500080.translation: To the unconquered god Mithras Sol. Septimius Messorinus, ordinary centurion of the Second Adjutant Legion, fulfilled his vow willingly and deservedly.
EDCS-17500080.summary: Septimius Messorinus, a centurion in the Second Adjutant Legion, dedicated this offering to the god Sol Invictus Mithras.

EDCS-17500081.text_edition: \n Dis Manibus Ulpiae Antonillae quondam Ulpi Candidiani |(centurionis) filiae Titus Aelius Verinus decurio coloniae Aquincensium flamem sic dumviralis sic sacerdos Urbis Romae coniugi pientissimae quae vixit annis XXIIII\n
EDCS-17500081.translation: To the departed spirits. Titus Aelius Verinus, decurion of the colony of Aquincum, former flamensic and former duovir of the city of Rome, erected this for his most dutiful wife, Ulpia Antonilla, formerly the daughter of Ulpius Candidianus the centurion, who lived 24 years.
EDCS-17500081.summary: This is a tombstone for Ulpia Antonilla, set up by her husband Titus Aelius Verinus, a local magistrate in Aquincum, dated likely to the 2nd-3rd century CE.

EDCS-17500082.text_edition: \n Libero Patri et Liberae pro salute Claudi Marc<e=I>lli et Iuli Marcelli votum solvit libens merito \n
EDCS-17500082.translation: Freely and deservedly fulfilled the vow to Father Liber and Libera for the health of Claudius Marcellus and Iulius Marcellus.
EDCS-17500082.summary: This is a votive inscription dedicated to Liber Pater and Libera, likely by a patron or priest, for the health of Claudius Marcellus and Iulius Marcellus, probably dating from the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-17500083.text_edition: \n Dis Inferis Manibus Aurelio Vero memoria sic posuit coniux Audentia e<g=O>erunt coniu<g=C>es annos I vixsit sic annos XXXV \n
EDCS-17500083.translation: To the departed spirits of the underworld. His wife Audentia placed this memorial for Aurelius Verus, who lived 35 years and was her husband for 1 year.
EDCS-17500083.summary: This is a tombstone for Aurelius Verus, erected by his wife Audentia, who notes their short marriage and his age at death, likely dating from the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-17500084.text_edition: \n Caius Iulius Ma<g=C>imarus annorum XXXX Klaudia Namusonis filia Ressona annorum <L=I>II hic siti sunt Caius Iulius Ma<g=C>io et Caius Iulius Comatumarus filii titulum memoriae posuerunt \n
EDCS-17500084.translation: Caius Julius Maximarus, aged 40, and Klaudia Ressona, daughter of Namuso, aged 72, are buried here. Caius Julius Magio and Caius Julius Comatumarus, their sons, placed this memorial.
EDCS-17500084.summary: This is a tombstone recording the burial of Caius Julius Maximarus and Klaudia Ressona, erected by their sons, dating from the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-17500085.text_edition: \n An<n=T>amato Seiam<i=T> filio annorum tri<gi>ntha sic \n
EDCS-17500085.translation: To Annamatus, son of Seius, aged thirty.
EDCS-17500085.summary: This is a tombstone fragment recording the name and age of Annamatus, son of Seius, likely dating from the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-17500086.text_edition: \n Dis Manibus Septimiae Valentinae quondam filiae Septimiae Probes quaee vixit nnos fecerunt Septimii et \n
EDCS-17500086.translation: To the departed spirits of Septimia Valentina, formerly the daughter of Septimia Proba. She lived [X] years. Septimius and [others] made [this].
EDCS-17500086.summary: This is a fragmentary tombstone for Septimia Valentina, daughter of Septimia Proba, erected by Septimius, likely dating from the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-17500087.text_edition: \n Silvano Silvestri Titus Caecilius Magnio ex voto solvit libens merito\n
EDCS-17500087.translation: To Silvanus the Forest God. Titus Caecilius Magnio fulfilled his vow freely and deservedly.
EDCS-17500087.summary: This is a votive inscription dedicated to Silvanus, a rural deity, by Titus Caecilius Magnio, probably dating from the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-17500088.text_edition: \n Dis Manibus Rituris Veladati filius nnorum LX hic situs est Vercombera Scupi filia uxor? an(norum) L hic sita est Ved fili-- posuit\n
EDCS-17500088.translation: To the departed spirits. Ritur is buried here, son of Velada, aged 60. Vercombera, daughter of Scupus, wife? of Ved, aged 50, is buried here. His son placed [this].
EDCS-17500088.summary: This is a tombstone for Ritur and his wife Vercombera, detailing their ages and parentage, erected by a son, likely dating from the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-17500089.text_edition: \n Flavius Iovinus ex praeposito militum Histricorum et Flavius Paulus biarcus pater et filius domum a fundamentis iusserunt fabricari\n
EDCS-17500089.translation: Flavius Iovinus, formerly commander of the Histrian troops, and Flavius Paulus, his father, a biarcus, ordered this house to be built from the foundations.
EDCS-17500089.summary: This is a building inscription commissioned by Flavius Iovinus and his father Flavius Paulus, likely dating from the Late Roman period.

EDCS-17500091.text_edition: \n Annamatus Tripponis filius nnorum LXXX hic situs est et Basiae Bageetonis filia coniugi vivae fili \n
EDCS-17500091.translation: Annamatus, son of Trippon, aged 80, is buried here. And his son [erected this] for his wife Basia, daughter of Bageeto, while she was still alive.
EDCS-17500091.summary: This is a tombstone for Annamatus and his wife Basia, erected by their son, likely dating from the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-17500092.text_edition: \n Atressus Avavilli filius nnorum LXXX hic situs est Ulpia Coimona patri pientissimo\n
EDCS-17500092.translation: Atressus, son of Avavillus, aged 80, lies here. Ulpia Coimona placed this for her most devoted father.
EDCS-17500092.summary: This is a funerary epitaph for Atressus, who died at the age of 80, set up by his daughter Ulpia Coimona.

EDCS-17500093.text_edition: \n Pr<ae=EN>sens Annamati filius nnorum VII hic situs est pater filio titulum memoriae posuit \n
EDCS-17500093.translation: Praesens, son of Annamatus, aged 7, lies here. His father placed this monument for his son.
EDCS-17500093.summary: This is a funerary epitaph for Praesens, who died at the age of 7, set up by his father.

EDCS-17500094.text_edition: \n Marcus Ulpius Provincialis annorum L hic situs est Ulpia Mattuia coniugi pientissimo et sibi viva posuit\n
EDCS-17500094.translation: Marcus Ulpius Provincialis, aged 50, lies here. Ulpia Mattuia placed this for her most devoted husband and for herself while she was alive.
EDCS-17500094.summary: This is a funerary epitaph for Marcus Ulpius Provincialis, who died at the age of 50, set up by his wife Ulpia Mattuia.

EDCS-17500095.text_edition: \n Dis Manibus Memoriae quondam Publi Aeli Firmini qui vixit nnos VIII me<n>sibus VII diebus XV Publius Aelius Firminianus veteranus legionis II adiutricis pater et Aelia Domitina mater filio dulcissimo faciendum curaverunt\n
EDCS-17500095.translation: To the spirits of the departed. For the memory of Publius Aelius Firminus, who lived 8 years, 7 months, and 15 days. Publius Aelius Firminianus, a veteran of the II Adjutrix legion, and Aelia Domitina, his parents, had this made for their sweetest son.
EDCS-17500095.summary: This is a funerary epitaph for Publius Aelius Firminus, who died at the age of 8, erected by his veteran father and mother.

EDCS-17500096.text_edition: \n Publius Aelius M<a=E>x<im=LLI>us decurio municipii Publius Aelius \n
EDCS-17500096.translation: Publius Aelius Maximus, decurion of the town, Publius Aelius
EDCS-17500096.summary: This is an incomplete inscription mentioning Publius Aelius Maximus, a decurion of a municipium.

EDCS-17500097.summary: This is an incomplete inscription mentioning Flavianus, son of Trippon.

EDCS-17500100.text_edition: \n Dis Manibus Publio Aelio <F=E>rontoni decurioni coloniae Aquincensium flaminico qui vixit annis LIII et Publio Aelio Vindici filio qui vixit annis I <e=I>t Aeliae V<i>c<t>orinae filiae quae vixit nnis VIII et Publio Aelio Inpetrato filio qui vixit nnis VI cui vita parva mors valde citata fuit quem flentes doleunt miserique parentes Septimia Decorata coniugi pientissimo et filiis communibus et Aelii Inpetratus et Decoratus \n
EDCS-17500100.translation: To the spirits of the departed. For Publius Aelius Fronto, decurion of the colony of Aquincensium and priest, who lived 53 years; and for Publius Aelius Vindicus, son, who lived 1 year; and Aelia Victorina, daughter, who lived 8 years; and Publius Aelius Impetratus, son, who lived 6 years, for whom death came very quickly in their young life. His miserable parents, Septimia Decorata for her most devoted husband and their common children, and Aelius Impetratus and Decoratus mourn them weeping.
EDCS-17500100.summary: This is a funerary epitaph for Publius Aelius Fronto, his wife Septimia Decorata, and three young children, recording their ages at death and set up by the surviving parents.

EDCS-17500101.text_edition: \n Meitima Casamonis filia annorum XV titulum posuit pater filiae pientissimae\n
EDCS-17500101.translation: Meitima, daughter of Casamon, aged 15, placed this monument for her most devoted daughter.
EDCS-17500101.summary: This is a funerary epitaph for Meitima, who died at the age of 15, set up by her father.

EDCS-17500102.text_edition: \n Dis Manibus Septimio Constantino qui vixit annos XVI Septimiae Constantinae quae vixit annos XXXVI matri eiius Septimius Vibius avunculus et heres faciendum curavit ex testamento volete o anpli sic sorori bsene merenti posuit opto sit tibi terra levis\n
EDCS-17500102.translation: To the spirits of the departed. For Septimius Constantinus, who lived 16 years, and Septimia Constantina, his mother, who lived 36 years. Septimius Vibius, his uncle and heir, had this made by will. He wished that the earth be light upon you, sister, well deserving.
EDCS-17500102.summary: This is a funerary epitaph for Septimius Constantinus (16) and his mother Septimia Constantina (36), set up by his uncle and heir.

EDCS-17600045.text_edition: \n Ursulus Dis Manibus Aelio Sereno militi cohortis I Ulpiae Pannoniorum stipendiorum VII vixit nnos XXVIII et Aelio Sereniano militi cohortis II Alpinorum stipendiorum X vixit nnos XXV et Aeliae Concordiae vixit nnos XXI \n
EDCS-17600045.translation: To the spirits of the departed. For Aelius Serenus, soldier of the I Ulpia Pannoniorum cohort, 7 years of service, who lived 28 years; and for Aelius Serenianus, soldier of the II Alpinorum cohort, 10 years of service, who lived 25 years; and for Aelia Concordia, who lived 21 years.
EDCS-17600045.summary: This is a funerary epitaph for three individuals, including two soldiers from different cohorts and Aelia Concordia, recording their ages and military service where applicable.

EDCS-17600046.text_edition: \n Iovi Optimo Maximo Cocceius Senecio decurio alae I Thracum ob natalem Marci Coccei Senecae fili votum solvit libens merito\n
EDCS-17600046.translation: To Jupiter Best and Greatest, Cocceius Senecio, decurion of the I Thracum cavalry wing, happily and deservedly fulfilled his vow on the birthday of his son Marcus Cocceius Senecio.
EDCS-17600046.summary: A votive inscription dedicated to Jupiter by a decurion of the I Thracum cavalry wing to celebrate his son's birthday, likely from the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-17600047.text_edition: \n Iovi Optimo Maximo Ulpius Optimus veteranus legionis II adiutricis votum solvit libens merito\n
EDCS-17600047.translation: To Jupiter Best and Greatest, Ulpius Optimus, a veteran of the II Adjutrix legion, happily and deservedly fulfilled his vow.
EDCS-17600047.summary: A votive inscription to Jupiter by a veteran of the II Adjutrix legion, likely from the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-17600050.text_edition: \n Dis Manibus Ave Basse Caio Licinio Basso annorum XXI mensium II dierum IIII Caius Licinius Mansuetus \n
EDCS-17600050.translation: To the Spirits of the Dead. Ave Basse, daughter of Caius Licinius Bassus, aged 21 years, 2 months, and 4 days. Caius Licinius Mansuetus [erected this].
EDCS-17600050.summary: A funerary epitaph for Ave Basse, aged 21 years, 2 months, and 4 days, erected by Caius Licinius Mansuetus, likely from the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-17600051.text_edition: \n Iulia Matrona Acuilaes sic filia annorum hic sita est \n
EDCS-17600051.translation: Iulia Matrona Acuilaes, daughter of Acuilaes, lies here.
EDCS-17600051.summary: A funerary epitaph for Iulia Matrona Acuilaes, daughter of Acuilaes, likely from the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-17600052.text_edition: \n Dis Manibus Vibius Respectus miles cohortis <I=T> Alpinorum annorum XXV Oxetia Vervic<i=L> mater annorum LX \n
EDCS-17600052.translation: To the Spirits of the Dead. Vibius Respectus, a soldier of the I Alpini cohort, aged 25. Oxetia Vervic<i=L> [Vervicii?], mother, aged 60.
EDCS-17600052.summary: A funerary epitaph for Vibius Respectus, a soldier aged 25, and his mother Oxetia Vervic<i=L>, aged 60, likely from the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-17600053.text_edition: \n Libero Patri et Liberae Iulia Ingenua ex voto posuit libens merito\n
EDCS-17600053.translation: To Liber Pater and Libera, Iulia Ingenua erected this according to her vow, happily and deservedly.
EDCS-17600053.summary: A votive inscription dedicated to Liber Pater and Libera by Iulia Ingenua, likely from the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-17600054.text_edition: \n Aveta Adnamati filia annorum LI hic sita est Marcus Ulpius Cassius matri titulum memoriae posuit\n
EDCS-17600054.translation: Aveta, daughter of Adnamatus, aged 51, lies here. Marcus Ulpius Cassius erected this tombstone in memory of his mother.
EDCS-17600054.summary: A funerary epitaph for Aveta, aged 51, erected by her son Marcus Ulpius Cassius, likely from the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-17600055.text_edition: \n Dianae sacrum Marcus Aurelius Constantinus veteranus ex praetorio votum solvit libens merito PRETO Au<r=A>(elius) Constanti<n=M>us impens<a=E> sua posuit \n
EDCS-17600055.translation: Sacred to Diana. Marcus Aurelius Constantinus, a veteran from the Praetorian Guard, happily and deservedly fulfilled his vow. PRETO Au<r=A>(elius) Constanti<n=M>us placed this at his own expense.
EDCS-17600055.summary: A votive inscription to Diana by a veteran of the Praetorian Guard, Marcus Aurelius Constantinus, with a subsequent addition by someone named PRETO Au<r=A>(elius) Constanti<n=M>us, likely from the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-17600057.text_edition: \n Te amo XXX Adnama Asionis filia annorum XXXV hic sita est titulum memoriae posuit Magimarus coiunx sic VV R\n
EDCS-17600057.translation: I love you. Adnama, daughter of Asionis, aged 35, lies here. Her husband Magimarus placed this tombstone in memory of her. VV R [perhaps Vivas Vivas Requiescas].
EDCS-17600057.summary: A funerary epitaph for Adnama, daughter of Asionis, aged 35, erected by her husband Magimarus, likely from the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-17600058.text_edition: \n Atuquus Ausquuti filius annorum XX Capacita Ausquuti filia annorum XVI titulus positus natis caris obitis\n
EDCS-17600058.translation: Atuquus, son of Ausquutus, aged 20. Capacita, daughter of Ausquutus, aged 16. This inscription was placed for dear children after their death.
EDCS-17600058.summary: A funerary epitaph for two siblings, Atuquus (aged 20) and Capacita (aged 16), children of Ausquutus, likely from the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-17600059.text_edition: \n Blatuna Vagaimoni filia annorum LXV hic sita est titulum memoriae posuit Tosuta \n
EDCS-17600059.translation: Blatuna, daughter of Vagaimon, aged 65, lies here; Tosuta placed this memorial stone.
EDCS-17600059.summary: A tombstone recording the death of Blatuna, daughter of Vagaimon, with the monument erected by Tosuta.

EDCS-17600060.text_edition: \n Domitinus Raudomaei filius nnorum XIV hic situs est Raudomaeus pater titulum memoriae posuit\n
EDCS-17600060.translation: Domitinus, son of Raudomaeus, aged 14, lies here; Raudomaeus, his father, placed this memorial stone.
EDCS-17600060.summary: A tombstone recording the death of Domitinus, son of Raudomaeus, with the monument erected by his father.

EDCS-17600061.text_edition: \n Dis Manibus Trovcetimarus Angulati annorum L Ricmara Atali viva sibi et coniugi Domitius filius parentibus titulum posuerunt\n
EDCS-17600061.translation: To the departed spirits. Trovcetimarus, son of Angulatus, aged 50, and Ricmara, daughter of Atali, aged [unknown], who lived for themselves and their spouse, Domitius, son of parents, placed this monument.
EDCS-17600061.summary: A tombstone dedicated to the departed spirits, recording Trovcetimarus and Ricmara, with the monument placed by their son Domitius.

EDCS-17600062.text_edition: \n Dis Manibus Vibiano Deivonis annorum LXX patri et Atezissae annorum XL matri et Valentino annorum XXX fratri Vibius Saturnus duplarius alae I Thracum bene merentibus posuit\n
EDCS-17600062.translation: To the departed spirits. Vibius Saturnus, duplarius of the I Thracum ala, placed this for his father Vibiano Deivonis, aged 70, his mother Atezissa, aged 40, and his brother Valentino, aged 30, who deserved well.
EDCS-17600062.summary: A tombstone dedicated to the departed spirits, recording Vibius Saturnus's parents and brother, erected by himself.

EDCS-17600063.text_edition: \n Dis Manibus Marciae Ulpiae Marci filiae Respectae quae vixit an(nis) L in matrimonio pia vixit annis mensibus diebus VI item pia vixit in matrimonio anteriori sic nnis V mense I diebus III et Marco Ulpio Iustino iuniori filio eius vivo Lucius Septimius Optatus coniugi rarissimae posuit\n
EDCS-17600063.translation: To the departed spirits. Lucius Septimius Optatus placed this for his exceedingly rare wife Marcia Ulpia Respecta, daughter of Marcus, who lived 50 years in marriage, piously, lived in a previous marriage 5 years, 1 month, 3 days, and for his son Marco Ulpio Iustino the Younger, alive.
EDCS-17600063.summary: A tombstone dedicated to the departed spirits, recording Marcia Ulpia Respecta and her son Marco Ulpio Iustino, erected by her husband Lucius Septimius Optatus.

EDCS-17600065.text_edition: \n Putiolano Aurelius Valentianus miles? legionis II adiutricis piae fidelis votum solvit libens merito\n
EDCS-17600065.translation: Aurelius Valentianus, soldier of the II Adiutrix legion, pious, faithful, fulfilled his vow willingly, deservedly.
EDCS-17600065.summary: A votive inscription by Aurelius Valentianus, a soldier of the II Adiutrix legion, to a deity, possibly Putiolanus.

EDCS-17600067.text_edition: \n Silvano Silvestri Septimius Iulianus miles legionis II adiutricis votum solvit libens merito\n
EDCS-17600067.translation: Septimius Iulianus, soldier of the II Adiutrix legion, fulfilled his vow willingly, deservedly, to Silvanus Silvestris.
EDCS-17600067.summary: A votive inscription by Septimius Iulianus, a soldier of the II Adiutrix legion, to the god Silvanus.

EDCS-17600068.text_edition: \n Dis Manibus Canu<s> Loi filius annorum LX Luba coiuce sic Vimbriplia annorum L fecit patri\n
EDCS-17600068.translation: To the departed spirits. Luba, wife, aged 50, and Vimbriplia, aged 50, made this for their father Canus, son of Loi, aged 60.
EDCS-17600068.summary: A tombstone dedicated to the departed spirits, recording Canus and his wife Vimbriplia, with the monument erected by their daughters Luba and Vimbriplia.

EDCS-17600069.text_edition: \n Sibulla Iopari filia annorum XXX titulus positus\n
EDCS-17600069.translation: Sibulla, daughter of Iopar, aged 30; the inscription was placed.
EDCS-17600069.summary: A tombstone recording the death of Sibulla, daughter of Iopar.

EDCS-17600078.text_edition: \n Iunoni Reginae Publia Aelia Valentina pro se et suis ex voto posuit Sabino et Anullino consulibus\n
EDCS-17600078.translation: To Juno Regina. Publia Aelia Valentina placed this, in accordance with her vow, for herself and her family, under the consulship of Sabinus and Anullinus.
EDCS-17600078.summary: A votive inscription to Juno Regina by Publia Aelia Valentina for herself and her family, dated to the consulship of Sabinus and Anullinus.

EDCS-17600079.summary: An inscription dedicated to the goddess Diana by Aurelius Victorinus.

EDCS-17600080.summary: An inscription dedicated to the goddess Fortuna by Marcus Sosius Iulianus.

EDCS-17600085.text_edition: \n Dis Manibus Aelia Sura posuit matri suae Mam<er>t<i>nae a<n>(norum) <LX=IA> \n
EDCS-17600085.translation: To the Divine Manes, Aelia Sura set this up for her mother Mamertina, aged 60.
EDCS-17600085.summary: A funerary monument set up by Aelia Sura for her mother Mamertina, who died at the age of 60.

EDCS-17600086.text_edition: \n inae vixit nnos XXIII coniugi merentissimae Claudius Valerius miles legionis I Italicae et Claudio Valerino filio et Claudiae Valerinae filiae vivis opto tibi terram levem\n
EDCS-17600086.translation: lived 23 years, for a most deserving wife, Claudius Valerius, a soldier of the I Italica Legion, and for Claudius Valerinus, son, and Claudia Valerine, daughter. While living, I wish you light earth.
EDCS-17600086.summary: A funerary epitaph recording the life span of a woman and expressing wishes for her and her children by her husband, Claudius Valerius, a soldier.

EDCS-17600838.text_edition: \n Iovi Optimo Maximo Iunius votum libens solvit\n
EDCS-17600838.translation: To Jupiter Best and Greatest, Iunius gladly paid his vow.
EDCS-17600838.summary: A dedication recording a vow paid to Jupiter Optimus Maximus by Iunius.

EDCS-17600842.text_edition: \n Libero Patri Augusto sacrum Aurelius Antonius votum solvit libens merito\n
EDCS-17600842.translation: Sacred to Liber Pater Augustus. Aurelius Antonius gladly paid his vow for a deserved reason.
EDCS-17600842.summary: A dedication to Liber Pater Augustus, with Aurelius Antonius gladly fulfilling a vow.

EDCS-17600843.text_edition: \n Silvano Domestico Flavius Ingenuinus votum solvit libens merito\n
EDCS-17600843.translation: To Silvanus Domesticus, Flavius Ingenuinus gladly paid his vow for a deserved reason.
EDCS-17600843.summary: A dedication to Silvanus Domesticus, with Flavius Ingenuinus gladly fulfilling a vow.

EDCS-17600844.text_edition: \n Iulius Candidus dis deabusque votum solvit libens merito\n
EDCS-17600844.translation: Iulius Candidus paid his vow to the gods and goddesses gladly for a deserved reason.
EDCS-17600844.summary: A dedication to the gods and goddesses by Iulius Candidus, who gladly fulfilled a vow.

EDCS-17600846.text_edition: \n Dis Manibus Claudius Tyrannus tribunus legionis XVI Flaviae firmae tribunus cohortis III Batavorum Ulpia Saturnina coniugi pientissimo et Claudius Concordianus eques Romanus patri desidesid(eratissimo)\n
EDCS-17600846.translation: To the Divine Manes, Claudius Tyrannus, tribune of the XVI Flavia Firma legion, tribune of the III Cohort of Batavians, for his most pious wife Ulpia Saturnina, and Claudius Concordianus, Roman knight, for his most beloved father.
EDCS-17600846.summary: A funerary monument honoring both a wife and a father, set up by Claudius Tyrannus, a military officer, and his son Claudius Concordianus.

EDCS-17600851.text_edition: \n filius et heres patri pientissimo titulum posuit et Ulpiae Vindeliae matri vivae\n
EDCS-17600851.translation: son and heir for his most pious father, set up this monument, and for Ulpia Vindelia, his living mother.
EDCS-17600851.summary: A funerary monument set up by a son and heir for his father and his living mother, Ulpia Vindelia.

EDCS-17600852.text_edition: \n Deo Libero Patiri Aurelius Meaximus veteranus votum solvit libens merito\n
EDCS-17600852.translation: To the god Liber Pater, Aurelius Meaximus, a veteran, gladly and deservedly fulfilled his vow.
EDCS-17600852.summary: This inscription records a veteran's vow fulfilled to the god Liber Pater, likely from the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-17600854.text_edition: \n Silvano Conservatori pro salute Iuli BIRSami veterani Septimius Celsus et Aurelius Atellanus votum solverunt\n
EDCS-17600854.translation: To Silvanus Conservator, for the health of the veteran Iulius Birsamus, Septimius Celsus and Aurelius Atellanus fulfilled their vow.
EDCS-17600854.summary: This inscription records a vow made by Septimius Celsus and Aurelius Atellanus to Silvanus Conservator for the well-being of the veteran Iulius Birsamus, likely from the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-17600855.text_edition: \n coniugi vivae et V et et Messalin filiis ius Firminus\n
EDCS-17600855.translation: To his living wife and [---] children, Firminus [dedicated this?].
EDCS-17600855.summary: This is a fragmented inscription seemingly recording dedication by Firminus to his wife and children, origin unknown.

EDCS-17600856.translation: To Juno Regina, Octavia Corneliana [dedicated this?].
EDCS-17600856.summary: This is a fragmented inscription recording a dedication, possibly by Octavia Corneliana, to Juno Regina.

EDCS-17600862.text_edition: \n Terrae Matri Flavia Maximina votum solvit libens merito\n
EDCS-17600862.translation: To Mother Earth, Flavia Maximina gladly and deservedly fulfilled her vow.
EDCS-17600862.summary: This inscription records Flavia Maximina fulfilling a vow to Terra Mater (Mother Earth), likely from the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-17600864.text_edition: \n Iovi Optimo Maximo Publius Aelius Veranus veteranus ex decurione alae I Thracum veteranae votum solvit libens merito\n
EDCS-17600864.translation: To Jupiter Optimus Maximus, Publius Aelius Veranus, a veteran and former decurion of the First Thracian Veteran Cavalry Regiment, gladly and deservedly fulfilled his vow.
EDCS-17600864.summary: This inscription records a veteran and former decurion fulfilling a vow to Jupiter Optimus Maximus, likely from the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-17800001.text_edition: \n Vindo Saturnini flius nnorum LX et Ammuia Mogetionis filia annorum L hic siti sunt Marcus Ulpius Brogimarus gener titulum posuit\n
EDCS-17800001.translation: Vindo, son of Saturninus, aged 60, and Ammuia, daughter of Mogetio, aged 50, lie here; Marcus Ulpius Brogimarus, their son-in-law, erected this tombstone.
EDCS-17800001.summary: This inscription is a funerary monument recording the death of Vindo and Ammuia, with the tombstone erected by their son-in-law Marcus Ulpius Brogimarus, likely from the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-17800002.text_edition: \n Bato Trantonis filius Araviscus sic nnorum L hic situs est Firmus hic situs est Mogitmarus titulum memoriae posuit\n
EDCS-17800002.translation: Bato, son of Tranto, an Araviscus, aged 50, lies here; Firmus lies here; Mogitmarus erected this tombstone in memory.
EDCS-17800002.summary: This inscription is a funerary monument recording the burial of Bato and Firmus, with Mogitmarus erecting the tombstone, likely from the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-17800003.text_edition: \n Iovi Optimo Maximo Conservatori Titus L Titus votum solvit libens decurio coloniae\n
EDCS-17800003.translation: To Jupiter Optimus Maximus Conservator, Titus, a decurion of the colony, gladly fulfilled his vow.
EDCS-17800003.summary: This inscription records Titus, a decurion of the colony, fulfilling a vow to Jupiter Optimus Maximus Conservator, likely from the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-17800004.summary: This inscription records Septimius Valentinus, an optio, dedicating something to the deity Cautus, likely from the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-17800005.translation: To the Fountains of the Gods. Septimius Valentinus, optatio.
EDCS-17800005.summary: This is a dedication to the Fountains of the Gods by Septimius Valentinus, an optatio, likely from the Roman period.

EDCS-17800006.translation: At the Crossing of the Gods. Septimius Valentinus, optatio.
EDCS-17800006.summary: This is a dedication at a crossing point dedicated to the Gods by Septimius Valentinus, an optatio, likely from the Roman period.

EDCS-17800035.text_edition: \n Dis Manibus Hic iacet in tumulo Aurelia Sabina pientissima coniux nnum XXV quem lapis iste tegit rapta est de luce serena quae magis debuerat fessos sepelire parentes o dolor o pietas o funera tristia coniugis Marcus Aurelius Attianus decurio alae I Trh sicacum et Aurelia Sabinilla filia eorundum sic vivi sibi faciundum curarunt\n
EDCS-17800035.translation: To the Spirits of the Departed. Here lies in this tomb Aurelia Sabina, a most devoted wife, aged 25 years, who was snatched from life while she should have been burying her weary parents. O grief! O devotion! O sad funeral of a wife! Marcus Aurelius Attianus, decurion of the first cavalry regiment of Thracians, and Aurelia Sabinilla, their daughter, had this monument made for themselves while alive.
EDCS-17800035.summary: This is a funerary epitaph for Aurelia Sabina, a 25-year-old wife, set up by her husband Marcus Aurelius Attianus and daughter Aurelia Sabinilla, likely from the 2nd or 3rd century CE.

EDCS-17800036.text_edition: \n Resatus Sapurdae filius nnorum XXX hic situs est Sapurda et Ressila parentes filio pientissimo et sibi vivi\n
EDCS-17800036.translation: Resatus, son of Sapurda, aged 30 years, lies here. Sapurda and Ressila, his parents, [made this] for their most devoted son and for themselves while alive.
EDCS-17800036.summary: This is a funerary epitaph for Resatus, a 30-year-old man, set up by his parents Sapurda and Ressila, likely from the Roman period.

EDCS-17800037.text_edition: \n Domno Domnae Titus Pompulenius Iunius |(centurio) legionis II adiutricis piae fidelis votum solvit libens merito\n
EDCS-17800037.translation: To the Lord and Lady. Titus Pompulenius Junius, centurion of the Second Legion Adjutrix, Pious and Faithful, fulfilled his vow willingly and deservedly.
EDCS-17800037.summary: This is a dedication to a divine lord and lady by Titus Pompulenius Junius, a centurion in the Second Legion Adjutrix, likely from the 2nd or 3rd century CE.

EDCS-17800038.text_edition: \n eques Honori et Herculi votum solverunt libens merito\n
EDCS-17800038.translation: To Honor and Hercules. They fulfilled their vow willingly and deservedly.
EDCS-17800038.summary: This is a dedication to the gods Honor and Hercules by an unknown individual or group, likely from the Roman period.

EDCS-17800040.translation: Cauti. Tusdius fulfilled his vow willingly and deservedly. Aurelius [---] L[---]SE[---]
EDCS-17800040.summary: This is a dedication by Cautus Tusdius to an unspecified deity, with additional damaged text including a name Aurelius, likely from the Roman period.

EDCS-17800392.text_edition: \n Lucia Septim<i>a Mamertina Luci Septimi Mamertini filia Marci Aureli Casii coniux diem funi sic annorum XXX\n
EDCS-17800392.translation: Lucia Septimia Mamertina, daughter of Lucius Septimius Mamertinus, wife of Marcus Aurelius Cassius, lived 30 years.
EDCS-17800392.summary: This is a funerary epitaph for Lucia Septimia Mamertina, who died at the age of 30 and was the wife of Marcus Aurelius Cassius, likely from the Roman period.

EDCS-17800393.text_edition: \n Marcus Tutilius Vale<n>s annorum Tutilius Paulus annorum hic siti sunt\n
EDCS-17800393.translation: Marcus Tutilius Valens, aged [---] years. Tutilius Paulus, aged [---] years. They lie here.
EDCS-17800393.summary: This is a funerary epitaph recording the burial of two individuals named Marcus Tutilius Valens and Tutilius Paulus, with their ages missing, likely from the Roman period.

EDCS-17800394.text_edition: \n annorum X et Vibillae coniugi et Valentinae filiae et Saturnino filio tertio et suis vivis posuit Aurelius Sabinus et Aurelius Ius<t=I>us heredes bene merentibus fecerunt \n
EDCS-17800394.translation: Aurelius Sabinus and Aurelius Justus, heirs, made this for [their family members] aged 10 years, and for their wife Vibilla, and for their daughter Valentina, and for their son Saturninus, and for themselves while alive, as deserving ones.
EDCS-17800394.summary: This is a funerary epitaph recording a tomb made by Aurelius Sabinus and Aurelius Justus for their deceased family members, including children and spouse, likely from the Roman period.

EDCS-17800396.text_edition: \n Saturnin(us) quondam armatura legionis II adiutricis stipendiorum XVI oriundus ex regione Bassianensi desideratus in Dacia quondam qui vixit nnos XXXIIII Septimius Maximianus\n
EDCS-17800396.translation: Saturninus, formerly of the legion II Adiutrix with sixteen years of service, from the Bassianensis region, missed in Dacia, who lived 34 years. Septimius Maximianus [erected this].
EDCS-17800396.summary: This inscription records the memorial for Saturninus, a soldier of the legion II Adiutrix, who died at the age of 34 and was from the Bassianensis region, likely erected by Septimius Maximianus.

EDCS-17800411.summary: This is a very fragmentary inscription beginning with a possible praenomen and nomen, making its purpose and subject unclear.

EDCS-18000394.text_edition: \n Tiberius Claudius Severus Icepni filius Vangio missicius eques ala Hispanorum prima nnorum LX hic situs est Albanus frater titulum memoriae posuit\n
EDCS-18000394.translation: Tiberius Claudius Severus, son of Icepnius Vangio, a discharged cavalryman of the first ala of Spaniards, aged 60, lies here. His brother Albanus placed this memorial.
EDCS-18000394.summary: This is a tomb inscription for Tiberius Claudius Severus, a veteran cavalryman of a Spanish unit, erected by his brother Albanus.

EDCS-18300306.text_edition: \n Silvano Domestico Aurelius Silvanus veteranus votum solvit libens merito\n
EDCS-18300306.translation: To Silvanus Domestus, Aurelius Silvanus, a veteran, willingly fulfilled his vow as deserved.
EDCS-18300306.summary: This inscription records a veteran, Aurelius Silvanus, fulfilling a vow to the deity Silvanus Domestus.

EDCS-18300307.text_edition: \n Dis Manibus Aurelius Isauricius Verecundus Surus veteranus cohortis |(miliariae) Hemesenorum ex decurione vixit nnos LXX Aurelia Isauri<c=G>ia Scribonia coniux et Isauricius Verecundus et Isauricius Marcus et Aurelia Verecunda fili conheredes vivi sibi et patri pientissimo posuerunt \n
EDCS-18300307.translation: To the Spirits of the Departed. Aurelius Isauricius Verecundus Surus, a veteran of the 1000-strong cohort of Hemesenoi, formerly a decurion, lived 70 years. Aurelia Isauricia Scribonia, his wife, and his sons Isauricius Verecundus, Isauricius Marcus, and Aurelia Verecunda, co-heirs, erected this while alive for their most devoted father.
EDCS-18300307.summary: This is a family tomb inscription for Aurelius Isauricius Verecundus Surus, a veteran decurion of a Hemesenoi cohort, erected by his wife and children.

EDCS-18300318.text_edition: \n Iovi Optimo Maximo Lucius Cornelius Crescens decurio alae Frontonianae pro se et suis votum solvit libens merito\n
EDCS-18300318.translation: To Jupiter Best and Greatest, Lucius Cornelius Crescens, a decurion of the ala Frontoniana, willingly fulfilled his vow for himself and his family as deserved.
EDCS-18300318.summary: This inscription records Lucius Cornelius Crescens, a decurion of the ala Frontoniana, fulfilling a vow to Jupiter Optimus Maximus for himself and his family.

EDCS-18300320.text_edition: \n alae Aurianae stipendiorum XXdefunctus nnorum LXX Claudia Da patri et sibi viva\n
EDCS-18300320.translation: Of the ala Auriana, with twenty years of service, died at 70 years of age. Claudia Da, alive, [erected this] for her father and herself.
EDCS-18300320.summary: This inscription likely commemorates a soldier of the ala Auriana who served for twenty years and died at 70, possibly erected by his daughter Claudia Da.

EDCS-18300323.text_edition: \n Invicto Soli Mithrae Valerius Valens veteranus ex decurione alae I T<hr=RH>acum veteranae votum solvit libens merito \n
EDCS-18300323.translation: To the Unconquered Sun Mithras, Valerius Valens, a veteran and former decurion of the first ala of Thracians, willingly fulfilled his vow as deserved.
EDCS-18300323.summary: This inscription records Valerius Valens, a veteran decurion of the ala I Thracum, fulfilling a vow to the god Mithras.

EDCS-18300328.summary: This is an identifying marker for the Cohors III Batavorum Antoniniana, likely part of a building or monument.

EDCS-18300329.summary: This is an identifying marker for the Cohors III Batavorum Antoniniana, likely part of a building or monument.

EDCS-18300330.translation: The Third Cohort of Batavians, Antoninian.
EDCS-18300330.summary: This inscription records the designation of the Third Cohort of Batavians, likely indicating it was reorganized or incorporated into the Antonine legions.

EDCS-18300332.translation: The First Cohort of Alpiners, equipped with cavalry.
EDCS-18300332.summary: This inscription identifies the First Cohort of Alpiners, noting its equestrian status, meaning it included cavalry units.

EDCS-21000720.translation: Aurelius Ingenuus of the Second Adjutant Legion.
EDCS-21000720.summary: This inscription is a name and unit identification for Aurelius Ingenuus, a soldier in the Second Adjutant Legion.

EDCS-24400401.inscription_type: diplomata militaria, tituli operum, tituli sacri
EDCS-24400401.text_edition: \n Nero Claudius divi Claudi filius Germanici Caesaris nepos Tiberi Caesaris Augusti pronepos divi Augusti abnepos Caesar Augustus Germanicus pontifex maximus tribunicia potestate VII imperator VII consul IIII equitibus qui militant in alis IIII quae apellantur I Hispanorum et Aravacorum et I Hispanorum Auriana et II Hispanorum et Aravacorum et II Ast<u=O>rum et sunt in Illyrico sub Lucio Salvidieno Salviano Rufo qui quina et vicena stipendia aut plura meruerant quorum nomina subscripta sunt ipsis liberis posterisque eorum civitatem dedit et conubium cum uxoribus quas tunc habuissent cum est civitas iis data aut siqui caelibes essent cum iis quas postea duxissent dumtaxat singuli singulas ante diem VI Nonas Iulias Cnaeo Pedanio Salinatore Lucio Velleio Paterculo consulibus alae II Hispanorum et Aravacorum cui praeest Sextus Gavius Gallus decurioni Dasio Carmai filio Breuco et Proculo filio eius et Priscillae filiae eius et Proculae filiae eius et Procellae filiae eius descriptum et recognitum ex tabula aenea quae fixa est in Capitolio ad latus sinistrum aedis thensarum extrinsecus Nero Claudius divi Claudi filius Germanicii Caesaris nepos Tiberi Caesaris Augusti pronepos divi Augusti abnepos Caesar Augustus Germanicus pontifex maximus tribunicia potestate VII imperator VII consul IIII equitibus qui militant in alis IIII quae appellantur I Hispanorum et Aravacorum et I Hispanorum Auriana et II Hispanorum et Aravacorum et II Ast<u=O>rum et sunt in Illyrico sub Lucio Salvidieno Salviano Rufo qui quina et vicena stipendia aut plura meruerant quorum nomina subscripta sunt ipsis liberis posterisque eorum civitatem dedit et conubium cum uxoribus Quas tunc habuissent cum est civitas iis data aut siqui caelibes essent cum iis quas postea duxissent dumtaxat singuli singulas ante diem VI Nonas Iulias Cnaeo Pedanio Salinatore Lucio Velleio Paterculo consulibus alae II Hispanorum et Aravacorum cui praeest Sextus Gavius Gallus decurioni Dasio Carmai filio Breuco et Proculo filio eius et Priscillae filiae eius et Proculae filiae eius et Procellae filiae eius descriptum et recognitum ex tabula aenea quae fixa est in Capitolio ad latus sinistrum aedis thensarum extrinsecus Marci Petroni Flacci equitis Romani Publi Corneli Crispini equitis Romani Sexti Pompili Flori equitis Romani Cai Titi Firmi Titi Minuci Modesti Luci Ovidi Prisci Publi Critti Narcissi Marci Petroni Narcissi \n
EDCS-24400401.translation: Nero Claudius, son of the deified Claudius, grandson of Germanicus Caesar, great-grandson of Tiberius Caesar Augustus, great-great-grandson of the deified Augustus, Caesar Augustus Germanicus, chief priest, with the seventh tribunician power, imperator for the seventh time, consul for the fourth time. To the cavalrymen who serve in the four alae named I Hispanorum et Aravacorum, I Hispanorum Auriana, II Hispanorum et Aravacorum, and II Astorum, and are in Illyricum under Lucius Salvidienus Rufus Salvianus, who had completed twenty-five years of service or more, whose names are listed below, he granted citizenship and the right to marry to them, their children, and their descendants. He granted the right to marry to the wives they then had when citizenship was granted to them, or if they were unmarried, to those they would marry later, provided each man married only one woman. This was granted before the 6th day before the Nones of July, during the consulship of Gnaeus Pedanius Salinator and Lucius Velleius Paterculus. To the ala II Hispanorum et Aravacorum, commanded by Sextus Gavius Gallus, to the decurion Dasus, son of Carmaius, a Breuci, and to his son Proculus, his daughter Priscilla, his daughter Procula, and his daughter Procella. This was transcribed and confirmed from a bronze tablet which is fixed in the Capitol, on the left side of the Temple of the Thunderbolt, on the outside. Nero Claudius, son of the deified Claudius, grandson of Germanicus Caesar, great-grandson of Tiberius Caesar Augustus, great-great-grandson of the deified Augustus, Caesar Augustus Germanicus, chief priest, with the seventh tribunician power, imperator for the seventh time, consul for the fourth time. To the cavalrymen who serve in the four alae named I Hispanorum et Aravacorum, I Hispanorum Auriana, II Hispanorum et Aravacorum, and II Astorum, and are in Illyricum under Lucius Salvidienus Rufus Salvianus, who had completed twenty-five years of service or more, whose names are listed below, he granted citizenship and the right to marry to them, their children, and their descendants. He granted the right to marry to the wives they then had when citizenship was granted to them, or if they were unmarried, to those they would marry later, provided each man married only one woman. This was granted before the 6th day before the Nones of July, during the consulship of Gnaeus Pedanius Salinator and Lucius Velleius Paterculus. To the ala II Hispanorum et Aravacorum, commanded by Sextus Gavius Gallus, to the decurion Dasus, son of Carmaius, a Breuci, and to his son Proculus, his daughter Priscilla, his daughter Procula, and his daughter Procella. This was transcribed and confirmed from a bronze tablet which is fixed in the Capitol, on the left side of the Temple of the Thunderbolt, on the outside. Marcus Petronius Flaccus, Roman knight. Publius Cornelius Crispinus, Roman knight. Sextus Pompilius Florus, Roman knight. Gaius Titus Firmus. Titus Minucius Modestus. Lucius Ovinius Priscus. Publius Crittius Narcissus. Marcus Petronius Narcissus.
EDCS-24400401.summary: This is a major imperial rescript issued by Nero, granting Roman citizenship and the right to legal marriage to auxiliary cavalry soldiers in Illyricum who had completed long service, along with their families, with specific individuals being named as recipients, dated to July 2nd, 59 AD.

EDCS-24500525.text_edition: \n Dis Manibus Aeliorum Faventinus vixit annis XLV Floriana annorum XL Faventinus miles cohortis |(miliariae) Maurorum Severianae stipendiorum IIII vixit annis DO \n
EDCS-24500525.translation: To the departed spirits. Faventinus lived 45 years. Floriana 40 years. Faventinus, a soldier of the First Cohort (milliaria) of Moors Severiana, with 4 years of service, lived 40 years.
EDCS-24500525.summary: This is a funerary inscription for Faventinus and Floriana, with Faventinus identified as a soldier in the First Cohort of Moors Severiana, providing their ages at death and his service length.

EDCS-26600559.text_edition: \n Iovi Optimo Maximo Neptuno Serapidi pro salute et victoria et perpetuitate Imperatoris Caesaris Marci Aureli Antonini Pii Felicis Augusti Lucius Alfenus Avitianus legatus eius pro praetore provinciae nnoniae inferioris\n
EDCS-26600559.translation: To Jupiter Best and Greatest, to Neptune, to Serapis, for the health, victory, and eternal perpetuity of the Emperor Caesar Marcus Aurelius Antoninus Pius Felix Augustus, by Lucius Alfenus Avitianus, his legate pro praetore of the province of Lower Pannonia.
EDCS-26600559.summary: This is a dedication made by Lucius Alfenus Avitianus, governor of Lower Pannonia, to Jupiter, Neptune, and Serapis for the well-being and victory of Emperor Marcus Aurelius Antoninus Pius Felix Augustus.

EDCS-26600560.text_edition: \n Imperatori Caesari Marco Aurel[io] [Severo] Alexan[dro] Pio Felici invicto Augusto pontifici maximo tribuniciae potestatis VIII consuli [III] patri patriae cohors I |(milliaria) nova Severiana Surorum sagittariorum devota numini eius\n
EDCS-26600560.translation: To the Emperor Caesar Marcus Aurelius [Severus] Alexander Pius Felix invincible Augustus, chief priest, with the eighth tribunician power, consul for the third time, father of the country, the First Cohort (milliaria) Nova Severiana of Syrian archers is devoted to his divine will.
EDCS-26600560.summary: This inscription records the devotion of the First Cohort (milliaria) Nova Severiana of Syrian archers to Emperor Alexander Severus, listing his imperial titles and the cohort's designation.

EDCS-26600561.text_edition: \n Iuliae Mamaeae Augustae matri domini nostri Invicti Imperatoris Severi Alexandri Pii Felicis Augusti et castrorum cohors I |(milliaria) nova Severiana Surorum sagittariorum devota numini eorum\n
EDCS-26600561.translation: To Julia Mamaea Augusta, mother of our lord, the invincible Emperor Severus Alexander Pius Felix Augustus, and of the camp, the First Cohort (milliaria) Nova Severiana of Syrian archers is devoted to their divine will.
EDCS-26600561.summary: This inscription states the devotion of the First Cohort (milliaria) Nova Severiana of Syrian archers to Julia Mamaea, mother of Emperor Alexander Severus, and to the camp, indicating her significant influence.

EDCS-26600562.text_edition: \n Iovi Optimo Maximo pro salute ddominorum nnostrorum Titus Iulius Valens miles cohortis |(milliariae) novae Severianae Surorum sagittariorum et suis votum solvit libens merito\n
EDCS-26600562.translation: To Jupiter Best and Greatest, for the health of our lords, Titus Julius Valens, a soldier of the First Cohort (milliaria) Nova Severiana of Syrian archers, has paid his vow willingly and deservedly, and for himself.
