EDCS-28600102.translation: Emperor Caesar Titus Aelius Hadrianus Antoninus Pius, son of the deified Hadrian, grandson of the deified Trajan Parthicus, great-grandson of the deified Nerva, chief priest, holding tribunician power.
EDCS-28600102.summary: A titulature inscription for Emperor Antoninus Pius, listing his imperial titles and lineage, dating to his reign (138-161 AD).

EDCS-28600103.text_edition: \n Imperator Nerva Caesar Augustus pontifex maximus tribuniciae potestatis consul III pater patriae a Malata Cusum milia passuum <XVI=N> PI Imperatori Caesari Marco Aurelio Antonino Augusto Pio Felici Malata Cusum milia passuum XVI \n
EDCS-28600103.translation: Emperor Nerva Caesar Augustus, chief priest, holding tribunician power, consul for the third time, father of the fatherland, [from] Malata to Cusum, 16 miles. To Emperor Caesar Marcus Aurelius Antoninus Augustus Pius Felix, from Malata to Cusum, 16 miles.
EDCS-28600103.summary: A milestone inscription recording a road measurement between Malata and Cusum, mentioning both Emperor Nerva and Emperor Marcus Aurelius, likely dating to the period of their respective reigns.

EDCS-28600104.text_edition: \n Imperatori Caesari Marco Antonio Gordiano Pio Felici Augusto pontifici maximo tribuniciae potestatis patri patriae consuli proconsuli a Mal[ata ------\n
EDCS-28600104.translation: To Emperor Caesar Marcus Antonius Gordianus Pius Felix Augustus, chief priest, holding tribunician power, father of the fatherland, consul, proconsul, from Mal[ata] [...]
EDCS-28600104.summary: A fragmentary milestone inscription for Emperor Gordian III, recording a distance measurement from Malata, dating to his reign (238-244 AD).

EDCS-28600105.text_edition: \n Imperatori Caesari Marco Aurelio Claudio Pio Felici Invicto a Malata Cusum milia passuum XVI\n
EDCS-28600105.translation: To Emperor Caesar Marcus Aurelius Claudius Pius Felix Invictus, from Malata to Cusum, 16 miles.
EDCS-28600105.summary: A milestone inscription recording a road measurement between Malata and Cusum, dedicated to Emperor Claudius Gothicus, dating to his reign (268-270 AD).

EDCS-28600204.text_edition: \n Iovi Optimo Maximo Iulius Ianuarius veteranus ex praetorio votum posuit\n
EDCS-28600204.translation: To Jupiter, Best and Greatest, Julius Ianuarius, veteran of the Praetorian Guard, set up [this] vow.
EDCS-28600204.summary: A votive inscription dedicated to Jupiter Optimus Maximus by a Praetorian Guard veteran named Julius Ianuarius, likely from the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-28600205.text_edition: \n Iovi Optimo Maximo et omnibus dibus deabusque Ulpius Flaus veteranus ex decurione votum solvit libens merito\n
EDCS-28600205.translation: To Jupiter, Best and Greatest, and to all the gods and goddesses, Ulpius Flaus, veteran and former decurion, paid his vow willingly and deservedly.
EDCS-28600205.summary: A votive inscription to Jupiter and other deities, dedicated by a veteran and former decurion named Ulpius Flaus, likely from the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-28600206.text_edition: \n coniugi bene merenti posuit S Faventinu(s) Aurelio Tertullino fratri et <F=I>lavio Larrito decurioni alae \n
EDCS-28600206.translation: S. Faventinus dedicated this to his well-deserving wife, and to his brother Flavius Larritus, a decurion of the ala.
EDCS-28600206.summary: A funerary dedication set up by S. Faventinus for his wife and his brother, a cavalry decurion, likely from the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-28600207.text_edition: \n Iulio Aelio duplicario classis Flaviae nnonicae vixit nnos XLV Statoria Sura coniux et Iulius Ianuarius filius Aemilius Carus tr[ierarcha ------\n
EDCS-28600207.translation: To Julius Aelius, duplicarius of the Flavian fleet of Noricum, who lived 45 years. Statoria Sura, his wife, and Julius Ianuarius, his son, Aemilius Carus, trierarch [restored].
EDCS-28600207.summary: This inscription is a tombstone for Julius Aelius, a naval officer, set up by his wife and son, dating to the Roman period.

EDCS-28600208.text_edition: \n Scemaes Liccaui filius Amantinus hosef a<n=M>norum decem gente Undius centuria secunda in flumen periit Hemona posuere Liccaus pater Loriqus et Licaios cognati \n
EDCS-28600208.translation: Scemaes, son of Liccaus, an Amantine, decurio of the tenth century of the Undian troop, perished in the river. His father Liccaus and his kinsmen Loriqus and Licaios set this up.
EDCS-28600208.summary: This inscription records the death of Scemaes, a decurio, who drowned and was commemorated by his father and relatives in the Roman period.

EDCS-28600209.translation: To the departed spirits. Titus Publicius Surio.
EDCS-28600209.summary: This is a simple funerary dedication to Titus Publicius Surio, a Roman period tomb inscription.

EDCS-28600212.text_edition: \n Iovi Optimo Maximo Monitori pro salute adque incolumitate domini nostri Gallieni Augusti et militum vexillationum legionum Germanicianarum et Brittannicinarum cum auxiliis earum Vitalianus praefectus Augusti nostri somno moniitus posuit\n
EDCS-28600212.translation: To Jupiter Best and Greatest, Monitor. For the safety and well-being of our lord Gallienus Augustus and the soldiers of the vexillations of the Germanician and Britannic legions with their auxiliaries, Vitalianus, prefect of our Augustus, set this up, prompted by a dream.
EDCS-28600212.summary: This is a dedication to Jupiter by the prefect Vitalianus for the safety of Emperor Gallienus and his troops, dating to Gallienus' reign (253-268 AD).

EDCS-28600213.text_edition: \n Iovi Optimo Maximo sacrum Titus Flavius Candidianus pro se et suis votum solvit libens merito\n
EDCS-28600213.translation: Sacred to Jupiter Best and Greatest. Titus Flavius Candidianus paid his vow freely and deservedly for himself and his family.
EDCS-28600213.summary: This is a votive inscription to Jupiter by Titus Flavius Candidianus, likely set up during the Roman imperial period.

EDCS-28600214.text_edition: \n Iovi Optimo Maximo Ulpius Priscus Candidianus decurio coloniae Sirmiensis votum solvit libens merito\n
EDCS-28600214.translation: To Jupiter Best and Greatest. Ulpius Priscus Candidianus, decurio of the colony of Sirmium, paid his vow freely and deservedly.
EDCS-28600214.summary: This is a votive inscription to Jupiter by Ulpius Priscus Candidianus, a decurio of Sirmium, dating to the Roman imperial period.

EDCS-28600216.text_edition: \n Iovi Optimo <M=N>(aximo) et Marti Custodi Publius Helvius Pertinax praefectus \n
EDCS-28600216.translation: To Jupiter Best and Greatest and Mars the Guardian. Publius Helvius Pertinax, prefect, [set this up].
EDCS-28600216.summary: This is a dedication to Jupiter and Mars by Publius Helvius Pertinax, a prefect, likely from the Roman imperial period.

EDCS-28600218.text_edition: \n Libero et Liberae Augusti Flavius Paternus votum solvit libens merito\n
EDCS-28600218.translation: Sacred to Liber and Libera, Augusti. Flavius Paternus paid his vow freely and deservedly.
EDCS-28600218.summary: This is a votive inscription to the deities Liber and Libera (Bacchus and Ariadne) by Flavius Paternus, likely from the Roman imperial period.

EDCS-28600220.text_edition: \n Domnae et Domno sacrum abius Hilarus votum solvit libens merito\n
EDCS-28600220.translation: Sacred to Domna and Domnus. Abius Hilarus paid his vow freely and deservedly.
EDCS-28600220.summary: This is a votive inscription to an unnamed patron deity or deities, 'Domna and Domnus', by Abius Hilarus, likely from the Roman imperial period.

EDCS-28600221.text_edition: \n Imp(eratori) Caes(ari) M(arco) Aur(elio) Augusto cohors I Campanorum voluntariorum civium Romanorum Antoniniana cura agente Publio Aelio Valerio tribuno ex veterano Asppris consulibus\n
EDCS-28600221.translation: To Emperor Caesar Marcus Aurelius Augustus. The first cohort of Campanian volunteers, Roman citizens, Antoniniana, while Publius Aelius Valerius, tribune, was in charge, in the consulship of the veterans Asppris.
EDCS-28600221.summary: This inscription records a military dedication or command under Emperor Marcus Aurelius, involving the first cohort of Campanian volunteers, during a specific consulship in the Roman imperial period.

EDCS-28600224.text_edition: \n Publio Aelio Respecto equiti Romano a militiis filio piissimo et studiis omni praedito Aelius Trophimianus pater vir egregius posuit\n
EDCS-28600224.translation: To Publius Aelius Respectus, a Roman knight, son of one who held military office, most dutiful and endowed with every talent, Aelius Trophimianus, his father, an outstanding man, erected this.
EDCS-28600224.summary: This is a funerary dedication set up by a father for his deceased son, Publius Aelius Respectus, who was a Roman knight and held military offices.

EDCS-28600225.text_edition: \n Dis Manibus XXXVII Salvio nnorum XXXIII frumentario legionis II adiutricis Iulius Asclepiades caris suis hunc titulum posui miserabile munus non satis ut meruistis quattuor amissis te quintum Salvi flere necesse est adsiduae dum mea fata resistent te nunc amisso domus inclinata recumbit deponam luctus cum te sequar care per umbras dulcem meum Salvium liceat mihi dicere semper optaveram tumulum tu mihi ut faceres tot bona tum carui merito te care requiro\n
EDCS-28600225.translation: To the spirits of the departed. To Salvius, aged 33, a corn-supplier of the II Adjutrix legion. I, Julius Asclepiades, placed this monument for my dearest ones; a pitiable gift, not enough as you deserved. Having lost four, it is necessary for me to mourn you, my fifth, Salvius. As long as my fate resists, my house, now that I have lost you, collapses. I will lay down my grief when I follow you through the shadows, my sweet Salvius. It was always my wish, my dearest, that you would make a tomb for me; for so many good deeds, I deservedly miss you, my dearest.
EDCS-28600225.summary: This is a poignant funerary epitaph for Salvius, a corn-supplier in the II Adjutrix legion, written by his grieving father, Julius Asclepiades, lamenting the loss of his son and expressing his own sorrow.

EDCS-28600226.text_edition: \n Civilis decurio coloniae nnorum L et Caius Iulius Cai filius Florus decurio coloniae nnorum XX et Iuliae Festae nnorum XII Anicia Macrina marito et filiis\n
EDCS-28600226.translation: To Civilis, decurion of the colony, aged 50, and to Caius Julius Florus, son of Caius, decurion of the colony, aged 20, and to Iulia Festa, aged 12. Anicia Macrina for her husband and sons.
EDCS-28600226.summary: This is a funerary dedication set up by Anicia Macrina for her husband, Civilis, and her son, Caius Julius Florus, both decurions of the colony, and her daughter, Iulia Festa.

EDCS-28600228.text_edition: \n ------] issia adulescenti ex provincia inferiore Aelia Victoria M Auggustorum nnostrorum fratri\n
EDCS-28600228.translation: [To] Issia, a young woman from the province below, Aelia Victoria, sister of our Emperors Augustus, placed this.
EDCS-28600228.summary: This funerary dedication, with some damage, is from Aelia Victoria to her deceased sister, Issia, a young woman from a lower province.

EDCS-28600229.inscription_type: inscriptiones christianae, tituli operum, tituli sepulcrales
EDCS-28600229.text_edition: \n In hanc arcam posita est Aurelia Macrina quae vixit annos XXXV et Aurelius Iustinianus filius pius qui vixit annum unum et menses quinque\n
EDCS-28600229.translation: In this tomb was placed Aurelia Macrina, who lived 35 years, and Aurelius Iustinianus, her devoted son, who lived one year and five months.
EDCS-28600229.summary: This is a funerary inscription recording the burial of Aurelia Macrina and her young son, Aurelius Iustinianus, within a tomb.

EDCS-28600230.text_edition: \n Titulum quem testamento fieri iussit faciendum curavit\n
EDCS-28600230.translation: He arranged for the monument, which he ordered to be made in his will, to be constructed.
EDCS-28600230.summary: This brief inscription records the execution of a testamentary request for the construction of a monument.

EDCS-28600233.text_edition: \n Deo Silvano Domestico curans Hatena procurator ducenarius\n
EDCS-28600233.translation: To the god Silvanus Domestius. Hatena, procurator of ducenarius rank, dedicated this in fulfillment of a vow.
EDCS-28600233.summary: This is a dedication to the god Silvanus Domestius, made by Hatena, a procurator of ducenarius rank, likely in fulfillment of a vow.

EDCS-28600235.text_edition: \n Iovi Optimo Maximo Dolicheno Aurelius Iustianus decurio et Ulpius Silvinianus duplicarius alae P<a=X>nnoniorum pro se et suis \n
EDCS-28600235.translation: To Jupiter Optimus Maximus Dolichenus. Aurelius Iustianus, decurion, and Ulpius Silvinianus, duplicarius of the ala of Pannonia, for themselves and their families.
EDCS-28600235.summary: This is a religious dedication to Jupiter Optimus Maximus Dolichenus made by Aurelius Iustianus, a decurion, and Ulpius Silvinianus, a duplicarius, for their families.

EDCS-28600236.text_edition: \n Iovi Optimo Maximo Dolicheno Aurelii Sabinianus et Maximus et Apollinarius saccerdotes votum libentes laeti posuerunt\n
EDCS-28600236.translation: To Jupiter Optimus Maximus Dolichenus. Aurelii Sabinianus, Maximus, and Apollinarius, priests, willingly and joyfully placed this vow.
EDCS-28600236.summary: This is a religious dedication to Jupiter Optimus Maximus Dolichenus made by three priests, Aurelii Sabinianus, Maximus, and Apollinarius, in fulfillment of a vow.

EDCS-28600237.text_edition: \n Iovi Optimo Maximo Tiberius Dexter |(centurio) exploratorum votum solvit libens merito\n
EDCS-28600237.translation: To Jupiter Optimus Maximus. Tiberius Dexter, centurion of scouts, willingly paid his vow, as deserved.
EDCS-28600237.summary: This is a religious dedication to Jupiter Optimus Maximus made by Tiberius Dexter, a centurion of scouts, in fulfillment of a vow.

EDCS-28600238.text_edition: \n Imperatori Caesari Publio Licinio Valeriano Pio Felici Augusto pontifici maximo tribunicia potestate III consuli patri patriae proconsuli ordo col PRAPPART de<v>otus numini eorum\n
EDCS-28600238.translation: To Emperor Caesar Publius Licinius Valerianus Pius Felix Augustus, pontifex maximus, with tribunician power for the 3rd time, consul, father of the fatherland, proconsul, the council of the town of Praepartum, devoted to their divine majesty.
EDCS-28600238.summary: This is a dedication by the town council of Praepartum to Emperor Valerian, likely dating to his third tribunician power or consulship.

EDCS-28600239.text_edition: \n ------] Virssuccius SI eques imaginifer cohortis I Brittonum turmae Montani nnorum XXXV stipendiorum XV hic situs est Bodiccius imaginifer et Albanus heredes posuerunt\n
EDCS-28600239.translation: Virssuccius, a cavalryman, standard-bearer of the 1st Cohort of Britons, of the troop of Montanus, aged 35, with 15 years of service, lies here. Bodiccius, standard-bearer, and Albanus, his heirs, erected this.
EDCS-28600239.summary: This is a tombstone for Virssuccius, a cavalryman and standard-bearer in the 1st Cohort of Britons, erected by his heirs.

EDCS-28600240.text_edition: \n MManibus Quinti Maeci Donati pausavit nnos XVI filio pientissimo fecit Arethusa mater\n
EDCS-28600240.translation: To the departed spirits. Quintus Maecius Donatus passed away aged 16. His most devoted mother, Arethusa, made this.
EDCS-28600240.summary: This is a funerary monument for Quintus Maecius Donatus, erected by his mother.

EDCS-28600243.text_edition: \n Marci Quinti Valeri Severiani equitis cohortis III praetoriae qui vixit nnos XVIIII menses VIIII dies VIII Flavia Ianuaria mater viva sibi et eidem Severiano optimo carissimoque posuit\n
EDCS-28600243.translation: To the departed spirits. Marcus Quintus Valerius Severianus, a cavalryman of the 3rd Praetorian Cohort, who lived 17 years, 9 months, and 8 days. Flavia Ianuaria, his mother, set this up for herself and for Severianus, her best and dearest.
EDCS-28600243.summary: This is a funerary monument for Marcus Quintus Valerius Severianus, a cavalryman in the 3rd Praetorian Cohort, erected by his mother.

EDCS-28600244.text_edition: \n Dis Manibus Marco Ulpio Tito Sirmio |(centurio) legionis II adiutricis piae fidelis legionis I adiutricis legionis XIII geminae legionis V Macedonicae legionis XIIII geminae legionis II adiutricis \n
EDCS-28600244.translation: To the departed spirits. Marcus Ulpius Titus Sirmio, centurion of the 2nd Adjutant Legion, pious and faithful; of the 1st Adjutant Legion; of the 13th Gemina Legion; of the 5th Macedonian Legion; of the 14th Gemina Legion; of the 2nd Adjutant Legion.
EDCS-28600244.summary: This is a funerary inscription listing the career of Marcus Ulpius Titus Sirmio, a centurion who served in multiple legions.

EDCS-28600245.text_edition: \n Soli Invicto Mythrae sic Donatus sacerdos posuit O L sacratis antrum votum solvit libens animo\n
EDCS-28600245.translation: To Sol Invictus Mithras. Thus Donatus, priest, erected this in the sacred cave, gladly fulfilling his vow with a willing spirit.
EDCS-28600245.summary: This is a dedication to Sol Invictus Mithras by a priest named Donatus, likely fulfilling a vow.

EDCS-28600246.text_edition: \n Dalmata miles cohortis II Alpinorum vexillarius nnorum XXX stipendiorum XVI \n
EDCS-28600246.translation: Dalmatian, soldier of the 2nd Alpine Cohort, standard-bearer, aged 30, with 16 years of service.
EDCS-28600246.summary: This is a tombstone for Dalmatian, a soldier and standard-bearer in the 2nd Alpine Cohort.

EDCS-28600248.text_edition: \n Imperator Caesar Nerva Traianus divi Nervae f(ilius) Aug(ustus) --- \n
EDCS-28600248.translation: Emperor Caesar Nerva Trajan, son of the deified Nerva, Augustus...
EDCS-28600248.summary: This is the beginning of an inscription, likely an imperial dedication or rescript, naming Emperor Trajan.

EDCS-28600249.summary: This is a dedication to the god Somnus by Carminius, a prefect.

EDCS-28600250.text_edition: \n Libero et Liberae sacrum Marcus Ulpius Fronto Aemilianus decurio municipii Cibalensium libens fecit\n
EDCS-28600250.translation: Sacred to Liber and Libera. Marcus Ulpius Fronto Aemilianus, decurion of the municipium of Cibalae, gladly made this.
EDCS-28600250.summary: This is a dedication to Liber and Libera by Marcus Ulpius Fronto Aemilianus, a decurion from Cibalae.

EDCS-28600251.text_edition: \n Dis Manibus Caio Aponio Memmio Celeri tribuno militum legionis primae Italicae Iulia Pia coniugi bene merenti et Aponia Gallitia fratri\n
EDCS-28600251.translation: To the Divine Spirits. Iulia Pia for her well-deserving husband Gaius Aponius Memmius Celer, tribune of soldiers of the First Italian Legion, and for his brother Aponia Gallitia.
EDCS-28600251.summary: This is a tombstone dedicated to a military tribune of the First Italian Legion by his wife, likely dating to the 2nd or 3rd century CE.

EDCS-28600252.text_edition: \n Iovi Depulsori pro salute domini nostri Imperatoris Marci Aureli Antonini Pii Felicis Augusti et Iuliae Domnae Augustae Marcus Sperati dispensatoris vikarius\n
EDCS-28600252.translation: To Jupiter the Repeller, for the safety of our lord Emperor Marcus Aurelius Antoninus Pius Felix Augustus and Julia Domna Augusta, by Marcus Speratius, a deputy freedman.
EDCS-28600252.summary: This is a dedication to Jupiter for the safety of Emperor Marcus Aurelius and Empress Julia Domna, made by a subordinate official, likely from the late 2nd century CE.

EDCS-28600253.text_edition: \n Iovi Optimo Maximo Caius Oppius Baebius beneficiarius consularis legionis II Italicae piae fidelis Severianae pro se et suis votum solvit libens merito Imperatore domino nostro Severo Alexandro Augusto II et Marcello consulibus\n
EDCS-28600253.translation: To Jupiter Most High and Great, Gaius Oppius Baebius, a beneficiary of the consular governor of the Second Italian Legion, Pius Fidelis Severiana, willingly and deservedly paid his vow for himself and his family. With our Emperor Severus Alexander Augustus as consul for the second time, and Marcellus as consul.
EDCS-28600253.summary: This is a votive inscription to Jupiter by a soldier of the Second Italian Legion, dedicating his vow during the consulship of Severus Alexander, likely in the early 3rd century CE.

EDCS-28600254.text_edition: \n Tiberio Claudio Britti filio Valerio decurioni alae II Aravacorum domo Hispano nnum L stipendiorum XXX et Claudiae Ianuariae coniugi eius et Claudiae Hispanillae filiae vivis ex testamento Flaccus decurio frater et Hispanilla filia heredes faciundum curaverunt\n
EDCS-28600254.translation: For Tiberius Claudius Britti's son Valerius, decurion of the Second Ala of Aravaci, from Hispania, aged 50 years and 30 years of service, and for Claudia Ianuaria, his wife, and Claudia Hispanilla, his daughter. Flaccus, decurion, brother, and Hispanilla, daughter, his heirs, had this monument erected during their lifetime in accordance with his will.
EDCS-28600254.summary: This is a tombstone erected by heirs for a decurion of the Second Ala of Aravaci, his wife, and daughter, indicating his age and service length, likely from the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-28600255.text_edition: \n Marcus Ulpius Super decurio alae praetoriae civium Romanorum ex summo curatore alae I civium Romanorum annorum XXXII stipendiorum XVI hic situs est Marcus Ulpius Similis sesquiplcarius alae I civium Romanorum frater et Ulpia Siscia soror fratri pientissimo iuventutique eius parum sibi frugi faciendum curaverunt\n
EDCS-28600255.translation: Marcus Ulpius Super, decurion of the Praetorian Wing of Roman Citizens, formerly the chief curator of the First Wing of Roman Citizens, aged 32 years and 16 years of service, lies here. Marcus Ulpius Similis, a sesquiplicarius of the First Wing of Roman Citizens, his brother, and Ulpia Siscia, his sister, took care to have this erected for their most devoted brother and his youthful life, which was too short.
EDCS-28600255.summary: This tombstone records the burial of Marcus Ulpius Super, a decurion and former curator, along with his age and service length, erected by his siblings, likely from the 2nd or 3rd century CE.

EDCS-28600256.text_edition: \n ------] Cominius decurio missicius alae II Aravacorum annorum hic situs est testamento fieri iussit sibi et Cominio Clementi fratri et Cominiae Antoniae sorori \n
EDCS-28600256.translation: [...]. Cominius, a supernumerary decurion of the Second Ala of Aravaci, aged [...], lies here. He ordered by his will that this should be made for himself and for Cominius Clemens, his brother, and Cominia Antonia, his sister.
EDCS-28600256.summary: This is a tombstone for Cominius, a decurion of the Second Ala of Aravaci, who by his will arranged for the monument to be erected for himself and his siblings, likely from the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-28600257.text_edition: \n Dibus Deabusque Aurelia votum solvit libens laeta\n
EDCS-28600257.translation: To the Gods and Goddesses, Aurelia willingly and joyfully paid her vow.
EDCS-28600257.summary: This is a short votive inscription to the gods and goddesses by Aurelia, likely from the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-28600258.text_edition: \n Iovi Optimo Maximo Marcus Ulpius Ianuarius cornicularius procuratoris Augusti votum solvit libens merito\n
EDCS-28600258.translation: To Jupiter Most High and Great, Marcus Ulpius Ianuarius, a cornicularius (aide) to the imperial procurator, willingly and deservedly paid his vow.
EDCS-28600258.summary: This is a votive inscription to Jupiter by Marcus Ulpius Ianuarius, an aide to an imperial official, likely from the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-28600259.text_edition: \n Silvano Domestico sacrum Caius Iulius Demetrius votum libens\n
EDCS-28600259.translation: Sacred to Silvanus Domestus. Gaius Julius Demetrius, his vow willingly paid.
EDCS-28600259.summary: This is a dedication to the household god Silvanus by Gaius Julius Demetrius, likely from the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-28600260.text_edition: \n Silvano Silvestri sacrum Mestrius Florus votum solvit libens merito\n
EDCS-28600260.translation: Sacred to Silvanus of the Forest. Mestrius Florus paid his vow willingly and deservedly.
EDCS-28600260.summary: This is a dedication to Silvanus, the god of the wilderness, by Mestrius Florus, likely from the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-28600262.translation: To the deified Hadrian, their founder, the people of Mursa.
EDCS-28600262.summary: This inscription is a dedication to the deified Emperor Hadrian as the founder of Mursa, likely erected by the city's inhabitants.

EDCS-28600263.text_edition: \n Imperator Caesar divi Traiani Parthici filius divi Nervae nepos Traianus Hadrianus Augustus pontifex maximus tribunicia potestate XVII consul III pater patriae per legionem II adiutricem piam fidelem col(oniam) Aeliam Mursam fec(it)\n
EDCS-28600263.translation: Emperor Caesar Traianus Hadrianus Augustus, son of the deified Trajan the Parthian, grandson of the deified Nerva, pontifex maximus, holding tribunician power for the 17th time, consul for the 3rd time, father of the fatherland, founded the colony Aelia Mursa through the Second Adjutant Legion, pious and faithful.
EDCS-28600263.summary: This imperial inscription records the foundation of the colony Aelia Mursa by Emperor Hadrian, built through the efforts of the Legio II Adjutrix, dated to Hadrian's reign.

EDCS-28600264.text_edition: \n Dis Manibus Quinti Corvini Severiani Aemilianus procurator Auggustorum filio fecit\n
EDCS-28600264.translation: To the spirits of the underworld. Aemilianus, procurator of the Emperors, erected this for his son Quintus Corvinus Severianus.
EDCS-28600264.summary: This funerary inscription is a dedication to the deceased son of a procurator, likely dating to the period when such procurators served the emperors.

EDCS-28600265.text_edition: \n Caius Iulius Geminius Capellianus legatus Augusti pro praetore Mursensibus decreto decurionum\n
EDCS-28600265.translation: Gaius Julius Geminius Capellianus, imperial legate with praetorian power, by decree of the decurions, to the people of Mursa.
EDCS-28600265.summary: This inscription records an action or decree made by the imperial legate Gaius Julius Geminius Capellianus concerning the people of Mursa, likely during his governorship.

EDCS-28600266.text_edition: \n Titus Aurelius Avitus veteranus legionis II adiutricis nnorum LXXVI vivos sic sibi et Ulpiae Appianae coniugi pientissimae vivi sibi posuerunt\n
EDCS-28600266.translation: Titus Aurelius Avitus, a veteran of the Second Adjutant Legion, aged 76, erected this for himself and for his most devoted wife Ulpia Appiana while he was alive.
EDCS-28600266.summary: This funerary epitaph records a veteran of the Legio II Adjutrix, Titus Aurelius Avitus, who set up the monument for himself and his wife during their lifetime.

EDCS-28600267.text_edition: \n Dis Manibus Aurelio Secundo |(centurioni) legionis XIIII geminae Martiae victricis qui vixit nnos XL menses VIII dies XV Aureli Annianus |(centurio) et Maximianus signifer parenti bene merito posuerunt\n
EDCS-28600267.translation: To the spirits of the underworld. For Aurelius Secundus, a centurion of the Fourteenth Legion Gemina Martia Victrix, who lived 40 years, 8 months, and 15 days. Aurelius Annianus, a centurion, and Maximianus, a standard-bearer, erected this for their deserving father.
EDCS-28600267.summary: This funerary inscription is dedicated to a centurion of Legio XIIII Gemina Martia Victrix by his sons, one of whom is also a centurion and the other a standard-bearer, providing details of the deceased's life and rank.

EDCS-28600268.text_edition: \n Sisiae et Aurelio Sibiaeno vexillario et communicipii \n
EDCS-28600268.translation: To Sisia and Aurelius Sibiaenus, a standard-bearer and member of the association.
EDCS-28600268.summary: This inscription appears to be a dedication or memorial to Sisia and Aurelius Sibiaenus, who held positions as a standard-bearer and a member of a collegium or association.

EDCS-28600269.text_edition: \n Niger Bataronis filius Sveltrius eques ala I Aravacorum nnorum XXXVII stipendiorum XVII hic situs est Marcellus et Publius consobrini idem heredes pos<u=I>erunt \n
EDCS-28600269.translation: Niger, son of Bataro, a cavalryman of the First Wing of Aravacorum, aged 37 and with 17 years of service, lies here. Marcellus and Publius, his cousins and also his heirs, erected this.
EDCS-28600269.summary: This funerary epitaph records the burial of Niger, a cavalryman in the ala I Aravacorum, detailing his age and service length, and is erected by his cousins who were also his heirs.

EDCS-28600274.text_edition: \n Iovi Optimo Maximo Publius Aelius Callimorphus Augustalis coloniae pro se et suis votum solvit libens merito\n
EDCS-28600274.translation: To Jupiter Best and Greatest. Publius Aelius Callimorphus, an Augustalis of the colony, fulfilled his vow willingly and deservedly for himself and his family.
EDCS-28600274.summary: This votive inscription records Publius Aelius Callimorphus, a prominent freedman in the colony, fulfilling a vow to Jupiter for himself and his household.

EDCS-28600275.text_edition: \n Sacrum Dis Magnis Maioribus et Sanctissimae Sanctitati voto libens posuit Marcus Flavius Felix\n
EDCS-28600275.translation: Sacred to the Great and Mighty Gods and to the Most Holy Sanctity. Marcus Flavius Felix willingly erected this vow.
EDCS-28600275.summary: This votive inscription is dedicated by Marcus Flavius Felix to the 'Great Gods' and a personified 'Sanctity', indicating a personal vow to these deities or concepts.

EDCS-28600276.text_edition: \n Iustus cives Sopianenses vivus sibi et c<u=O>(m) qu[a vixit ------ \n
EDCS-28600276.translation: Iustus, a citizen of Sopianenses, while alive, for himself and for [his wife... who lived...].
EDCS-28600276.summary: This is a funerary epitaph for Iustus, a citizen of Sopianenses, and likely his wife, from the Roman province of Pannonia.

EDCS-28600277.text_edition: \n Deo Libero Patri Aurelius Conserus ex procuratore armamentarii instrumentorumqcui viribus suis inseruit per instantia Venanti filii sui vineae arpennes CCCC ex his V CVPENS V TERMINIID V VALLESIBUS V caballi RI\n
EDCS-28600277.translation: To Liber Pater, Aurelius Conserus, formerly procurator of the armaments depot, erected by his own strength, by the urging of his son Venantius, 400 measures of vineyard, of which 5 are CVPENS, 5 are TERMINIID, 5 are in the valleys, 5 are in the RI.
EDCS-28600277.summary: This dedication to Liber Pater was set up by Aurelius Conserus, a former procurator, and his son Venantius, recording the dimensions of a vineyard.

EDCS-28600278.summary: This is a fragmentary dedication to Liber Pater, likely to be completed with the name of the dedicator.

EDCS-28600279.text_edition: \n cilla c-rissi pietati vit et SAP mater\n
EDCS-28600279.translation: ... cilla with piety and SAP, mother...
EDCS-28600279.summary: This is a very fragmentary inscription, possibly a dedication or epitaph, mentioning a person named cilla and their mother.

EDCS-28600281.text_edition: \n Aesculapio et Hygiae Domitius Magnus praefectus alae X X ala<r=B>um Severianae <et=IE> I <T=P>hra<c=E>(um) veteranae \n
EDCS-28600281.translation: To Aesculapius and Hygieia, Domitius Magnus, prefect of the X XX ala Severiana and the veteran ala I Thracum, [dedicated this].
EDCS-28600281.summary: This is a dedication to the healing deities Aesculapius and Hygieia by Domitius Magnus, a military officer commanding two cavalry units.

EDCS-28600282.text_edition: \n Genio Titi Iuli Oviniani exs sic voto solvit merito libens laetus\n
EDCS-28600282.translation: To the Genius of Titus Iulius Ovinianus, he fulfilled his vow willingly, happily, joyfully, deservedly.
EDCS-28600282.summary: This is a votive inscription dedicated to the Genius of Titus Iulius Ovinianus.

EDCS-28600283.text_edition: \n Herculi Augusto pro salute dddominorum nnnostrorum Augggustorum Oetcius VB Corvinus I Faustino et Rufino consulibus\n
EDCS-28600283.translation: To Hercules Augustus, for the safety of our lords Augustuses, Oetcius VB Corvinus [dedicated this] under the consulship of Faustinus and Rufinus.
EDCS-28600283.summary: This dedication to Hercules Augustus, likely made by Oetcius VB Corvinus, is dated by the consuls Faustinus and Rufinus and includes a prayer for the emperors' safety.

EDCS-28600284.text_edition: \n Iovi Optimo Maximo pro salute ddominorum nnostrorum Severi et Antonini Auggustorum [[et Gaetae n]]obilissmi Caesaris Caius Iulius Euticus sic votum solvit libens merito\n
EDCS-28600284.translation: To Jupiter Optimus Maximus, for the safety of our lords Severus and Antoninus Augustuses [and of the most noble Caesar Gaetae], Gaius Iulius Euticus fulfilled his vow willingly, deservedly.
EDCS-28600284.summary: This votive inscription to Jupiter Optimus Maximus was set up by Gaius Iulius Euticus for the safety of Emperors Severus and Antoninus, and the Caesar Gaetae.

EDCS-28600285.text_edition: \n Silvano Domestico Aurelius Regulianus actarius alae votum solvit libens merito\n
EDCS-28600285.translation: To Silvanus Domesticius, Aurelius Regulianus, scribe of the ala, fulfilled his vow willingly, deservedly.
EDCS-28600285.summary: This is a votive inscription to Silvanus Domesticius, dedicated by Aurelius Regulianus, an administrative officer in a cavalry unit.

EDCS-28600286.text_edition: \n Silvanas Auggustas Aurelius Lipor veteranus alae et Aurelius Valens miles legionis II adiutricis piae <f=E>(idelis) Severianae <fil=ET>(ius) eius et Aurelia Severa filia \n
EDCS-28600286.translation: To the Silvanus Augustuses, Aurelius Lipor, veteran of the ala, and Aurelius Valens, soldier of the II adiutrix pia fidelis Severiana legion, his son, and Aurelia Severa, his daughter, [dedicated this].
EDCS-28600286.summary: This dedication to Silvanus Augustuses was made by a veteran soldier, his son who is currently serving in a legion, and his daughter.

EDCS-28600287.text_edition: \n Mucatra duplicarius alae I Thracum veteranae Antoninianae pro sa<l=I>ute sua et suaorum votum solvit libens merito \n
EDCS-28600287.translation: Mucatra, a duplicarius (cavalryman with double pay) of the First Thracian Wing, the 'Antoninian' veterans, fulfills his vow for the safety of himself and his relatives, gladly and deservedly.
EDCS-28600287.summary: This is a votive inscription by a cavalryman of the First Thracian Wing, dedicated for his own and his family's safety.

EDCS-28600288.text_edition: \n Aurelio Iulio ex decurione alae I Thracum vixit annos et Aureliae Ie vivae coniugi eius et Aureliae Maximianae vixit nnos XXVII et Aurelio Caesiano veterano cohortis X praetoriae vixit annos et Aurelio Caesio evokato sic exsercitatori eqquitum praetorianorum vixit nnos X filiis eorum et Aurelio Crescentino militi legionis II adiutricis stipendiorum III vixit nnos XXI Claudius Pertinax strator consularis parentibus bene merentibus faciundum curavit\n
EDCS-28600288.translation: To Aurelius Iulius, formerly decurion of the First Thracian Wing, who lived [X] years, and to Aurelia Ieva, his living wife, and to Aurelia Maximiana, who lived 27 years, and to Aurelius Caesianus, veteran of the Tenth Praetorian Cohort, who lived [X] years, their sons, and to Aurelius Caesius, evocatus and instructor of the Praetorian cavalry, who lived 10 years, their sons, and to Aurelius Crescentinus, soldier of the Second Adjutant Legion, with 3 years of service, who lived 21 years. Claudius Pertinax, consular staff officer, had this made for his well-deserving parents.
EDCS-28600288.summary: This is a funerary inscription commemorating multiple family members, with the final part dedicated by a consular official for his parents.

EDCS-28600290.text_edition: \n Dis Manibus Iulio Probo veterano legionis II adiutricis qui vixit nnos LIIII patri et Caio Iulio Honorato militi cohortis |(milliariae) Numidarum stipendiorum III fratri qui vixit nnos XXII et Aeliae Decoratae matri vivae Caius Iulius Probianus candidatus legionis supra scriptae - quam et ceteri fratres patri et fratri matri vivae faciendum curaverunt\n
EDCS-28600290.translation: To the Spirits of the Departed: For Iulius Probus, veteran of the Second Adjutant Legion, who lived 54 years, father, and for Gaius Iulius Honoratus, soldier of the thousand-man Numidian Cohort, with 3 years of service, who lived 22 years, brother, and for Aelia Decorata, mother, alive. Caius Iulius Probianus, candidate of the aforementioned legion, along with his other brothers, had this made for their father and brother and alive mother.
EDCS-28600290.summary: This is a funerary inscription for a veteran, his brother, and their mother, commissioned by a son who is a candidate in the same legion.

EDCS-28600291.text_edition: \n Dis Manibus Septimiae Iunillae coniugi karae quae vixit annos XXV Publius Aelius Constans quaestor armorum legionis II adiutricis vivus sibi fecit et Constantinae filiae optamus cuncti\n
EDCS-28600291.translation: To the Spirits of the Departed: For Septimia Iunilla, beloved wife, who lived 25 years. Publius Aelius Constans, quaestor of arms of the Second Adjutant Legion, made this for himself while alive, and for Constantina, his daughter. We all wish [her peace].
EDCS-28600291.summary: This is a funerary inscription commissioned by a military officer for his deceased wife and daughter, and also for himself.

EDCS-28600292.text_edition: \n Terso Precionis filius Scordiscus eques alae Frontonianae turma Lobasini armorum custos annorum XXX stipendiorum XVI hic situs est \n
EDCS-28600292.translation: Tersus, son of Precionis, a Scordiscan, cavalryman of the Frontonian Wing, in the turma of Lobasinus, armor-bearer, aged 30 years, with 16 years of service, lies here.
EDCS-28600292.summary: This is a tombstone for a 30-year-old cavalryman of the Frontonian Wing, detailing his origin and military service.

EDCS-28600293.text_edition: \n Dis Manibus Ulpio Valentino equiti alae annorum XL<V=X> hic situs est et Valentino filio annorum XII Meitimae f<iliae=EC> Aelius Provincialis heres posuit \n
EDCS-28600293.translation: To the Spirits of the Departed: For Ulpius Valentinus, cavalryman of the wing, aged 45 years, lies here, and for Valentinus, his son, aged 12 years, and for Meitima, his daughter. Aelius Provincialis, the heir, placed this.
EDCS-28600293.summary: This is a funerary inscription for a cavalryman, his son, and his daughter, erected by the heir.

EDCS-28600294.text_edition: \n Dis Manibus Antonius Filoquirius Augustalis coloniae Aquincensium Vibiae Serapiae\n
EDCS-28600294.translation: To the Spirits of the Departed: For Antonius Filoquirius, Augustalis of the colony of Aquincum, and for Vibius Serapias.
EDCS-28600294.summary: This is a funerary inscription dedicated to two individuals, one of whom is an Augustalis from the colony of Aquincum.

EDCS-28600295.text_edition: \n Dis Manibus Aeliae Marcellae coniugi pientissimae vixit annos XVIIII et Aeliae Verae socrae sic vixit annos LV et sibi vivus et Aurelio Magniano filio suo Marcus Aurelius Zosimus posuit si qui huic titulo manus intulerit extra bonos mores opto tale dolorem AT--V\n
EDCS-28600295.translation: To the Spirits of the Departed: For Aelia Marcella, most dutiful wife, who lived 19 years, and for Aelia Vera, mother-in-law, who lived 55 years, and for himself while alive, and for Aurelius Magnianus, his son. Marcus Aurelius Zosimus placed this. Whoever lays hands on this inscription, beyond proper customs, I wish him such sorrow. AT--V.
EDCS-28600295.summary: This is a funerary inscription set up by a husband for his wife and mother-in-law, also for himself and his son, and includes a curse against desecration.

EDCS-28600296.translation: To Artio, son of Miletusmari, [aged] C.
EDCS-28600296.summary: This is a very brief funerary inscription, likely for a child or young person, stating the name and age.

EDCS-28600298.text_edition: \n Marcus Ulpius Sabinus vilicus stationis et Claudia Pusinna parentes filiae Sabinae nnorum XIIII hic sita est Dis Manibus\n
EDCS-28600298.translation: Marcus Ulpius Sabinus, overseer of the station, and Claudia Pusinna, parents, for their daughter Sabina, aged 14 years, lies here. To the Spirits of the Departed.
EDCS-28600298.summary: This is a tombstone erected by parents for their 14-year-old daughter, who was the overseer of a station.

EDCS-28600299.text_edition: \n Caio Iulio Vale--- vixit an(nos) LX dies \n
EDCS-28600299.translation: To Gaius Julius Valerius, who lived 60 years and [some] days.
EDCS-28600299.summary: This is a funerary epitaph recording the lifespan of Gaius Julius Valerius.

EDCS-28600300.summary: This is a funerary epitaph recording the lifespan of Matomarus, son of Tti.

EDCS-28600301.text_edition: \n Dis Manibus Marcus Ulpius Italicus annorum XL et Aelia Publi filia Verodumna annorum XXX hic siti sunt Ulpii Veranus Proculus Verina filii et heredes faciendum curaverunt\n
EDCS-28600301.translation: To the Spirits of the Underworld. Marcus Ulpius Italicus, aged 40, and Aelia Verodumna, daughter of Publius, aged 30, are buried here. Ulpius Veranus Proculus and Verina, children and heirs, saw to the construction.
EDCS-28600301.summary: This is a funerary monument for Marcus Ulpius Italicus and Aelia Verodumna, erected by their children and heirs.

EDCS-28600303.text_edition: \n Aesculapio et Hygiae Augustis Aurelius Artemidorus beneficiarius legati legionis II adiutricis piae fidelis Severianae agens curam custodiarum votum solvit libens merito Modesto et Probo consulibus\n
EDCS-28600303.translation: To Aesculapius and Hygieia, divine patrons. Aurelius Artemidorus, a beneficiarius of the legate of Legio II Adiutrix Pia Fidelis Severiana, in charge of the guard rooms, willingly and deservedly paid his vow, under the consulship of Modestus and Probus.
EDCS-28600303.summary: This is a votive inscription dedicated to Aesculapius and Hygieia by Aurelius Artemidorus, an officer in Legio II Adiutrix, during the consulship of Modestus and Probus.

EDCS-28600304.text_edition: \n Asclepio et Hygiae Marcius Marcellus medicus sub capsario Valerio Praesente evokato sic votum solvit libens merito\n
EDCS-28600304.translation: To Asclepius and Hygieia. Marcius Marcellus, physician under Valerius Praesens, an evocatus serving as subcapsarius, willingly and deservedly paid his vow.
EDCS-28600304.summary: This is a votive inscription to Asclepius and Hygieia made by Marcius Marcellus, a physician, who served under Valerius Praesens.

EDCS-28600306.text_edition: \n Deo Arimanio Libella leo fratribus voto dicavit\n
EDCS-28600306.translation: To the god Arimanius. Libella dedicated [this] to her brothers, by vow.
EDCS-28600306.summary: This is a votive inscription dedicated to the god Arimanius by Libella for her brothers.

EDCS-28600307.text_edition: \n Danuvio Defluenti Haterius Callinicus votum V \n
EDCS-28600307.translation: To the Flowing Danube. Haterius Callinicus paid his vow 5 times.
EDCS-28600307.summary: This is a votive inscription to the river god Danuvius, dedicated by Haterius Callinicus.

EDCS-28600308.text_edition: \n August- ceterisque dis huiusce loci Caius Valerius Sabinianus legatus Augusti pro praetore\n
EDCS-28600308.translation: To the Emperor [Augustus] and the other local gods. Gaius Valerius Sabinianus, imperial legate with praetorian authority.
EDCS-28600308.summary: This is a dedication to the Emperor and local deities by Gaius Valerius Sabinianus, an imperial legate.

EDCS-28600310.text_edition: \n Dis Conservatoribus Caius Iulius Geminus Capellianus legatus Augusti pro praetore sodalis Titius\n
EDCS-28600310.translation: To the Guardian Spirits. Gaius Iulius Geminus Capellianus, imperial legate with praetorian authority, a member of the Titius sodality.
EDCS-28600310.summary: This is a dedication to guardian deities by Gaius Iulius Geminus Capellianus, an imperial legate and member of a sodality.

EDCS-28600311.text_edition: \n Eponae Augustae Apulenus Ianuarius eques legionis II adiutricis piae fidelis votum solvit libens merito\n
EDCS-28600311.translation: To Epona, the divine patroness. Apulenus Ianuarius, an equestrian officer of Legio II Adiutrix Pia Fidelis, willingly and deservedly paid his vow.
EDCS-28600311.summary: This is a votive inscription to the goddess Epona by Apulenus Ianuarius, an equestrian officer of Legio II Adiutrix.

EDCS-28600312.text_edition: \n Fortunae Reduci Publius Cosinius Felix vir clarissimus legatus Auggustorum pro praetore\n
EDCS-28600312.translation: To Fortune the Bringer-Back, Publius Cosinius Felix, most distinguished man, governor of the emperors.
EDCS-28600312.summary: This is a dedication to Fortuna Redux by Publius Cosinius Felix, a high-ranking imperial governor, likely from the 3rd century CE.

EDCS-28600313.translation: Iulius Aelianus, standard-bearer of the first century, erected this to the genius of the century.
EDCS-28600313.summary: This is a dedication by Iulius Aelianus to the genius of his military century, likely Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-28600314.text_edition: \n Genio collegio scaenicorum Titus Flavius Secundus monitor dono dedit\n
EDCS-28600314.translation: Titus Flavius Secundus, monitor of the guild of actors, gave this as a gift to the genius of the guild.
EDCS-28600314.summary: This records a gift by Titus Flavius Secundus to the genius of the actors' guild, likely Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-28600315.text_edition: \n Genio Imperatoris Publi Licini Gallieni Invicti Augusti Clementius Silvius vir egregius agens vices praesidis et Valerius Marcellinus praefectus legionis protector Augusti nostri agens vices legati municipes ex provincia Raetia solverunt laeti libentes merito Paterno et Archesilao consulibus\n
EDCS-28600315.translation: To the Genius of the Emperor Gallienus Augustus the Unconquered, Clementius Silvius, most excellent man, acting in place of the governor, and Valerius Marcellinus, prefect of the emperor's elite guards, acting in place of the legate, from the citizens of the province of Raetia, happily, gladly, and deservedly offered this, under the consulship of Paternus and Archesilaus.
EDCS-28600315.summary: This is a dedication by officials and citizens from Raetia to the Genius of Emperor Gallienus, dating to the consulship of Paternus and Archesilaus (likely c. 260s CE).

EDCS-28600317.text_edition: \n Herculi Augusto Titus Flavius Victor tribunus militum pro praefecto legionis II adiutricis votum solvit libens merito\n
EDCS-28600317.translation: Titus Flavius Victor, tribune of soldiers and acting prefect of the Second Adjutant Legion, gladly and deservedly fulfilled his vow to Hercules Augustus.
EDCS-28600317.summary: This records a soldier's fulfillment of a vow to Hercules Augustus, by Titus Flavius Victor, a military officer of Legio II Adiutrix, likely 3rd century CE.

EDCS-28600318.text_edition: \n Herculi Augusto sacrum ob salutem et reditum domini nostri Imperatoris Severi Alexandri Pii Felicis Augusti et Iuliae Mamaeae Augustae matris Augusti nostri et castrorum Gaius Iulius Carinus praefectus legionis II adiutricis piae fidelis A[lexandrianae] Severianae ex trecenario votum solvit libens merito Maximo et Paterno consulibus\n
EDCS-28600318.translation: Sacred to Hercules Augustus. For the safety and return of our lord Emperor Severus Alexander Pius Felix Augustus, and Julia Mamaea Augusta, mother of our emperor and of the camp, Gaius Julius Carinus, prefect of the loyal and faithful Second Adjutant Legion of Alexandria and Severiana, from the 300-man unit, gladly and deservedly fulfilled his vow, under the consulship of Maximus and Paternus.
EDCS-28600318.summary: This is a vow fulfillment by Gaius Julius Carinus to Hercules Augustus for the safety of Emperor Severus Alexander and Julia Mamaea, dating to the consulship of Maximus and Paternus (likely c. 230s CE).

EDCS-28600319.text_edition: \n Iovi Accioni Patrio Suetrius Sabinus legatus Augusti pro praetore \n
EDCS-28600319.translation: To Jupiter Accion, the ancestral god, Suetrius Sabinus, governor of the emperor.
EDCS-28600319.summary: This is a dedication to Jupiter Accion by Suetrius Sabinus, a high-ranking imperial governor, likely from the 3rd century CE.

EDCS-28600320.text_edition: \n Dis Reducibus Patriis Suetrius Sabinus legatus Augusti pro praetore et consularis \n
EDCS-28600320.translation: To the Ancestral Gods Who Brought Back [the Emperor], Suetrius Sabinus, governor of the emperor and ex-consul.
EDCS-28600320.summary: This is a dedication to the Dis Redux Patriis by Suetrius Sabinus, a former governor, likely from the 3rd century CE.

EDCS-28600323.text_edition: \n Iovi Optimo Maximo pro salute et reditu et victoria Imperatorum duorum Augustorum Titus Aelius Celsus cum Petronia Respecta coniuge et Tito Aelio Celsin<o=U> filio Kalendis Augustis dedicaverunt feliciter Macrino et Celso consulibus votum libentes merito Marti Titus Aelius Celsinus Genio Augusto Titus Aelius Celsus Minervae Aug(ustae) Petr(onia) Resp(ecta) \n
EDCS-28600323.translation: For Jupiter Best and Greatest, for the safety, return, and victory of the two emperors Augusti, Titus Aelius Celsus with his wife Petronia Respecta and their son Titus Aelius Celsinus dedicated this happily on the Kalends of August, gladly and deservedly. [Vow to] Mars. Titus Aelius Celsus to the Genius Augustus. Petronia Respecta to Augusta Minerva. Dated under the consulship of Macrinus and Celsus.
EDCS-28600323.summary: This is a family dedication to Jupiter Optimus Maximus, Mars, the Genius Augustus, and Minerva Augusta for imperial safety and victory, dated to the consulship of Macrinus and Celsus (likely c. 224 CE).

EDCS-28600324.text_edition: \n Iovi Optimo Maximo Aelius Rufus mensor legionis II adiutricis votum solvit libens merito\n
EDCS-28600324.translation: Aelius Rufus, surveyor of the Second Adjutant Legion, gladly and deservedly fulfilled his vow to Jupiter Best and Greatest.
EDCS-28600324.summary: This records a soldier's fulfillment of a vow to Jupiter Optimus Maximus, by Aelius Rufus, a surveyor of Legio II Adiutrix, likely Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-28600325.text_edition: \n Iovi Optimo Maximo Lucius Aemilius Optatus tribunus militum legionis II adiutricis votum solvit libens merito\n
EDCS-28600325.translation: To Jupiter, Best and Greatest, Lucius Aemilius Optatus, military tribune of the Second Legion Adiutrix, willingly and deservedly fulfilled his vow.
EDCS-28600325.summary: This inscription records a vow fulfilled by Lucius Aemilius Optatus, a military tribune, to Jupiter, likely dating to the Roman imperial period.

EDCS-28600326.text_edition: \n Iovi Optimo Maximo Titus Arsinius Sita veteranus legionis II adiutricis votum solvit libens merito\n
EDCS-28600326.translation: To Jupiter, Best and Greatest, Titus Arsinius Sita, a veteran of the Second Legion Adiutrix, willingly and deservedly fulfilled his vow.
EDCS-28600326.summary: This inscription records a vow fulfilled by Titus Arsinius Sita, a veteran, to Jupiter, likely dating to the Roman imperial period.

EDCS-28600327.text_edition: \n Iovi Optimo Maximo Marcus Aurelius Pompeius decurio coloniae Aquincensium votum solvit libens laetus merito\n
EDCS-28600327.translation: To Jupiter, Best and Greatest, Marcus Aurelius Pompeius, decurion of the colony of Aquincum, willingly, joyfully, and deservedly fulfilled his vow.
EDCS-28600327.summary: This inscription records a vow fulfilled by Marcus Aurelius Pompeius, a decurion of Aquincum, to Jupiter, likely dating to the Roman imperial period.

EDCS-28600328.text_edition: \n Iovi Optimo Maximo Batsio Gupi filius votum solvit libens merito\n
EDCS-28600328.translation: To Jupiter, Best and Greatest, Batsio, son of Gupius, willingly and deservedly fulfilled his vow.
EDCS-28600328.summary: This inscription records a vow fulfilled by Batsio, son of Gupius, to Jupiter, likely dating to the Roman imperial period.

EDCS-28600329.text_edition: \n [Iovi Optimo Maximo pro salute] [Imperatoris Caesaris ?] []] Claudius Pompeius Faustus decurio coloniae Aquincensium aedilicius IIviralis praefectus collegii fabrum itemque patronus duxit collegium supra scriptum in ambulativis V Kalendas Augustas [Imperatore et ?] [ consulibus]\n
EDCS-28600329.translation: For Jupiter, Best and Greatest, for the health of the Emperor Caesar [?], Claudius Pompeius Faustus, decurion of the colony of Aquincum, formerly aedile and twice duovir, prefect of the guild of fabric workers, and also patron, led the aforementioned guild in the processional walkways on the fifth day before the Kalends of August, during the consulship of [Emperor and ?].
EDCS-28600329.summary: This inscription records the dedication of a guild's processional walkway by Claudius Pompeius Faustus, a prominent official from Aquincum, to Jupiter, for the emperor's health, likely dating to the Roman imperial period.

EDCS-28600330.text_edition: \n Iovi Optimo Maximo Tiberius Claudius Valens beneficiarius votum solvit libens merito\n
EDCS-28600330.translation: To Jupiter, Best and Greatest, Tiberius Claudius Valens, a beneficiary, willingly and deservedly fulfilled his vow.
EDCS-28600330.summary: This inscription records a vow fulfilled by Tiberius Claudius Valens, a beneficiary, to Jupiter, likely dating to the Roman imperial period.

EDCS-28600331.text_edition: \n Iovi Optimo Maximo Flavius Decimus votum solvit libens laetus merito\n
EDCS-28600331.translation: To Jupiter, Best and Greatest, Flavius Decimus, willingly, joyfully, and deservedly fulfilled his vow.
EDCS-28600331.summary: This inscription records a vow fulfilled by Flavius Decimus to Jupiter, likely dating to the Roman imperial period.

EDCS-28600332.text_edition: \n Iovi Optimo Maximo Quintus Host<i=F>us Fortunatus beneficiarius procuratoris votum solvit libens merito \n
EDCS-28600332.translation: To Jupiter, Best and Greatest, Quintus Hostilius Fortunatus, a beneficiary of the procurator, willingly and deservedly fulfilled his vow.
EDCS-28600332.summary: This inscription records a vow fulfilled by Quintus Hostilius Fortunatus, a beneficiary of a procurator, to Jupiter, likely dating to the Roman imperial period.

EDCS-28600333.text_edition: \n Iovi Optimo Maximo Marcus Livius Iuvenalis beneficiarius procuratoris Augusti votum solvit libens animo\n
EDCS-28600333.translation: To Jupiter, Best and Greatest, Marcus Livius Iuvenalis, a beneficiary of the procurator of Augustus, willingly and wholeheartedly fulfilled his vow.
EDCS-28600333.summary: This inscription records a vow fulfilled by Marcus Livius Iuvenalis, a beneficiary of the procurator of Augustus, to Jupiter, likely dating to the Roman imperial period.

EDCS-28600334.text_edition: \n I NLIL M Iovi Optimo Maximo Iunoni Verus BENE VONLIL solvit libens merito m\n
EDCS-28600334.translation: To Jupiter Best and Greatest, Juno, Verus BENE VONLIL fulfilled [his] vow willingly and deservedly.
EDCS-28600334.summary: This inscription records a vow fulfilled by Verus to Jupiter and Juno, likely dating to the Roman imperial period, with some text missing or unclear.

EDCS-28600335.text_edition: \n Iovi Optimo Maximo Oratius Proculus eques cohortis |(milliariae) Maurorum votum solvit libens merito\n
EDCS-28600335.translation: To Jupiter, Best and Greatest, Oratius Proculus, a cavalryman of a milliary cohort of Moors, gladly and deservedly fulfilled his vow.
EDCS-28600335.summary: A vow fulfilled by Oratius Proculus, a cavalryman of the cohort of Moors, to Jupiter, likely dating to the Imperial period.

EDCS-28600336.text_edition: \n Iovi Optimo Maximo pro salute domini nostri Marci Aureli A[nton]ini Pii Felicis Augusti Lucius Septimius Constantinus optio spei legionis II adiutricis piae fidelis Antoninianae votum solvit libens merito [Imperatore Antonino] et Advento consulibus\n
EDCS-28600336.translation: To Jupiter, Best and Greatest, for the health of our lord Marcus Aurelius Antoninus Pius Felix Augustus, Lucius Septimius Constantinus, junior officer (optio) of the II Adjutrix Pius Fidelis Antoniniana legion, gladly and deservedly fulfilled his vow, with Antoninus and Adventus as consuls.
EDCS-28600336.summary: A vow for the emperor's health made by Lucius Septimius Constantinus, an optio of the II Adjutrix legion, to Jupiter, dated by the consulship of Antoninus and Adventus, likely in the 3rd century CE.

EDCS-28600337.text_edition: \n Iovi Optimo Maximo Lucius Septimius Lister veteranus alae I Etureorum ex IC votum solvit libens merito\n
EDCS-28600337.translation: To Jupiter, Best and Greatest, Lucius Septimius Lister, a veteran of the I Etureorum ala, from the province of Hispania Citerior, gladly and deservedly fulfilled his vow.
EDCS-28600337.summary: A vow fulfilled by Lucius Septimius Lister, a veteran of the I Etureorum cavalry unit, to Jupiter, likely dating to the Imperial period.

EDCS-28600338.text_edition: \n Iovi Optimo Maximo Valerius Victor beneficiarius procuratoris votum libens merito\n
EDCS-28600338.translation: To Jupiter, Best and Greatest, Valerius Victor, a deputy (beneficiarius) of a procurator, gladly and deservedly fulfilled his vow.
EDCS-28600338.summary: A vow fulfilled by Valerius Victor, a beneficiarius of a procurator, to Jupiter, likely dating to the Imperial period.

EDCS-28600339.text_edition: \n Iovi Optimo Maximo Vibius Augustalis beneficiarius procuratoris Augusti votum solvit libens laetus merito\n
EDCS-28600339.translation: To Jupiter, Best and Greatest, Vibius Augustalis, a deputy (beneficiarius) of the emperor's procurator, gladly, joyfully, and deservedly fulfilled his vow.
EDCS-28600339.summary: A vow fulfilled by Vibius Augustalis, a beneficiarius of an imperial procurator, to Jupiter, likely dating to the Imperial period.

EDCS-28600340.text_edition: \n Iovi Optimo Maximo votum vovit Victor Ressati libertus libens\n
EDCS-28600340.translation: To Jupiter, Best and Greatest, Victor, freedman of Ressatius, made his vow gladly.
EDCS-28600340.summary: A vow made by Victor, a freedman of Ressatius, to Jupiter, likely dating to the Imperial period.

EDCS-28600341.text_edition: \n Iovi Optimo Maximo Marcus Ulpius Carus beneficiarius procuratoris votum libens merito\n
EDCS-28600341.translation: To Jupiter, Best and Greatest, Marcus Ulpius Carus, a deputy (beneficiarius) of a procurator, gladly and deservedly fulfilled his vow.
EDCS-28600341.summary: A vow fulfilled by Marcus Ulpius Carus, a beneficiarius of a procurator, to Jupiter, likely dating to the Imperial period.

EDCS-28600342.text_edition: \n Iovi Optimo Maximo Marcus Ulpius Quintianus votum libens merito\n
EDCS-28600342.translation: To Jupiter, Best and Greatest, Marcus Ulpius Quintianus gladly and deservedly fulfilled his vow.
EDCS-28600342.summary: A vow fulfilled by Marcus Ulpius Quintianus to Jupiter, likely dating to the Imperial period.

EDCS-28600343.text_edition: \n Iovi Optimo Maximo Ulpius Romanus veteranus legionis II adiutricis piae fidelis votum libens solvit merito\n
EDCS-28600343.translation: To Jupiter, Best and Greatest, Ulpius Romanus, a veteran of the II Adjutrix Pius Fidelis legion, gladly and deservedly fulfilled his vow.
EDCS-28600343.summary: A vow fulfilled by Ulpius Romanus, a veteran of the II Adjutrix legion, to Jupiter, likely dating to the Imperial period.

EDCS-28600344.text_edition: \n Iovi Optimo CO Iovi Optimo Maximo Marcus Ulpius Ursinus beneficiarius procuratoris Augusti votum solvit libens merito\n
EDCS-28600344.translation: To Jupiter, Best and Greatest, Marcus Ulpius Ursinus, a deputy (beneficiarius) of the emperor's procurator, gladly and deservedly fulfilled his vow.
EDCS-28600344.summary: A vow fulfilled by Marcus Ulpius Ursinus, a beneficiarius of an imperial procurator, to Jupiter, likely dating to the Imperial period.

EDCS-28600345.text_edition: \n Iovi Optimo Maximo et Dianae Patr sacrum Caius Iulius Artemo tribunus militum legionis II adiutricis votum solvit libens merito\n
EDCS-28600345.translation: Sacred to Jupiter Best and Greatest and to Diana. Gaius Julius Artemo, tribune of soldiers of the Second Adiutrix Legion, willingly and deservedly fulfilled his vow.
EDCS-28600345.summary: This is a votive inscription dedicated to Jupiter and Diana by a military tribune of the Second Adiutrix Legion.

EDCS-28600346.text_edition: \n Iovi Optimo Maximo Dis Deabusque omnibus GAI Atilius Pollianus decurio aedilicius et Pollio Augustalis coloniae Aquincensium votum solverunt libentes merito\n
EDCS-28600346.translation: To Jupiter Best and Greatest and to all the Gods and Goddesses. Gaius Atilius Pollianus, decurion and former aedile, and Pollio Augustalis of the colony of Aquincensium, willingly and deservedly fulfilled their vow.
EDCS-28600346.summary: This votive inscription records two prominent citizens of the colony of Aquincensium dedicating offerings to Jupiter and all the gods.

EDCS-28600347.text_edition: \n Iovi Optimo Maximo et Genio |(centuriae) Septimius Cogitatus custos armorum et Septimius Carus milites legionis II adiutricis piae fidelis Severianae votum solverunt libentes merito Pompeiano et Paeligniano consulibus\n
EDCS-28600347.translation: To Jupiter Best and Greatest and to the Genius of the century. Septimius Cogitatus, keeper of the arms, and Septimius Carus, soldiers of the Second Adiutrix Legion, pious and faithful, Severian, willingly and deservedly fulfilled their vow, in the consulship of Pompeianus and Paelignianus.
EDCS-28600347.summary: Two soldiers of the Second Adiutrix Legion, one an armor keeper, dedicate a vow to Jupiter and the Genius of their century, dated by consuls.

EDCS-28600348.text_edition: \n Iovi Optimo Maximo et Iunoni Aurelius Vindex duplarius legionis II adiutricis votum solvit libens laetus merito\n
EDCS-28600348.translation: To Jupiter Best and Greatest and to Juno. Aurelius Vindex, duplarius of the Second Adiutrix Legion, willingly, gladly, and deservedly fulfilled his vow.
EDCS-28600348.summary: This votive inscription records Aurelius Vindex, a duplarius of the Second Adiutrix Legion, dedicating an offering to Jupiter and Juno.

EDCS-28600349.text_edition: \n Iovi Optimo Maximo Iunoni Reginae Gaius Titius Eutyches votum solvit libens merito\n
EDCS-28600349.translation: To Jupiter Best and Greatest and to Queen Juno. Gaius Titius Eutyches willingly and deservedly fulfilled his vow.
EDCS-28600349.summary: This is a personal votive inscription by Gaius Titius Eutyches to Jupiter and Juno Regina.

EDCS-28600350.text_edition: \n Iovi Optimo Maximo et Laribus militaribus ceterisque dis Caius Iulius Pisibanus Maximus Aemilius Papus tribunus laticlavius legionis II adiutricis votum solvit libens merito\n
EDCS-28600350.translation: To Jupiter Best and Greatest and to the military Lar(es) and all other gods. Gaius Julius Pisibanus Maximus Aemilius Papus, a broad-striped tribune of the Second Adiutrix Legion, willingly and deservedly fulfilled his vow.
EDCS-28600350.summary: A high-ranking tribune of the Second Adiutrix Legion dedicates a vow to Jupiter and the military Lares and other gods.

EDCS-28600351.text_edition: \n Iovi Optimo Maximo Conservatori Caius Roscius Crispinus decurio coloniae votum solvit libens merito\n
EDCS-28600351.translation: To Jupiter Best and Greatest the Preserver. Gaius Roscius Crispinus, decurion of the colony, willingly and deservedly fulfilled his vow.
EDCS-28600351.summary: A decurion of a colony dedicates a votive offering to Jupiter Optimus Maximus Conservator.

EDCS-28600352.text_edition: \n Iovi Optimo Maximo Dulceno sic Heliopolitano sacrum feciit Aurelius Secundus veteranus legionis II adiutricis piae pro se et suis posuit votum solvit libens merito\n
EDCS-28600352.translation: Sacred to Jupiter Best and Greatest Dulcenus, also called Heliopolitanus. Aurelius Secundus, a veteran of the Second Adiutrix Legion, pious, set this up for himself and his family, and willingly and deservedly fulfilled his vow.
EDCS-28600352.summary: A veteran of the Second Adiutrix Legion dedicates a votive offering to Jupiter Dulcenus Heliopolitanus for himself and his family.

EDCS-28600353.text_edition: \n Hammoni Iovi Optimo Maximo et Laribus militaribus ceterisque Dis Marcus Caecilius Rufinus Marianus tribunus laticlavius legionis IV Flaviae votum solvit libens merito\n
EDCS-28600353.translation: To Ammon, Jupiter Best and Greatest, and to the military Lar(es) and all other Gods. Marcus Caecilius Rufinus Marianus, broad-striped tribune of the Fourth Flavia Felix Legion, willingly and deservedly fulfilled his vow.
EDCS-28600353.summary: A high-ranking tribune of the Fourth Flavia Felix Legion dedicates a vow to Ammon, Jupiter, and the military Lares and other gods.

EDCS-28600354.text_edition: \n Libero Augusto Caius Clodius Saturninus primus pilus legionis II adiutricis piae fidelis\n
EDCS-28600354.translation: To Liber Augustus. Gaius Clodius Saturninus, primus pilus of the Second Adiutrix Legion, pious and faithful.
EDCS-28600354.summary: Gaius Clodius Saturninus, a primus pilus of the Second Adiutrix Legion, is honored or records his service, potentially in relation to Liber Augustus.

EDCS-28600355.text_edition: \n Libero Patri Augusto sacrum Valerius Emeritus ex decurione alae I Thracum posuit\n
EDCS-28600355.translation: Sacred to Liber Pater Augustus. Valerius Emeritus, formerly a decurion of the I Thracum cavalry unit, set this up.
EDCS-28600355.summary: This is a dedication to Liber Pater Augustus, set up by Valerius Emeritus, a former decurion, likely from the 2nd or 3rd century CE.

EDCS-28600356.text_edition: \n Libero et Liberae Augustis Marcus Aurelius Latio aram votum posuit pro salute Marci Aureli Iovini frumentarii legionis II adiutricis stipendiorum VIIII\n
EDCS-28600356.translation: To the August deities Liber and Libera, Marcus Aurelius Latio, a frumentarius (corn supply officer) of the II Adiutrix legion, set up this altar in fulfillment of a vow for the health of Marcus Aurelius Iovinus, after nine years of service.
EDCS-28600356.summary: This is a votive altar dedicated to Liber and Libera by Marcus Aurelius Latio, a frumentarius in the II Adiutrix legion, for his own and his relative's health, dating to the later 2nd or 3rd century CE.

EDCS-28600357.text_edition: \n Liber<ae=EI> Aucci Sineca sexvir coloniae Aquincensium pro se et suis votum solvit libens merito \n
EDCS-28600357.translation: To Libera Auggusta, Aucci Sineca, a sexvir (member of a college of six) of the colony of Aquincum, willingly and deservedly fulfilled a vow for himself and his family.
EDCS-28600357.summary: This is a votive inscription to Libera Auggusta by Aucci Sineca, a sexvir of the colony of Aquincum, likely dating to the 2nd or 3rd century CE.

EDCS-28600358.text_edition: \n Maioribus sanctis Lucius Naevius Campanus kastrensis legionis IIII Flaviae pro salute sua et suorum votum solvit libens merito\n
EDCS-28600358.translation: To the holy Ancestors, Lucius Naevius Campanus, a castrensis (camp prefect or quartermaster) of the IIII Flavia Felix legion, willingly and deservedly fulfilled a vow for his own health and that of his family.
EDCS-28600358.summary: This is a votive inscription to the 'holy Ancestors' by Lucius Naevius Campanus, a camp official of the IIII Flavia Felix legion, likely dating to the 3rd century CE.

EDCS-28600359.text_edition: \n Marti Augusto pro salute et incolumitate domini nostri Carini Pii Felicis Augusti Aelius Paternianus vir egregius praefectus legionis II adiutricis agens vices legati votum solvit libens merito\n
EDCS-28600359.translation: To Mars Augustus, for the health and safety of our lord Carinus Pius Felix Augustus, Aelius Paternianus, an outstanding man and prefect of the II Adiutrix legion, acting in place of a legate, willingly and deservedly fulfilled a vow.
EDCS-28600359.summary: This is a votive inscription to Mars Augustus by Aelius Paternianus, prefect of the II Adiutrix legion, for the health of the emperor Carinus, likely dating to 282-285 CE.

EDCS-28600360.text_edition: \n Marti Augusto sacrum Caius Cuspius Secundus exercitator legionis II adiutricis pro salute militum et sua quod evocatus vovit centurio solvit libens merito\n
EDCS-28600360.translation: Sacred to Mars Augustus. Caius Cuspius Secundus, a trainer of the II Adiutrix legion, for the health of the soldiers and his own, fulfilled a vow which he had made when he was an evocatus (a recalled veteran), a centurion, willingly and deservedly.
EDCS-28600360.summary: This is a dedication to Mars Augustus by Caius Cuspius Secundus, a trainer and former evocatus and centurion of the II Adiutrix legion, for the health of himself and his soldiers, likely from the 2nd or 3rd century CE.

EDCS-28600361.translation: To the Great Mother of the Gods.
EDCS-28600361.summary: This is a brief dedication to the Great Mother of the Gods (likely Cybele), the exact context and date are unknown.

EDCS-28600362.text_edition: \n Dis Militaribus et Genio loci pro salute et reditu Imperatoris Caesaris Marci Aureli Antonini Pii Invicti Augusti Clodius Marcellinus S C tribuni militum legionis II adiutricis piae fidelis Antoninianae translatus ex legione X Fretensi Antoniniana numini eius semper devotissimus\n
EDCS-28600362.translation: To the Military Gods and the Genius of the Place, for the health and safe return of our Emperor Caesar Marcus Aurelius Antoninus Pius Felix Invictus Augustus, Clodius Marcellinus, a military tribune of the II Adiutrix Pia Fidelis Antoniniana legion, transferred from the X Fretensis Antoniniana legion, always most devoted to his divinity, fulfilled a vow.
EDCS-28600362.summary: This is a votive inscription to the Military Gods and Genius loci by Clodius Marcellinus, a military tribune of the II Adiutrix legion, for the health and return of Emperor Marcus Aurelius Antoninus (likely Caracalla), dating to his reign (198-217 CE).

EDCS-28600363.text_edition: \n Dis Militaribus Salutaribus Haterius Saturninus legatus Auggustorum pro praetore cum Latroniano filio tribuno militum\n
EDCS-28600363.translation: To the Military Gods, the Salutary Ones. Haterius Saturninus, imperial legate pro praetore, with his son Latronianus, a military tribune.
EDCS-28600363.summary: This inscription records Haterius Saturninus, an imperial legate pro praetore, and his son Latronianus, a military tribune, likely dating to the 2nd or 3rd century CE.

EDCS-28600364.text_edition: \n Minitrae Aurelius Florianus veteranus ex beneficiario consularis pro salutem sic meam sic et omnium meorum numini eius d<e=I>dicavi Sabino II et Venusto consulibus \n
EDCS-28600364.translation: To Minitra. Aurelius Florianus, a veteran formerly a beneficiarius consularis (staff officer), dedicated himself to her divinity for the health of myself and of all my family, under the consulship of Sabinus II and Venustus.
EDCS-28600364.summary: This is a dedication to the goddess Minitra by Aurelius Florianus, a veteran and former staff officer, for his and his family's health, dating to 164 CE.

EDCS-28600365.text_edition: \n Deo Sancto Iovi Optimo Maximo Soli Invict<o=I> pro bono communi sacrum \n
EDCS-28600365.translation: Sacred to the Holy God, Jupiter Best and Greatest, the Unconquered Sun, for the common good.
EDCS-28600365.summary: This inscription is a dedication to Jupiter Optimus Maximus and Sol Invictus, likely from the imperial period, for the general welfare.

EDCS-28600366.text_edition: \n Invicto Devo votum solvit libens merito P BO CO M CO T B\n
EDCS-28600366.translation: Invictus, having fulfilled his vow, gladly and deservedly.
EDCS-28600366.summary: This inscription records a vow fulfilled to the god Invictus, likely from the Roman imperial period, with abbreviated phrases.

EDCS-28600367.text_edition: \n Deo Invicto Allidius Hermes votum solvit libens merito\n
EDCS-28600367.translation: Allidius Hermes gladly and deservedly fulfilled his vow to the Unconquered God.
EDCS-28600367.summary: This inscription records a vow fulfilled by Allidius Hermes to the Unconquered God, likely from the Roman imperial period.

EDCS-28600368.text_edition: \n Deo Invicto Cornelius Abascantus libertus Corneli Paulli primi pili legionis II adiutricis votum solvit libens merito\n
EDCS-28600368.translation: Cornelius Abascantus, freedman of Cornelius Paullus, primipilaris of the Second Legion Adjutrix, gladly and deservedly fulfilled his vow to the Unconquered God.
EDCS-28600368.summary: This inscription records a vow fulfilled by Cornelius Abascantus, a freedman and former primipilaris of the Second Legion Adjutrix, to the Unconquered God, likely from the Roman imperial period.

EDCS-28600369.text_edition: \n Deo Invicto pro salute familiae Tiberi Hateri Saturnini legati Auggustorum pro praetore Arpocras pater posuit\n
EDCS-28600369.translation: Arpocras, father, set this up to the Unconquered God for the safety of the family of Tiberius Haterius Saturninus, legate of the Augusti pro praetore.
EDCS-28600369.summary: This inscription records the dedication of a monument by Arpocras to the Unconquered God for the safety of the family of Tiberius Haterius Saturninus, a high-ranking imperial legate, likely from the Roman imperial period.

EDCS-28600370.text_edition: \n Deo Invicto Mithrae Caius Iulius Castinus legatus Auggustorum pro praetore\n
EDCS-28600370.translation: Gaius Julius Castinus, legate of the Augusti pro praetore, [dedicated this] to the Unconquered God Mithras.
EDCS-28600370.summary: This inscription records a dedication by Gaius Julius Castinus, a high-ranking imperial legate, to the Unconquered God Mithras, likely from the Roman imperial period.

EDCS-28600371.text_edition: \n Invicto Mythrae sic Nabarze Tiberius Pontius Pontianus tribunus laticlavius legionis II adiutricis piae fidelis dat dedicat votum solvit libens merito\n
EDCS-28600371.translation: Nabarze, Tiberius Pontius Pontianus, broad-striped tribune of the Second Legion Pia Fidelis, gladly and deservedly gives, dedicates, and fulfilled his vow to Invictus Mithras.
EDCS-28600371.summary: This inscription records the fulfillment of a vow by Tiberius Pontius Pontianus, a tribune of the Second Legion Pia Fidelis, to Invictus Mithras, likely from the Roman imperial period.

EDCS-28600372.text_edition: \n Soli Invicto Mithrae pro salute Sexti Iuventi NIL ex visu Pannonius libertus\n
EDCS-28600372.translation: Sextus Iuventius NIL Pannonius, freedman, vowed, as seen in a vision, to Sol Invictus Mithras for his safety.
EDCS-28600372.summary: This inscription records a vow made by Sextus Iuventius NIL Pannonius, a freedman, to Sol Invictus Mithras for his safety, likely from the Roman imperial period.

EDCS-28600373.text_edition: \n Soli Invicto Valerius Vitalis votum solvit libens merito\n
EDCS-28600373.translation: Valerius Vitalis gladly and deservedly fulfilled his vow to Sol Invictus.
EDCS-28600373.summary: This inscription records a vow fulfilled by Valerius Vitalis to Sol Invictus, likely from the Roman imperial period.

EDCS-28600374.text_edition: \n Nemesi Augustae Marcus Aurelius Valens quot sic clavicularius vovit veteranus legionis II adiutricis Antoninianae persolvit Imperatore domino nostro Antonino II Sacerdot<e=I> consulibus \n
EDCS-28600374.translation: Marcus Aurelius Valens, clavicularius, veteran of the Second Antonine Legion, paid his vow under our lord Emperor Antoninus and the consuls Sacerdos and [another consul], as vowed.
EDCS-28600374.summary: This inscription records the payment of a vow by Marcus Aurelius Valens, a veteran and member of a collegium, to the goddess Nemesis Augusta during the consulship of Antoninus and Sacerdos, likely in the mid-3rd century CE.

EDCS-28600375.text_edition: \n Deae Nemesi Augustae pro salute Aureliae Audentiae filiae suae et pro sua incolumitate Aurelius Audentius curator rei publicae Aquincensium e sacerdotalibus provinciae libenti animo suscepta vota conplevit\n
EDCS-28600375.translation: For the goddess Nemesis Augusta, for the health of his daughter Aurelia Audentia and for his own safety, Aurelius Audentius, curator of the Aquincensian republic and priest of the province, fulfilled his vows with a willing heart.
EDCS-28600375.summary: This inscription records a votive offering to Nemesis Augusta by Aurelius Audentius, a local official, for the health of his daughter and himself, likely in the Roman province of Pannonia Inferior.

EDCS-28600376.text_edition: \n Neptuno Caius Iulius Geminus Capellianus legatus Augusti pro praetore\n
EDCS-28600376.translation: To Neptune, Caius Julius Geminus Capellianus, imperial legate with praetorian authority.
EDCS-28600376.summary: This inscription records a dedication to Neptune by Caius Julius Geminus Capellianus, a high-ranking Roman military official, likely in Pannonia Inferior.

EDCS-28600377.text_edition: \n Numini Augusto et Genio Imperatoris Caesaris Titi Aeli Hadriani Antonini collegium Augustalium impendiis suis fecerunt praefecto Caio Iulio Crescente locus publice datus decreto decurionum\n
EDCS-28600377.translation: To the August divinity and the genius of the Emperor Caesar Titus Aelius Hadrianus Antoninus, the Augustal college made this at their own expense, for the prefect Caius Julius Crescens, a place was publicly granted by decree of the decurions.
EDCS-28600377.summary: This inscription records the erection of a monument by the Augustal college to the emperor and its genius, with a public space granted by the town council for the prefect Caius Julius Crescens, in Aquincensium.

EDCS-28600378.text_edition: \n Nymphis Marcus Fo<v=N>iacius Verus quinquennalis sacerdotalis dignissimis merito \n
EDCS-28600378.translation: To the Nymphs, Marcus Foviacius Verus, quinquennial priest and holder of the office of quinquennalis, with just deserts.
EDCS-28600378.summary: This inscription records a dedication to the Nymphs by Marcus Foviacius Verus, a local magistrate in Aquincensium, likely during the 2nd century CE.

EDCS-28600379.text_edition: \n N<y=I>mphis sacrum Iulius Pusinnio immunis legionis II adiutricis <p=R>(iae) fidelis votum libens merito \n
EDCS-28600379.translation: Sacred to the Nymphs, Julius Pusinnio, exempt soldier of the Second Legion Adjutrix, pious and faithful, fulfilled his vow willingly and deservedly.
EDCS-28600379.summary: This inscription records a votive offering to the Nymphs by Julius Pusinnio, a soldier of the Legio II Adjutrix, likely in Pannonia Inferior.

EDCS-28600380.text_edition: \n I(ovi) O(ptimo) M(aximo) D(olicheno) Harfuar<e=II>nus Surus ex regione Dolica vico Arfuaris Silva votum solvit Modesto et Probo consulibus \n
EDCS-28600380.translation: To Jupiter Optimus Maximus Dolichenus, Harfuanus Surus from the district of Dolica, village of Arfuaris Silva, paid his vow under the consulship of Modestus and Probus.
EDCS-28600380.summary: This inscription records a votive offering to Jupiter Dolichenus by Harfuanus Surus, identified by his place of origin, under the consuls Modestus and Probus (likely AD 209).

EDCS-28600381.translation: To Silvanus Domesticius and the Lares.
EDCS-28600381.summary: This is a short dedication to Silvanus Domesticius and the household gods (Lares), likely a private offering.

EDCS-28600382.text_edition: \n Silvano Silvestri Accius Maximus d<e=I>curio coloniae Aquincensium quinquennalis votum solvit libens merito \n
EDCS-28600382.translation: To Silvanus Silvestris, Accius Maximus, decurion and quinquennial magistrate of the colony of Aquincensium, paid his vow willingly and deservedly.
EDCS-28600382.summary: This inscription records a votive offering to Silvanus Silvestris by Accius Maximus, a local official in the colony of Aquincensium.

EDCS-28600383.text_edition: \n Silvano Domestico Aurelius <Ph=F>yylargus p<ro=OR> se et suis votum solvit libens merito \n
EDCS-28600383.translation: To Silvanus Domesticius, Aurelius Phylargus paid his vow for himself and his family with a willing heart and deservedly.
